gmw_00003625.name: ILTJ0037+2410
gmw_00003625.overview: ILTJ0037+2410 is a high-redshift quasar [arXiv:2210.01811]. Spectroscopic observations of the source reveal consistent Lyman-alpha and nitrogen V emission line luminosities and equivalent widths [arXiv:2210.01811].

sources.summary: The paper notes that ILTJ0037+2410 was observed with both HET and Subaru, with consistent Ly+N V luminosities, equivalent widths, and spectroscopic redshifts.

quotes.quote: The quasar ILTJ0037+2410 was observed with both by HET and Subaru, allowing a comparison of the absolute flux scale and the wavelength calibration. Both the derived Ly +N v luminosities and the measured EW _ Ly + N v are in good agreement with luminosities of 10 ^ 43.68^ +0.05 _ 0.14 and 10 ^ 43.69^ +0.16 _ 0.17 erg s ^ 1 and EWs of 14 ^ +2 _ 6 and 16 ^ +2 _ 3 for the Subaru and HET spectrum, respectively. The spectroscopic redshifts also agree very well with a difference of 0.01 from our fitting procedure (see Section sec:spectroscopic_obs ).

gmw_00003626.name: ILTJ0121+2940
gmw_00003626.overview: ILTJ0121+2940 is a high-redshift quasar with an uncertain spectroscopic redshift estimated at either z=5.24 or z=5.34 [arXiv:2210.01811]. The object may potentially be a broad absorption line quasar or host extremely high-velocity outflows [arXiv:2210.01811].

sources.summary: The paper highlights ILTJ0121+2940 as a quasar with additional emission lines, uncertain redshift estimates, and possible BAL behavior or extremely high-velocity outflows.

quotes.quote: This quasar and ILTJ1043+4048 are the only quasars where we have detected additional emission lines. In the spectrum of ILTJ0121+2940 we have identified as Ly , O i , Si iv , and C iv , which can also be used to determine the spectroscopic redshift. A redshift of z=5.24 0.01 would match the Ly , O i , Si iv emission lines with a blueshift of C iv . However, this redshift does not match the Ly line at 7700 A , which would be shifted to 7585 A for z=5.24 and our template fitting procedure yielded a redshift of z=5.34 0.02 instead (see Section subsec:redshifts ).

Coincidentally, this wavelength region (7529-7600 A ) had to be masked out, due to a cosmic ray at that exact location. Therefore, the redshift of this quasar remains unclear and both redshift estimates are quoted in Table tab:quasar_properties .

This quasar could potentially be a broad absorption line (BAL) quasar with narrow Ly (e.g. Ross2015MNRAS.453.3932R ) or extremely high velocity outflows (e.g. Rodriguez2020ApJ...896..151R ) causing Ly and N v absorption.

gmw_00003627.name: ILTJ1043+4048
gmw_00003627.overview: ILTJ1043+4048 is a high-redshift quasar located at a redshift of z=4.89 [arXiv:2210.01811]. Its spectrum exhibits Ly, O I, Si IV, and C IV emission lines, and it hosts a supermassive black hole with a high mass of approximately 6.9 billion solar masses [arXiv:2210.01811].

sources.summary: The paper describes ILTJ1043+4048 as a quasar selected despite catalogue issues, with identified Ly, O I, Si IV, and C IV lines and a high inferred SMBH mass.

quotes.quote: Similarly to ILTJ1013+3518, this quasar was omitted from the final sample due to a g band S/N of >5 in the Legacy Surveys DR8 catalogue used in . However, in the Legacy Surveys DR7 catalogue the quasar has a negative g band S/N of 4.9 and was therefore previously selected for follow-up observations. Also for this quasar we identify Ly , O i , Si iv , and C iv in the spectrum. In this case the observed wavelengths of the lines are consistent with the redshift determined by template fitting of z=4.89 0.03 .

Performing the same fitting procedure of the C iv line yields supermassive black hole mass of M_ BH M_ = 9.84^ +0.02 _ 0.01 , which is slightly higher than expected from its absolute UV magnitude ( M_ BH M_ = 9.1 ) when assuming a constant Eddington ratio and bolometric correction Inayoshi2020ARA&A..58...27I . However, similar masses have been reported for other quasars around z 5 , but it is at the high end of the expected SMBH mass range (see e.g. Vestergaard2008ApJ...674L...1V, Wang2015ApJ...807L...9W, Wu2015Natur.518..512W, Sbarrato2021A&A...655A..95S ).

gmw_00003628.name: BCG
gmw_00003628.overview: The SpARCS1049+56 BCG is a distant brightest cluster galaxy at z=1.709 [arXiv:2001.05776] that hosts a radio active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1905.05875] and exhibits a non-compact morphology [arXiv:1912.10716]. Although the galaxy itself lacks detected molecular gas [arXiv:2001.05776], it is surrounded by gas-rich companion galaxies [arXiv:2001.05776] and an off-center, clumpy stellar tail aligned with a massive molecular gas reservoir [arXiv:2203.13764].

sources.summary: SpARCS1049+56 BCG is a z=1.709 distant BCG with no detected CO(4-3) or continuum at its position; observations set H2 upper limits while gas-rich companions may merge into it.

quotes.quote: We report CO(4 3) and continuum map observations of the SpARCS1049+56 BCG at z=1.709 , one of the most distant known BCGs. Our observations yield M_ H _2 <1.1 10^ 10 M_ for the BCG; while in CO(4 3), we detect two gas-rich companions at the northeast and southeast of the BCG, within 20 kpc, with L^ _ CO(4 3) =(5.8 0.6) 10^ 9 K km s ^ 1 pc ^2 and (7.4 0.7) 10^ 9 K km s ^ 1 pc ^2 , respectively. The northern companion is associated with a pair of merging cluster galaxies, while the southern one shows a southern tail in CO(4 3), which was also detected in continuum, and we suggest it to be the most distant jellyfish galaxy for which ram pressure stripping is effectively able to strip off its dense molecular gas. This study probes the presence of rare gas-rich systems in the very central region of a distant cluster core, which will potentially merge into the BCG itself.

Molecular gas mass reservoir of the BCG Neither CO(4 3) nor continuum emission was found at the location of the BCG. However, by using Eqs. ( eq:LpCO , eq:MH2 ), we set a 3 upper limit for the H _2 molecular gas mass of the BCG that is equal to M_ H_2 <1.1 10^ 10 M_ , corresponding to L^ _ CO(4 3) <1.2 10^9 K km s ^ 1 pc ^2 . We adopted a resolution of 300 km s ^ 1 , a Galactic CO-to-H _2 conversion factor _ CO =4.36 M_ ( K km s ^ 1 pc ^2)^ 1 , and an excitation ratio r_ 41 =0.46 Carilli_Walter2013 . By using Eq. eq:Mmol_continuum and the upper limit of the BCG continuum, we also set a 3 upper limit M_ H_2 <1.7 10^ 10 M_ , which is consistent with what was obtained from the nondetection in CO(4 3).

sources.summary: The paper describes the SpARCS1049+56 BCG as a powerful radio BCG with high SFR after limited AGN correction, low-power radio-galaxy luminosity, and non-compact Galfit morphology.

quotes.quote: MRC 1138-262 and the SpARCS1049+56 BCG are powerful radio sources. They have L_ 4.5 GHz =1.9 10^ 28 W/Hz e.g., Carilli1997,Pentericci1997,Miley2006 and L_ 1.4 GHz =4.2 10^ 24 W/Hz Trudeau2019 , respectively.

Concerning the SpARCS1049+56 BCG at z 1.7 we have adopted the SFR =(860 130)M_ /yr reported by Webb2015b , who corrected the SFR for a limited AGN contribution of 20 to the far-infrared luminosity. The source has in fact a rest frame 1.4 GHz luminosity density of L_ 1.4 GHz =4.2 10^ 24 W/Hz Trudeau2019 , similar to those of low-power radio galaxies e.g., Chiaberge2009 .

For the remaining two BCGs ( and the SpARCS1049+56 BCG), our Galfit analysis did not show a compact morphology. However, it might be that this is due to the lower resolution images (pixel size 0.1 arcsec) used for the analysis.

sources.summary: The paper detects radio AGN emission coincident with the BCG in SpARCS1049, but argues the infrared-traced star formation is extended or clumpy and probably not centered in the BCG.

quotes.quote: We detect a radio source coincident with the Brightest Cluster Galaxy (BCG) that has a spectral index of =0.44 0.29 and is indicative of emission from an Active Galactic Nucleus. The radio luminosity is consistent with the average luminosity of the lower redshift BCG sample, but the flux densities are 6 below the predicted values of the star-forming Spectral Energy Distribution based on far infrared data. Our new fit fails to simultaneously describe the far infrared and radio fluxes. This, coupled with the fact that no other bright source is detected in the vicinity of the BCG implies that the star formation region, traced by the infrared emission, is extended or clumpy and not located directly within the BCG. Thus, we suggest that the star-forming core might not be driven by a single major wet merger, but rather by several smaller galaxies stripped of their gas or by a displaced cooling flow, although more data are needed to confirm any of those scenarios.

sources.summary: The paper studies stellar-mass growth near the SpARCS1049+56 BCG, finding an off-BCG clumpy stellar tail aligned with a massive molecular gas reservoir that may fuel intense star formation and build ICL.

quotes.quote: Here, we study the growth of the stellar mass in the vicinity of the Brightest Cluster Galaxy (BCG) in a z=1.7 cluster, SpARCS1049+56. We synthesize a reanalysis of existing HST imaging, a previously published measurement of the star formation rate, and the results of new radio molecular gas spectroscopy. These analyses represent the past, present and future star formation respectively within this system. We show that a large amount of stellar mass -- between (2.2 0.5) 10^ 10 M_ and (6.6 1.2) 10^ 10 M_ depending on the data processing -- exists in a long and clumpy tail-like structure that lies roughly 12 kpc off the BCG. Spatially coincident with this stellar mass is a similarly massive reservoir (1.0 0.7 10 ^ 11 M _ ) of molecular gas that we suggest is the fuel for the immense star formation rate of 860 130 M _ yr ^ 1 , as measured by infrared observations.

gmw_00003629.name: J1211-0118
gmw_00003629.overview: J1211-0118 is a luminous Lyman-break galaxy at a redshift of approximately 6.029 [arXiv:1910.10927, arXiv:2206.01879]. It exhibits weak Lyman-alpha emission [arXiv:1910.10927], a velocity gradient in its ionized carbon kinematics [arXiv:1910.10927], and detected dust continuum emission at both 120 and 160 microns [arXiv:1910.10927].

sources.summary: The paper targets J1211-0118 as a z~6 luminous LBG, finding weak Ly emission, Cii kinematics consistent with rotation, and detected dust continuum at 120 and 160 microns.

quotes.quote: As the first step to understand ISM properties of high redshift galaxies, we select three luminous LBGs, J1211-0118, J0235-0532, and J0217-0208 as targets for ALMA observations (Table tab_prop ). All targets are spectroscopically confirmed with Ly in the SHELLQs project 2018PASJ...70S..35M , and their redshifts, 6.0<z_ Ly <6.3 , are suitable for ALMA Band 6, 7, and 8 observations targeting Cii 158 m, Nii 122 m, and Oiii 88 m, respectively. J1211-0118 shows a weak Ly emission line with a rest-frame Ly equivalent width (EW) of EW^0_ Ly =6.9 0.8 \ .

The Cii velocity maps of J1211-0118 clearly shows a 220 km s ^ 1 velocity gradient. We also identify a gradient of 250 km s ^ 1 in J0217-0208. The velocity dispersions ( _ tot FWHM /2.35 ) are 70 , 110 , and 130 km s ^ 1 for J1211-0118, J0235-0532, and J0217-0208, respectively.

We detect dust continuum emission at 120 \ m from J1211-0118 and J0217-0208 at 5.3 and 7.1 significance levels, respectively. We also identify dust continua of J1211-0118 and J0217-0208 at 160 \ m , which are useful to constrain dust temperatures of these galaxies (see Section ss_LIR_Td ).

sources.summary: The paper selects J1211-0118 as a promising high-redshift ALMA follow-up target, forecasting possible 52 micron detection and improved gas-density and metallicity posteriors.

quotes.quote: If the galaxies in this data set tend to have high gas densities, n_ H 10^2-10^3 cm ^ 3 , then the [ ] 52 m lines from SXDF-NB1006-2, B14-65666, J0217-0208, and J1211-0118 can be detected within 10 hours, in agreement with the earlier work of Jones2020. As noted in Jones2020, modeling of [ ] and [ ] doublet emission lines in the spectra of LBGs at z 2-3 indicates gas densities in the n_ H 200-300 cm ^ 3 range 2016ApJ...816...23S,2016ApJ...826..159S,Strom:2016nae . If the ALMA galaxies are similar, or still more dense -- as one might expect for galaxies at higher redshifts -- then this bodes well for detecting their 52 m emission.

Using the Yang2020 model we forecast that the [ ] 52 m lines from SXDF-NB1006-2, B14-65666, J0217-0208, and J1211-0118 can be detected within 10 hours of on-source observing time, provided these galaxies have gas densities larger than n_ H 10^2-10^3 cm ^ 3 . We forecast the parameter space improvements that will be possible for the four most promising targets, SXDF-NB1006-2, B14-65666, J0217-0208, and J1211-0118, with a ten hour ALMA 52 m measurement. We find that the gas density constraint should tighten by 1-3 dex for these sources, while the metallicity posterior will narrow by as much as 1 dex for SXDF-NB1006-2, B14-65666, and J1211-0118.

sources.summary: The paper reports that J1211 has co-spatial FIR line detections consistent with prior work, with carbon-line emission more extended than oxygen-line emission.

quotes.quote: For the remaining systems (MACSJ0416-Y1, B14-65666, J0235, J1211, and J0217) for which co-spatial \ and \ emissions have been already reported in the literature Tamura:2019, Hashimoto:2019,Harikane:2019, Bakx:2020 , we have found results consistent with previous works. In Table tab:summary we list the \ and \ luminosities. Since for these galaxies the two FIR lines have been detected with high signal-to-noise ratio (SNR >8 ), we take advantage of these observations to investigate the extent of the FIR lines, as shown in Fig. fig:size (and Table tab:appendix ). We notice that \ is systematically larger than the \ and its extent is about two times larger than that of oxygen line. This is consistent with recent simulations by where the \ is concentrated in a compact region of 0.85 kpc, while the \ arise from a more extended area with a radius of 1.54 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper treats J1211-0118 as a z=6.029 high-redshift galaxy with multiwavelength ALMA FIR data but poorly constrained dust temperature, using published fluxes for dust-model estimates.

quotes.quote: J1211-0118 ( z=6.029 ) and J0217-0208 ( z=6.204 ) have been characterized by multiwavelength ALMA observations using band-6 ( _ rest 200 m ), band-7 ( _ rest 160 m ), and band-8 ( _ rest 90 m ) Harikane2020 . Although these galaxies have multiwavelength FIR observations, dust temperatures are only poorly constrained due to weak or non-detection in the band-8 continuum, leaving a narrow wavelength coverage of dust continuum detections. Thus, we treat these galaxies as having unconstrained dust temperatures. We used flux density measurements from Harikane2020 .

gmw_00003630.name: J0217-0208
gmw_00003630.overview: J0217-0208 is a luminous Lyman-break galaxy at a redshift of z=6.204 [arXiv:1910.10927, arXiv:2206.01879]. It exhibits strong Lyman-alpha emission [arXiv:1910.10927], a 250 km/s velocity gradient consistent with a rotating disk [arXiv:1910.10927], and carbon-line emission that is more extended than its oxygen-line emission [arXiv:2006.09402].

sources.summary: The paper targets J0217-0208 as a z~6 luminous LBG/LAE with strong Ly emission, a 250 km/s Cii velocity gradient, rotation-like kinematics, and low IRX.

quotes.quote: As the first step to understand ISM properties of high redshift galaxies, we select three luminous LBGs, J1211-0118, J0235-0532, and J0217-0208 as targets for ALMA observations (Table tab_prop ). All targets are spectroscopically confirmed with Ly in the SHELLQs project 2018PASJ...70S..35M , and their redshifts, 6.0<z_ Ly <6.3 , are suitable for ALMA Band 6, 7, and 8 observations targeting Cii 158 m, Nii 122 m, and Oiii 88 m, respectively. J1211-0118 shows a weak Ly emission line with a rest-frame Ly equivalent width (EW) of EW^0_ Ly =6.9 0.8 \ . J0235-0532 and J0217-0208 show strong Ly emission lines with EW^0_ Ly =41 2 , and 15 1 \ , respectively.

We also identify a gradient of 250 km s ^ 1 in J0217-0208. The velocity dispersions ( _ tot FWHM /2.35 ) are 70 , 110 , and 130 km s ^ 1 for J1211-0118, J0235-0532, and J0217-0208, respectively. Given the low angular resolution of the observations, there are various interpretations of the velocity gradients. A rotating galaxy disk would be one interpretation. t We apply an observational criterion for the classification of rotation- and dispersion-dominated systems, v_ obs /2 _ tot =0.4 , where v_ obs and _ tot are the full observed velocity gradient and the velocity dispersions 2009ApJ...706.1364F . We find that v_ obs /2 _ tot =1.6 and 0.96 for J1211-0118 and J0217-0208, respectively, indicating that these two galaxies are consistent with rotation dominated systems.
The IRX value of J0217-0208 is lower than the SMC relation, even if the IR luminosity is L_ IR =2.2 10^ 12 \ L_ (the dashed error bar).

sources.summary: The paper includes J0217-0208 among the strongest 52 micron follow-up candidates, noting detectability forecasts, large L10/SFR, and improved gas-density constraints despite weaker metallicity gains.

quotes.quote: If the galaxies in this data set tend to have high gas densities, n_ H 10^2-10^3 cm ^ 3 , then the [ ] 52 m lines from SXDF-NB1006-2, B14-65666, J0217-0208, and J1211-0118 can be detected within 10 hours, in agreement with the earlier work of Jones2020. As noted in Jones2020, modeling of [ ] and [ ] doublet emission lines in the spectra of LBGs at z 2-3 indicates gas densities in the n_ H 200-300 cm ^ 3 range 2016ApJ...816...23S,2016ApJ...826..159S,Strom:2016nae . If the ALMA galaxies are similar, or still more dense -- as one might expect for galaxies at higher redshifts -- then this bodes well for detecting their 52 m emission.

The improvements forecast are weakest for the case of J0217-0208. This galaxy has a very large L_ 10 / SFR and so high densities are already excluded for this system (at least under our Z Z_ prior). Nevertheless, we expect the gas density determination to improve for this galaxy although the metallicity constraint will not tighten.

sources.summary: The paper reports that J0217 has co-spatial FIR line detections consistent with prior work, with carbon-line emission more extended than oxygen-line emission.

quotes.quote: For the remaining systems (MACSJ0416-Y1, B14-65666, J0235, J1211, and J0217) for which co-spatial \ and \ emissions have been already reported in the literature Tamura:2019, Hashimoto:2019,Harikane:2019, Bakx:2020 , we have found results consistent with previous works. In Table tab:summary we list the \ and \ luminosities. Since for these galaxies the two FIR lines have been detected with high signal-to-noise ratio (SNR >8 ), we take advantage of these observations to investigate the extent of the FIR lines, as shown in Fig. fig:size (and Table tab:appendix ). We notice that \ is systematically larger than the \ and its extent is about two times larger than that of oxygen line. This is consistent with recent simulations by where the \ is concentrated in a compact region of 0.85 kpc, while the \ arise from a more extended area with a radius of 1.54 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper treats J0217-0208 as a z=6.204 high-redshift galaxy with multiwavelength ALMA FIR data but poorly constrained dust temperature, using published fluxes for dust-model estimates.

quotes.quote: J1211-0118 ( z=6.029 ) and J0217-0208 ( z=6.204 ) have been characterized by multiwavelength ALMA observations using band-6 ( _ rest 200 m ), band-7 ( _ rest 160 m ), and band-8 ( _ rest 90 m ) Harikane2020 . Although these galaxies have multiwavelength FIR observations, dust temperatures are only poorly constrained due to weak or non-detection in the band-8 continuum, leaving a narrow wavelength coverage of dust continuum detections. Thus, we treat these galaxies as having unconstrained dust temperatures. We used flux density measurements from Harikane2020 .

sources.summary: The paper’s ALMA [OIII] model gives J0217 at redshift 6.2 one of the tightest constraints, requiring roughly half-solar metallicity and relatively low gas density.

quotes.quote: For example, the 1- ( 2- ) lower bound on the metallicity for J0217, at redshift 6.2, is 0.59 Z_ ( 0.42 Z_ ). On the other hand, the lower bound on metallicity is rather loose for some of the galaxies in the sample, such as Y1 at redshift 8.3, for which Z 1.2 10^ 2 Z_ and Z 1.6 10^ 3 Z_ at 1- and 2- confidence, respectively. Likewise, the gas density bounds are variable with n_ H 25 (63) cm ^ 3 at 1- ( 2- ) confidence for J0217, but n_ H 1.0 10^3 (6.3 10^3) cm ^ 3 at 1- ( 2- ) for Y1.

The broad implication of these results is that the ISM is highly enriched with heavy elements in at least some of the current sample of ALMA galaxies; this occurs within only 500 Myr to 1 Gyr after the big bang. For example, Z 0.6 Z_ in J0217 at z=6.2 , when the age of this universe is 880 Myr.

gmw_00003631.name: A1689-zD1
gmw_00003631.overview: A1689-zD1 is a gravitationally lensed, high-redshift galaxy [arXiv:2004.12612, arXiv:2108.13479] classified as a sub-L* Lyman-break galaxy [arXiv:1505.01841, arXiv:2108.13479]. Its millimeter thermal dust emission reveals two spatial components [arXiv:1603.03222], suggesting a perturbed dynamical state that may indicate a major merger or an early forming disk [arXiv:1603.03222].

sources.summary: The paper models A1689zD1 as a lensed dusty star-forming z>7 galaxy, using three ALMA continuum detections to estimate dust temperature and mass.

quotes.quote: A1689zD1 was discovered by Bradley08 as an apparently bright ( m 25 AB) z>7 galaxy candidate thanks to strong gravitational-lensing (the magnification factor is _ GL 9 ; Bradley08 ) by the foreground galaxy cluster, A1689, at z=0.1832 Struble99 . The de-lensed magnitude of this galaxy is m 27 , which corresponds to a star-formation rate (SFR) of 6 M_  yr ^ 1 , using Kennicutt's conversion Kennicutt98 . Thus, this galaxy is much less active than sub-millimetre galaxies and even Lyman-break galaxies (see Watson15 ).

The galaxy was detected at the wavelength of 1.3 mm in ALMA Band 6, being the first discovery of dust beyond z=7 Watson15 . Follow-up ALMA observations in Band 7 brought another significant continuum detection at 0.87 mm Knudsen17 . We have also made follow-up ALMA observations in Band 8 and have detected the dust continuum at 0.73 mm, as described in the next subsection. There are thus three continuum detections of A1689zD1, making it an ideal galaxy to test our method for estimating the dust temperature and mass in z>7 galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper treats A1689-zD1 as a bright, lensed z>7 sub-L* galaxy, combining ALMA data to infer dust temperature, dust mass, star-formation rate, and tension with dust-production estimates.

quotes.quote: A1689-zD1 was identified in as a bright (m _ AB 25 ) z>7 galaxy. Due to the foreground galaxy cluster (A1689; Struble1999 ), it is magnified While is high, there is only little shear, and we do not account for any differential lensing effects in this paper by 9.3 Knudsen2017 . Its intrinsic UV magnitude indicates it is a sub- L * galaxy representing the bulk of galaxies at z = 7 Ono2018 . Band \ 6 observations at 1.3 mm by reported the first detection of dust beyond redshift 7, and indicated an intrinsic star-formation rate of 12 M_ yr^ 1 . Notably, the estimated dust mass of this normal galaxy (assuming 35 K) was found in tension to star-formation history and dust production estimates in .

Applying our method to A1689-zD1, we find a dust temperature T_ d =40_ 7 ^ +13 \ K and mass of M_ d =2.0^ +1.8 _ 1.0 \ 10^ 7 \ M_ . These values are obtained assuming a wide range of values for the metallicity Z=0.2-1 \ Z_ , and the burstiness parameter _ s =1-50 2021arXiv210605279V .

sources.summary: The paper studies A1689-zD1 as a gravitationally lensed z=7.5 sub-L* galaxy with detected dust emission, mapped millimeter components, and morphology suggesting disturbance or early disk formation.

quotes.quote: The gravitationally-lensed galaxy A1689-zD1 is one of the most distant spectroscopically confirmed sources ( z=7.5 ). It is the earliest known galaxy where the interstellar medium (ISM) has been detected; dust emission was detected with the Atacama Large Millimetre Array (ALMA). A1689-zD1 is also unusual among high-redshift dust emitters as it is a sub-L* galaxy and is therefore a good prospect for the detection of gaseous ISM in a more typical galaxy at this redshift.

We observed A1689-zD1 with ALMA in bands 6 and 7 and with the Green Bank Telescope (GBT) in band Q . To study the structure of A1689-zD1, we map the mm thermal dust emission and find two spatial components with sizes about 0.4-1.7 kpc (lensing-corrected). The rough spatial morphology is similar to what is observed in the near-infrared with HST and points to a perturbed dynamical state, perhaps indicative of a major merger or a disc in early formation.

sources.summary: The paper uses A1689-zD1's ALMA dust detection to argue that its large dust mass requires unusually efficient grain growth and dense cold gas at z = 7.5.

quotes.quote: Even assuming maximally efficient supernova dust production, the observed dust mass of the z = 7.5 galaxy A1689-zD1 requires very efficient grain growth. This, in turn, implies that in this galaxy the average density of the cold and dense gas, where grain growth occurs, is comparable to that inferred from observations of QSO host galaxies at similar redshifts.
The z = 7.5 LBG A1689-zD1 is currently the most distant UV-selected galaxy for which a dust continuum detection has been obtained with ALMA (Watson et al. 2015).

Finally, in the lower panel we show that the dust mass detected in A1689-zD1 requires very efficient grain growth, with a timescale _ acc,0 = 0.2 Myr , one order of magnitude shorter than required to reproduce the observed dust-to-gas ratio in the Milky Way and in most local dwarf galaxies (de Bennassuti et al. 2014). This, in turn, implies that the cold atomic and molecular phases of the ISM, where grain growth is more efficient, must have an average density of 10^4 cm^ 3 (see eq. eq:acc ).

sources.summary: The paper treats A1689-zD1 as an evolved dusty z=7.5 galaxy with high stars, metals, and dust but modest baryon mass, requiring rapid dust-production explanations.

quotes.quote: Watson15 have recently reported a detection of an evolved, dusty, normal galaxy (A1689-zD1) at redshift z = 7.5 0.2 . This intriguing discovery provides an important piece of the puzzle needed to fully understand the origins of cosmic dust. A1689-zD1 is not necessarily more challenging to the theory of cosmic dust formation than any previously detected high-redshift galaxies with large dust masses, but it has two distinctive properties which has not been anticipated to appear in combination at that redshift: (1) a very high content of stars, metals and dust; (2) a relatively modest baryon mass (and thus it is not an extreme SBG). It has been shown that stellar sources of dust are likely insufficient Michalowski15 , which opens up for other interpretations.

In the models constructed specifically for A1689-zD1 (see Fig. dts , right panel), the time between z = 7.5 and the point when the dust-to-stellar mass ratio M_ d /M_ stars starts to decline rapidly is about 2.5 Gyr.

sources.summary: The paper uses A1689-zD1 as a lensed z=7.5 Lyman-break galaxy with ALMA dust emission to constrain dust growth and supernova destruction efficiencies.

quotes.quote: Watson:2015aa recently detected a lensed LBG at z=7.5 , A1689-zD1 see also Knudsen:2016aa . By assuming a typical accretion time-scale of nearby galaxies (2 Myr at solar metallicity), they compared the calculated redshift evolution dust mass with the observation data of A1689-zD1, finding that the predicted dust mass is too small in comparison with the observation. Since those parameters may be uncertain, it is still worth making an effort of constraining the time-scales and efficiencies of dust production and destruction using A1689-zD1, in order to quantify how fast each process driving dust evolution works.

Likely values for the parameters Knudsen:2016aa derived the SFR of A1689-zD1 as 12 M _ yr ^ 1 from the submm continuum. Mancini:2015aa showed, using their semi-analytic models of galaxy formation and evolution, that A1689-zD1 requires very short accretion time-scale, _ acc,0 =0.2 Myr.

sources.summary: The paper compares its simulated galaxies with ALMA-observed A1689-zD1, describing the z=7.5 source as a sub-L* normal galaxy with measured submillimeter flux, SFR, dust mass, and intermediate dust parameters.

quotes.quote: We can compare Halo-11 and Halo-12 with one of the high- z galaxies observed by ALMA, A1689-zD1 at z 7.5 Watson15 . The sub-mm flux of the galaxy was 0.61 0.12 mJy , and the estimated SFR and dust mass were 12 yr^ 1 and 4 10^ 7 . The A1689-zD1 is a sub- L_ * galaxy, thus it was considered as one of the `normal' galaxies which are the dominant population at that epoch. The dust masses of Halo-11 and Halo-12 at z=7.5 are 5 10^ 6 and 8 10^ 7 , respectively; thus A1689-zD1 has intermediate parameters of Halo-11 and Halo-12. In addition to A1689-zD1, the LBGs with sub-mm fluxes of 0.1 mJy have been detected by recent ALMA observations Bowler18,Hashimoto18b,Tamura18 .

sources.summary: The paper describes A1689-zD1 as a strongly lensed z=7.13 galaxy with multi-band ALMA observations, robust dust-temperature constraints, and adopted flux and size measurements for modeling.

quotes.quote: A1689-zD1 ( z=7.13 ) is a strongly lensed galaxy with a magnification factor of =9.3 Knudsen2017 . It has been characterized by ALMA observations using band-6 ( _ rest 160 m ), band-7 ( _ rest 100 m ), band-8 ( _ rest 90 m ), and band-9 ( _ rest 50 m ). These multiwavelength observations indicate that A1689-zD1 has one of the most robust dust temperature constraints at z>7 Watson2015,Knudsen2017,Bakx2021 . We used flux density measurements from Bakx2021 , and size measurements from I20.

gmw_00003632.name: B14-65666
gmw_00003632.overview: B14-65666 is a high-redshift Lyman-break galaxy at z = 7.15 [arXiv:1802.05720, arXiv:2206.01879]. It exhibits a clumpy, multi-component morphology that suggests an ongoing merger [arXiv:2106.05279, arXiv:2206.01879], with a physical offset between its dust continuum emission and rest-frame ultraviolet light [arXiv:1802.05720].

sources.summary: The paper finds B14 is better justified by a one-component fit; its younger component dominates IRAC fluxes and Subaru spectroscopy revealed only Ly alpha.

quotes.quote: Currently, four of the 13 z> 7 spectroscopically-confirmed galaxies with Spitzer /IRAC excesses listed in Table tab:z7 have the appropriate ALMA data: JD1, A2744 YD4, MACS0416 Y1 and B14-65666 (henceforth YD4, Y1 and B14 for convenience), see Table tab:z7oiii . To determine accurate SEDs, for each of the aforementioned galaxies we use the relevant references in Table tab:z7 (and references therein) to compile HST ACS+WFC3/IR, ( B_ 435 , V_ 606 , I_ 814 , Y_ 105 , J_ 125 , JH_ 140 and H_ 160 bands), VLT/HAWK-I K _ s , as well as Spitzer /IRAC [ 3.6 ] and [ 4.5 ] photometry. For B14 we use near-infrared z , Y , J , and H photometry from VISTA, in addition to data in the VLT/HAWK-I K _ s and Spitzer /IRAC [ 3.6 ] and [ 4.5 ] bands. All HST upper limits and error bars represent 1 uncertainties, whilst those redward of these are 2 .

For Y1 and B14, on the other hand, the HST photometry and Spitzer /IRAC excess are well reproduced, but the models are unable to simultaneously match both the [ ] 88 m flux and dust continuum (in the case of B14, the [ ] 88 m flux is matched but the dust continuum is underpredicted, and in the case of Y1 the [ ] 88 m flux is underpredicted and dust continuum overpredicted). In comparing these fits to those assuming a single-component only, although the HST photometry can be reasonably well-reproduced for JD1 and YD4, the single-component fits fail to simultaneously reproduce both the ALMA constraints and the Spitzer /IRAC excess. In the cases of Y1 and B14, the ALMA constraints are better matched by the one-component fit, however the Spitzer /IRAC excess is only partially matched. In our two-component fits, a sizeable contribution to the IRAC flux arises from a more mature stellar component. For JD1, YD4 and Y1, the contribution to the IRAC fluxes from the recent burst of star formation is only 10-30 and the older component (characterised by Balmer ratios of 2) dominates the flux at 70 . This is due primarily to the relative weakness of the [ ] +H lines, for which we measure an equivalent width, EW( [ ] +H ) 25-106 \ (compared to EW( [ ] +H ) 450-7700 \ for a single-component). The exception to this trend is B14, whose IRAC fluxes remain dominated by the younger component by 60-75 (EW( [ ] +H ) 200 \ for the two-component model and EW( [ ] +H ) 3460 \ for the one-component model).

With this consideration, the additional free parameters in the fit to the B14 data are sufficiently penalised to justify only the one-component fit. Whilst such comparisons no doubt gloss over the complexities of defining the birth of such galaxies, they serve as an important illustration of the potential consequences from overlooking the consideration of multiple episodes of star formation. We provide a summary of the above comparison and the main properties of our fits in Table tab:sedres . Possible AGN contributions Throughout the SED analyses described above, we assumed only thermal contributions to the heating of the metal-rich gas. However, given recent observations suggesting possible AGN contributions at high redshift (e.g., tilvi16,laporte17b,mainali18 ), it is natural to consider how our results might change should there be non-thermal radiation components in our four galaxies. Influence on the [ ] ratio To quantify differences in the FIR/optical [ ] ratio arising from excitation by stellar or AGN contributions, we run Cloudy using v2.1 of the Binary Population and Spectral Synthesis (BPASS; eldridge17 ) models as well as the code's own AGN option (based on the model of mathews87 ). For both stellar and AGN cases the [ ] line ratio is computed over a range of electron densities n _ e = [ 3,10,100,1000 ] (cm ^ 3 ) and gas-phase metallicities Z= [ 0.05,0.2,0.4,1 ] (Z _ ) for a fixed ionisation parameter log U = 2.5. We show the results of these simulations in Figure fig:AGN , which demonstrates the AGN case makes little difference at fixed electron density and gas-phase metallicity (a result, we note, is unaffected by a change in the ionisation parameter). The similarity between the stellar and AGN results is particularly evident for electron densities n_ e 1000 cm ^ 3 , where relatively large ratios ( [ ] 88 m / [ ] 5007 0.1-1) are seen that increase with metallicity. Such a trend is consistent with the results in Section subsec:almaoiii and Figure fig:ratio and arises because of relatively low electron temperatures ( T_ e 15,000-20,000 ) where, for low density regimes, the line ratio is influenced primarily by temperature fluctuations rather than by collisional de-excitation. As the electron density increases to n_ e 1000 cm ^ 3 , however, order-of-magnitude differences appear: while both the stellar and AGN cases probe line ratios < 0.1 favouring particularly strong [ ] 5007 \ emission, collisional de-excitation of the [ ] 88 m line can occur and, combined with the higher temperatures ( T_ e > 20,000 ) possible in the AGN case, this increases excitation of [ ] 5007 \ whereas [ ] 88 m is quenched. As a result the ratio is driven down to low values of 0.01. For our sample of four sources, the measured electron densities and temperatures (see Section subsec:almaoiii ) probe regions of the parameter space where AGN do not have a significant effect and thus our [ ] line ratios are unlikely to be strong affected by non-thermal radiation. Observational evidence for AGN at z>7 ? Although we have found that the [ ] line ratio is only affected by a AGN contribution in regions of very high temperature and electron densities unlikely to be representative of the general z>7 population, it is nonetheless informative to consider whether our sample of four galaxies and those in the extended list of IRAC-excess sources in Table tab:z7 show any observational evidence for AGN activity, and if so at what level. At z> 7, the most useful diagnostics for distinguishing between thermal and non-thermal contributions are rest-frame UV emission lines and their ratios, in particular \ 1240,1243 , \ 1549 , \ 1640 \ and ] \ 1907,1909 \ feltre16,laporte17b,stark17,mainali18 . With an ionising energy of nearly 80 eV, the detection of \ alone is a strong indicator of non-thermal radiation feltre16 . Each of our four Spitzer /IRAC- and ALMA-selected objects have been targeted with NIR spectroscopy where the aforementioned emission lines reside at z> 7. 7.5-10 hrs of VLT/X-Shooter observations revealed only Ly in JD1 hashimoto18 and YD4 laporte17 and no rest-frame UV lines were seen in Y1 tamura19 . Furthermore, 4 hrs of Subaru/FOCAS observations revealed only Ly in B14 furusawa16 .

sources.summary: The paper argues B14-65666 has relatively high metallicity, likely pre-enriched gas, and evidence for extended star formation despite photometry also allowing a young starburst model.

quotes.quote: The strongest indications of extended star formation based on chemical evolution are for MACS1149-JD1 and B14-65666, where our method indicates relatively high metallicity. The lower bound on metallicity is 0.1 solar in both cases, considering both systematic and measurement uncertainties. Such enrichment suggests previous star formation lasting several tens of Myr or more as discussed above.

In the case of B14-65666, the photometric data are consistent with a young starburst of age 10 Myr and strong nebular emission Hashimoto2019 , but the data also permit extended star formation with a moderate Balmer break Roberts-Borsani2020 . Such a young single-starburst age would imply extremely high effective yields to reach the metallicity we derive. A more likely scenario in our view is that the gas may have been pre-enriched by previous generations of star formation, which do not necessarily dominate the observed SED nor the IRAC excess, but can nonetheless dominate the total stellar mass and chemical enrichment. This is demonstrated by the 2-component SED fit of : a young (3 Myr) starburst contributes most of the broadband photometric flux, while an extended star formation component constitutes 92 of the stellar mass (and we note that this model provides the best fit to the data based solely on log-likelihood).

sources.summary: The paper identifies ID65666 as a z ~ 7.17 Lyman-break galaxy with ALMA-detected dust emission offset from UV light, implying obscured star formation.

quotes.quote: We find that one galaxy in our sample (ID65666) is clearly detected in the ALMA images, with a measured peak flux of 124 24 Jy or 5.2 significance. ID65666 is the highest-redshift galaxy we targeted, with a best-fitting photometric redshift of z = 7.17_ 0.06 ^ +0.09 . The inclusion of new HSC data as shown in Fig. fig:sed , results in a slightly higher (but consistent) photometric redshift than previously obtained ( z = 7.04_ 0.15 ^ +0.16 ; Bowler2016 ). The photometric redshift we find is consistent with the tentative detection of the Lyman- emission line at z = 7.168 for this object by Furusawa2016 , and the recently the spectroscopic redshift of z = 7.152 derived from the FIR lines OIII 88 m and CII 158 m by Hashimoto2018 .

We find a physical offset of 3 kpc between the dust continuum emission and the rest-frame UV light probed by Hubble Space Telescope imaging for galaxy ID65666 at z = 7.17^ +0.09 _ 0.06 . The offset is suggestive of an inhomogeneous dust distribution, where 75 of the total star formation activity (SFR 70 M _ / yr ) of the galaxy is completely obscured.

sources.summary: The paper treats B14-65666 as one of the most promising high-redshift ALMA targets, forecasting 52 micron detectability and improved density and metallicity constraints.

quotes.quote: If the galaxies in this data set tend to have high gas densities, n_ H 10^2-10^3 cm ^ 3 , then the [ ] 52 m lines from SXDF-NB1006-2, B14-65666, J0217-0208, and J1211-0118 can be detected within 10 hours, in agreement with the earlier work of Jones2020. As noted in Jones2020, modeling of [ ] and [ ] doublet emission lines in the spectra of LBGs at z 2-3 indicates gas densities in the n_ H 200-300 cm ^ 3 range 2016ApJ...816...23S,2016ApJ...826..159S,Strom:2016nae . If the ALMA galaxies are similar, or still more dense -- as one might expect for galaxies at higher redshifts -- then this bodes well for detecting their 52 m emission.

Using the Yang2020 model we forecast that the [ ] 52 m lines from SXDF-NB1006-2, B14-65666, J0217-0208, and J1211-0118 can be detected within 10 hours of on-source observing time, provided these galaxies have gas densities larger than n_ H 10^2-10^3 cm ^ 3 . We forecast the parameter space improvements that will be possible for the four most promising targets, SXDF-NB1006-2, B14-65666, J0217-0208, and J1211-0118, with a ten hour ALMA 52 m measurement. We find that the gas density constraint should tighten by 1-3 dex for these sources, while the metallicity posterior will narrow by as much as 1 dex for SXDF-NB1006-2, B14-65666, and J1211-0118.

sources.summary: The paper includes B14-65666 among bright z 6-9 [OIII] and [CII] emitters, notes its multi-component morphology, and models its two resolved clumps separately.

quotes.quote: More than 30 EoR galaxies have been so far detected, and often spatially resolved in [ CII ] e.g. for compilations carniani2018, matthee2019 and nine very bright galaxies (see Table tab:higzsources ) at z 6-9 have been detected in [ OIII ] . The list includes MACS1149-JD1 hashimoto2018 , A2744-YD4 laporte2017 , MACS416-Y1 tamura2019 , SXDF-NB1006-2 inoue2016 , B14-65666 hashimoto2019 , BDF3299 carniani2017 , J0121 J0235, J1211 harikane2020 . All of them have been detected also in [ CII ] , albeit at fainter fluxes with respect to [ OIII ] carniani2020, bakx2020, hashimoto2019, maiolino2015, harikane2020 , and therefore are suitable for use in our model.

Among the galaxies in our sample, B14-65666 and BDF3299 show a clear multi component morphology, MACS416-Y1 and BDF3299 have spatial offsets between [ CII ] and [ OIII ] . B14-65666 has been spatially resolved in two clumps (A and B) which are both detected in [ CII ] , [ OIII ] and IR continuum hashimoto2019 . This allow us to apply our model to B14-5666 and its two clumps separately. Doing the same for BDF3299 is impossible because the [ CII ] and the [ OIII ] are not co-spatial.

sources.summary: The paper reports that B14-65666 has co-spatial FIR line detections consistent with prior work, with carbon-line emission more extended than oxygen-line emission.

quotes.quote: For the remaining systems (MACSJ0416-Y1, B14-65666, J0235, J1211, and J0217) for which co-spatial \ and \ emissions have been already reported in the literature Tamura:2019, Hashimoto:2019,Harikane:2019, Bakx:2020 , we have found results consistent with previous works. In Table tab:summary we list the \ and \ luminosities. Since for these galaxies the two FIR lines have been detected with high signal-to-noise ratio (SNR >8 ), we take advantage of these observations to investigate the extent of the FIR lines, as shown in Fig. fig:size (and Table tab:appendix ). We notice that \ is systematically larger than the \ and its extent is about two times larger than that of oxygen line. This is consistent with recent simulations by where the \ is concentrated in a compact region of 0.85 kpc, while the \ arise from a more extended area with a radius of 1.54 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper classifies object 61432 as a robust high-redshift galaxy candidate whose spectral break and near-infrared slope reject low-redshift galaxy and dwarf-star explanations.

quotes.quote: Finally, object 61432 has a flat near-infrared spectral slope and has a strong spectral break, with only a marginal detection in the z' band. The somewhat red 3.6 m - 4.5 m colour might appear difficult to reconcile with a high-redshift SED as illustrated in Fig. fig:Jch1ch2 , but the error bars are large and neither a late-type dwarf star nor a low-redshift galaxy provide remotely acceptable solutions. As with the first object above (object 277912) allowance for a possible contribution from Lyman- emission in the SED fitting does not change the photometric redshift, with zero Lyman- emission remaining the preferred option.

sources.summary: The paper describes B14-65666, alias Big Three Dragons, as a z=7.15 clumpy high-redshift galaxy likely undergoing a merger and used as a dust-temperature-constrained ALMA sample.

quotes.quote: B14-65666 ( z=7.15 ; also known as Big Three Dragons ) shows two clumps in rest-UV and dust, suggesting an ongoing merger event Bowler2018,Hashimoto2019,Hashimoto2022 . B14-65666 has been characterized by multiwavelength ALMA observations using band-6 ( _ rest 160 m ), band-7 ( _ rest 120 m ), and band-8 ( _ rest 90 m ) and is treated as a dust temperature constrained sample. For the analysis presented herein, we used flux density and size measurements from Sugahara2021 .

gmw_00003633.name: XID 2028
gmw_00003633.overview: XID 2028 is an obscured active galactic nucleus and quasar at a redshift of approximately 1.59 [arXiv:2301.11060, arXiv:2303.08952]. The host galaxy features extended star-forming regions [arXiv:1603.06419] and a complex, multi-phase outflow driven by interactions between the interstellar medium, a radio jet, and the central quasar [arXiv:2301.11060, arXiv:2303.08952].

sources.summary: The paper discusses ULASJ1002+0137 as a COSMOS X-ray AGN/quasar with conflicting redshift indicators, absorbed X-ray spectrum, partial broad-line visibility, and tentative lensing evidence.

quotes.quote: ULASJ1002+0137 : ULASJ1002+0137 is in the COSMOS field and and has previously been studied by Mainieri:07 in their analysis of X-ray AGN from the XMM-Newton Wide Field Survey. The optical counterpart to the X-ray source is presented in Brusa:10 . Although a redshift of z= 0.784 was initially derived by Mainieri:07 and Trump:09 , the optical spectroscopic redshift of z= 1.592 in Brusa:10 agrees well with our H redshift and the photometric redshift of Salvato:09 .

The X-ray spectrum is best fit by an absorbed power-law and an Fe K line at z= 0.784. The multi-wavelength analysis and optical spectroscopy produces a classification as a Narrow-Line/Type 2 AGN Brusa:10 . Our measured H FWHM of 3000 kms ^ 1 would suggest that the broad-line region is at least partially visible.

In Fig. fig:1002_2 we show a 15 15 ^ COSMOS BiK composite around the source at a resolution of 0.15 ^ per pixel McCracken:10 . An excess of blue sources is seen around the red quasar and the three blue sources to the southwest of ULASJ1002+0137 could be a lensed arc although the suggestion is highly tentative.

sources.summary: JWST/NIRSpec observations of XID2028 reveal hidden ionized-gas structures and interactions among the host-galaxy ISM, quasar-driven outflow, wind, and radio jet in this z=1.59 obscured AGN.

quotes.quote: Here we present Early Release Science JWST NIRSpec IFU observations of the z =1.59 prototypical obscured Active Galactic Nucleus (AGN) XID2028: This target represents a unique test case for studying quasar feedback at the peak epoch of AGN-galaxy co-evolution because extensive multi-wavelength coverage is available and a massive and extended outflow is detected in the ionised and molecular components. With the unprecedented sensitivity and spatial resolution of the JWST, the NIRSpec dataset reveals a wealth of structures in the ionised gas kinematics and morphology that were previously hidden in the seeing-limited ground-based data. In particular, we find evidence of an interaction between the interstellar medium of the galaxy and the quasar-driven outflow and radio jet that produces an expanding bubble from which the fast and extended wind detected in previous observations emerges. The new observations confirm the complex interplay between the AGN jet, wind and the interstellar medium of the host galaxy, highlighting the role of low-luminosity radio jets in AGN feedback.

XID2028 ( z =1.5930), originally discovered in the XMM-COSMOS survey (RA 10:02:11.27 DEC +01:37:06.6; Brusa et al. brusa10 ), is one of the best-studied objects in this crucial feedback phase. From an optical point of view, XID2028 is classified as a type 1.8-1.9 quasar on the basis of the significant extinction in the broad line region (BLR) component as measured from the H /H ratio (see Brusa et al. brusa15a ), and the lack of broad emission lines in the rest-frame UV. Based on the large multi-wavelength coverage, it has reasonably well-constrained bolometric luminosity log(L_ AGN )=46.3 , stellar mass M _* 4.5 10^ 11 \ M _ , and SFR 250 \ M _ / yr , which were derived from fits of the UV to far-IR (FIR) spectral energy distribution (Lusso et al. lusso12 , Brusa et al. brusa15a , Perna et al. perna15 ). The presence of a massive ( M 300 \ M _ / yr ) and extended ( 13 kpc) ionised outflow, traced by \ emission, has independently been advocated by X-shooter slit spectroscopy (Brusa et al. brusa15a , Perna et al. perna15 ) and SINFONI Spectrograph for INtegral Field Observations in the Near Infrared IFU observations (Cresci et al. cresci15a ; C15).

sources.summary: The paper uses JWST/NIRSpec data to reassess XID 2028, a z=1.6 obscured quasar, finding AGN-ionized outflow structures and radiative feedback that may reduce the host's molecular gas fraction.

quotes.quote: A good case in point is the z = 1.6 quasar WISEA J100211.29 + 013706.7 (XID 2028) where past analyses of the same ground-based data have come to different conclusions. Here we revisit this object with the integral field unit of the Near Infrared Spectrograph (NIRSpec) on board the James Webb Space Telescope ( ) as part of Early Release Science program Q3D. The excellent angular resolution and sensitivity of the \ data reveal new morphological and kinematic sub-structures in the outflowing gas plume.

An analysis of the emission line ratios indicates that photoionization by the central quasar dominates the ionization state of the gas with no obvious sign for a major contribution from hot young stars anywhere in the host galaxy. Rest-frame near-ultraviolet emission aligned along the wide-angle cone of outflowing gas is interpreted as a scattering cone. The outflow has cleared a channel in the dusty host galaxy through which some of the quasar ionizing radiation is able to escape and heat the surrounding interstellar and circumgalactic media. The warm ionized outflow is not powerful enough to impact the host galaxy via mechanical feedback, but radiative feedback by the AGN, aided by the outflow, may help explain the unusually small molecular gas mass fraction in the galaxy host.

sources.summary: The paper uses XID2028 as a main-sequence AGN with ionized and molecular gas studies, a powerful outflow, high star-formation efficiency, low gas fraction, and possible cold-gas depletion.

quotes.quote: XID2028 from brusa15 is also a main sequence AGN. This source has been extensively studied in ionized perna15,cresci15 as well as molecular brusa15a gas phases and has been shown to be in an active outflowing phase.

As an example, for XID2028, it has been shown from observations with SINFONI cresci15 that this galaxy hosts a powerful outflow traced by the ionized gas. The PdBI molecular gas observation for this object using CO(3-2) emission line is shown by the green star in all the plots brusa15 . Similar to the properties of our AGN sample, this target seems to have a higher SFE, lower depletion time scale and lower gas fraction compared to the ``average'' best fit functions of the MS galaxies. A confirmation of the depleted gas content in XID2028 comes from the molecular gas mass measurements obtained from the dust mass, which is a factor of 2 lower than the values obtained assuming _ CO =3.6 , supporting the hypothesis that the outflow observed in the ionized gas phase may be depleting the cold molecular gas in the host galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper says XID 2028 has a powerful but molecularly weak outflow, with detached lopsided molecular gas and an even weaker ionized outflow consistent with a fossil component.

quotes.quote: XID 2028 has a very powerful outflow with E _ out 0.04 L_ bol Perna2015AA , however the molecular component is extremely weak, with E _ out, mol 2.5 10^ 3 L_ bol Brusa2018AA . In the latter paper, we see in Fig. 9, left panel, that the blue wing of the molecular outflow has a spatially detached component with a distance from the nucleus of 1'' 8.5 kpc. No counterpart is visible in the red wing.

The connected component, visible in both blue and red wings, has a radius of 0.5'' 4.2 kpc. The detached lopsided component may represent a weak fossil outflow. An ionized outflow exists as well, but it is 20 times weaker Fluetsch2021MNRAS , in agreement with the expectation for a fossil.

sources.summary: The paper finds extended star-forming regions around ULASJ1002+0137, cites combined narrow H-alpha emission consistent with far-infrared star formation, and notes evidence for both positive and negative feedback.

quotes.quote: In ULASJ1002+0137, VHSJ2028-4631, VHSJ2144-0523, VHSJ2235-5750 and VHSJ2332-5240 the S/N of the resulting narrow emission is improved by increasing the aperture size and therefore we find five sources in which there is evidence for extended and resolved star forming regions lying around the quasar centres.

The limits on the star formation rates in the remaining eight quasars are not constraining enough to rule out significant star formation therefore feedback effects cannot be assessed. combine their detections of narrow H emission in ULASJ1002+0137 (see Appendix sec:1002 ) with [ OIII ] and HST U band observations and propose that ULASJ1002+0137 contains both positive and negative feedback. They detect an outflow in [ OIII ] extending up to 13 kpc from the AGN; the AGN appears to reside in a region with little star formation, surrounded by areas experiencing elevated star formation.

Using the same H data, highlight two regions (at 9 and 4 significance levels) of narrow H emission in ULASJ1002+0137 from which they measure a combined H star formation rate of 230 M_ /yr, consistent with the star formation rate of 275 M_ /yr constrained from far-infrared observations Brusa14 . The narrow H profile of ULASJ1002+0137 presented in both and is present in our integrated spectrum (see Paper I).

gmw_00003634.name: NGC 1155
gmw_00003634.overview: NGC 1155 is an early-type galaxy that exhibits extended mid-infrared emission following a de Vaucouleurs profile [arXiv:astro-ph/0006328]. The galaxy contains an additional ultraviolet source, which is likely composed of young stars [arXiv:astro-ph/0006328].

sources.summary: The paper finds extended mid-infrared emission and a de Vaucouleurs profile in NGC 1155, detects its [CII] and [OI] lines, and infers an additional UV source, probably young stars.

quotes.quote: With the exception of the 15 image of NGC 1052, the mid-IR emission from NGC 1052, NGC 1155, NGC 5866 and NGC 6958 at 4.5, 7 and 15 show extended emission. Mid-IR emission from NGC 1052, NGC 1155, and NGC 6958 follows a de Vaucouleurs profile. The ratio of 15/7 flux decreases with radius in these galaxies, approaching the values empirically observed for purely stellar systems.

However, the much larger difference (about a factor of 20) between the UV radiation from old stars and that needed to produce the far-infrared lines for NGC 1155 is strong evidence for the presence of an additional UV source, probably young stars, in NGC 1155.

gmw_00003635.name: G1
gmw_00003635.overview: G1 is a massive elliptical galaxy located in a dense group or cluster environment [arXiv:0712.1145]. It hosts a Fanaroff-Riley type I radio-loud active galactic nucleus, which exhibits a diffuse lobed morphology without highly collimated jets [arXiv:0712.1145].

sources.summary: The paper identifies G1 as the massive main lensing galaxy in CLASS B2108+213, finds a dense group environment, and argues component C is an FR I radio-loud AGN hosted by G1.

quotes.quote: A spectroscopic survey of the environment around the main lensing galaxy has found at least 40 group/cluster galaxies at the same redshift as G1 ( z = 0.365). Also, the central stellar velocity dispersion of G1 was found to be 360 km s ^ 1 (McKean et al. in preparation). These new data confirm that G1 is a massive galaxy in a dense group/cluster environment.

The classification of component C as the core of an AGN within G1 is also consistent with the extended lobe emission on either side of the lensing galaxy detected by MERLIN at 1.4 GHz. Moreover, the diffuse lobed morphology of the extended emission is as expected for a Fanaroff-Riley type I radio source (FR I; fanril74 ). There are no highly collimated jets or hotspots which are typically seen in the more powerful FR II sources.

gmw_00003636.name: G2
gmw_00003636.overview: G2 is a companion galaxy located near the galaxy G1 [arXiv:0712.1145]. It acts as a companion lensing galaxy that contributes to the local gravitational lens potential [arXiv:0712.1145].

sources.summary: The paper treats G2 as a companion lensing galaxy near G1; adding it as a singular isothermal sphere slightly improves the lens model and may explain a steeper G1 density profile.

quotes.quote: We now test what contribution G2 has if it is included as part of the lens potential. Hence, we introduce galaxy G2 as a companion lensing galaxy at the same redshift as G1. A SIS centred on the optical position of G2 was added to the SIS+shear model for G1.

Including G2 lowers the Einstein radius of G1 to 1.71 arcsec and reduces the shear to 0.04. Note that if the ratio of the masses is not fixed and the Einstein radius of G2 is left as a free parameter, then the Einstein radius of G1 is lowered further to 0.76 arcsec and the shear is reduced to 0.01. Also, the Einstein radius of G2 increases to 1.5 arcsec. Here, the simplest model that can be fitted needs to have the main lensing galaxy positioned collinearly between the two lensed images. Since G2 is closest to this, it is taken as the main perturber and the shear is reduced.

gmw_00003637.name: NGC 450
gmw_00003637.overview: NGC 450 is a spiral galaxy [arXiv:0711.1171] classified as an asymmetric SABcd system [arXiv:astro-ph/0311030]. It features an older nucleus, younger outskirts [arXiv:0711.1171], and three bright eastern star-forming complexes [arXiv:astro-ph/0311030], while a background galaxy is superimposed on its disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0603682].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 450 as a spatially resolved decomposition example, finding an older nucleus, younger outskirts, spiral-arm age structure, and inner-arm star-forming knots.

quotes.quote: The image on the left of the top panel shows a SDSS spiral galaxy, NGC 450 (SDSS J011530.44-005139.5) and the middle image shows the distribution of best fitting stellar population ages in Gyr throughout this galaxy. We see an older population in the nucleus of the galaxy (ranging from 5-12 Gyr). In the outskirts there is a younger population, whose ages range from 0.001 to 0.2 Gyr. The distribution of this parameter also traces out some of the spiral arm structure. Around the inner spiral arms, some of the ``knots'' of star formation seen in the original ' band image can be detected in the age image too as yellowish-green regions (with ages between 0.1 and 2 Gyr).

sources.summary: UGC 806 is highlighted as an asymmetric SABcd system with three bright eastern star-forming complexes, possible Wolf-Rayet character, and a deficient northwest H II distribution.

quotes.quote: A particularly striking example of this is UGC 806 (Fig. Fige ), classified SABcd, which contains 3 extremely bright star formation complexes to the east of the bulge. Schaerer et al. ( ) refer to this object as a Wolf-Rayet galaxy, and note that it may be part of NGC 450 (UGC 806) which appears likely given the close agreement in recession velocity (1693 km s ^ 1 for the star formation complexes .f. 1761 km s ^ 1 systemic). However, even with these three bright regions removed, the H ii region distribution is significantly asymmetric, with a substantial deficit to the north and west.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 0450 through SDSS surface photometry, noting its visible disk structure and using its radial profile features to classify the galaxy structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 0450 : J011530.8-005138 A background galaxy (UGC 00807, 11587 ) is superimposed. The mean ellipticity of the outer disk is difficult to determine. The inner disk looks fairly round whereas the outer disk looks rather elliptical.

gmw_00003638.name: SDSS J075642.69+364430.0
gmw_00003638.overview: SDSS J075642.69+364430.0 is a disk galaxy featuring an old stellar bulge and a much younger disk [arXiv:0711.1171]. The galaxy exhibits a lower current star formation rate in its central bulge population compared to its outer disk [arXiv:0711.1171].

sources.summary: The paper presents SDSS J075642.69+364430.0 as a decomposed disk galaxy with an old bulge, younger disk, lower central current star formation, and low nuclear obscuration.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:sdss2 is a result of the template decomposition for another SDSS galaxy (SDSS J075642.69+364430.0). There is evidence for a bulge in this disk galaxy - the stellar populations are older - as much as 10 to 15 Gyr, whereas those in the disk are much younger, typically less than 1 Gyr. The population in the core also has lower e folding time ( 4< <5 Gyr) than in the outer disk. Together these results point to a lower current SFR in the central (bulge) population, compared to the disk population. The nucleus itself has a relatively low obscuration with E(B-V) 0.0 , while the stellar populations in the outer parts of the disk typically show a higher obscuration ( E(B-V) 0.9 ).

gmw_00003639.name: SDSS J080800.99+483807.7
gmw_00003639.overview: SDSS J080800.99+483807.7 is an elliptical host galaxy [arXiv:1308.4096] at a redshift of z=0.2805 [arXiv:1308.4096]. It is classified as a double-lobed radio galaxy [arXiv:1308.4096] that exhibits high star-formation activity [arXiv:1308.4096].

sources.summary: The paper characterizes SDSS J080800.99+483807.7 as a compact double-lobed radio galaxy at z=0.2805, with high star formation, an elliptical host, and possible jet back-flow interaction.

quotes.quote: SDSS J080800.99+483807.7 has been serendipitously discovered in MERLIN 18 cm observations to be a double-lobed radio galaxy. Because it is an optically faint source, basic information like redshift, linear size, and structure has been incomplete until now. Furthermore, there are no spectra of this source available in any databases.

The redshift of the galaxy is z=0.2805 0.0003 , resulting in a linear size of the observed radio structure of 26.3 kpc . The optical line emission as well as the infrared and radio continuum emission suggest a high star-formation activity. In addition, we estimated the mass of the central black hole to be M_ BH /M_ 6.9 . The MODS spectrum and the optical images from Sloan Digital Sky Survey suggest that SDSS J080800.99+483807.7 is an elliptical host galaxy. In combination with the overall radio structure, we argue that the star formation could be the result of the back-flow along the jet and the interstellar medium of the host.

gmw_00003640.name: IC 4710
gmw_00003640.overview: IC 4710 is classified as an SB(s)m galaxy [arXiv:0912.0731]. Its structure features a central bar-like component situated within a roundish outer envelope [arXiv:0912.0731].

sources.summary: The paper uses IC 4710 as a GALFIT example, describing its barred SB(s)m morphology and comparing one- and two-component higher-order image models.

quotes.quote: IC 4710 is an SB(s)m galaxy, which has a bar-like feature in the middle of a roundish outer structure, as shown in the R band image of Figure ic4710 , which comes from the CINGS (Carnegie-Irvine Nearby Galaxy Survey) project http://users.obs.carnegiescience.edu/lho/projects/CINGS/CINGS.html . Prior to the analysis, we masked out the stars using the SExtractor software bertin96 . We analyze this galaxy using, for comparison, both one- and two-component regular and higher order models with Fourier modes, shown in Figures ic4710 b-i . The best-fit parameters are given in Table ic4710-table , which illustrates three different sets of analysis parameters: best fit using two components (Figure ic4710 ), a model using just the traditional ellipsoid component (Figure ic4710 g ), and the same single-component model with Fourier modes added (Figure ic4710 b ).

gmw_00003641.name: NGC 289
gmw_00003641.overview: NGC 289 is a barred spiral Seyfert 2 galaxy [arXiv:1305.4938] featuring a weak, multi-component bar [arXiv:0912.0731] and a low-surface-brightness outer region [arXiv:1802.04043]. It is interacting with a dwarf companion [arXiv:1305.4938, arXiv:0806.0471] and hosts an extended ultraviolet disc with star formation occurring beyond its optical disc [arXiv:1802.04043].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 289 as a barred Seyfert 2 spiral interacting with a dwarf companion, with nuclear dust, stellar absorption features, and evolved stars dominating its H+K continuum.

quotes.quote: NGC 289 is a barred spiral galaxy at a redshift of 0.0054 interacting with its dwarf companion Arp 1981. It has a dustlane crossing its red nuclear region (see also Fig. fig:NGC289 ). A several million years old starburst is supposed to have occurred in the nuclear region of NGC 289 .

NGC 289 shows strong stellar absorption features in H-band (e.g., Si I and CO(6-3)) and K-band (e.g., the CO band heads from 2.29 m on). All these features are fitted well by our stellar template taken from a red supergiant. Hence, the main continuum emission is coming from evolved stars, either from AGB stars or old main sequence stars.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 289 as a complex barred-spiral example, describing a weak bar, ring-like bifurcated spiral structure, multicomponent bar, and detailed Fourier-mode decomposition.

quotes.quote: NGC 289 is an SAB(rs)bc galaxy, with a weak bar and a complex inner spiral system (Figure n289 , Table n289-table ) that resembles a ring. Upon closer examination, the ring appearance comes about because there exists a bifurcation in the spiral structure that connects up with the opposing spiral arm. Furthermore, the bar is also multi-component, with the inner component oriented at an angle nearly 45^ from the strong outer bar. The best-fit analysis involves three spiral components, an inner and an outer bar component, and a bulge (Table n289-table , top). All except for the bulge component are modified by five Fourier modes, and are shown in Figures n289 e-h .

sources.summary: The paper notes NGC 289 as one of the two largest H I-to-stellar diameter cases, describing it as low-surface-brightness outside a high-surface-brightness inner disc with an extended UV disc.

quotes.quote: The two galaxies with the largest \ to stellar diameter ratios and \ diameters above 150 kpc are NGC 289 and ESO075-G006. The ``smaller'' of the two galaxies is NGC 289.

Walsh1997 has classified NGC 289 as a low surface brightness galaxy with a high surface brightness inner disc. Latest photometry shows a central surface brightness in \ of 17 Li2011 , i. e. the central disc is a high surface brightness disc for detailed photometric profiles see https://cgs.obs.carnegiescience.edu/CGS/object html pages/NGC289.html . In addition, NGC 289 has been found to host an extended UV disc (XUV disc) and has star forming regions beyond the optical disc Meurer2017 .

sources.summary: The model reproduces NGC 289 inner spirals, bifurcations, and inner outer spiral, but not the outermost spiral structure, possibly due to a small companion interaction.

quotes.quote: NGC 289 : this SBbc ( T=4 ) galaxy has a small bar, the inner spiral arms start from the ends of the bar. There is also an extended outer spiral structure, which bifurcates from the inner spiral arms. The best-fitting model, which sets R 2.6 reproduces the inner spiral, the bifurcations and the inner part of the outer spiral (see Fig. modsel1 ). The outermost part of the outer spiral structure is not reproduced, but it can be related to an interaction with a small companion galaxy, LSBG F411-024.

sources.summary: The paper notes NGC 289 has a previously reported nuclear star cluster, but dust obscures its center enough to prevent reliable morphology or unambiguous NSC fitting.

quotes.quote: Two of these galaxies (NGC 289 and NGC 1566) have NSCs presented in carollo2002 ; due to the bright AGN component (in NGC 1566) and dust (in NGC 289), we are unable to obtain reliable, unambiguous NSC fits for them, and so we focus on the other three objects, below.
In two galaxies (NGC 289 and NGC 7177) we are unable to determine accurate NSC morphologies due to dust obscuring the centers of all three bands.

In three galaxies, the presence of a nuclear cluster can't be constrained due to dust opaque enough to obscure the nucleus even in F160W (NGC 289 and NGC 7177), or the presence of a very bright AGN (NGC 1566) as discussed in Section subsec:qualitymodel .

gmw_00003642.name: SDSS J0046+0104
gmw_00003642.overview: SDSS J0046+0104 is a broad-absorption line quasar [arXiv:1805.01544]. The active galactic nucleus features a wide absorption trough alongside narrower troughs associated with other outflows [arXiv:1805.01544].

sources.summary: The paper gives individual BAL-quasar notes for SDSS J0046+0104, describing its wide absorption troughs, Ly-forest contamination, and Gaussian-template column-density extraction.

quotes.quote: SDSS J0046+0104 has a wide \ absorption trough, with narrower ones on the left wing from other outflows. For the / \ region, contamination by the Ly forest is present on the right wing of \ and both wings of . Using the \ Gaussian template, we were able to factor out the contamination and obtain reliable column density measurements.

gmw_00003643.name: SDSS J0825+0740
gmw_00003643.overview: SDSS J0825+0740 is a quasar that hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1805.01544]. The system is characterized by a mini-broad-absorption line outflow with a narrow, uncontaminated absorption trough [arXiv:1805.01544].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J0825+0740 as a mini-BAL quasar with narrow, partly uncontaminated absorption, Ly contamination, and a template fit to the left wing of its trough.

quotes.quote: SDSS J0825+0740 has one of the two mini-BAL outflows in our sample. The \ is quite narrow and without any contamination, as is the \ absorption trough. However, the \ absorption trough is heavily contaminated by a strong, likely Ly , absorption trough from an intervening system. In this case, the template was fit to the left wing of the \ trough.

gmw_00003644.name: SDSS J1135+1615
gmw_00003644.overview: SDSS J1135+1615 is a broad-absorption line quasar [arXiv:1805.01544]. The active galactic nucleus displays saturated, flat absorption troughs, with its CIV absorption trough being deeper than its SiIV absorption trough [arXiv:1805.01544].

sources.summary: The paper notes saturated, flat absorption troughs in SDSS J1135+1615, explains its template choice, and finds one absorption trough deeper than another.

quotes.quote: For SDSS J1135+1615, the \ absorption troughs are saturated and flat. Therefore, we used \ 1854.72 \ as the template, since it is not blended with other troughs. Fitting the \ and \ absorption troughs with this template shows that the \ absorption trough is actually deeper than the \ absorption trough (see table tableCompare for more details).

gmw_00003645.name: PSO J352.4034--15.3373
gmw_00003645.overview: PSO J352.4034--15.3373 is a radio-loud quasar located at a systemic redshift of approximately 5.83 [arXiv:2108.04257, arXiv:2103.03879]. It is one of the radio-loudest quasars in the early universe and features extended, kiloparsec-scale radio jets [arXiv:2108.04257, arXiv:2103.03879].

sources.summary: The paper studies the z~6 radio-loud quasar PSO J352.4034--15.3373, measuring FIR/millimeter lines and continuum, radio brightness, synchrotron contamination, and extended radio-jet feedback.

quotes.quote: We investigate the radio-loud quasar PSO J352.4034--15.3373 at z 6 at the edge of the Epoch of Reionization. This quasar is among the most powerful radio emitters and the first one with direct evidence of extended radio jets ( 1.6 kpc) at these high redshifts. We analyze NOEMA and ALMA millimeter data targeting the CO (6--5) and \ far-infrared emission lines, respectively, and the underlying continuum. The broad 440 80 km s ^ 1 and marginally resolved \ emission line yields a systemic redshift of z = 5.832 0.001 . Additionally, we report a strong 215 MHz radio continuum detection, 88 7 mJy, using the GMRT.

This measurement significantly improves the constraints at the low-frequency end of the spectral energy distribution of this quasar. In contrast to what is typically observed in high-redshift radio-quiet quasars, we show that cold dust emission alone cannot reproduce the millimeter continuum measurements. This is evidence that the strong synchrotron emission from the quasar contributes substantially to the emission even at millimeter (far-infrared in the rest-frame) wavelengths.

sources.summary: The paper presents deep Chandra observations of PSO J352.4034 15.3373, measuring its X-ray spectrum and identifying possible extended jet-axis emission.

quotes.quote: We present deep (265 ks) Chandra X-ray observations of PSO J352.4034 15.3373, a quasar at z=5.831 that, with a radio-to-optical flux ratio of R>1000 , is one of the radio-loudest quasars in the early universe and is the only quasar with observed extended radio jets of kpc-scale at z 6 . Modeling the X-ray spectrum of the quasar with a power law, we find a best fit of = 1.99^ +0.29 _ 0.28 , leading to an X-ray luminosity of L_ 2-10 = 1.26^ +0.45 _ 0.33 10^ 45 \ erg \ s ^ 1 and an X-ray to UV brightness ratio of _ OX = -1.36 0.11 . We identify a diffuse structure 50 kpc ( 8^ ) to the NW of the quasar along the jet axis that corresponds to a 3 enhancement in the angular density of emission and can be ruled out as a background fluctuation with a probability of P=0.9985 .

While with few detected photons the spectral fit of the structure is uncertain, we find that it has a luminosity of L_ 2-10 = 1.1^ +0.9 _ 0.7 10^ 44 \ erg \ s ^ 1 . Combining these X-ray observations with 6 GHz radio observations taken by the Very Long Baseline Array, we argue that this excess is the X-ray counterpart of its radio jet. We discuss how this radio-loud quasar looks different from other z 6 quasars with deep X-ray observations and explore the possibility that its excess X-ray emission is due to sources associated with its relativistic jet.

gmw_00003646.name: NGC 2810
gmw_00003646.overview: NGC 2810 is a field elliptical galaxy that exhibits strong, extended emission lines across its entire radial profile [arXiv:astro-ph/0702605]. Its center hosts a young, metal-rich stellar population with high magnesium-to-iron ratios, likely triggered by a minor merger that induced a nuclear starburst [arXiv:astro-ph/0702605].

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 2810 has strong extended emission, a young metal-rich center, high nuclear Mg/Fe, and evidence for a minor-merger-induced nuclear star formation burst.

quotes.quote: NGC 2810, Fig. 5. The observations are shallower reaching only 1.4 r_ e . We determined the effective radius of the galaxy from our SCORPIO data in two ways: from the broad-band V-image that always precedes the spectral-mode exposure and from integration of the flux obtained in the spectral mode across the slit. The surface brightness profiles are well-fitted by a de Vaucouleurs' law 1948AnnAp...11..247V , and both approaches have consistently given r_ e = 14 . The galaxy, though red and homogeneous photometrically, revealed strong emission lines in the spectrum, not only in the nucleus, but also over the full radial extension.

NGC 2810, Fig. 10b. The stellar population in the centre of this field elliptical is not very old, with a mean age of only 5--6 Gyr. The metallicity of stars in the centre is very high, the highest in our sample (though the galaxy is not the most massive and luminous one). Beyond the radius of 3 to 5 , the stellar population is homogeneously old, about 10 Gyr, and the metallicity is approximately solar. As we have also seen, there is a sharp increase in [ Mg/Fe ] ratio in the nucleus of NGC 2810, so by taking a considerable amount of ionized gas in this galaxy into account, we may conclude that a minor merger has taken place some billion years ago to have provoked a short, intense nuclear star formation burst that increased the mean metallicity and decreased the mean age of stars.

gmw_00003647.name: NGC 3348
gmw_00003647.overview: NGC 3348 is an elliptical galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0702595] that features a partially depleted core [arXiv:astro-ph/0702595]. It contains diffuse dust and ionized gas [arXiv:astro-ph/0702605], with stellar age and metallicity gradients that suggest the galaxy experienced a recent minor merger [arXiv:astro-ph/0702605].

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 3348 as a partially depleted-core elliptical, fitting its R-band light profile and comparing orbit integrations and central density slopes.

quotes.quote: In the present study, we focus on the bulge in the center of the Milky Way and the two galaxies from the sample used in Terzi Graham (2005): an elliptical galaxy NGC 1379, and an elliptical galaxy with partially depleted core -- NGC 3348. The second galaxy, NGC 3348, is an example of a galaxy with partially depleted cores for which only 5-parameter Terzi -Graham model provides good fits in density space. figd , the 3 parameter Dehnen and Prugniel--Simien models, along with the 5 parameter Terzi -Graham were fitted to the deprojected major-axis R band light-profile of a core elliptical galaxy NGC 3348.

(Expressions for density, potential and forces for the Dehnen model are given in Appendix C.) We compare integration times for equivalent sets of orbits in the three models obtained after parametric fitting in density space to the deprojected light-profile of NGC 3348 (Table Tab_Mod ). For the NGC 3348 (parameters are given in Table Tab_Mod ), we integrated 600 box orbits and 600 tube orbits (see Appendix B) at 50 logarithmically spaced energy levels in the range E(10^ 2 ) E() E(5 ) (where E()= (,0,0) denotes a isoenergy surface pierced by the x axis at x= ), for roughly 3 Hubble times (length-scale and Hubble times are given in Table Tab_Hubble ). For NGC 3348, the central density slopes of the Dehnen ( _D=0.71 ) is nearly identical to that of the Prugniel--Simien ( p=0.72 ), but somewhat larger than that of Terzi -Graham ( =0.44 ). This means that the comparison of execution speeds for orbits in potentials modeling NGC 3348 will accurately reflect efficiency of force evaluations in the Prugniel--Simien model relative to that of the Dehnen model, while the Terzi -Graham will have a slight ``advantage'', having the shallower central slope.
