sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 3348 as an unusual luminous group-center elliptical whose old nucleus, younger outer stars, positive outer metallicity gradient, gas, dust, and emission suggest a minor merger.

quotes.quote: NGC 3348, Fig. 10c. The galaxy was classified by RSA as a regular E0, but it has now shown itself to be very unusual. The radial variations of the mean stellar age in this galaxy look like a mirror of NGC 2810: the nucleus is old, and the stars become younger and younger along the radius becoming 3--4 Gyr old at >r_ e . The metallicity gradient, though negative at =0-0.5 r_ e , becomes positive in the radius range of 0.5 r_ e 1.3 r_ e . Since NGC 3348 also shows extended emission lines in the spectrum, we may suggest that the galaxy has also experienced a minor merger. But this time the satellite involved had a smaller amount of gas than that of NGC 2810.

Evidently, this `prototype of E0', as 1994cag..book.....S characterized NGC 3348 in their Atlas, contains in fact a noticeable amount of diffuse dust and ionized gas coupled with this dust , which may be accreted rather recently.

gmw_00003648.name: IC 2179
gmw_00003648.overview: IC 2179 is an elliptical galaxy that serves as a fainter companion to the giant spiral galaxy NGC 2347 [arXiv:astro-ph/0702605]. It lacks emission lines and exhibits a stellar population that increases smoothly in age from 8 Gyr in the nucleus to 15 Gyr in its outer regions [arXiv:astro-ph/0702605].

sources.summary: The paper reports that IC 2179 is an elliptical companion of NGC 2347 with reliable index profiles, no emission lines, a younger nucleus, and smoothly increasing age outward.

quotes.quote: IC 2179, Fig. 8. This elliptical galaxy, being the fainter neighbour of a giant spiral galaxy NGC 2347, has not been studied in detail to now. In particular, the close bright star might preclude any attempts at surface photometry. We checked the brightness profile along the slit of the spectrograph and ensured that two halves of the profile diverge at > 30 . However, the surface brightness profile is very good inside this radius and can be fitted by a de Vaucouleurs' law 1948AnnAp...11..247V with r_ e 18 ; if we take only the `fainter' half of the profile, we obtain r_ e 15 in the radius range between 5 and 56 - this value is used to parametrise the profiles in Fig. 8.

The metal-line profiles show two breaks -- at (/r_ e ) 0.3 - -0.2 and at (/r_ e ) 0 - +0.1 ; the H index profile only reveals the latter break. The emission lines are absent in the spectrum, so the H index measurements are reliable.

In IC 2179 the nucleus is also younger than the outer part; but unless NGC 2810 and NGC 4339, IC 2179 does not seem to have a young circumnuclear stellar disk producing a `step' in age radial variations in the former galaxies. Instead we observe the gradual smooth age increase along the radius, from 8 Gyr to 15 Gyr toward 2 r_ e .

gmw_00003649.name: MACS1149 1644
gmw_00003649.overview: MACS1149 1644 is a high-mass, dispersion-dominated galaxy with a high velocity dispersion of 200 +/− 12 km/s [arXiv:1610.03075]. It exhibits a low specific star formation rate, suggesting it may be falling off the star-forming main sequence [arXiv:1610.03075].

sources.summary: The paper identifies MACS1149 1644 as the high-mass dispersion-dominated galaxy in its sample, with the highest measured velocity dispersion and low specific star formation rate.

quotes.quote: The three dispersion dominated galaxies in our sample are extremely different from each other - one is very low mass (MACS0416 706, = 8.31 ), one is intermediate mass (MACS0416 94, = 9.88 ) and the other high mass (MACS1149 1644, = 10.48 ). The velocity dispersion of MACS1149 1644 is the highest in the sample ( 200 12 km/s) and much more like dispersion dominated systems in previous work. This object has a low sSFR 0.2 and could be a galaxy falling off the main sequence of star formation.

gmw_00003650.name: MACS0416 706
gmw_00003650.overview: MACS0416 706 is a compact, low-mass galaxy located at a redshift of z=1.35 [arXiv:1610.03075]. It is a dispersion-dominated system with a very low measured velocity dispersion, disturbed kinematics, and a high specific star formation rate [arXiv:1610.03075].

sources.summary: The paper describes MACS0416 706 as a compact, low-mass, z=1.35 dispersion-dominated galaxy with very low measured velocity dispersion, high specific star formation rate, and disturbed low-mass kinematics.

quotes.quote: From the kinematic maps (Figures fig:postage1 fig:postage5 ) and Figure fig:sfcorr we see that none of these low mass galaxies are regularly rotating: 2 are irregular rotators (MACS1149 1757 and RXJ1347 1230), 1 is dispersion dominated (MACS0416 706), and 2 have unknown/merging kinematic structure (MACS1149 683 and MACS1149 1237).

The three dispersion dominated galaxies in our sample are extremely different from each other - one is very low mass (MACS0416 706, = 8.31 ), one is intermediate mass (MACS0416 94, = 9.88 ) and the other high mass (MACS1149 1644, = 10.48 ).

MACS0416 706 is a compact low mass galaxy at z=1.35 also with a very low measured velocity dispersion of <36 km/s, and high sSFR 3 and may represent a new class of low mass compact dispersion dominated objects, forming an elliptical structure in situ, which are undetected in surveys lacking the increase in depth and resolution that gravitational lensing provides in KLASS.

gmw_00003651.name: 4C 60.07
gmw_00003651.overview: The high-redshift radio galaxy 4C 60.07, located at z = 3.79 [arXiv:astro-ph/0209173, arXiv:0808.2188], is undergoing an early-stage merger [arXiv:0808.2188, arXiv:astro-ph/0402464] that contains two distinct active galactic nuclei [arXiv:1010.1385]. This system features a clumpy structure aligned with its radio source [arXiv:astro-ph/0209173] and holds massive reservoirs of molecular gas [arXiv:astro-ph/0402464].

sources.summary: The paper presents 4C60.07 as a z=3.790 radio galaxy with a Ly-alpha nebula, foreground extinction, lobe-associated emission, clumpy aligned structure, and possible induced star formation.

quotes.quote: 4C60.07 at z = 3.790 Because of its very similar redshift the radio galaxy 4C60.07 could be observed with the same narrow--band filter as 4C41.17. The total observing time on source was 7,200 s, resulting in a surface brightness detection limit of 2 10^ 18 erg s^ 1 cm^ 2 arcsec^ 2 (3 limit in a 2.0 \ diameter aperture). Figure 2 shows the \ nebula in relationship to the radio source, rest--frame UV HST structure, and the extended dust and molecular gas that has been found in this system . Unfortunately the \ image is not as deep as for 4C41.17, not only because of the shorter intergration time, but also because 4C60.07 has significant Galactic foreground extinction ( 1.6 magnitude at the central wavelength of the narrow band filter).

First, the brightest \ emission is associated with the radio lobe which is closest to the AGN. This is presumably because of asymmetries in gas density distribution, with denser gas providing more rampressure on the hotspots of the expanding radio source . Second, the galaxy structure is very clumpy and approximately aligned with the radio source. This may be due to induced star formation by radio lobes which expand sideways into the cold, dense gas seen in the mm--maps.

sources.summary: The paper describes 4C 60.07 as a z=3.8 high-redshift radio galaxy in an early-stage merger with offset dusty components, radio activity, and tidal gas structure.

quotes.quote: High-resolution submillimetre (submm) imaging of the high-redshift radio galaxy (HzRG), 4C 60.07, at z=3.8 , has revealed two dusty components of roughly equal integrated flux. Spitzer imaging shows that one of these components (`B') is coincident with an extremely red active galactic nucleus (AGN), offset by 4 arcsec ( 30 kpc) from the HzRG core. The other submm component (`A') -- resolved by our synthesised beam and devoid of emission at 3.6--8.0- m -- lies between `B' and the HzRG core. Since the radio galaxy was discovered via its extremely young, steep-spectrum radio lobes and the creation of these lobes was likely triggered by the interaction, we argue that we are witnessing an early-stage merger, prior to its eventual equilibrium state. The interaction is between the host galaxy of an actively-fueled black hole (BH), and a gas-rich starburst/AGN (`B') marked by the compact submm component and coincident with broad CO(4--3) emission.

New, high-resolution submm imaging of the HzRG, 4C 60.07, at z=3.8 , reveals two clumps of emission separated by 3.3 arcsec ( 25 kpc). Although one clump is resolved by our 2-arcsec beam, both are relatively compact ( 15 kpc) and there is little evidence for a galaxy-wide starburst on larger scales from our observations.

sources.summary: The paper describes 4C 60.07 as a z=3.79 high-redshift radio galaxy with two CO components, massive molecular gas, active star formation, and a formative giant-elliptical merger state.

quotes.quote: We report on the detection of the lowest 1 0 transition in the powerful high-redshift radio galaxy 4C 60.07 at z = 3.79. The CO emission is distributed in two spatially and kinematically distinct components as was previously known from the observations of the higher excitation 4 3 line. The total molecular gas mass in 4C 60.07 inferred from the 1 0 emission is M( H _2) 1.3 10^ 11 , sufficient to fuel the inferred star-formation rate of 1600 yr ^ 1 for 10^8 yrs. From our high-resolution 1 0 VLA maps we find the dynamical mass of 4C 60.07 to be comparable to that of a giant elliptical at the present time.

A significant fraction of the mass is in the form of molecular gas suggesting that 4C 60.07 is in an early state of its evolution. The merging nature of 4C 60.07 along with its large dynamical mass imply that this system is a giant elliptical caught in its formative stages.

sources.summary: The paper reports excess X-ray photons near 4C 60.07 but argues the off-nuclear sources are perpendicular to the radio lobes and lobe-region counts match background.

quotes.quote: For 4C 60.07, the radial photon density exceeds the background level within 5 from the central \ source position (middle panel of Fig. other_ctsden_fig ). This excess emission appears to be mainly composed by two off-nuclear sources, respectively 3 away in the N direction and 5 away in the SW direction (see the middle right panel of Fig. other_fig ). However, these sources are located in a direction which is nearly perpendicular to that of the galaxy radio lobes (see Fig. 1 of smail+2009 ). At the same time, the measured \ counts within the region corresponding to the location of the radio lobes is consistent with the background level.

sources.summary: 4C 60.07 is only marginally detected at 350 microns because of limited integration, despite being among the strongest previous 850 micron sources.

quotes.quote: 4C 60.07 This source received by far the least amount of integration time of the 5 HzRGs observed with sharc-ii , and as a result the source is only just detected at the 3 level ( S_ 350 m =59 22 mJy). This source was one of the strongest sources detected at 850- m by Stevens et al. \ and even showed some, albeit marginal, evidence of being extended. A faint 850- m source found 30 north of the RG coincides with extended 3 emission in the sharc-ii map. However, given its small 850- m flux ( S_ 350 m =5.9 1.3 mJy), and the somewhat larger noise level in that part of the sharc-ii map, we do not consider this source robustly detected at 350- m. We derive a 3 upper flux limit of S_ 350 m 91 mJy

sources.summary: The survey notes that 4C 60.07 contains two AGNs separated by about 30 kpc, with the radio galaxy lacking hot-dust emission while its companion appears obscured.

quotes.quote: 4C 60.07 ( z=3.788 ): ivi08 showed that this system consists of two AGNs separated by 4 \ (30 kpc). The radio galaxy (A), as identified by the radio core, remains undetected in the hot dust emission beyond 4.5 m, while the companion object B, which currently lacks a spectroscopic redshift, has very red hot dust emission characteristic of an obscured AGN. An initial claim of a CO(1-0) redshift for this second AGN greve04 could not be confirmed in the re-analysis of those data ivi08 , but the (sub)mm dust continuum morphology looks consistent with both targets being at the same redshift.

gmw_00003652.name: host galaxy of Swift detected GRB 080517
aliases_local: host galaxy of Swift detected GRB 080517
gmw_00003652.overview: The host galaxy of Swift detected GRB 080517 is a local, low-luminosity star-forming galaxy [arXiv:1409.5791]. It features a bright near-infrared continuum [arXiv:1808.09228], high dust obscuration [arXiv:1409.5791], and an older post-starburst stellar population alongside its ongoing star formation [arXiv:1409.5791, arXiv:1808.09228].

sources.original_name: host galaxy of Swift detected GRB 080517
sources.summary: The paper identifies the GRB 080517 host as a local dusty star-forming galaxy with radio emission, high obscuration, and an older post-starburst population alongside ongoing star formation.

quotes.quote: We present an analysis of the photometry and spectroscopy of the host galaxy of Swift detected GRB 080517. From our optical spectroscopy, we identify a redshift of z=0.089 0.003 , based on strong emission lines, making this a rare example of a very local, low luminosity, long gamma ray burst. The galaxy is detected in the radio with a flux density of S_ 4.5 GHz = 0.22 0.04 mJy - one of relatively few known GRB hosts with a securely measured radio flux. Both optical emission lines and a strong detection at 22 m suggest that the host galaxy is forming stars rapidly, with an inferred star formation rate 16 M _ yr ^ 1 and a high dust obscuration (E (B-V)>1 , based on sight-lines to the nebular emission regions).

However, fitting of the remarkably flat spectral energy distribution from the ultraviolet through to the infrared suggests that an older, 500 Myr post-starburst stellar population is present along with the ongoing star formation. We conclude that the host galaxy of GRB 080517 is a valuable addition to the still very small sample of well-studied local gamma-ray burst hosts.

sources.summary: The paper observes the GRB 080517 host, notes its CO detection and bright near-infrared continuum, and identifies it as an important target for future infrared spectroscopy.

quotes.quote: In addition to these sources, we observed the host of nearby GRB 080517 080517 . This last source is of particular interest because of a detection of an emission line of carbon monoxide ( 080517CO ).

The host galaxy of GRB 080517 is the only one in our sample with a detection of a CO emission line ( 080517CO ). It is also a somewhat unusual source for its bright NIR continuum, caused by a combination of mass, redshift and the presence of a relatively bright older stellar population in addition to the ongoing star formation, see . While our WHT spectroscopy is too shallow to give useful limits on the CO/ \ ratio, this source will be a key target for higher resolution IR spectrographs on bigger telescopes.

gmw_00003653.name: ULASJ0123 + 1525
gmw_00003653.overview: ULASJ0123 + 1525 is a heavily reddened, intrinsically luminous quasar host galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 2.63 [arXiv:1805.09825]. The host galaxy has a dust mass of log10(M_dust/M_sun) = 8.63 and exhibits a high star formation rate of 2000 solar masses per year [arXiv:1805.09825].

sources.summary: The paper studies ULASJ0123 + 1525 as a heavily reddened quasar host with CO emission, JVLA continuum, and a revised high star-formation rate and dust mass.

quotes.quote: Heavily Reddened Quasars and Companions The sample studied consists of three heavily reddened quasars from Banerji:12, Banerji:15 ULASJ0123 + 1525 ( z_ H =2.630 ), ULASJ1234 + 0907 ( z_ H =2.503 ) and ULASJ2315 + 0143 ( z_ H =2.561 ) - where CO(3-2) emission has already been detected (B17). These quasars are among the reddest sources that have been discovered in our near infra-red search for a population of heavily reddened, intrinsically luminous quasars that are not present in wide-field optical surveys such as the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS). The three quasars studied here have (H-K) colours of 2 (Vega), which corresponds to typical dust extinctions towards the quasar continuum of E(B-V) 1 (A _ V 3 mag for R _ V =3.1).

For ULASJ0123, where continuum emission at _ obs 33 GHz is detected with the JVLA, we do not have enough photometric data points to be able to carry out the full SED-fitting described above. Based on the SEDs of ULASJ1234 and ULASJ2315 we assume that free-free emission makes a negligible contribution to the SED, while synchrotron emission dominates the JVLA continuum emission with a power-law index of =0.7 . Adopting such assumptions, we can estimate the non-thermal emission contributing to the _ obs 100 GHz continuum used to infer star formation rates and dust masses in B17. The result is 13 per cent, giving a revised SFR for the quasar host galaxy of 2000 400M yr ^ 1 (assuming T _ d =47K and =1.6) and a dust mass of log _ 10 (M _ dust /M _ )=8.63 ^ 0.07 _ 0.09 . These results are consistent, within the error-bars, with those derived in B17.

gmw_00003654.name: G1234N
gmw_00003654.overview: G1234N is a highly obscured companion galaxy located near the quasar ULASJ1234 [arXiv:1805.09825]. First detected with ALMA at a redshift of z = 2.514, the galaxy exhibits both dust continuum and CO emission [arXiv:1805.09825].

sources.summary: The paper identifies G1234N as a highly obscured companion galaxy near ULASJ1234, first detected with ALMA and later detected in CO(1-0) and dust continuum.

quotes.quote: In B17, we also detected CO(3-2) emission associated with two neighbouring galaxies near ULASJ1234 + 0907 - G1234N ( 21 from quasar; z_ CO =2.514 ) and G1234S ( 11.5 from quasar z_ CO =2.497 ), which are also studied further in this paper. Although both galaxies are also covered by the UKIDSS-LAS survey, from which the quasar was initially selected, they are too faint at near infra-red wavelengths to be detected in UKIDSS. Both galaxies are likely to be highly obscured and our ALMA observations in B17 therefore correspond to the first detections of these new galaxies.

Finally, we have also detected dust continuum emission from the two companion galaxies to ULASJ1234 - G1234S and G1234N (Table tab:all_obs ). No continuum emission was detected at lower frequencies with the JVLA for these sources, so it is reasonable to assume that any contribution from non-thermal emission to the ALMA data points is negligible. Estimates of the dust mass and star formation rates in these companion galaxies depend heavily on the assumptions made regarding the dust temperature and dust emissivity index in these galaxies.

gmw_00003655.name: G1234S
gmw_00003655.overview: G1234S is a highly obscured companion galaxy located near the quasar ULASJ1234 [arXiv:1805.09825]. Although undetected in CO(1-0), the galaxy exhibits extreme CO excitation that suggests X-ray or shock heating may play a role in the system [arXiv:1805.09825].

sources.summary: The paper identifies G1234S as a highly obscured companion near ULASJ1234, undetected in CO(1-0) but showing extreme CO excitation suggestive of X-ray or shock heating.

quotes.quote: In B17, we also detected CO(3-2) emission associated with two neighbouring galaxies near ULASJ1234 + 0907 - G1234N ( 21 from quasar; z_ CO =2.514 ) and G1234S ( 11.5 from quasar z_ CO =2.497 ), which are also studied further in this paper. Although both galaxies are also covered by the UKIDSS-LAS survey, from which the quasar was initially selected, they are too faint at near infra-red wavelengths to be detected in UKIDSS. Both galaxies are likely to be highly obscured and our ALMA observations in B17 therefore correspond to the first detections of these new galaxies.

The second companion galaxy near the quasar ULASJ1234 - G1234S - is undetected in CO(1-0) and we derive an upper limit on the CO(1-0) brightness temperature, assuming that the line has the same width and velocity centroid as the CO(3-2) emission from this source, which is detected at high S/N in Paper II.

The companion galaxy G1234S is similarly extreme in terms of its CO excitation properties. We did not detect CO(1-0) emission from this source but the CO(7-6) emission is considerably higher intensity compared to the CO(3-2) emission and based on the lines available, the CO SLED for this galaxy appears to be very similar to ULASJ1234. This strongly suggests that X-ray or shock-heating could be important in this system even though we currently do not have any evidence for an AGN within it.

gmw_00003656.name: J1631+4426
gmw_00003656.overview: J1631+4426 is a local, extremely metal-poor dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2110.05030, arXiv:2108.03850] with a low stellar mass [arXiv:2110.05030, arXiv:2108.03850] and a negative metallicity gradient [arXiv:2110.05030]. Structurally, it consists of a star-forming blue clump and a diffuse tail that are kinematically discontinuous from each other [arXiv:2206.04709].

sources.summary: The paper compares models to J1631+4426, reproducing one strong observed line ratio but noting another high ratio remains underpredicted and matches S04590.

quotes.quote: Using a slightly different approach, umeda2022 appeal to Markov Chain Monte Carlo techniques to select the combination of a blackbody and power-law ionizing spectra and gas parameters providing the best fits to the emission-line properties of three extremely metal-poor galaxies with strong emission (included in the sample of Section sec:res_sample ): J1631+4426, J104457 and I Zw 18 NW. umeda2022 then show that, for each galaxy, the best-fitting combination of blackbody+power-law ionizing spectra can be approached by a combination of the accretion-disc model spectrum of gierlinski2009 and the spectrum of a single-star SSP with an age between about 2 and 8 Myr (we note that the emission-line properties actually produced by these spectra are not compared to the observations in their paper). The /SFR ratios of these models are 5.0 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 / yr ^ 1 (J1631+4426), 1.0 10^ 41 erg s ^ 1 / yr ^ 1 (J104457) and 3 10^ 41 erg s ^ 1 / yr ^ 1 (I Zw 18 NW), reaching again up to 11 above the mean observed value in the douna2015 sample of metal-poor star-forming galaxies.

Remarkably, among the restricted library of models at hand, those shown in Fig. fig:umeda_fit_lines appear to provide reasonable fits to all but one emission line. In particular, the models reproduce the strong observed luminosities of these galaxies, corresponding to / ratios of 0.023 (J1631+4426), 0.018 (J104457) and 0.034 (I Zw 18 NW), i.e., totally out of reach of previous stellar population synthesis models (Fig. fig:diags_oldmodels b). The only line that the limited set of models considered here does not seem to naturally reproduce is , whose observed luminosities in two of the three galaxies are higher than predicted by the best-fitting models, especially in the case of J1631+4426. The observed / ratios are 0.048 (J1631+4426), 0.032 (J104457) and 0.036 (I Zw 18 NW).

For reference, the /SFR ratios of the best-fitting models in Fig. fig:umeda_fit_lines , computed by estimating the SFR from the 1500- \ luminosity by analogy with see footnote foot:umedasfr umeda2022 , span the range 3.6--7.2 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 / yr ^ 1 , compatible with the observations of lehmer2019 and douna2015 . The best-fitting metallicities and volume-averaged ionization parameters are (0.0005, -2.0) , (0.001, -1.4) and (0.0005, -1.7) for J1631+4426, J104457 and I Zw 18 NW, respectively, close to the values (0.0004, -1.6) , (0.001, -2.0) and (0.0005, -1.7) derived by umeda2022 using blackbody+power-law ionizing spectra. For reference, the best-fitting and values are (0.09,0.1) , (0.44,0.3) and (0.09,0.1) , respectively.

sources.summary: The paper observes HSC J1631+4426 with Subaru/FOCAS IFU, identifying it as a very metal-poor local dwarf and measuring its negative metallicity gradient and basic size and mass properties.

quotes.quote: With Subaru/Faint Object Camera And Spectrograph (FOCAS) Integral Field Unit (IFU), we have observed a nearby, low-mass EMPG, HSC J1631+4426, whose oxygen abundance and stellar mass are known to be 12+log(O/H) =6.9 and _ 10 (M_*/ M _ )=5.8 , respectively. The measured metallicity gradient is 0.36 0.04 dex kpc ^ 1 corresponding to 0.049 0.006 dex R _ e ^ 1 for the continuum effective radius of R_ e = 0.14 kpc. Our observation has successfully demonstrated that three-dimensional spectroscopy with 8m-class telescopes is powerful enough to reveal the metallicity distribution in local EMPGs, providing precious information of the baryon cycle in local analogs of primordial galaxies in the early Universe.

Our target, HSC J1631+4426, is identified to be the most metal-poor galaxy with a 1.6 solar metallicity (i.e., 12+log(O/H) = 6.90; ). HSC J1631+4426 is a local dwarf galaxy at redshift z = 0.03125 , which has been discovered in a metal-poor galaxy survey program named Extremely Metal-Poor Representatives Explored by the Subaru Survey (EMPRESS; ). Its stellar mass is (M_ / M_ )=5.8 and effective radius is measured as R_ e = 137^ +9 _ 7 pc using the Subaru Hyper Suprime-Cam (HSC) i band surface brightness profile isobepress .

sources.summary: The paper uses J1631+4426 as the most metal-poor detected galaxy and part of a nearby EMPG sample for optical-line ionizing-spectrum modelling.

quotes.quote: J1631+4426 is the most metal-poor galaxy detected, with Z=1.6 Z_ hereafter, K21 2021ApJ...913...22K .

All three galaxies in our sample have optical spectroscopic data available. In Figure BPT , we present the locations of all three galaxies on a diagram of 6583 / and 5007 / emission line ratios, so-called the Baldwin-Philips-Terlevich diagram BPT diagram; 1981PASP...93....5B . The black dots represent the emission line ratios of z 0 star forming galaxies (SFGs) and AGNs from the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) Data Release 7 (DR7) 2009ApJS..182..543A . 6583 / and 5007 / emission line ratios for all three galaxies can be explained by only stellar radiation 2003MNRAS.346.1055K .
We avoid using for the fitting of J1631+4426 because the reported flux is not consistent with the Balmer decrements.

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1631+4426 as an extremely metal-poor, low-mass EMPG with unusually high Fe/O, unlikely to be explained by Type Ia supernovae.

quotes.quote: EMPRESS has identified J1631+4426 , whose metallicity is 1.6 (O/H) _ . J1631+4426 shows the lowest metallicity reported so far, with a low stellar mass of 10^ 6 M _ . While J1631+4426 (Paper I) and J0811+4730 Izotov2018 have extremely low-metallicities of 2 (O/H) _ , Kojima2021 ( Kojima2021 ; hereafter Paper II) have reported that the 2 EMPGs show high Fe/O ratios of ( Fe/O)_ .

Even considering the offsets originated from the ICF difference between Izotov2006 and Rodriguez2005 , we confirm that J1631+4426 and J0811+4730 having the lowest O/H ratios of 2 (O/H) _ show (Fe/O) _ , which are higher than those of the other 3 EMPGs with 10 (O/H) _ . As discussed in Paper II, however, Type Ia SNe are less likely to be main contributors to enhance Fe/O ratios of J1631+4426 and J0811+4730 because of their low O/H and N/O ratios.

sources.summary: The paper maps J1631+4426 as an extremely metal-poor galaxy with a star-forming blue clump, diffuse tail, very low metallicity, and discontinuous internal kinematics.

quotes.quote: 1 J1631+4426 : The HSC gri image illustrates that J1631+4426 consists of a blue clump (indicated by the cyan arrow) and a white diffuse structure elongated from north to south (white arrow) . We refer to the white structure as the ``EMPG tail'' (Paper III). The H flux map shows that the H flux of J1631+4426 is dominated by the blue clump, which indicates that star formation in J1631+4426 should mainly occur in that region . Paper I has confirmed that the blue clump has the very low metallicity of 12+ ( O/H)=6.90 (Section sec:sample ) based on the direct-temperature method, while the EMPG tail can have a metallicity even lower than the blue clump based on the [ O iii ] 5007/H ratio Kashiwagi2021 . The velocity map shows that the blue clump is discontinuously redshifted by 20 km s ^ 1 with respect to the EMPG tail, which indicates that the blue clump and the EMPG tail are not in the same kinematic structure.

gmw_00003657.name: I Zw 18 NW
gmw_00003657.overview: I Zw 18 NW is an extremely metal-poor starburst galaxy [arXiv:2312.08432, arXiv:2201.06593] that exhibits strong emission-line luminosities [arXiv:2312.08432]. It is connected by a neutral hydrogen bridge to a faint companion galaxy, which has experienced lower recent star formation [arXiv:1303.3909].

sources.summary: The paper compares models to I Zw 18 NW, reproducing strong observed luminosities and matching best-fitting metallicity and ionization parameters from prior spectra.

quotes.quote: Using a slightly different approach, umeda2022 appeal to Markov Chain Monte Carlo techniques to select the combination of a blackbody and power-law ionizing spectra and gas parameters providing the best fits to the emission-line properties of three extremely metal-poor galaxies with strong emission (included in the sample of Section sec:res_sample ): J1631+4426, J104457 and I Zw 18 NW. umeda2022 then show that, for each galaxy, the best-fitting combination of blackbody+power-law ionizing spectra can be approached by a combination of the accretion-disc model spectrum of gierlinski2009 and the spectrum of a single-star SSP with an age between about 2 and 8 Myr (we note that the emission-line properties actually produced by these spectra are not compared to the observations in their paper). The /SFR ratios of these models are 5.0 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 / yr ^ 1 (J1631+4426), 1.0 10^ 41 erg s ^ 1 / yr ^ 1 (J104457) and 3 10^ 41 erg s ^ 1 / yr ^ 1 (I Zw 18 NW), reaching again up to 11 above the mean observed value in the douna2015 sample of metal-poor star-forming galaxies.

Remarkably, among the restricted library of models at hand, those shown in Fig. fig:umeda_fit_lines appear to provide reasonable fits to all but one emission line. In particular, the models reproduce the strong observed luminosities of these galaxies, corresponding to / ratios of 0.023 (J1631+4426), 0.018 (J104457) and 0.034 (I Zw 18 NW), i.e., totally out of reach of previous stellar population synthesis models (Fig. fig:diags_oldmodels b). The only line that the limited set of models considered here does not seem to naturally reproduce is , whose observed luminosities in two of the three galaxies are higher than predicted by the best-fitting models, especially in the case of J1631+4426. The observed / ratios are 0.048 (J1631+4426), 0.032 (J104457) and 0.036 (I Zw 18 NW).

For reference, the /SFR ratios of the best-fitting models in Fig. fig:umeda_fit_lines , computed by estimating the SFR from the 1500- \ luminosity by analogy with see footnote foot:umedasfr umeda2022 , span the range 3.6--7.2 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 / yr ^ 1 , compatible with the observations of lehmer2019 and douna2015 . The best-fitting metallicities and volume-averaged ionization parameters are (0.0005, -2.0) , (0.001, -1.4) and (0.0005, -1.7) for J1631+4426, J104457 and I Zw 18 NW, respectively, close to the values (0.0004, -1.6) , (0.001, -2.0) and (0.0005, -1.7) derived by umeda2022 using blackbody+power-law ionizing spectra. For reference, the best-fitting and values are (0.09,0.1) , (0.44,0.3) and (0.09,0.1) , respectively.

sources.summary: The paper treats I Zw 18 NW as a well-known extremely metal-poor galaxy with optical and X-ray data, using it to test ionizing-spectrum models and photometry.

quotes.quote: I Zw 18 NW is one of the most well-known EMPG that has optical and X-ray data hereafter, TI05 2005ApJS..161..240T . This allows us to check the consistency between the ionizing spectrum shapes beyond 54.4 eV and X-ray luminosity data for . All three galaxies in our sample have optical spectroscopic data available.

Multi-Wavelength Comparison and Relation with Cosmic Reionization We investigate the consistency between our ionizing spectrum shapes and the observed values of I Zw 18, where multi-wavelength data from the X-ray to optical photometry data are available. We assume that whole I Zw 18 has the same ionizing spectrum shape as our result for to compare the model with the observed values of I Zw 18 as a whole. We use the X-ray luminosity obtained by the X-ray Multi-Mirror Mission ( XMM-Newton ) 2013ApJ...770...20K,2017AA...602A..45L . For optical photometry, we use SDSS i' and z' band magnitudes from DR16 2020ApJS..249....3A for I Zw 18 NW and I Zw 18 SE to compare the fluxes of the continuum.

sources.summary: The paper describes IZw18C as IZw18's fainter companion, with young stellar populations and properties consistent with being a Very Young Galaxy proxy.

quotes.quote: Moreover, as shown in the comprehensive analysis of all available data for the prototype XMP blue compact galaxy IZw18 and its fainter companion IZw18C by PO12 , despite the detection with HST of red giant branch stars, the conclusion that the main stellar mass was formed during the very recent cosmological epoch ( 1 Gyr) remains valid. The reddish light at the periphery of the main star-forming regions is dominated by the strong nebular emission of the ionized gas. The presence of this highly inhomogeneous nebular emission in peripheral regions complicates background subtraction and may affect the analysis of the HST Color-Magnitude Diagram of stars in these regions. For IZw18C, the analysis of all available data leads to ages of stellar populations of 0.1 Gyr.

That is, in this context, IZw18 and IZw18C also should be treated as the best proxies for VYGs. We mention also the results of the HST data analysis for the system of IZw18 and IZw18C by Annibali2013 .

sources.summary: The paper treats I Zw 18's secondary body as a faint companion galaxy, resolves its stars, notes region E/D structure, and finds lower recent star formation.

quotes.quote: HI observations have revealed a neutral hydrogen bridge connecting I Zw 18 with a faint companion galaxy (the so called C component or secondary body), which has also been resolved into stars with HST imaging dou96,it04,alo07 , and a 13.5 kpc HI tail extending to the south of I Zw 18 main body lelli12 .
In the secondary body, region E follows the comma-shaped distribution of the brightest blue stars, while region D comprises the redder less crowded ``halo''.

On the other hand, the secondary body was much less active than the main body during the last 10 Myr, in agreement with the absence of nebular emission everywhere but in its central region see also papa12 . The peak of SF is found to occur 15 Myr ago in the more crowded region E and 250 Myr ago in the less crowded region D.

gmw_00003658.name: NGC 98
gmw_00003658.overview: NGC 98 is a bright, low-inclination barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1409.2876, arXiv:1002.1103]. It hosts a central pseudobulge [arXiv:1409.2876] and a box/peanut-shaped bulge farther out [arXiv:1409.2876, arXiv:0804.3589], which is characterized by a double minimum in its h4 radial profile [arXiv:0804.3589, arXiv:1505.00590].

sources.summary: The paper links NGC 0098's spiral-arm residuals, small bulge radius, inner kinematic drop, disk-like Sersic index, and h4 minimum to a pseudobulge plus outer B/P bulge.

quotes.quote: NGC 0098: The residuals of the photometric decomposition are large due to the prominence of the spiral arms. The best fit bulge model has an effective radius r_ e =1.6 which corresponds fairly well with the size of the drop present in the stellar kinematics ( r_ drop = 1.5 ). The rotation curve shows a step rise in this inner region and anti-correlates with the h_3 moment. The S ersic index ( n=1.2 ) is compatible with a disk-like structure.

The spectroscopic observations of this galaxy were already analyzed in mendezabreu08b and the characteristic minimum in the h_4 radial profile produced by a B/P bulges was found at r_ B/P =5 . We suggest this galaxy hosts a pseudobulge at the very center and B/P bulge farther out.

sources.summary: The paper finds robust evidence that NGC 98 has a face-on box/peanut-shaped bulge, traced by h_4 minima and LOSVD broadening along its bar.

quotes.quote: In NGC 98, instead, we find a double minimum in the h_4 profile. The two minima are symmetric with respect to the galaxy center ( || 5'' ). In the simulations of deb_etal_05 the minima in h_4 are 0.05 - 0.1 deep, comparable to the minimum in the profile of NGC 98.
A clear peak in the broadening is apparent at || 5 in NGC 98. We conclude that the evidence for a B/P-shaped bulge in NGC 98 is robust.

sources.summary: The review cites NGC 98 as a B/P-bulge case studied with h4 kinematic diagnostics, with the B/P size measured at 0.35 times the bar semi-major axis.

quotes.quote: However, in spite of the smart idea, this method has been applied only for a few galaxies with B/P bulges, like NGC 98 abreu2008 . The reason is that the observations are very demanding and require a large amount of observing time at large telescopes. The diagnostics for NGC 98 is shown in Figure 13. The size of the B/P is estimated from the radius of the minimum in the h_4 profile, which in this case is 0.35 times the bar semi-major axis length.

sources.summary: The paper observes NGC 98 as a bright, low-inclination barred galaxy and finds the h_4 double minimum expected for a face-on box/peanut-shaped bulge.

quotes.quote: Their main results are given here. t ccc & & The barred galaxies NGC 98 and NGC 600 were selected as bright and undisturbed galaxies with a low inclination, a large bar, and modest extinction in the bar. The nearly face-on unbarred galaxy NGC 1703 was added as a control object. The spectroscopic observations were carried out in service mode at the Very Large Telescope (VLT) at the European Southern Observatory (ESO) using The Focal Reducer Low Dispersion Spectrograph 2 (FORS2). The spectra were taken along the bar major axis of NGC 98 and NGC 600 and along the disk major axis of NGC 1703.
Of the two barred galaxies, NGC 98 has clear evidence of a B/P bulge while NGC 600 does not.

gmw_00003659.name: NGC 600
gmw_00003659.overview: NGC 600 is a bright, nearly face-on barred galaxy with a low inclination [arXiv:0804.3589, arXiv:1002.1103]. It is characterized by a lumpy bar structure and lacks both a substantial bulge and a boxy- or peanut-shaped bulge kinematic signature [arXiv:0804.3589, arXiv:1002.1103].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 600 as a nearly face-on barred galaxy but finds no significant kinematic B/P-bulge signature, consistent with its almost bulgeless, lumpy-bar morphology.

quotes.quote: In contrast, in NGC 600, which is also a barred galaxy but lacks a substantial bulge, we do not find any significant kinematic signature for a B/P-shaped bulge.

The same is true for the almost bulgeless, barred NGC 600. In both these galaxies > 30 , including within the bar in NGC 600. Thus, our failure to find an h_4 minimum cannot be ascribed to low spectral resolution.

Of the two barred galaxies, NGC 98 has clear evidence of a B/P bulge while NGC 600 does not. The absence of a B/P shape in the latter galaxy is not surprising since it appears to not have a bulge and its bar has a lumpy structure.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 600 as a bright, low-inclination barred galaxy, but finds no significant B/P signature, consistent with its lack of a substantial bulge.

quotes.quote: Their main results are given here. t ccc & & The barred galaxies NGC 98 and NGC 600 were selected as bright and undisturbed galaxies with a low inclination, a large bar, and modest extinction in the bar. The nearly face-on unbarred galaxy NGC 1703 was added as a control object. The spectroscopic observations were carried out in service mode at the Very Large Telescope (VLT) at the European Southern Observatory (ESO) using The Focal Reducer Low Dispersion Spectrograph 2 (FORS2). The spectra were taken along the bar major axis of NGC 98 and NGC 600 and along the disk major axis of NGC 1703.
Of the two barred galaxies, NGC 98 has clear evidence of a B/P bulge while NGC 600 does not. The absence of a B/P shape in this galaxy is not surprising since it appears to not have a bulge.

gmw_00003660.name: NGC 1703
gmw_00003660.overview: NGC 1703 is a nearly face-on unbarred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1002.1103]. Spectroscopic observations of the galaxy show no evidence of a boxy- or peanut-shaped bulge [arXiv:0804.3589, arXiv:1002.1103].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1703 as an unbarred control galaxy and finds no h_4 minimum or B/P bulge evidence, matching expectations for that control object.

quotes.quote: In NGC 1703, which is an unbarred control galaxy, we found no evidence of a B/P bulge.
For the unbarred galaxy, NGC 1703, the h_4 profile shows no sign of a minimum.
In the unbarred control galaxy NGC 1703 we had not expected to find a B/P bulge. The failure to find a double-minimum in h_4 is therefore fully consistent with previous results chu_bur_04 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1703 as a nearly face-on unbarred control galaxy and finds no B/P bulge or h_4 double-minimum, as expected.

quotes.quote: The nearly face-on unbarred galaxy NGC 1703 was added as a control object. The spectroscopic observations were carried out in service mode at the Very Large Telescope (VLT) at the European Southern Observatory (ESO) using The Focal Reducer Low Dispersion Spectrograph 2 (FORS2). The spectra were taken along the bar major axis of NGC 98 and NGC 600 and along the disk major axis of NGC 1703.

In the unbarred galaxy NGC 1703 we had not expected to find a B/P bulge and we included it in our sample as a control. The failure to find a double-minimum in h_4 is therefore fully consistent with previous results chu_bur_04 .

gmw_00003661.name: J1613 + 5616
gmw_00003661.overview: J1613 + 5616 is a low-redshift giant radio galaxy located at z=0.18 [arXiv:2008.02530]. It hosts a currently active galactic nucleus with a flat-spectrum core and highly resolved radio lobes [arXiv:2008.02530].

sources.summary: The survey identifies J1613 + 5616 as a low-redshift giant radio source with a flat-spectrum active core, resolved lobes, and possible double-double structure.

quotes.quote: J1613 + 5616 This is a low redshift GRS at z=0.18 (Figure fig:GRG1 , top left panel). The angular size is 8.5 arcmin which makes the linear size 1.5 Mpc. The flux density of the core is 11.5 mJy at 610 MHz, 7.8 mJy from VLA FIRST at 1.4 GHz and 8.7 mJy in NVSS at 1.4 GHz.

Due to the large angular size of the object and high angular resolution, the lobes are highly resolved, and hence the total flux density may be underestimated due to missing flux. The spectral index of the core is flat suggesting a currently active AGN, despite its large size. Assuming a hotspot advance speed of 0.1c gives the kinematic age as 50 million years.

gmw_00003662.name: J1607 + 5447
gmw_00003662.overview: J1607 + 5447 is a giant radio source with a photometric redshift of 1.14 [arXiv:2008.02530]. It has a linear size of 1.25 Mpc, featuring faint radio lobes and a compact core [arXiv:2008.02530].

sources.summary: The survey classifies J1607 + 5447 as a giant radio source candidate at photometric redshift 1.14 with faint lobes and a compact detected core.

quotes.quote: J1607 + 5447 The angular size of the source is 2.5 ' (Figure fig:GRG1 , top panel, second from left). For this size, it will be a GRS with size >1 Mpc for any redshift above 0.6. The HSC Photometric redshift of this source is 1.14 and the corresponding linear size of the source is 1.25 Mpc, classifying this as a giant radio source.

The lobes are faint and the emission is confined to largely near the hotspots. In the VLA FIRST survey, only the core is detected, with a flux density of 2.5 mJy. The flux density of the core at 610 MHz is 2.8 mJy.

gmw_00003663.name: J1611 + 5432
gmw_00003663.overview: J1611 + 5432 is a giant radio galaxy located at a spectroscopic redshift of 0.714 [arXiv:2008.02530]. It has a linear size of 0.95 megaparsecs and features asymmetric radio lobes with different spectral indices [arXiv:2008.02530].

sources.summary: The survey includes J1611 + 5432 as a giant radio source, giving its spectroscopic redshift, nearly megaparsec size, lobe fluxes, and asymmetric spectral indices.

quotes.quote: J1611 + 5432 The spectroscopic redshift of this source (Figure fig:GRG1 , top panel, third from left) is 0.714 and for the angular size 2.2 ' , the linear size is 0.95 Mpc, hence we included this in the category of GRS fig:GRG1 . Only the southern lobe is detected in VLA FIRST, but both lobes are detected in the NVSS with a flux density of 4 mJy for the southern lobe and the northern lobe at 2.9 mJy. The corresponding values at 610 MHz are 11.9 mJy and 5.9 mJy respectively. The southern lobe has a steeper spectral index of 1.3 as compared to the northern lobe ( 0.9 ).

gmw_00003664.name: J1608 + 5434
gmw_00003664.overview: J1608 + 5434 is a high-redshift giant radio galaxy candidate with a host galaxy located at a spectroscopic redshift of 0.909 [arXiv:2008.02530]. The radio source has an angular size of 1.8 arcmin, which corresponds to a linear size of 0.85 Mpc [arXiv:2008.02530].

sources.summary: The survey describes J1608 + 5434 as a high-redshift giant radio source candidate with a spectroscopic host, 0.85 Mpc extent, and blended core/lobe structure.

quotes.quote: J1608 + 5434 The host galaxy of this GRS lies at spectroscopic redshift of 0.909. The angular size 1.8 arcmin of the source corresponds to a linear size of 0.85 Mpc Figure ( fig:GRG1 , top panel, last). The core and southern lobe are blended in the NVSS and hence this appears as a double source in NVSS. Though the size is <1 Mpc, we have included this source here due to its higher redshift since there are very few GRS known in this redshift range.

gmw_00003665.name: J1609 + 5354
gmw_00003665.overview: J1609 + 5354 is a giant radio quasar located at a redshift of approximately 0.99 [arXiv:2109.07825, arXiv:2008.02530]. It has a linear size of 1.1 megaparsecs and features structured radio lobes that suggest the presence of multiple hotspots [arXiv:2008.02530].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1609 + 5354 as a giant radio quasar with multiwavelength data; X-CIGALE SED fitting yields a steep optical continuum and small inclination angle with little dust contamination.

quotes.quote: In our sample we identified two GRQs, J1429 + 3356 ( =14 ^ h 29 ^ m 42.65 ^ s = + 33 ^ 56 ^ 54.8 ^ , z=1.12) and J1609 + 5354 ( =16 ^ h 09 ^ m 13.19 ^ s = + 53 ^ 54 ^ 29.7 ^ , z=0.99) with near and far UV, optical, near, mid and far IR photometry data along with radio at 1.4 GHz data available. For both quasars the SED fitting was performed using a new version of Code Investigating GAlaxy Emission (CIGALE; boquien2019 ) so-called X-CIGALE code yang2020 . We used different modules from X-CIGALE which correspond to a unique physical component or process taking place in an AGN host.

We obtained the reduced ^2 =2.1 and ^2 =1.4 for J1429 + 3356 and J1609 + 5354 respectively. The main physical parameters of GRQ's hosts are listed in Table param . As the GRQ J1429 + 3356 and J1609 + 5354 have steep optical continua with _ =-1.96 and _ =-1.77 respectively, it is expected that the reddening caused be dust or orientation effects should by very small in those two GRQs.

Also, both GRQs have small inclination angles resulting in less dust contamination of the central AGN. We compared the modelled inclination angles with lobe-to-core ratio and obtained the smaller value of inclination for the higher R parameter, as it is expected.

sources.summary: The paper reports J1609 + 5354 as a giant radio quasar at z=0.99258, with megaparsec-scale size, structured lobes, and evidence for multiple hotspots.

quotes.quote: J1609 + 5354 This giant radio quasar (Figure fig:GRG1 , bottom left panel) has a spectroscopic redshift of 0.99258. The source is about 2.5 ' in size which translates to a linear size of 1.1 Mpc. There are several interesting features for this giant radio quasar. There are structures present in both the lobes, which suggests more than one hotspot.

The other interesting aspect of this source is that the east lobe is resolved out in VLA FIRST (Figure fig:GRG1 , bottom middle panel), despite the fact that both our 610 MHz image and VLA FIRST have comparable angular resolution. The spectral index scaled equivalent rms noise at 1.4 GHz is 20 Jy beam ^ 1 for our 610 MHz images, which makes it much deeper than VLA FIRST, hence the clear detection of the east lobe with 610 MHz GMRT.

gmw_00003666.name: J1617 + 5432
gmw_00003666.overview: J1617 + 5432 is a compact double-double radio galaxy with an optical counterpart located at a spectroscopic redshift of 0.6999 [arXiv:2008.02530]. The source features diffuse outer lobes and compact inner lobes with a hotspot pair, indicating a restarted active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2008.02530].

sources.summary: The survey highlights J1617 + 5432 as a compact double-double radio source with a central optical counterpart, steep spectrum, diffuse outer lobes, and restarted-AGN evidence.

quotes.quote: J1617 + 5432 This is a `compact' double--double radio source (Figure fig:OTH1 , top left panel). The optical counterpart is located at the central region of the inner double and has a spectroscopic redshift of 0.6999. The total angular size of the source is less than an arcmin and hence the structure is not resolved in the NVSS. The total flux density at 610 MHz is 10.7 mJy and at NVSS is 4.5 mJy which corresponds to a spectral index of 1.0 .

The radio morphology has two distinct emission regions, the outer lobes are diffuse compared to compact inner lobes. The presence of a clear compact hotspot pair in VLA FIRST further supports that this is a re-started AGN.

gmw_00003667.name: J1611 + 5559
gmw_00003667.overview: J1611 + 5559 is a compact double radio galaxy located at a redshift of 0.314 [arXiv:2008.02530]. It features a pair of compact hotspots aligned in the north-east direction and shows clear evidence of relic radio emission [arXiv:2008.02530].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1611 + 5559 as a compact double galaxy with steep-spectrum radio emission, compact hotspots, and clear relic-emission evidence.

quotes.quote: J1611 + 5559 This is a very compact double having an angular extent less than 0.5 ' (Figure fig:OTH1 , top middle panel). The redshift of the galaxy is 0.314. The total flux density at 610 MHz is 15.7 mJy. The source is not catalogued in the VLA FIRST.

A compact pair of hotspots separated by about 6 '' , aligned in the north-east direction is seen. We have estimated the flux around this using the AIPS task TVSTAT which yielded a flux density of 3.6 mJy. The source is detected in the NVSS with a flux density of 4.8 mJy which puts the spectral index between 610 MHz and 1.4 GHz at 1.4 . This is a clear indication of presence of relic emission.

gmw_00003668.name: J1613 + 5608
gmw_00003668.overview: J1613 + 5608 is a radio galaxy with a possible core identified as the SDSS galaxy SDSS J161312.25 + 560800.5 [arXiv:2008.02530]. The source features a western radio lobe that is resolved out in the VLA FIRST survey and exhibits a steep spectral index [arXiv:2008.02530].

sources.summary: The paper notes that J1613 + 5608 has initially normal radio morphology but interesting details, including a possible SDSS core and a resolved-out steep-spectrum western lobe.

quotes.quote: J1613 + 5608 The radio morphology appears `normal' in the first look, but when investigated in detail, this source has some interesting features (Figure fig:OTH1 , bottom left panel). The SDSS galaxy SDSS J161312.25 + 560800.5 with photometric redshift of 0.2 at the `expected' location could be a possible core - there is no other galaxy or quasar in the central region. The west lobe is resolved out in the VLA FIRST survey (Figure fig:OTH1 , bottom right panel). The total flux density at 610 MHz for the west lobe is 8.8 mJy and the NVSS flux density is 3.2 mJy which gives a steep spectral index of 1.2 .

gmw_00003669.name: NGC 6143
gmw_00003669.overview: NGC 6143 is a face-on spiral galaxy located at a redshift of 0.0177 [arXiv:2008.02530]. It exhibits extended radio emission at 610 MHz characterized by low surface brightness and a steep spectral index of 0.9 [arXiv:2008.02530].

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 6143 as a nearby face-on spiral detected in extended 610 MHz radio emission, with low surface brightness and a steep radio spectral index.

quotes.quote: NGC 6143 NGC 6143 is a face on spiral galaxy at a redshift of 0.0177 (Figure fig:SFG1 , top left panel). We have detected extended radio emission from this galaxy at 610 MHz. This source was not catalogued with PyBDSF due to its low surface brightness and the smaller rms box size we adopted in PyBDSF. The integrated flux density at 610 MHz is 13.2 mJy excluding the point source at the edge.

The source is detected in the NVSS with flux density of 6.2 mJy. The point source is not detected in the VLA FIRST survey and in the high resolution VLA survey by this team, hence its contribution at 1.4 GHz is negligible. The spectral index of the source comes to be 0.9 0.2 .

gmw_00003670.name: CGCG 276-004
gmw_00003670.overview: CGCG 276-004 is a face-on spiral galaxy located within a galaxy group [arXiv:2008.02530]. It has been detected at both 610 MHz and 1.4 GHz, exhibiting a radio spectral index of 0.95 [arXiv:2008.02530].

sources.summary: The paper identifies CGCG 276-004 as a face-on spiral in a galaxy group, detected at 610 MHz and NVSS with a spectral index similar to NGC 6143.

quotes.quote: CGCG 276 004 CGCG 276-004 is another face on spiral galaxy at redshift of 0.0529 which has been clearly detected in our observations (Figure fig:SFG1 , top right panel). This lies in a galaxy group 2015AJ....149..171T . The integrated flux density is 12.8 mJy at 610 MHz. The source has been detected in NVSS with a flux density of 5.8 mJy. The radio spectral between 610 MHz and 1.4 GHz is 0.95 0.2 which is similar to NGC 6143 discussed in the previous section.

gmw_00003671.name: GALEXMSC J160603.68 + 552527.9
gmw_00003671.overview: GALEXMSC J160603.68 + 552527.9 is a broad-line active galaxy located at a redshift of 0.0311 [arXiv:2008.02530]. It exhibits faint, diffuse radio emission and has a flatter spectral index than nearby comparison galaxies [arXiv:2008.02530].

sources.summary: The paper describes GALEXMSC J160603.68 + 552527.9 as a broad-line galaxy with faint diffuse radio emission, NVSS detection, and a flatter spectral index than nearby comparisons.

quotes.quote: GALEXMSC J160603.68 + 552527.9 This galaxy (Figure fig:SFG1 , bottom left panel) is at redshift of 0.0311 and SDSS classified it as `broad line galaxy'. This object has not been studied in detail in the literature. The radio emission is extremely diffuse and faint. The integrated flux density at 610 MHz is 10 mJy.

The galaxy is detected in the NVSS with a flux density of 7.4 mJy. The spectral index is much flatter than NGC 6143 and CGCG 276-004. This could be due to missing flux at 610 MHz because GALEXMSC J160603.68 + 552527.9 is close to a bright source hence the noise in this part of the image is more than a factor of two worse compared to rest of the image.

gmw_00003672.name: 2MASX J16142202 + 5432180
gmw_00003672.overview: 2MASX J16142202 + 5432180 is a radio galaxy located at a redshift of 0.273 [arXiv:2008.02530]. It exhibits very diffuse radio emission at 610 MHz, but it is not detected in the NVSS or VLA FIRST surveys [arXiv:2008.02530].

sources.summary: The paper reports 2MASX J16142202 + 5432180 as a z=0.273 galaxy with diffuse 610 MHz emission and no NVSS or FIRST detection.

quotes.quote: 2MASX J16142202 + 5432180 2MASX J16142202 + 5432180 is a galaxy at a redshift of 0.273 (Figure fig:SFG1 ,bottom right panel). The radio emission is very diffuse at 610 MHz with a total flux density of 5 mJy. The galaxy is not detected in the NVSS or VLA FIRST surveys.

gmw_00003673.name: DYNAMO G04-1
gmw_00003673.overview: DYNAMO G04-1 is a compact rotating disk galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.13 [arXiv:2112.00764, arXiv:2301.05251]. It is a clumpy, turbulent system experiencing an enhanced mode of star formation, with a molecular gas fraction of 0.31 [arXiv:2112.00764, arXiv:1405.7410].

sources.summary: The paper studies G04-1 as a nearby clumpy turbulent disk, measuring stellar clump masses, radial clump trends, enhanced resolved star formation, and feedback implications.

quotes.quote: We present a case study of stellar clumps in G04-1, a clumpy, turbulent disk galaxy located at z = 0.13 from the DYNAMO sample, using adaptive optics enabled K-band imaging ( 2.25 kpc/arcsec) with Keck/NIRC2. We identify 15 stellar clumps in G04-1 with a range of masses from 3.6 10^ 6 to 2.7 10^ 8 \ , and with a median mass of 2.9 10^ 7 \ . Note that these masses decrease by about one-half when we apply a light correction for the underlying stellar disk.

A majority (12 of 15) of clumps observed in the K_ P band imaging have associated components in H maps ( 2.75 kpc/arcsec; < R _ clump > \ 500 pc) and appear co-located ( x 0.1 ). Using Hubble Space Telescope WFC/ACS observations with the F336W and F467M filters, we also find evidence of radial trends in clump stellar properties: clumps closer to the centre of G04-1 are more massive (consistent with observations at high- z ) and appear more red, suggesting they may be more evolved. Using our high-resolution data, we construct a star forming main sequence for G04-1 in terms of spatially-resolved quantities and find that all regions (both clump and intra-clump) within the galaxy are experiencing an enhanced mode of star formation routinely observed in galaxies at high- z . In comparison to recent simulations, our observations of a number of clumps with masses 10^ 7 10^ 8 \ is not consistent with strong radiative feedback in this galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper observes G 04-1 as a DYNAMO galaxy with z=0.075-0.15 sample context, higher star formation, rotating-disk classification, and a measured molecular gas fraction of 0.31.

quotes.quote: The systems observed were D 13-5, G 04-1, G 10-1 and H 10-2. Stellar masses for these systems range from 1-7 10^ 10 M _ and redshifts span the range z=0.075-0.15 . Star formation rates for D 13-5, G 10-1 and H 10-2 are 20 10 M _ yr ^ 1 . The star formation rate of G 04-1 is somewhat higher at 50 10 M _ yr ^ 1 . Galaxies G 04-1 and D 13-5 are classified as rotating disks, while H 10-2 and G 10-1 are classified as a perturbed rotators due to asymmetries in their velocity fields, and may be experiencing merging.
The DYNAMO galaxies have f_ gas =0.18 0.07 , 0.31 0.03 and 0.22 0.21 for D 13-5, G 04-1, and G 10-1 respectively.

sources.summary: The paper treats G04-1 as a compact rotating DYNAMO disk and reports spatially resolved extinction mapping with mild extinction variability and no highly attenuated clumps.

quotes.quote: In this work, we consider a sub-sample of six DYNAMO galaxies selected to build on a multi-wavelength study of these objects: DYNAMO D13-5, D15-3, G04-1, G08-5, G14-1, and G20-2, which are classified as rotating discs based on H kinematics.
Galaxies G04-1, G14-1, and G20-2 are furthermore classified as ``compact'' rotating discs green14 . These galaxies are ones for whom the SDSS band exponential scale lengths are less than 3 kpc.

Using HST H observations and Keck/OSIRIS Pa observations, bassett17 created spatially resolved ( 0.8-1 kpc) H extinction maps for galaxies D13-5, G04-1, and G20-2, showing that there is only mild spatial variability in the amount of extinction within a given galaxy, and finding no evidence for highly attenuated star-forming clumps.

sources.summary: The paper classifies G04-1 as a compact rotating disk, uses DYNAMO G04-1 for channel-map data, and reports G04-1 spiral-arm/inter-arm R_43 line-ratio variations.

quotes.quote: Galaxies C22-2, G04-1, G14-1, and G20-2 are furthermore classified as ``compact'' rotating disks, because their SDSS band exponential scale lengths are smaller than 3 kpc.
We present channel maps of for DYNAMO G04-1 in Figure fig:chann_maps to show an example of the final data, with the circularized beam shown in the bottom left corner of each panel.
Finally, Figure fig:line_ratio shows in particular for galaxy G04-1, variations in R_ 43 between the spiral arm and inter-arm region, a trend also observed for / in M51 koda12 .

gmw_00003674.name: DYNAMO G14-1
gmw_00003674.overview: DYNAMO G14-1 is a compact rotating disk galaxy [arXiv:2107.01217] that exhibits a strong gradient in its carbon monoxide line ratio [arXiv:2301.05251]. Its hydrogen-alpha emission reveals two bright clumps connected by a faint stream, which may indicate an ongoing interaction [arXiv:2301.05251].

sources.summary: The paper finds DYNAMO G14-1 has a strong CO line-ratio gradient, clumpy H emission linked to elevated ratios, rotating-disk kinematics, and no central enhancement seen in most sample galaxies.

quotes.quote: However, galaxy DYNAMO G14-1 shows a strong gradient in the line ratio, with values approaching unity. The H image of G14-1 in Figure fig:sum_obs shows two bright clumps with a fainter ``stream'' connecting the two. The H contours we over-plot in Figure fig:line_ratio show that these two bright features with the connecting filament coincide with the elevated line ratio values and the strong gradient. This may be indicative of an interaction taking place; however, the H kinematics of G14-1 show a rotating disk and no complex kinematics green14 .
Indeed, we find enhanced / values in the central kpc of all galaxies in Figure fig:line_ratio , except for G04-1 and G14-1, on the order of 10 (see Table tab:lrs ).

sources.summary: The paper treats G14-1 as a compact rotating DYNAMO disk and cites its clump colour profile as having strong extinction change but little age change.

quotes.quote: In this work, we consider a sub-sample of six DYNAMO galaxies selected to build on a multi-wavelength study of these objects: DYNAMO D13-5, D15-3, G04-1, G08-5, G14-1, and G20-2, which are classified as rotating discs based on H kinematics.
Galaxies G04-1, G14-1, and G20-2 are furthermore classified as ``compact'' rotating discs green14 . These galaxies are ones for whom the SDSS band exponential scale lengths are less than 3 kpc.

A significant fraction of the clumps in our sample show colour (age/extinction) profiles such as the ones we show for galaxies D13-5 and G08-5 in Figure fig:g04-1-c2 , where there is a strong increase in age, while fewer clumps show profiles such as the ones we show for galaxies G14-1 and G20-2, where there is no strong change in age but a strong change in extinction across the clump.

gmw_00003675.name: SDSSJ1347-0101
gmw_00003675.overview: SDSSJ1347-0101 is an early-type gravitational lensing system featuring a foreground lens galaxy at a redshift of z = 0.39 [arXiv:1011.0993]. This system lenses a compact, massive background source galaxy at z = 0.63 that is magnified by a factor of 12 [arXiv:1011.0993].

sources.summary: The paper presents SDSSJ1347-0101 as an early-type/early-type strong lens, modeling its foreground lens and compact z=0.63 background source and measuring the source as massive, magnified, and very small.

quotes.quote: Here we focus on the system SDSSJ1347-0101 with lens deflector redshift z = 0.39 and lens source redshift z = 0.63 , which was observed on 21 April 2010 UT in clear skies using a tip-tilt star with magnitude = 16.8 offset by 56 from the lens system.

We model the lens surface mass distribution as a singular isothermal ellipsoid SIE; e.g., kormann and allow for external shear. The light of the lensing galaxy is modelled as an elliptical Sersic profile with Sersic index n free to vary between 0.5 and 6, but we do not assume that the mass and the light of the lens are aligned. The background source was also initially modelled as a Sersic profile with Sersic index between 0.5 and 6.

The source has a total Kp magnitude of 20.3 (20.6 if a single-component is fit) and, again assuming a formation redshift z = 3 and solar metallicity, this implies that the rest-frame K-band magnitude is 21.0, the luminosity is L _ K,src = 10^ 10.8 L_ K, , and the stellar mass is M _ *,src = 10^ 10.9 M _ . The lensed (i.e., observed) source magnitude is 17.6, and we therefore find a factor of 12 magnification due to lensing. The background source size is 0.5 kpc if a single de Vaucouleurs profile is assumed or 1.1 kpc if a two-component de Vaucouleurs and Sersic profile is used.

gmw_00003676.name: A2261-BCG
gmw_00003676.overview: A2261-BCG is a massive elliptical brightest cluster galaxy located in the Abell 2261 cluster [arXiv:2006.05706, arXiv:2011.04663]. The galaxy features an exceptionally large depleted core [arXiv:2011.04663] and exhibits a constant stellar-population mass-to-light ratio [arXiv:2006.05706].

sources.summary: The paper presents the BCG in Abell 2261 as a dynamical-model example, finding tangential anisotropy, a good constant-mass-to-light fit, and constant stellar-population mass-to-light ratio.

quotes.quote: We plot the best-fitting dynamical mass profile for the BCG in Abell 2261 as an example in Figure A2261example , and we tabulate the best-fitting parameters in Table DynModsTable (for + CEN + DM), and in Table AdditionalTable in Appendix Additional (for and + CEN for completeness).

Here, we show the best-fitting model for the BCG in Abell 2261, as an example. If we consider the + CEN + DM fit for the BCG in Abell 2261, the best-fitting _ z is negative ( _ z = --0.29), with _ DYN = 3.44 ( ^ 2 /DOF = 0.79, solid red line in Figure A2261_varML ). If we, for illustration, force _ z = 0.25, then the best-fitting _ DYN = 2.80, yields a worse fit at ^ 2 /DOF = 61.01 (black dashed line). To fit the kinematics profile with _ z = 0 , _ DYN would have to increase from 2.80 in the centre to 4.20 for the outer two data points (a combination of the dotted and dot-dashed lines in Figure A2261_varML ). For Abell 2261 we find constant _ POP in our stellar population analysis.

sources.summary: The simulations target A2261-BCG's unusually large depleted core, arguing that binary scouring plus gravitational-wave recoil can grow it and that observed central knots are likely stalled minor-merger perturbers.

quotes.quote: However, the most massive cores, like the 3 core in A2261-BCG, remain challenging to explain in this way. In this paper, we run a suite of dry galaxy merger simulations to explore three different scenarios for central core formation in massive elliptical galaxies: `binary scouring', `tidal deposition' and `gravitational wave (GW) induced recoil'. Using the griffin code, we self-consistently model the stars, dark matter and SMBHs in our merging galaxies, following the SMBH dynamics through to the formation of a hard binary.

We find that we can only explain the large surface brightness core of A2261-BCG with a combination of a major merger that produces a small 1 core through binary scouring, followed by the subsequent GW recoil of its SMBH that acts to grow the core size. Key predictions of this scenario are an offset SMBH surrounded by a compact cluster of bound stars and a non-divergent central density profile. We show that the bright `knots' observed in the core region of A2261-BCG are best explained as stalled perturbers resulting from minor mergers, though the brightest may also represent ejected SMBHs surrounded by a stellar cloak of bound stars.

gmw_00003677.name: PID 114
gmw_00003677.overview: PID 114 is an active galactic nucleus that is possibly Compton-thick [arXiv:1209.0916]. It exhibits a strong iron K spectral line and a reflection-dominated spectrum from a cold medium [arXiv:1209.0916].

sources.summary: The paper identifies PID 114 as a possible Compton-thick AGN, noting its strong Fe K line, reflection-dominated spectrum, and moderate fitted column density.

quotes.quote: This is probably the case for PID 114, in which a strong Fe K line is detected (see below and Table 5), whereas the apparently moderate column density is inferred from the absorption model for the poor quality continuum spectrum (Table 4).
Two of them (PID 114 and PID 252) are possibly Compton thick AGN with a reflection-dominated spectrum.
The large EW observed in PID 114 (Table 5) agrees with an Fe K line expected from a reflection-dominated spectrum from cold medium, giving a support to the possibility of a Compton thick source.

gmw_00003678.name: PID 252
gmw_00003678.overview: PID 252 is an infrared-luminous galaxy that exhibits a power-law spectral energy distribution [arXiv:1204.1152]. The object is a candidate Compton-thick active galactic nucleus characterized by a hard X-ray color and weak iron K emission [arXiv:1209.0916].

sources.summary: The paper presents PID 252 as a possible Compton-thick AGN with the hardest X-ray color, weak Fe K emission, and similarity to weak-line Mrk 231.

quotes.quote: PID 252 has the hardest spectrum in terms of the hard X-ray colour h/m (Fig. 6) although no obvious Fe line is seen. For these two objects, a pure reflection spectrum from cold matter, modelled by pexrav (Magdziarz Zdziarski 1995) or pexmon (Nandra et al 2007), provides a comparable fit to their spectra, compared to the absorption model. This indicates that these two objects might be Compton thick AGN with a larger \ than that given in Table 4, e.g., 10^ 25 .

The spectrum of PID 252 does not show clear Fe K emission with EW 0.5 keV ( 2 upper limit of a narrow line at 6.4 keV). This weak-line source may be a high-redshift analogue of Mrk 231, a Compton thick AGN with a weak Fe K line in the local Universe (e.g., Braito et al 2004; Gallagher et al 2002; Iwasawa et al 2011 and other references therein).

sources.summary: U5877 is identified as an IR-luminous source with significant AGN emission, a power-law SED, strong X-ray counts, and absorption near 6e23 cm^-2.

quotes.quote: The SED-fitting procedure found that for all but one source (U428) the presence of an AGN is consistent with the photometry, although for only nine sources ( 35 of the sample: U4639, U4950, U4958, U5150, U5152, U5153, U5652, U5805, and U5877) its presence is significant at the 3 confidence level (i.e., solutions with no torus emission have ^ 2 ^ 2 _ min +28.5).

At a visual inspection, the presence of a nuclear component can be inferred for sources with a power-law SED (i.e., U4950 and U5877), for sources where the stellar component alone is not able to reproduce the datapoints around 3 m (i.e., U4958, U5153) or for sources where the starburst component, normalized to the far-IR datapoints, has already declined around 5--6 m (i.e. U5152, U5153, and U5805).

U4950 and U5877 are characterized by 1420 and 360 counts in the 0.5--8 keV band, which allow a moderate-quality \ spectral analysis. Both sources can be fitted using a powerlaw model, but the flat photon index ( 1 and + 1, respectively) is indicative of absorption, which has been estimated to be 1.2 ^ +0.3 _ 0.2 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 and 6.0 ^ +2.0 _ 1.5 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 , respectively, after fixing the photon index to 1.8.

gmw_00003679.name: A
gmw_00003679.overview: SGAS1723+34 is a bright, gravitationally lensed Lyman-break galaxy [arXiv:2312.10465, arXiv:2312.12518] located at a redshift of z = 1.3293 [arXiv:2312.12518]. It is characterized as a low-attenuation emission-line galaxy [arXiv:2312.10465, arXiv:2009.12383] with extreme emission line ratios and high equivalent widths [arXiv:2009.12383].

sources.summary: The overview treats SGAS1723 + 34 as a low-attenuation lensed TEMPLATES galaxy with HST, Spitzer, and lens-model data used for physical-property analysis.

quotes.quote: This paper gives an overview of TEMPLATES, a JWST Early Release Science program that targeted four extremely bright, gravitationally lensed galaxies: two extremely dusty, two with low attenuation, as templates for galaxy evolution studies with JWST. TEMPLATES obtains a common set of spectral diagnostics for these 1.3 z 4.2 galaxies, in particular H , Paschen , and the rest-frame optical and near-infrared continua. In addition, two of the four targets have JWST coverage of [ O III ] 5007 and H ; the other two targets have have JWST coverage of PAH 3.3 m and complementary ALMA data covering the [ C II ] 158 micron emission line. The science goals of TEMPLATES are to demonstrate attenuation-robust diagnostics of star formation, map the distribution of star formation, compare the young and old stellar populations, and measure the physical conditions of star formation and their spatial variation across the galaxies.

SGAS1723 + 34 SGAS1723+34 We delivered reduced HST imaging data in WFC3-UVIS/F390W, F775W and WFC3-IR/F110W,F160W, and Channel 1 and Channel 2 Spitzer data. Sharon.2020 describe the HST data, data reduction, and strong lensing analysis. The Spitzer data are described in Florian.2021 , who also use this lens model in their analysis of the physical properties of the lensed galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper studies lensed arc A as a bright multiply imaged emission-line galaxy, publishing broad wavelength spectra and deriving diagnostic constraints on its gas conditions.

quotes.quote: For the extremely bright lensed galaxy \ at , we analyze spatially integrated MMT, Keck, and Hubble Space Telescope spectra that fully cover the rest-frame wavelength range of 1400  \ to 7200  . We also analyze near-IR spectra from Gemini that cover H for a portion of the lensed arc. We report fluxes for \ detected emission lines, and upper limits for an additional . This galaxy has extreme emission line ratios and high equivalent widths that are characteristic of extreme emission-line galaxies.

Image families used in the lensing analysis of Sharon:2020hc are marked; the bright, multiply-imaged arc ``A,'' encompassing both images A1 and A2, is the subject of this paper. The lens model yields a total magnification for the giant arc of 52.7^ +3.3 _ 1.2 (Florian \ submitted)

sources.summary: The methods paper uses SGAS1723+34 as a bright lensed Lyman-break galaxy example for NIRSpec IFU outlier rejection, mapping its spatial light with [O III].

quotes.quote: For visualization purposes, we show as an example /NIRSpec IFS observations of SGAS1723+34, a bright, highly-magnified galaxy at z=1.3293 from the TEMPLATES program (Rigby et al., submitted). Unless otherwise specified, the IFS data are reduced using calibration reference files under calibration reference data system pipeline mapping (CRDS, pmap) 1105 and \ calibration pipeline version 1.11.3.

The result of the initial science target pixel mask is shown in Figure fig:mask a, using the bright lensed Lyman-break galaxy SGAS1723+34 from the TEMPLATES program. For this galaxy, we used the \ 5008 emission line to generate the S/N map shown. The straightforward S/N map, created from a series of IFU slices around the bright \ feature, clearly maps out the spatial light from the target galaxy.

gmw_00003680.name: ESO074-G015
gmw_00003680.overview: ESO074-G015 is a nearby barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1303.2463] with a visible bulge [arXiv:1303.2463] and a strong line-of-sight outer warp [arXiv:1303.2463, arXiv:1608.05559]. It features high-latitude neutral hydrogen gas [arXiv:1003.3110], a substantial star-forming region [arXiv:1003.3110], and a single ultraluminous X-ray source [arXiv:1512.06188].

sources.summary: The paper reports IC 5052 as a nearby SBd galaxy with a visible bulge, a newly identified line-of-sight outer warp, asymmetric H I kinematics, and an inner temperature plateau.

quotes.quote: IC 5052 is a nearby SBd galaxy. The bulge is clearly visible in figure. fig:IC5052-DSS . We find a previous unreported, line-of-sight warp in the outskirts of the galaxy. This is particularly clear in the zeroth moment map of the low resolution cube (figure fig:IC5052-doublemoment0 ).

The PV diagram of this galaxy (figure fig:IC5052-PV ) shows signs of this warp beyond 250 arcseconds. The main part of the profile looks like a solid-body rotator. The high- and low velocity sides are asymmetric, which is also clear from the integrated profile. The shape of the spectrum does agrees well with the profile from HIPASS. The maximum surface brightness temperature profile of figure fig:IC5052-maxtemperature shows a plateau around 80-100 K for most of the inner part of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The survey observed IC 5052 with XMM-Newton, finding sources in and near its optical extent and identifying its brightest internal point source as the only ULX in the sample.

quotes.quote: An X-ray image of the galaxy is shown in Figure  fig:xray_pgc65603 . Two sources are inside the D _ 25 ellipse and three are overlapping, with their centroids outside the D _ 25 region. One of the sources outside the galaxy is extended with a measured extent of 31.8 . The source extent is defined as the Gaussian sigma or beta model core radius. This source is discussed in section  sec:cluster .

The two sources within the galaxy appear to be point like, each with an extent of less than 6 . The flux of source 1 calculated using WebPIMMS corresponds to an unabsorbed luminosity of (2.87 0.05) 10 ^ 39 erg s ^ 1 . Thus, we classify it as a ULX.

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO074-G015 as an SBd galaxy with high-latitude HI gas but a surprisingly symmetric column-density distribution and XV diagram despite a nearby star-forming region.

quotes.quote: ESO074-G015 is a SBd galaxy with a substantial star formation region extending above and below the galactic plane on the west side of the galactic centre. However despite this, the \ column density distribution (shown in Fig. fig:ch2-eso074-g015-combi-plot ) is surprisingly symmetric. Although there is extensive high latitude \ gas extending to 3.7 kpc above and 4.2 kpc below the galactic equator, the distribution of elevated gas appears to be similar on both sides of the galactic centre.

Similarly the XV diagram is also fairly symmetric. There is a slight decrease in flux along the line-of-nodes of the west side of the galaxy possibly indicating gas depletion due to a star burst. However there is no apparent localised region of high velocity dispersion gas that could be associated with the star burst.

sources.summary: The paper finds IC 5052 hard to model because of its strong warp, which affects its measured thickness and rotation curve; self-absorption gives a larger H I mass than the optically thin model.

quotes.quote: IC 5052 Galaxy IC 5052 has proven hard to model. We show both the optically thin model and the self-absorption model in Figure fig:IC5052-velocitydispersion-thin . As we noted in Section 5.2 of Paper I, the galaxy has a very strong warp. Looking back at Figure 3 in Paper I, we can see that the onset of the warp occurs just beyond that same radius (180''). This warp is responsible for the very large thickness that is found beyond 5 kpc. The declining rotation curve beyond 5 kpc is also due to this component.
The self-absorption models have a total mass of 9.5 0.9 10^8 M _ , while the optically thin model recovers only 7.4 0.7 10^8 M _ .
