sources.summary: The paper finds IC 5052 asymmetric and hard to model, fits its two sides separately, reports a very short left-side scale length, and drops it from later analysis.

quotes.quote: IC 5052 Galaxy IC 5052 has proven hard to model, as the galaxy showed clear asymmetries. We therefore opted to model both sides of the galaxy separately (Figures fig:stellar-IC5052_left1 , fig:stellar-IC5052_left2 , fig:stellar-IC5052_right1 and fig:stellar-IC5052_right2 ). This forced us to fix the centre of the galaxy, which has clearly hampered the results. From an inspection of the images, we conclude that the left side better represents the galaxy, although the fit is far from perfect. The scale length for the left side h_ R ,* is 857 68 pc and the scale height h_ z ,* is 124 80 pc, which makes this the galaxy with the shortest scale length in our sample.
Overall, we find that the galaxy is very clumpy in its light distribution, which cannot be fit properly by the models.

sources.summary: The paper examines IC 5052 as the closest of three edge-on disks, finding 60 compact 8 micron cores, a 74 pc core-disk half-thickness, and detailed star-forming structure.

quotes.quote: To study the vertical distribution of the earliest stages of star formation in galaxies, three edge-on spirals, NGC 891, NGC 3628, and IC 5052 observed by the Spitzer Space Telescope InfraRed Array Camera (IRAC) were examined for compact 8 m cores using an unsharp mask technique; 173, 267, and 60 cores were distinguished, respectively.

The rms dispersion of the midplane distance, considered to be the half-thickness of the disk distribution of 8 m cores, is 105 pc for NGC 891, 438 pc for NGC 3628, and 74 pc for IC 5052. For NGC 891 and IC 5052, these are good representations of the disk half-thickness, but NGC 3628 is warped as a result of a recent interaction it is part of the Leo triplet, Arp 317, arp66 , and it is much thicker on the western side.
IC 5052 has a nearly constant, or possibly decreasing, midplane distance, varying as H( pc )=(123 29)-(15.4 14.8) R( kpc ) with an average inside 3.5 kpc of 100 pc.

gmw_00003681.name: ESO146-G014
gmw_00003681.overview: ESO146-G014 is a low-mass, metal-poor Sd disk galaxy [arXiv:1003.3110, arXiv:1608.05563] classified as a blue low-surface-brightness galaxy [arXiv:1602.05362]. It exhibits a slightly bent structure likely caused by a strong warp [arXiv:1303.2463], along with an asymmetric, patchy stellar distribution [arXiv:1608.05563].

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO 146-G014 as the faintest sample galaxy, slightly bent by a likely strong warp, with mostly symmetric PV structure, solid-body rotation, and a temperature plateau.

quotes.quote: ESO 146-G014 is the faintest galaxy in our sample. The moment zero maps (figure fig:ESO146-G014-maxtemperature ) show that the galaxy does not appear to have a fixed plane. Instead it looks slightly bent. This is most likely due a strong warp, which can also be seen in the visible in the DSS J-band image (figure fig:ESO146-G014-optical ).

The PV diagram (figure fig:ESO146-G014-PV ) looks symmetrical, although the integrated spectrum is more asymmetric than the HIPASS spectrum, with a stronger peak at the low-velocity side. Overall the galaxy appears to exhibit solid-body rotation. The maximum surface brightness temperatures appear to form a plateau over major part of the galaxy, averaging around 90 K (figure fig:ESO146-G014-maxtemperature ). On the left-hand side of the galaxy, this maximum drops relatively slowly towards the warp.

sources.summary: The paper compares ESO 146-G14 integrated and resolved measurements, reporting lower integrated H I surface densities and high dust-derived gas surface densities.

quotes.quote: Figure 1a contains the SFR surface density as a function of the total (solid symbols) or HI (open symbols) gas surface density for the auxiliary data (Sect. 2.2), plus the resolved observations for the XMPs Sextans A (red open and solid squares; Shi et al. 2014) and ESO 146-G14 (red open and solid circles; Shi et al. 2014), and the integrated measurements for the XMPs also contained in the DGS: SBS 0335-052, IZw 18 and VII Zw 403 (encircled brown open and solid triangles; Cormier et al. 2014). Figure 1b contains the SFR surface density as a function of the HI gas surface density, integrated over the optical radii, for the 43 sample XMP galaxies (blue open circles; Sect 2.1), plus the integrated values for the XMPs Sextans A (red open square; Sect. 2.1) and ESO 146-G14 (red open circle; Sect. 2.1), and for the metal-poor dIrr WLM (dark green open triangle; Sect. 2.2).

The adopted total HI masses of log(M _ HI /M _ ) 7.8 and 9.0 for Sextans A and ESO 146-G14, respectively (Filho et al. 2013 and references therein), are similar to the values quoted by Shi et al. (2014) for the disks of these two sources. However, the HI surface density is about an order of magnitude lower than the values provided for the individual star-forming regions (Shi et al. 2014). Similarly, the total HI mass for WLM (log(M _ HI /M _ ) 7.8; Hunter, Elmegreen Ludka 2010) is about two orders of magnitude above the estimated HI mass for the Northwestern component (Elmegreen et al. 2013). By averaging over the whole galaxy, although large-scale HI gas and HI gas from diffuse inter-star-forming regions are included, a significant part of the HI gas may be clumpy and concentrated in the star-forming regions. Hence, lower (by approximately an order of magnitude) integrated _ HI values (red open square and circle, and dark green open triangle, respectively; Fig. 1b) are obtained relative to the individual star-forming region values (red open squares and circles, and dark green open triangle, respectively; Fig. 1a).

The dust-derived total gas surface densities for Sextans A and ESO 146-G14 are high (Shi et al. 2014). Indeed, the disks of Sextans A and ESO 146-G14 (M _ dust /M _ 10 ^4 and 6 10 ^5 , respectively; Shi et al. 2014) and individual star-forming regions (M _ dust /M _ 1 -- 2 10 ^3 and 8 10 ^4 - 3 10 ^5 , respectively; Shi et al. 2014) contain significant dust masses, that exceed the total dust mass (M _ dust /M _ 450 -- 1800) determined in the prototypical XMP, IZw 18 (Fisher et al. 2014; Hunt et al. 2014).

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO146-G014 as a low-mass Sd galaxy with a resolved major and vertical structure, low rotation speed, differential inner rotation, and no HI bar evidence.

quotes.quote: ESO146-G014 This Sd galaxy extends to a maximum radial extent of 118 \ or 12.3 kpc (assuming the distance of 21.5 kpc determined from its systemic velocity). The galaxy is well-resolved by the synthesised beam along the major axis, extending to 15 FWHM beamwidths on each side of the galactic centre, where each FWHM beamwidth is 793 pc. In the vertical direction the measured scaleheight of the projected distribution is 2.6 times the HWHM beamwidth, indicating that the galaxy is well-resolved in the vertical direction also.

ESO146-G014 has a rotation speed of 70.2 , typical of low-mass Sd galaxies. The rotation of ESO146-G014 appears solid body from the XV diagram in Fig. fig:ch2-eso146-g014-combi-plot , however \ modelling (see Paper III) shows a differential inner rotation curve like other small disk galaxies when observed at high resolution e.g. swaters1999 . The XV diagram shows no evidence of a \ bar, which is as expected given the low oxygen abundance of 6 of the solar value br1995 .

sources.summary: The paper treats ESO 146-G14 as an EMP blue low-surface-brightness galaxy at 21.4 Mpc, with Herschel photometry used to study its star-forming regions.

quotes.quote: The Sample Our sample of EMP galaxies contains four objects including Sextans A, ESO 146-G14, DDO 68 and Holmberg II (Ho II). Sextans A is a dwarf irregular at 1.4 Mpc with 12+log(O/H)=7.49 based on the direct method Kniazev05 . ESO 146-G14 with a direct method based 12+log(O/H)=7.61 Bergvall95 is a blue low surface brightness galaxy at a distance of 21.4 Mpc. DDO 68 with a direct method based 12+log(O/H)=7.21 Pustilnik05 is a blue compact dwarf at a distance of 5.9 Mpc. Ho II is a Magellanic irregular dwarf at a distance of 3.3 Mpc McCall12 , with a direct method based 12+log(O/H)=7.92 Croxall09 . We also compared our results to the integrated measurements of the KINGFISH sample Kennicutt11 , the DGS (Dwarf Galaxy Survey) sample Madden13 , and other two EMP galaxies (I Zw 18 SBS 0335-052). These properties are listed in Table tab:properties .

Herschel broad-band images (PI: Y. Shi, PID: OT2 yshi 3) of Sextans A, ESO 146-G14 and DDO 68 were taken at 70 and 160 m through the scan map modes of PACS Poglitsch10 , and 250, 350 and 500 m through the small map modes with SPIRE Griffin10 . The exposure time (excluding overheads) in the two PACS bands is 1.9 hr, 1.6 hr and 2.7 hr for Sextans A, ESO 146-G14 and DDO 68, respectively, and in the three SPIRE bands is 6 min, 6 min and 10 min, respectively. The data of Sextans A and ESO 146-G14 have been partly published in Shi14 where the detailed data reduction was given, while the data of DDO 68 is newly presented here.

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO 146-G014 as a metal-poor, low-surface-brightness slow rotator with patchy, asymmetric structure that makes the FitSKIRT model far from perfect.

quotes.quote: ESO 146-G014 ESO 146-G014 is a known low-surface brightness galaxy and is extremely metal poor Roennback1995,Morales2011A . The galaxy is a slow rotator v_ max =84.1 km/s and is very patchy in nature, as can be seen in Figure fig:stellar-ESO146-G014 . Because of this, the fit is far from perfect. As can be seen in all three bands, the galaxy has a very bright central region, but also has a bright spot 50'' east of the centre. The galaxy is also asymmetric, as can be seen most clearly in the V-band image.

gmw_00003682.name: LBQS0109
gmw_00003682.overview: LBQS0109 is a type 1 quasar located at a redshift of approximately 2.4 to 2.5 [arXiv:1506.03096, arXiv:2106.05277]. Its spectrum reveals ionized outflows indicated by asymmetric emission-line profiles [arXiv:1506.03096], while its submillimeter continuum emission traces cold dust heated by star formation [arXiv:2106.05277].

sources.summary: The paper includes LBQS0109 among luminous z~2.4 QSOs whose SINFONI spectra show asymmetric emission-line profiles indicating ionised outflows, with broad BLR and narrower NLR components.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:spectrum shows the H-band spectra extracted from the nuclear region of each QSO from an aperture of 0.25 0.25 . The spectra clearly show the broad \ and the strong emission line doublet [ O iii ] 5007,4959 . In addition to these components, weak FeII emission lines are also visible in two out of the six QSOs (LBQS0109 and HE0251). The asymmetric \ profiles suggest the presence of ionised outflows in most of the targets (LBQS0109, 2QZJ0028, HB8905, HB8903 and HE0251).

Indeed the presence of a prominent blue wing is the typical signature of high velocity gas moving toward the line of sight. The \ emission line shows asymmetric velocity profiles as well as, in some cases, two distinct emission peaks (i.e HB8903). Indeed, in all spectra the \ line is a sum of two different components: a very broad ( FWHM>2000 km/s) one which is associated to the BLR emission and a fainter, narrower ( FWHM 500-1200 km/s) one likely associated to NLR emission.

sources.summary: The paper studies LBQS01 as a z 2.5 type 1 quasar whose ALMA band 7 continuum significantly traces cold dust from star formation, with modest AGN and synchrotron contamination.

quotes.quote: We selected three z 2.5 type 1 quasars for this work: 2QZJ002830.4-2817 from Canodiaz12 and; LBQS0109+0213 and HB89 0329-385 from Carniani16 . We note for the latter two objects the original [ O iii ] analyses were presented in Carniani15 . We will refer to these objects in this work as 2QZJ00, LBQS01 and HB8903, respectively. Originally, 2QZJ00, LBQS01 and HB8903 sources were selected as quasars with large [ O iii ] equivalent widths ( > 10 in the rest frame) and bright in H band ( < 16.5 mag) from a sample of Netzer04 and Shemmer04 (for more information see Carniani15 ).

The SED decomposition showed that the ALMA band 7 continuum observations have a significant component tracing the cold dust heated by star formation in objects LBQS01 and 2QZJ00. Based on the SED fitting, we estimated that the AGN contamination to the ALMA band 7 continuum for LBQS01 an 2QZJ00 is 12 ^ +26 _ 11 and 14 ^ +24 _ 13 , respectively, where the range accounts for 1 confidence interval.
There is a synchrotron contribution to the band 7 ALMA photometric point of 3 and 7 , for LBQS01 and 2QZJ00, respectively.

gmw_00003683.name: 2QZJ0028
gmw_00003683.overview: 2QZJ0028 is a type 1 quasar located at a redshift of approximately 2.5 [arXiv:2106.05277]. Its host galaxy contains cold dust heated by star formation [arXiv:2106.05277], as well as ionized outflows indicated by asymmetric emission-line profiles [arXiv:1506.03096].

sources.summary: The paper includes 2QZJ0028 among luminous z~2.4 QSOs whose SINFONI spectra show asymmetric emission-line profiles indicating ionised outflows, with broad BLR and narrower NLR components.

gmw_00003684.name: HB8905
gmw_00003684.overview: HB8905 is a luminous quasar at a redshift of approximately 2.4 [arXiv:1506.03096]. Its nuclear region exhibits ionized outflows [arXiv:1506.03096], while radio observations resolve a core, a jet-hotspot structure, and a counterjet spanning 4 arcseconds with little flux variability [arXiv:astro-ph/9911474].

sources.summary: The paper includes HB8905 among luminous z~2.4 QSOs whose SINFONI spectra show asymmetric emission-line profiles indicating ionised outflows, with broad BLR and narrower NLR components.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:spectrum shows the H-band spectra extracted from the nuclear region of each QSO from an aperture of 0.25 0.25 . The spectra clearly show the broad \ and the strong emission line doublet [ O iii ] 5007,4959 . In addition to these components, weak FeII emission lines are also visible in two out of the six QSOs (LBQS0109 and HE0251). The asymmetric \ profiles suggest the presence of ionised outflows in most of the targets (LBQS0109, 2QZJ0028, HB8905, HB8903 and HE0251).

Indeed the presence of a prominent blue wing is the typical signature of high velocity gas moving toward the line of sight. The \ emission line shows asymmetric velocity profiles as well as, in some cases, two distinct emission peaks (i.e HB8903). Indeed, in all spectra the \ line is a sum of two different components: a very broad ( FWHM>2000 km/s) one which is associated to the BLR emission and a fainter, narrower ( FWHM 500-1200 km/s) one likely associated to NLR emission.

sources.summary: The paper resolves 0504+030 into a core plus jet-hotspot and counterjet-like emission, measuring 0.48 Jy integrated flux, 4 arcsec extent, and little variability.

quotes.quote: 0504+030 We detect a 5 GHz integrated flux density of 0.48 Jy, of which 0.32 Jy is in an unresolved nucleus and 0.16 Jy in a jet-hotspot structure towards the NW as well as faint emission towards the SE (Fig. 8). A tapered map demonstrates the reality of this `counterjet' emission. The overall angular size of 0504+030 measures 4 arcsec. Our flux density determination is consistent with earlier ones (B91: 0.45 Jy, G95: 0.50 Jy), indicating little or no variability.

gmw_00003685.name: HB8903
gmw_00003685.overview: HB8903 is a type 1 quasar at a redshift of approximately 2.5 [arXiv:2106.05277]. Its spectra show asymmetric emission-line profiles that indicate ionized outflows [arXiv:1506.03096], while its submillimeter continuum is dominated by radio synchrotron emission from the active nucleus [arXiv:2106.05277].

sources.summary: The paper treats HB8903 as a z 2.5 type 1 quasar whose ALMA band 7 continuum is dominated by AGN radio synchrotron emission, leaving only an upper limit on star-formation infrared luminosity.

quotes.quote: We selected three z 2.5 type 1 quasars for this work: 2QZJ002830.4-2817 from Canodiaz12 and; LBQS0109+0213 and HB89 0329-385 from Carniani16 . We note for the latter two objects the original [ O iii ] analyses were presented in Carniani15 . We will refer to these objects in this work as 2QZJ00, LBQS01 and HB8903, respectively. Originally, 2QZJ00, LBQS01 and HB8903 sources were selected as quasars with large [ O iii ] equivalent widths ( > 10 in the rest frame) and bright in H band ( < 16.5 mag) from a sample of Netzer04 and Shemmer04 (for more information see Carniani15 ).

However, these photometric points indicate potentially strong radio synchrotron emission in the case of HB8903. Indeed HB8903 is `radio loud' with a radio luminosity of L_ 8.4GHz =10^ 27.7 W Hz ^ 1 Healey07 . It was, therefore, necessary to further constrain the SED in the sub-mm and mm regime for this source.

In object HB8903, the ALMA band 7 continuum emission is severally contaminated by the radio synchrotron emission from the AGN (over 99 ). We therefore only have an upper limit (3 ) on the infrared luminosity due to star formation, and hence on the derived star formation rate.

sources.summary: The paper includes HB8903 among luminous z~2.4 QSOs whose SINFONI spectra show asymmetric emission-line profiles indicating ionised outflows, with broad BLR and narrower NLR components.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:spectrum shows the H-band spectra extracted from the nuclear region of each QSO from an aperture of 0.25 0.25 . The spectra clearly show the broad \ and the strong emission line doublet [ O iii ] 5007,4959 . In addition to these components, weak FeII emission lines are also visible in two out of the six QSOs (LBQS0109 and HE0251). The asymmetric \ profiles suggest the presence of ionised outflows in most of the targets (LBQS0109, 2QZJ0028, HB8905, HB8903 and HE0251).

Indeed the presence of a prominent blue wing is the typical signature of high velocity gas moving toward the line of sight. The \ emission line shows asymmetric velocity profiles as well as, in some cases, two distinct emission peaks (i.e HB8903). Indeed, in all spectra the \ line is a sum of two different components: a very broad ( FWHM>2000 km/s) one which is associated to the BLR emission and a fainter, narrower ( FWHM 500-1200 km/s) one likely associated to NLR emission.

gmw_00003686.name: HE0251
gmw_00003686.overview: HE0251 is a luminous quasar located at a redshift of approximately 2.4 [arXiv:1506.03096]. Its nuclear spectrum displays weak iron emission lines and asymmetric profiles that indicate the presence of ionized outflows [arXiv:1506.03096].

sources.summary: The paper includes HE0251 among luminous z~2.4 QSOs whose SINFONI spectra show asymmetric emission-line profiles indicating ionised outflows, with broad BLR and narrower NLR components.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:spectrum shows the H-band spectra extracted from the nuclear region of each QSO from an aperture of 0.25 0.25 . The spectra clearly show the broad \ and the strong emission line doublet [ O iii ] 5007,4959 . In addition to these components, weak FeII emission lines are also visible in two out of the six QSOs (LBQS0109 and HE0251). gmw_00003687.name: RX J1131 1231
gmw_00003687.overview: RX J1131 1231 is a gravitationally lensed quasar located at a source redshift of 0.658 [arXiv:2007.14416] that hosts a supermassive black hole [arXiv:2007.14416, arXiv:2311.18088]. The system exhibits microlensing events [arXiv:2007.14416] and displays variable hard and soft X-ray emission [arXiv:2311.18088].

sources.summary: The paper uses the lensed quasar J1131 to model microlensing time delays, showing IMF-insensitive magnification distributions and a route to recover accretion-disk sizes from multiband light curves.

quotes.quote: We use RX J1131 1231 (hereafter J1131) as an example, as it has displays prominent microlensing events. The redshifts of lens and source are =0.295 and =0.658 , respectively SluseEtal03 . Based on the H_ line width from measured by SluseEtal03 , DaiEtal10 estimated the black hole mass to be = (1.3 0.3) 10^8 . tab:lensing_parameter lists the lensing parameters used to generate magnification maps, corresponding to a macro lens model with a stellar mass fraction of f_ =0.2 relative to a pure de Vaucouleurs model DaiEtal10,Tie&Kochanek18 .

We investigate the microlensing time delay using different IMFs taking the well-studied quasar J1131 as an example and show that the magnification probability distribution is insensitive to the IMF in the lensing galaxy. The probability distribution of microlensing time delay is wider when the mean stellar mass of the microlenses is small and narrow for larger masses. Still, for typical mean stellar masses between 0.3 and 1.0 , the distributions for microlensing time delays are nearly indistinguishable. This makes it possible to measure disk sizes even under the assumption of poorly known IMF or mean stellar masses, contrary to traditional microlensing methods, where the source size is very degenerate with the properties assumed for the microlenses.

sources.summary: The paper monitors the lensed quasar RX J1131 1231 in hard and soft X-rays, finding smaller hard-band variability, low-significance reflection-fraction changes, and a constrained cutoff energy.

quotes.quote: We present hard (10-80 keV) and soft (0.5-8 keV) X-ray monitoring of a gravitationally lensed quasar RX J1131 1231 (hereafter ) with NuSTAR, Swift, and XMM-Newton between 10 June 2016 and 30 November 2020. Comparing the amplitude of quasar microlensing variability at the hard and soft bands allows a size comparison, where larger sources lead to smaller microlensing variability. During the period between 6 June 2018 and 30 November 2020, where both the hard and soft light curves are available, the hard and soft bands varied by factors of 3.7 and 5.5, respectively, with rms variability of 0.40 0.05 and 0.57 0.02 . Both the variability amplitude and rms are moderately smaller for the hard X-ray emission, indicating that the hard X-ray emission is moderately larger than the soft X-ray emission region.

We found the reflection fraction from seven joint hard and soft X-ray monitoring epochs is effectively consistent with a constant with low significance variability. After decomposing the total X-ray flux into direct and reprocessed components, we find a smaller variability amplitude for the reprocessed flux compared to the direct emission. The power-law cutoff energy is constrained at 96 ^ +47 _ 24 keV, which position the system in the allowable parameter space due to the pair production limit.

gmw_00003688.name: SBM03 1
gmw_00003688.overview: SBM03 1 is a compact, high-redshift galaxy at z=5.83 [arXiv:astro-ph/0502385, arXiv:astro-ph/0308124] that exhibits a pronounced Balmer break [arXiv:0607039]. Its single asymmetric Lyman-alpha emission line and narrow velocity width indicate that its activity is powered by star formation rather than an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0508271].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SBM03 1 as a spectroscopically confirmed z=5.83 i-drop galaxy, robustly detected by Spitzer at 3.6 and 4.5 microns, with little dust reddening and a 2.5-3.6e10 solar-mass fit.

quotes.quote: Our four confirmed sources include two sources from Stanway, Bunker McMahon (2003): the brightest confirmed i' drop in the GOODS-South field (with z'=24.7 mag) SBM03 3 (with a spectroscopic redshift of z=5.78 from Keck/DEIMOS, Bunker et al. \ 2003); and the brightest i' drop in the UDF, SBM03 1 with z'=25.3 mag (spectroscopically confirmed with Keck/DEIMOS by Stanway et al. \ 2004a as having a redshift of z=5.83 ). Both of these spectroscopic redshifts were independently confirmed by Dickinson et al. \ (2004).
Only SBM03 1 is detected at 4.5 m -- SBM03 3 falls outside the region surveyed so far in this filter, and GLARE 3001 is undetected.

For the galaxy SBM03 1 (where the multi-waveband data was best, as it included NICMOS imaging in the near-infrared), we re-ran the `two-population' model fits (Section sec:age ) with a Chabrier (2003) IMF, instead of a Salpeter (1955) power law IMF. We used the same ongoing 10 Myr constant star formation rate burst, and fit for the age of an underlying older stellar population and the relative stellar masses of the burst and the old stars. Both IMFs produced comparably good fits (near-identical ^2 values), the same 450 Myr ages, and near-identical burst fractions (0.6 and 0.7 by mass for the Chabrier and Salpeter IMFs). The main difference came in the best-fitting total stellar mass: the Chabrier model produced a mass 30 less than the Salpeter IMF ( 2.5 10^ 10 M_ compared with 3.6 10^ 10 M_ for SBM03 1).

sources.summary: The paper uses SED fits for 23-6714 to show nebular emission can mimic a Balmer break, lowering the inferred age from hundreds of Myr to a few tens or even 6 Myr.

quotes.quote: For this object (23-6714) a non-zero extinction, of the order of A_V 0.2--0.8 depending on metallicity, is required. Since nebular lines at this redshift are expected to affect less the photometry at longer wavelenghts ( 5 ), it is interesting to examine SED fits including also the available 5.8 and 8.0 \ data (see Fig. \ fig_sed2 ). For consistency we therefore use the GOODS-MUSIC photometry in all bands.

A good agreement with the observations is found, and again significantly lower ages are obtained with nebular emission. For 23-6714 we find the best fit with Z=1/5 , a very young age of t_ 6 Myr and A_V 0.7 , compared to ages of 700 Myr with standard (purely stellar) templates. For the SED fit of 23-6714 shown in Fig. \ fig_sed1 for example, the current SFR 800 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies GOODS-S SBM03 1 as a compact i' drop with a single asymmetric emission line likely Lyman-alpha at z=5.83, near SBM03 3 in redshift and position.

quotes.quote: Spectroscopy of i' drops in GOODS-South Field GOODS-S SBM03 1 Our 5.5-hour spectrum of GOODS-S i' drop 1 in paper 1 (hereafter SBM03 1) reveals a single emission line at 8305.3 0.9 \ at the same location on the 1 wide longslit as the i' drop ( _ 2000 =03^h32^m40.0^s , _ 2000 =-27^ 48'15.0'' ).

Applying the same logic to our spectrum of GOODS-S SBM03 1, it seems likely that the emission line is Lyman- at z=5.83 - a view supported by the characteristic asymmetry in the emission line spectrum, with a sharp blue-wing cut-off This object has been independently confirmed as lying at z=5.8 by Dickinson et al. \ (2003) and corresponds to their object SiD002. . The redshift of SBM03 1 is very close to the redshift of SBM03 3 (the line centers are separated by 2000 km s ^ 1 ), and the spatial separation of 8 arcmin corresponds to a projected separation of only 2.9 h_ 70 ^ 1 Mpc. The flux in this emission line is comparable to that of SBM03 3 at z=5.78 , and is within 30 per cent of f=2 10^ 17 erg cm^ 2 s^ 1 , extracting over 17pixels (2 arcsec) and measuring between the zero-power points ( 8300-8318 ). This is a lower limit due to potential slit losses, although these are likely to be small, since like SBM03 3, SBM03 1 is compact ( R_ hl =0.1 , paper 1).
The equivalent width is W_ rest ^ Ly =30 10 , at the upper end of the distribution observed in z=3-4 Lyman break galaxies (Steidel et al. \ 1999).

sources.summary: The paper identifies SBM03 1 as a z=5.83 i-drop galaxy with a single asymmetric emission line, narrow Lyman-alpha velocity width, moderate rest equivalent width, and no N V detection, favoring star formation over AGN activity.

quotes.quote: For two of these objects, we discovered single emission lines (Fig. 3) at 8245 \ 8305 , consistent with Lyman- emission from galaxies at z=5.78 (SBM03 3, Bunker et al. \ 2003) and z=5.83 (SBM03 1, Stanway et al. \ 2004a). The spectrally-resolved profiles of the emission lines are asymmetric (as high- z Lyman- tends to be) with a P-Cygni-like profile and a sharp cut-off on the blue wing (Fig. 2). The line fluxes are 2 10^ 17 ergs cm ^ 1 s ^ 1 . The equivalent widths are W_ rest =20-30 \ using the z' band photometry from HST/ACS, which is within the range seen in high- z star-forming galaxies at z 3-4 .

The velocity widths of the Lyman- are comparatively narrow ( v_ FWHM =300 km s ^ 1 ) and we do not detect the high-ionization line N V 12140 , which strongly support the view that this line emission is powered by star formation rather than an AGN. Our spectroscopic redshifts for these objects confirms the validity of the i' drop selection technique to select star-forming galaxies at z 6 .

sources.summary: The paper obtains an excellent simulated SED fit for SBM03 1, emphasizing its pronounced Balmer break, old stellar ages around 130-200 Myr, and active star formation.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:many_objs shows that we obtain an excellent fit for SBM03 1 and satisfactory fits for SBM03 3 and GLARE 3001, with the chief difficulty in the latter objects being the anomalous fluxes in K_S as noted by eyl05 . For objects SBM03 1 dic04,sta04a,sta03 and SBM03 3 bun03,sta03 deduces stellar masses of 10^ 10 and mean ages that include the range 100--300 Myr irrespective of the assumed SFH, consistent with the findings of eyl05 . The minimum age for each model is older than the ages for the other objects in our sample because the small measured uncertainties on the 3.6 m fluxes for SBM03 1 and 3 create the strongest case for a pronounced Balmer break. In our simulations, such objects are roughly 130--200 Myr old and are forming stars at a healthy 30--60 (both 1 ).

gmw_00003689.name: SBM03 3
gmw_00003689.overview: SBM03 3 is a compact, high-redshift galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0302401, arXiv:astro-ph/0308124] with a spectroscopic redshift of z=5.78 [arXiv:astro-ph/0502385, arXiv:astro-ph/0508271]. Its single, asymmetric Lyman-alpha emission line and narrow velocity width indicate that its emission is powered by star formation rather than active galactic nucleus activity [arXiv:astro-ph/0508271].

sources.summary: The paper treats SBM03 3 as a z=5.78 GOODS-South i-drop galaxy with robust 3.6 micron detection, marginal 5.6 micron detection, anomalously faint K_s photometry, and a >1e10 solar-mass fit.

quotes.quote: Our four confirmed sources include two sources from Stanway, Bunker McMahon (2003): the brightest confirmed i' drop in the GOODS-South field (with z'=24.7 mag) SBM03 3 (with a spectroscopic redshift of z=5.78 from Keck/DEIMOS, Bunker et al. \ 2003); and the brightest i' drop in the UDF, SBM03 1 with z'=25.3 mag (spectroscopically confirmed with Keck/DEIMOS by Stanway et al. \ 2004a as having a redshift of z=5.83 ). Both of these spectroscopic redshifts were independently confirmed by Dickinson et al. \ (2004).

None of the sources were detected at >3 in the 5.6 8 m channels, although SBM03 3 has a very marginal ( 2 ) detection at 5.6 m ( AB=24.4 0.7 ). In the short-wavelength channels, SBM03 1 3 are well-detected ( >10 , AB 24 ), and we have a more marginal 3 detection of GLARE 3001 ( z'_ AB =26.4 ) at AB 26 . Only SBM03 1 is detected at 4.5 m -- SBM03 3 falls outside the region surveyed so far in this filter, and GLARE 3001 is undetected.

The galaxy SBM03 3 is towards the edge of the released ESO/VLT imaging, where fewer frames overlap and the noise is higher. Its K_s magnitude seems to be anomalously faint (a 3 detection at K_ AB =25.5 , twice as faint as the shorter-wavelength z' and J , which have AB=24.7 mag, and four times fainter than the longer-wavelength IRAC 3.6 m detection at AB=24.0 ). We still include the K_s filter in the fitting of the stellar populations (Section sec:SEDs ), but caution that the K_s magnitude may not be reliable.

sources.summary: The paper spectroscopically confirms SBM03 3 as a compact z=5.78 Lyman-emitting star-forming galaxy, reporting its resolved line profile, high UV luminosity, X-ray non-detection, and starburst interpretation.

quotes.quote: In this Letter we report on our spectroscopic confirmation with Keck/DEIMOS of one of our candidates -- object 3 in Stanway, Bunker McMahon (2003), hereafter called SBM03 3. We show that this z'_ AB =24.7 galaxy has Lyman- emission at a redshift of z=5.78 , and does indeed lie within the expected redshift range. This confirms the validity of i' drop technique of Stanway, Bunker McMahon (2003).

We have obtained deep spectroscopy with Keck/DEIMOS of SBM03 3, an i' drop in the Chandra Deep Field-South photometrically-selected from HST /ACS images to lie at z 6 . We discover a single emission line with peak intensity at 8245 , consistent with Lyman- emission form a galaxy at z=5.78 . The spectrally-resolved profile of the emission line is asymmetric (as high- z Lyman- tends to be) with a P Cygni-like profile and a sharp cut-off on the blue wing. The line flux is 2 10^ 17 ergs cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies SBM03 3 as a z=5.78 i-drop galaxy with a single asymmetric emission line, narrow Lyman-alpha velocity width, moderate rest equivalent width, and no N V detection, favoring star formation over AGN activity.

quotes.quote: For two of these objects, we discovered single emission lines (Fig. 3) at 8245 \ 8305 , consistent with Lyman- emission from galaxies at z=5.78 (SBM03 3, Bunker et al. \ 2003) and z=5.83 (SBM03 1, Stanway et al. \ 2004a). The spectrally-resolved profiles of the emission lines are asymmetric (as high- z Lyman- tends to be) with a P-Cygni-like profile and a sharp cut-off on the blue wing (Fig. 2). The line fluxes are 2 10^ 17 ergs cm ^ 1 s ^ 1 . The equivalent widths are W_ rest =20-30 \ using the z' band photometry from HST/ACS, which is within the range seen in high- z star-forming galaxies at z 3-4 .

The velocity widths of the Lyman- are comparatively narrow ( v_ FWHM =300 km s ^ 1 ) and we do not detect the high-ionization line N V 12140 , which strongly support the view that this line emission is powered by star formation rather than an AGN. Our spectroscopic redshifts for these objects confirms the validity of the i' drop selection technique to select star-forming galaxies at z 6 .

sources.summary: The paper treats SBM03 3 as a compact, spectroscopically confirmed z=5.78 galaxy whose Lyman-alpha line, flux, and proximity are compared with GOODS-S SBM03 1.

quotes.quote: Furthermore, in Bunker et al. \ (2003, hereafter paper 2) we presented deep spectroscopy for object SBM03 3, confirming its redshift as z=5.78 and illustrating the effectiveness of our i' drop selection.

The spectrum is very similar to that of GOODS-S SBM03 3 reported in Paper 2, where we argued that the most likely interpretation of the solo emission line at 8245 \ was Lyman- at z=5.78 , given the i' drop selection and the fact that we would resolve the [ OII ] doublet at lower redshift (and most other prominent emission lines would have neighboring lines within our wavelength range).

The redshift of SBM03 1 is very close to the redshift of SBM03 3 (the line centers are separated by 2000 km s ^ 1 ), and the spatial separation of 8 arcmin corresponds to a projected separation of only 2.9 h_ 70 ^ 1 Mpc. The flux in this emission line is comparable to that of SBM03 3 at z=5.78 , and is within 30 per cent of f=2 10^ 17 erg cm^ 2 s^ 1 , extracting over 17pixels (2 arcsec) and measuring between the zero-power points ( 8300-8318 ). This is a lower limit due to potential slit losses, although these are likely to be small, since like SBM03 3, SBM03 1 is compact ( R_ hl =0.1 , paper 1).

sources.summary: The paper finds a satisfactory fit for SBM03 3, linking its 3.6 micron flux and Balmer break to stellar ages around 130-200 Myr and substantial star formation.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:many_objs shows that we obtain an excellent fit for SBM03 1 and satisfactory fits for SBM03 3 and GLARE 3001, with the chief difficulty in the latter objects being the anomalous fluxes in K_S as noted by eyl05 . For objects SBM03 1 dic04,sta04a,sta03 and SBM03 3 bun03,sta03 deduces stellar masses of 10^ 10 and mean ages that include the range 100--300 Myr irrespective of the assumed SFH, consistent with the findings of eyl05 . The minimum age for each model is older than the ages for the other objects in our sample because the small measured uncertainties on the 3.6 m fluxes for SBM03 1 and 3 create the strongest case for a pronounced Balmer break. In our simulations, such objects are roughly 130--200 Myr old and are forming stars at a healthy 30--60 (both 1 ).

sources.summary: The paper models 31-2185 as a z about 6 galaxy whose apparent Balmer break can be fit with either old stellar populations or younger solutions including nebular emission.

quotes.quote: Figure fig_sed3 shows an object (31-2185 from E07) with an apparent Balmer break of similar strength. An old age t_ 700 Myr is again obtained here for standard stellar SED fits, whereas the inclusion of nebular emission lowers the age to 90--180 Myr, depending if we include or not the photometry in Channels 3 and 4. For this object and for the GOODS-MUSIC photometry it turns out that nebular emission is not crucial, since relatively short star-formation time scales and hence solutions with few ionising stars are preferred.

gmw_00003690.name: GLARE 3001
gmw_00003690.overview: GLARE 3001 is a spectroscopically confirmed high-redshift galaxy at z=5.79 [arXiv:astro-ph/0502385]. It is identified as a candidate low-equivalent-width Type II quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0512437] and may be undergoing a burst of star formation [arXiv:astro-ph/0607039].

sources.summary: The paper identifies GLARE 3001 as a strong low-equivalent-width Type II QSO candidate whose predicted optical and near-infrared colors match measured values.

quotes.quote: Particularly interesting is GLARE 3001, which lies directly on the predicted locus for the low equivalent Type II QSO in Figure fig:iz-zJ_idrop . Assuming the emission line is at z=5.79 (Stanway 2004a), (i_ 775 z_ 850 )_ AB =1.62 is predicted, while 1.66 0.20 is measured. Likewise, (J-K_s)_ AB =1.13 is predicted while 0.89 0.45 is measured (Eyles ).

It should be noted that the absence of a detectable 1240 line does not preclude a Type II QSO, as the to flux ratio can be very large (15--30) in Type II QSOs (Stern 2002; Mainieri 2005). The possible presence of and 4959, 5008 would also contribute to the measured Spitzer flux at 3.6 m . This object thus appears a prime candidate for a low equivalent width Type II QSO.

sources.summary: The paper identifies GLARE 3001 as a z=5.79 spectroscopically confirmed i-drop galaxy, marginally detected at 3.6 microns, undetected at 4.5 microns, and only weakly constrained in stellar age.

quotes.quote: The other two sources come from the Gemini Lyman- at Reionization Era (GLARE, Stanway et al. \ 2004b) spectroscopy with Gemini/GMOS: GLARE 3001 ( z=5.79 , z'=26.4 mag) and GLARE 3011 ( z=5.94 , z'=27.2 mag).

In the short-wavelength channels, SBM03 1 3 are well-detected ( >10 , AB 24 ), and we have a more marginal 3 detection of GLARE 3001 ( z'_ AB =26.4 ) at AB 26 . Only SBM03 1 is detected at 4.5 m -- SBM03 3 falls outside the region surveyed so far in this filter, and GLARE 3001 is undetected.

There is no significant evidence for a Balmer/4000 \ break in the two (fainter) spectroscopically-confirmed i' drops: the 3 detection of GLARE 3001 at 3.6 m suggests a break of 0 0.5 mag, and the non-detection of GLARE 3011 places a 3 upper limit of AB=1 mag on the break amplitude. Hence the constraints for the stellar ages of GLARE 3001 3011 are weak, but they are consistent with younger stellar populations than SBM03 1 3, which are brighter and presumably more massive.

sources.summary: The paper identifies GLARE 3001 as the hardest object to fit, with a flat SED, high K_s flux, and possible burst-like star formation outside the simulations.

quotes.quote: The object GLARE 3001 is an outlier that is poorly fit by our simulations, though in fairness the fits with one-parameter models are not optimal either. The best fits tend to favor models with younger stellar ages, lower masses, and higher SFRs than any simulated galaxies, perhaps indicating that this is a galaxy undergoing a burst. However, as discussed in the text, these fits are mainly driven by a high K_s band flux. If that data point were confirmed it would suggest that the simulations may be missing some physical process(es) that governs a small fraction of galaxies at this epoch.

gmw_00003691.name: GLARE 3011
gmw_00003691.overview: GLARE 3011 is a spectroscopically confirmed i-drop galaxy located at a redshift of z=5.94 [arXiv:astro-ph/0502385]. It was undetected at all Spitzer IRAC wavelengths, which places weak constraints on its stellar age and suggests a young stellar population [arXiv:astro-ph/0502385].

sources.summary: The paper identifies GLARE 3011 as a z=5.94 spectroscopically confirmed i-drop galaxy that was undetected by IRAC, with only an upper limit on any Balmer/4000 break and weak stellar-age constraints.

quotes.quote: The other two sources come from the Gemini Lyman- at Reionization Era (GLARE, Stanway et al. \ 2004b) spectroscopy with Gemini/GMOS: GLARE 3001 ( z=5.79 , z'=26.4 mag) and GLARE 3011 ( z=5.94 , z'=27.2 mag).
The fainter GLARE 3011 ( z'_ AB =27.2 ) was undetected at all IRAC wavelengths.

There is no significant evidence for a Balmer/4000 \ break in the two (fainter) spectroscopically-confirmed i' drops: the 3 detection of GLARE 3001 at 3.6 m suggests a break of 0 0.5 mag, and the non-detection of GLARE 3011 places a 3 upper limit of AB=1 mag on the break amplitude. Hence the constraints for the stellar ages of GLARE 3001 3011 are weak, but they are consistent with younger stellar populations than SBM03 1 3, which are brighter and presumably more massive.

gmw_00003692.name: NGC 2708
gmw_00003692.overview: NGC 2708 is a member of the NGC 2698 galaxy group [arXiv:2104.12805]. The galaxy exhibits tidal disturbances in its outer stellar disk and is connected to the dwarf companion NGC 2708-Dw1 by a faint, linear stellar stream [arXiv:2104.12805].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2708 as the host-group galaxy near NGC 2708-Dw1, with dwarf companions, a massive H I stream, and tidal disturbances in its outer stellar disk.

quotes.quote: NGC 2708 (Figure fig:NGC2708_mom0 ) is a member of the NGC 2698 group, an N=8 group identified by Makarov+2011 . NGC 2708 has two dwarf companions to the north, NGC 2709 and PGC 1075058 (or NGC 2708A). NGC 2709 is an S0 dwarf apparently devoid of gas, while PGC 1075058 is strongly detected in \ Pisano+2002 and has a faint, blue stellar component. There is a massive \ stream extending from NGC 2708 to the north.

Pisano+2002 argue that it is likely too massive to have originated from PGC 1075058, and suggest that it may have been stripped from NGC 2709. DECaLS Dark Energy Camera Legacy Survey, Dey+2019 images of NGC 2708 show signs of tidal disturbances in its outer stellar disk. The UDG NGC 2708-Dw1 is to the SE of NGC2708, away from the previously known dwarf companions. It is connected to its host galaxy by a faint, linear stellar stream (Figure fig:CFHT_tails , left).

gmw_00003693.name: NGC 2708-Dw1
gmw_00003693.overview: NGC 2708-Dw1 is an elongated ultra-diffuse dwarf galaxy companion to NGC 2708 [arXiv:2401.01931, arXiv:2104.12805]. It is connected to its host by a faint, linear stellar stream [arXiv:2401.01931, arXiv:2104.12805], exhibits optically red colors [arXiv:2401.01931], and is undetected in neutral hydrogen gas [arXiv:2104.12805].

sources.summary: The paper summarizes NGC 2708-Dw1 as a red, gas-poor elongated UDG companion with a stellar stream to NGC 2708, modest GC abundance, and older stellar population.

quotes.quote: Of this set of five, two of the UDGs - NGC 2708-Dw1 and NGC 5631-Dw1 - contain little evidence of recent star formation or neutral gas and exhibit optically red colors. The other three - UGC 9050-Dw1, KUG 0203-Dw1, and KDG 013 - in contrast have GALEX UV detections which is suggestive of recent star formation ( <100 Myr) and also exhibit bluer optical colors. Targets Previously Reported NGC 2708-Dw1 is an elongated UDG companion to NGC 2708 (of the NGC 2698 group), with a faint linear stellar stream connecting the two. jones2021 identified N_ GC = 3^ +1 _ 2 and S_ N =3.3^ +5.6 _ 1.0 for NGC 2708-Dw1.

Physical Properties In terms of size, NGC 2708-Dw1, NGC 5631-Dw1, KUG 0203-Dw1, and KDG 013 have comparable dimensions, with half-light radii ranging from 1.5 to 3.4 kpc. NGC 2708-Dw1, NGC 5631-Dw1, and KUG 0203-Dw1 have associated stellar streams connecting to a massive host. NGC 2708-Dw1 is the reddest ( g- = 0.5 ; bennet2018 ), followed by NGC 5631-Dw1 and KDG 013 ( g- = 0.4 ), UGC 9050-Dw1 ( g- = 0.3 ; fielder2023 ), and KUG 0203-Dw1 ( g- = 0.2 ). In contrast, NGC 2708-Dw1 and NGC 5631-Dw1 are significantly dwarfed by their companions.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2708-Dw1 as an ultra-diffuse dwarf beside a stellar stream, undetected in H I, with a small GC population and an over-luminous central GC candidate.

quotes.quote: With the HST F814W and F555W images we measure the globular cluster (GC) counts for NGC 2708-Dw1 and NGC 5631-Dw1 as 2^ +1 _ 1 and 5^ +1 _ 2 , respectively. NGC 2708-Dw1 is undetected in \ down to a 3 limit of (M_ HI / M_ ) = 7.3 , and there is no apparent \ associated with the nearby stellar stream.

The GC counts are consistent with those of normal dwarf galaxies, and the most probable formation mechanism is one where these UDGs were born as normal dwarfs and were later tidally stripped and heated. We also identify an over-luminous ( M_ V = -11.1 ) GC candidate in NGC 2708-Dw1, which may be a nuclear star cluster transitioning to an ultra-compact dwarf as the surrounding dwarf galaxy gets stripped of stars.
The UDG NGC 2708-Dw1 is to the SE of NGC2708, away from the previously known dwarf companions. It is connected to its host galaxy by a faint, linear stellar stream (Figure fig:CFHT_tails , left).

gmw_00003694.name: NGC 5631
gmw_00003694.overview: NGC 5631 is an elliptical galaxy situated on the edge of a loose group [arXiv:2104.12805]. It exhibits counter-rotating stellar and ionized gas components, misaligned neutral gas kinematics, and a curved stellar stream connecting it to a nearby dwarf companion [arXiv:2104.12805].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5631 as an elliptical host near NGC 5631-Dw1, with counter-rotating gas, disturbed H I kinematics, misaligned stellar motions, and a curved stellar stream.

quotes.quote: NGC 5631 (Figure fig:NGC5631_mom0 ) is an elliptical galaxy on the edge of a loose group also containing NGC 5667 and 5678 among various other potential members Pisano+2004 . The galaxy has previously been studied both in ionized and neutral gas. Silchenko+2009 found that the stellar and ionized gas components of NGC 5631 are counter-rotating and suggest its present form is the result of an ongoing, gas-rich, minor merger. Serra+2014 found further evidence of a highly disturbed object in the \ kinematics, which are (globally) not aligned with the ionized gas kinematics, but do twist in the inner region of the galaxy to align where there is overlap. Neutral gas and stellar kinematics are also misaligned Serra+2014 , varying from counter-rotation to an almost polar orbit, depending on the radius.
NGC 5631-Dw1 is to the SW of NGC 5631. It is connected to its host galaxy by a faint, curved stellar stream (Figure fig:CFHT_tails , right).

gmw_00003695.name: NGC 5631-Dw1
gmw_00003695.overview: NGC 5631-Dw1 is an elongated ultra-diffuse dwarf galaxy with a faint central surface brightness [arXiv:2104.12805, arXiv:2401.01931]. It is connected to its host galaxy, NGC 5631, by a faint curved stellar stream and a tentative coincident neutral hydrogen feature [arXiv:2104.12805, arXiv:2401.01931].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 5631-Dw1 as a UDG with tentative coincident H I blended into NGC 5631’s tail, giving an upper-limit H I mass and weakening a tidal-dwarf interpretation.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:NGC5631DW1_spec shows the \ spectrum within a single synthesized beam at the location of NGC 5631-Dw1. The prominent feature at 1800 \ corresponds to approximately the velocity of the SW tail and so is not necessarily associated with the UDG. However, an inspection of the channel maps (Figure fig:NGC5631DW1_chan_maps , in particular at 1794 ) indicates that there is a low S/N feature that is coincident with the UDG.

Integrating the flux in this feature gives an \ mass of (M_ HI / M_ ) = 7.2 . This should be treated as an upper limit on the \ mass of NGC 5631-Dw1 as this feature is certainly blended with the SW tail at the resolution of the D-array VLA data.

In the case of NGC 5631-Dw1 there is a tentative detection of \ emission that coincides with the UDG (Section sec:hi_props Figure fig:NGC5631DW1_chan_maps ) and is connected to the (highly disturbed) \ content of NGC 5631. This \ feature in the SW tail of NGC 5631 may be an indication of a tidal connection between UDG and host. Long-lived TDGs are generally expected to form at the tips of \ tails e.g Bournaud+2006 , but NGC 5631-Dw1 is midway along the ( ) tail in question (Figure fig:NGC5631DW1_chan_maps ), making this an unlikely explanation for the gas or the UDG.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5631-Dw1 as an elongated UDG connected to NGC 5631, with high specific GC frequency, plausible H I association, faint surface brightness, and redder population.

quotes.quote: NGC 5631-Dw1 is more notably elongated, connected to NGC 5631 with a faint curved stellar stream. Physical Properties In terms of size, NGC 2708-Dw1, NGC 5631-Dw1, KUG 0203-Dw1, and KDG 013 have comparable dimensions, with half-light radii ranging from 1.5 to 3.4 kpc. NGC 5631-Dw1 has a much fainter surface brightness than the other UDGs, as does UGC 9050-Dw1 outside of the ``core'' region.

In terms of central surface brightness-half light radius parameter space, UGC 9050-Dw1 is somewhat consistent with the \ UDG population (although still rather large) while NGC 5631-Dw1 is an outlier due to its central surface brightness. However, previous studies limited by surface brightness may not have thoroughly explored this parameter space, rather than NGC 5631-Dw1 being a significant outlier. NGC 2708-Dw1, NGC 5631-Dw1, and KUG 0203-Dw1 have associated stellar streams connecting to a massive host. NGC 2708-Dw1 is the reddest ( g- = 0.5 ; bennet2018 ), followed by NGC 5631-Dw1 and KDG 013 ( g- = 0.4 ), UGC 9050-Dw1 ( g- = 0.3 ; fielder2023 ), and KUG 0203-Dw1 ( g- = 0.2 ).

gmw_00003696.name: PACS-819
gmw_00003696.overview: PACS-819 is a rotation-dominated disk galaxy at redshift z=1.45 [arXiv:2303.16227, arXiv:2311.14809] that lies near the boundary separating star-forming galaxies and active galactic nuclei [arXiv:2303.16227]. The system exhibits a heavily obscured starbursting core [arXiv:2311.14809] alongside kinematically lopsided, non-circular gas motions [arXiv:2303.16227, arXiv:2311.14809].

sources.summary: ID3 is a far-infrared studied galaxy near the star-forming/AGN BPT boundary; its CO and UV morphologies differ and its disk shows lopsided, non-circular motions.

quotes.quote: ID3 This galaxy, also know as PACS819 is a Hershel detected galaxy Rodighiero_2011 whose global properties are extensively studied in the far-infrared wavelength range Silverman_2015, Silverman_2018, Chang_2020 . In addition, by examining the BPT diagram Baldwin_1981 , Silverman_2015 show that PACS-819 is close to the line separating star-forming and AGN galaxies due to the strong [ NII ] emission line Kewley_2013 . In the rest-frame UV HST image, ID3 shows two bright clumps, while the center of the CO(5-4) emission is located between them. Due to the different morphology between the CO and UV data, for ID3 we fixed the inclination angle to the one obtained with . The iso-velocity contours and the PVDs show that ID3 is kinematically lopsided, meaning that the velocity gradient in its approaching and receding sides are different from each other Swaters_1999, Bacchini_2023 .

The rotating disk models reproduce the bulk of the emission from ID3. However, the disturbances of the two external contours at 2.5 and 5 along the minor and major axis PVDs (see arrows in Fig. fig:pv1 ), as well as the residuals in the channel maps (Fig. fig:channel1 ), indicate the presence of non-circular motions, in particular at negative velocities. In Fig. fig:pv1 , we show with a green arrow the gas that is moving at lower rotation than those predicted by the model.

sources.summary: The paper resolves PACS-819 as a z=1.45 starburst with a disk-like rotating CO component, disturbed features, an obscured compact core, high gas fraction, and short depletion time.

quotes.quote: We present observations and analysis of the starburst, PACS-819, at z=1.45 ( M_*=10^ 10.7 M _ ), using high-resolution ( 0^ .1 ; 0.8 kpc) ALMA and multi-wavelength JWST images from the COSMOS-Web program. Dissimilar to HST/ACS images in the rest-frame UV, the redder NIRCam and MIRI images reveal a smooth central mass concentration and spiral-like features, atypical for such an intense starburst. Through dynamical modeling of the CO J=5--4 emission with ALMA, PACS-819 is rotation-dominated thus has a disk-like nature. However, kinematic anomalies in CO and asymmetric features in the bluer JWST bands (e.g., F150W) support a more disturbed nature likely due to interactions.

The CO J = 5 -- 4 and FIR dust continuum emission are co-spatial with a heavily-obscured starbursting core ( <1 kpc) which is partially surrounded by much less obscured star-forming structures including a prominent arc, possibly a tidally-distorted dwarf galaxy, and a clump, either a sign of an ongoing violent disk instability or a recently accreted low-mass satellite. With spatially-resolved maps, we find a high molecular gas fraction in the central area reaching 3 ( M_ gas / M_* ) and short depletion times ( M_ gas /SFR 120 Myrs) across the entire system.

gmw_00003697.name: NGC 2685
gmw_00003697.overview: NGC 2685, also known as the Helix galaxy, is a peculiar S0 lenticular Seyfert 2 galaxy [arXiv:1410.5120, arXiv:1702.02013] featuring a classical bulge [arXiv:1004.5393] and a heavily warped, low-surface-brightness disk containing gas, dust, and stars [arXiv:0810.3025, arXiv:1007.2059]. This complex structure exhibits disturbed gas kinematics [arXiv:2201.05080], helical dust lanes [arXiv:1008.5149], and irregular ultraviolet emission [arXiv:1702.02013] associated with outer-region star formation [arXiv:1609.03179].

sources.summary: The paper reinterprets NGC 2685 as a coherent, extremely warped gas-stellar disk system rather than two polar rings, with decoupled central and low-surface-brightness disks.

quotes.quote: Employing direct tilted-ring fits to the data cube as introduced in Paper I, we perform an analysis of the ``Spindle'' galaxy NGC 2685, previously regarded as two-ringed polar ring galaxy. Deep H i and optical (i ^ band) observations are presented. The H i observations strongly suggest that the gaseous structure of NGC 2685 does not consist of two separate mutually inclined regions, but forms a coherent, extremely warped disk, the appearance of two rings being due to projection effects. By comparing the H i total-intensity maps with the optical image we demonstrate that at large radii a faint stellar disk is well aligned with the outer H i disk. The shape of the dust-lanes obscuring the NE part of the inner stellar body indicates that also at smaller radii NGC 2685 possesses a disk containing gas, dust, and stars in which the various constituents are aligned.

Hence, in the region of the bright, central stellar body, NGC 2685 appears to consist of two disks that share a common centre, but have different orientation: a bright stellar lenticular body apparently devoid of dust and gas, and a heavily warped low-surface brightness disk containing stars, gas and dust. The low-surface-brightness disk changes its orientation gradually and at large radii assumes the orientation of the central stellar S0 disk. Since, according to our analysis, the intrinsic orientation of the low-surface-brightness disk changes through 70 , the gaseous disk is coherent, and is at no radius orientated perpendicularly with respect to the central stellar body, NGC 2685 is likely not a classical polar-ring galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2685 as a polar-ring S0 Seyfert, fits its HST nuclear light profile, and reports an upper limit on the nuclear black-hole mass.

quotes.quote: Our second galaxy is a Hubble Atlas polar ring galaxy, an S0 Seyfert 2. Schinnerer Scoville (2002) detected four giant molecular cloud associations within the polar ring in NGC 2685 (the Helix) with of order 10 ^7 M _ of molecular hydrogen.

NGC 2685 NGC 2685 is a polar ring galaxy, known as `the spindle'. The HST nuclear image is reproduced in Figure 3 and the light distribution has been fitted with a `nuker profile' (Lauer et al 2007). The profile appears in Figure 4, the model fit, and ^2 per degree of freedom implies that M _ > 3 10 ^7 with less than 20 probability. This is consistent with Beifiori et al (2009), who find an upper limit M _ < 1.1 10 ^7M_ .

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 2685 in the H I subsample, models its stellar and gas kinematics, notes possible gas-disc misalignment, and finds unchanged dark-matter fractions.

quotes.quote: However, only a subsample of four galaxies shows regular rotating features in their velocity fields in deeper observations with the WSRT: NGC 2685, NGC 4203, NGC 5582 and NGC 6798 (see Appendix sec:HI-fields for these velocity maps). The observing time for these follow-up observations was 9 12 hours for each galaxy. We note that the observations of NGC 2685 and NGC 4203 were presented in 2009A&A...494..489J and 2015MNRAS.451..103Y , respectively, and refer the readers to 2015MNRAS.451..103Y for more details about the data reduction.

There is a significant misalignment between the stellar disc (or the equatorial plane of the stellar component) and the disc for NGC 4203, and a clear misalignment for NGC 2685 and NGC 6798 (about 15 ignoring counter-rotation). Considering that q is a free parameter in the model, it is possible that there is a misalignment even for NGC 2685 when taking the misalignment in the inclinations into account. Among the four galaxies in the subsample, the dark matter fraction of NGC 2685 remains unchanged throughout all radii when include kinematics, while the dark matter fraction of NGC 4203 increases, and the dark matter fractions of NGC 5582 and NGC 6798 decrease, especially at f_ DM (5R_ e ) .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2685 as an outlier polar-ring early-type galaxy, with high-radius star formation, PAH and ionized-gas emission, young stellar populations, and post-merger evolution altered by interaction geometry.

quotes.quote: Outliers While the two classes described above neatly bring together two groups of early-type galaxies that have a large number of similar properties, several ``outlier" galaxies in our sample (NGC 2685, NGC 2974, NGC 4477) resist such straightforward classification. Here, we discuss the characteristics of each of these unique systems in turn and relate them to the two main modes of star formation. NGC 2685 is a prototypical polar ring galaxy, as is evident in a comparison of the morphology of the star-forming ring to the position angle of the galaxy and the stellar kinematics (Figure Main-SF ; see the molecular and atomic gas maps of SchSco02,Mor+06 ; but see also JozOosMor09 ).

This rare configuration is often attributed to the accretion of gas or a gas-rich satellite at a very specific orientation (see discussI ), but the relevant aspect for this discussion is that the resulting dynamics of the gas are unassociated with those of the stars, as in \ galaxies. Moreover, NGC 2685 also resembles this class of star-forming galaxies in that it contains ubiquitous younger stellar populations (SSP age of 6 Gyr; Kuntschner et al. in prep ) and ionized gas, the spatial distributions of which are unassociated with the current extent of the star formation event. In the following discussion, we therefore consider this system to be related to the \ galaxies (see blue-green symbols in Figures ks and starsgas ); we elaborate on this relationship in discussI .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC2685 as a dust-lane test case, combining HST/WFPC2 imaging with extinction analysis to show helical large-scale dust and smoother, scale-free dust structure on small scales.

quotes.quote: We study the case of the well-known dust-lane galaxy NGC2685 for which HST/WFPC2 data is available to test the dust distribution on different scales. Our results imply a scale-free dust distribution across the dust lanes, at least within 1 ( 60 pc) regions.

NGC2685 was observed with WFPC2 in the B (F450W), V (F555W) and I (F814W) passbands. The total exposures were 2300 sec (B), 1000 sec (V) and 730 sec (I), split into halves. The individual exposures in each filter were combined using the CRREJ task in STSDAS for cosmic-ray recognition and rejection. While on kpc scales the dust in NGC2685 seems to be organized in a well-defined helical structure, HST/WFPC2 images clearly reveal more complicated structures on much smaller scales.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 2685 as one of the bluest HI-rich early-type galaxies, with blue outer regions, late-type-like SFR surface density, and green-valley or blue-cloud integrated colors.

quotes.quote: It is striking that two rich ETGs, NGC 2685 and NGC 5173, show very blue outer regions (see Fig. fig:link1 ). Within the aperture 1-3 R _ eff or 3-10 R _ eff , the colours of these galaxies fall in the region occupied by star-forming late-type galaxies.

Moreover, the SFR surface density levels of NGC 2685 and NGC 5173 in the first aperture are the same as those of the inner regions of late type galaxies. Since our first aperture is between 1-3 R _ eff , even this region can be counted as an outer region. It is worth to note that these galaxies are the bluest galaxies in our sample and their colour is similar to those of star-forming galaxies. In Fig. fig:ellbyell , we show additional data points (red triangles) for two lenticular galaxies: NGC 404 and ESO 381-47. The values are calculated for the UV/ \ rings far from the optical body of the host galaxies by 2010ApJ...714L.171T and 2009AJ....137.5037D , respectively. We also show a grey hexagon data point in Fig. fig:ellbyell corresponding to a giant low-surface brightness galaxy (LSB): Malin 1. 2010A&A...516A..11L suggest that Malin 1 contains a double structure: an inner high surface brightness early-type spiral galaxy and an outer extended LSB disc. These authors find that the total \ mass is 6.7 10^ 10 M _ and the disc extends up to 110 kpc radii. In addition, a recent deep optical study has revealed that Malin 1 extends up to 150 kpc 2015ApJ...815L..29G . Although the SFR values of Malin 1 are obtained for a large area including the central regions see 2009ApJ...696.1834W , it is remarkable that our results are consistent with that of Malin 1. Since our rich galaxies have bright inner regions surrounded with inefficient star forming low-column density \ discs, we can ask this question: are the rich ETGs related to giant LSB objects such as Malin-1? However, we need more information and analysis to answer this question, therefore we will leave this topic to future studies. So far we have compared \ and SFR surface density within fixed apertures. Here we perform a pixel by pixel as done by previous authors e.g., 2008AJ....136.2846B,2010AJ....140.1194B,2012A&A...545A.142B,2015MNRAS.451..103Y . To do so, we smooth and re-grid the FUV images to the resolution and coordinate grid of the \ images. We show the smoothed FUV images together with column density contours and 3 ellipses indicating 1-3-10 R _ reff in Fig. fig:appendix3 . We focus on regions outside 3 R _ reff , and for galaxy we calculate the average FUV flux in bins of constant \ column density. The bins have a width of 0.3 dex and are identical for all galaxies. For each bin we then take the weighted mean of all the single-galaxy average FUV fluxes. These weighted mean values are indicated by the black markers in Fig. fig:intermsofhi , while the cyan boxes show the scatter of all pixels in each bin. 1.0 para,flushleft Note. Column (1): The name of the rich galaxies. The values in the next columns are for the first and second apertures (1-3 and 3-10 R _ eff ), respectively. Column (2-3): total \ mass. Column (4-5): average \ column density. Column (6-7): integrated star formation rate. Column (8-9): average star formation density. Column (10-11): star formation efficiency. It is clear from Fig. fig:intermsofhi that the _ SFR level is increasing with increasing \ column density (as seen also in Fig. fig:Integrated_SFR and fig:ellbyell ). However, the SF efficiency remains low at all \ column densities in the outermost regions (R > 3 R _ eff ) shown in this figure: the average SF efficiency is 1.3 (0.3 - 5) 10^ 11 yr ^ 1 . This is 5 times lower than in aperture 1. The time necessary to consume all the \ gas (without gas recycling) is 9 (1.5 - 23) t_ H (Hubble time) and 2 (0.7 - 9) t_ H for aperture 2 and 1, respectively. As can be seen from Fig. fig:intermsofhi , the outermost regions of early- and late-type galaxies are consistent with each others. The gas depletion time of the outer regions late-type galaxies is 10^ 11 yr ( 7 t_ H , 2010AJ....140.1194B ). 2015MNRAS.449.3700R analyse the same relation between _ gas and _ SFR in the \ dominated regions of different type of galaxies with a similar method to ours, and therefore we use their results (shown as colour lines in Fig. fig:intermsofhi ) to compare with ours. There is an offset between the lines of 2015MNRAS.449.3700R and our points, and this is most likely due to the fact that we explicitly select the outer regions of galaxies, while they include inner regions too (as long as they are \ dominated). It is worth to note that, for our sample, the scatter in the _ SFR is increasing with decreasing _ gas . Implication for the evolution of these galaxies From the UV-optical colours, we show that several galaxies are blue in the outer regions due to recent star formation related to the presence of \ gas. Their outer colours are so blue that if they were total colours, we would consider those ETGs to lie in the green valley or even blue cloud. Are these outer regions affecting the evolution of the host galaxies? Another way of asking this question is: can the \ gas in outer regions fuel the star formation at a sufficiently high level to rejuvenate them and change the morphology of the host by building a stellar disc? In this section, we will try to answer these questions. As mentioned in the previous sections, NGC 2685 and NGC 5173 are the bluest galaxies in our sample (in both apertures) If we integrate the flux of NGC 2685 and NGC 5173 up to 10 R _ eff , they reside in the green valley and blue-cloud regions, respectively. . The gas depletion time of these galaxies is 10 ^ 11 yr in the second aperture, accordingly, the outer regions of these galaxies will be bluer than the inner regions for a long time. However, given the low-level (inefficient) star formation, the \ gas cannot change the morphology of the host galaxies. If we integrate the total SF over 1 Gyr (assuming constant SF), the contribution to the total stellar mass will be 0.1 and 0.05 percent for NGC 2685 and NGC 5173, respectively.

sources.summary: The atlas describes NGC 2685 as a complex ringed galaxy with disturbed H I and ionized-gas kinematics, asymmetric nuclear emission, and a possible outflow missed by slit orientation.

quotes.quote: NGC 2685 (see Fig. Figure_AB1 ): This galaxy shows a complex structure, hosting an inner, polar ring and an outer ring, proposed to correspond to a warped disc, oriented as the galaxy disc Jozsa2009 . The H I gas morphology is disturbed with the presence of non-circular motions (likely due to the interaction between these two different rings), although its rotation curve is nearly that of a spiral Jozsa2009 . studied the [ O III ] kinematics for this galaxy within the ATLAS ^ 3D survey, finding a disturbed velocity map, probably ascribed to gas accretion. In the long-slit spectrum analysed shows very narrow line profiles ( 65 kms ^ 1 ) in both space- and ground-based spectra, although neither was oriented in the direction in which we see an extended ionised emission (PA 120 ; see middle-down panel in Fig. Figure_AB1 ). This asymmetric emission coming from the nucleus, extending up to 10 arcsec, was also pointed out by using long-slit spectroscopy around the [ O II ] 3727 line.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2685 as the Helix galaxy with metallicity, colors, molecular gas, a long-lived self-gravitating polar ring, and an important outer equatorial ring.

quotes.quote: NGC 2685 NGC 2685, the Helix galaxy, has been studied in detail by Eskridge Pogge (1997). Observations of a large metallicity, color indices, and molecular gas were claimed to argue against the idea that the ring forms from tidally captured dwarf galaxies, and that the polar ring would be a long-lived self-gravitating structure instead. Yet, we have shown that the accretion scenario does not require the donor to be a dwarf galaxy, and that a massive donor can form a massive (thus self-gravitating) polar ring; moreover we also show that most polar rings formed by tidal accretion are long-lived. Thus, the results of Eskridge Pogge (1997) do not seem to argue against the accretion scenario, but only to require that the donor was rather massive and the ring is stable. An important feature of NGC 2685 is the presence of an outer, equatorial ring.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 2685 as a classical-bulge galaxy, citing There is a dust lane that is offset from the center of the bulge, it is possibly disk-contamination; Otherwise the bulge is smooth, and appears similar to an E-type.

quotes.quote: NGC 2685 RLB.+P./S0 pec Classical Bulge. There is a dust lane that is offset from the center of the bulge, it is possibly disk-contamination. Otherwise the bulge is smooth, and appears similar to an E-type. The decomposition yields n_b=2.1 , and the bulges is not active with M_ 3.6 M_ 8.0 =-1.1 mag.

sources.summary: NGC 2685 is categorized as an H I disk/ring galaxy with a large disk beyond the bright optical body, and its warped disk suggests accreted gas.

quotes.quote: The final category (denoted D, for disc/ring) refers to galaxies where most of the \ is found in a fairly regularly rotating disc or ring. This category contains the galaxies NGC 2685, NGC 3032, NGC 3414, NGC 3489, NGC 4150 and NGC 4278, while also the only cluster galaxy detected (NGC 4262) falls in this category. In NGC 2685 and in NGC 4278 the \ disc is large, i.e. extending beyond the bright optical body, while in NGC 3032, NGC 3489 and in NGC 4150 the \ forms a small, inner gas disc.
The disc in NGC 2685 is heavily warped while the disc in NGC 3414 is polar, hence it is likely that also in these galaxies the gas has been accreted.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2685 as the Helix galaxy, a peculiar S0 with polar rings and dust lanes whose NUV emission is spectacular and very irregular.

quotes.quote: NGC 2685 The galaxy is known as the ``Helix" galaxy. RSA classifies this peculiar object, which shows polar rings and polar dust-lanes, as S0 _3 pec. Its polar rings/arcs/blobs, well known in the optical bands, become quite spectacular in NUV filters (Figure NGC2685-sb , top right panel). The NUV emission is very irregular in all UVOT filters.

sources.summary: The paper notes that NGC 2685, a well-known polar-ring galaxy, was not detected in the S4G 3.6 micron images because its ring lies within the main body and appears only with logarithmic scaling.

quotes.quote: On the other hand, it is interesting that the well-known polar ring galaxy NGC 2685 was not detected in the S ^ 4 G 3.6 m image. This is likely so because the polar ring is actually within the main body of the galaxy when looking at it with the histogram equalization scale in DS9 . It only shows up as a loop-like feature with the logarithmic intensity scale.

gmw_00003698.name: J0927+0004
gmw_00003698.overview: J0927+0004 is a high-mass quasar located at a redshift of z=1.4845 [arXiv:2304.01253]. It is characterized by a low mass accretion rate, a low Eddington ratio, and minimal intrinsic reddening [arXiv:2304.01253].

sources.summary: The paper selects J0927+0004 as a high-mass, low-accretion quasar whose optical/UV disc peak is observable, then shows relativistic ray tracing makes standard disc fits fail at high UV energies.

quotes.quote: We first explore the impact of the GR ray tracing on results from fitting pure disc models to the optical/UV spectra of Quasars. The best examples of these are from the sample of Capellupo15 with X-shooter data. The GR ray tracing affects the spectrum most around the disc peak, so we pick the object with the highest mass, lowest mass accretion rate in this sample so that it has the lowest predicted peak disc temperature, so that it can be studied in the observable UV region. This selects SDSS J092715.49+000400.9 (hereafter J0927+0004).

J0927+0004 has a high black hole mass, =9.2-9.3 low Eddington ratio, = -1.4 1.3 , and minimal intrinsic reddening Mejia-Restrepo16, Capellupo16, Fawcett22 . This, combined with an SDSS (DR7) redshift of z=1.4845 Schneider10 means that the disc SED peaks within the observable UV/Optical bandpass. We fit the data with the relagn code with r_w=r_h= (so it only produces disc emission) and set the colour temperature correction to that of Done12 .

gmw_00003699.name: SDSSJ143244.91+301435.3
gmw_00003699.overview: SDSSJ143244.91+301435.3 is a radio-loud narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.355 [arXiv:1403.3229, arXiv:1609.09501]. It is classified as a compact radio source with a steep, optically thin radio spectrum and non-blazar properties [arXiv:1609.09501].

sources.summary: The paper presents SDSSJ143244.91+301435.3 as a rare radio-loud NLS1 at z=0.355 with non-blazar properties, NLS1 optical lines, and compact radio emission.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we present the discovery of a rare example of RL NLS1 (SDSSJ143244.91+301435.3, z=0.355) with a radio-loudness parameter well in the radio-loud regime (R=160, computed at 5 GHz, and R _ 1.4 600, computed at 1.4 GHz) and showing non-blazar properties.

We conclude that SDSSJ143244.91+301435.3 can be confidently classified as NLS1 based on the H and H line widths and on the basis of the [ OIII ] 5007 /H flux ratio. Moreover, the source presents other peculiar properties, like the intensity of the iron emission and the presence of blue wings in the [ OIII ] emission lines, that are typical of this class of AGN.

SDSSJ143244.91+301435.3 is detected in many of the existing radio surveys (see Tab. tab_radio ): the NVSS ( Condon1998 ) and FIRST ( Becker1995 ), at 1.4 GHz, the WENSS survey ( Rengelink1997 ) at 325 MHz and the 7C ( Hales2007 ) at 151 MHz. The source is not present in the VLA Low-Frequency Sky Survey Redux (VLSSr) catalogue at 74 MHz ( Lane2012 ) and we have estimated an upper limit of 600 mJy.

sources.summary: The paper presents SDSSJ143244.91+301435.3 as a compact radio-loud narrow-line Seyfert 1 with steep radio emission, CSS-like morphology, and non-blazar orientation evidence.

quotes.quote: SDSSJ143244.91+301435.3 (z=0.355, implying a conversion factor of 4.9 pc/m.a.s.) is one of the few (a dozen in total discovered so far) RL NLS1 with a radio-loudness parameter, R _ 1.4 , of the order of 500 (corresponding to R_ 5 =120, computed at 5 GHz). The radio emission, however, is different from that usually observed in RL NLS1 with such a high value of R _ 1.4 , since it shows a steep ( =0.93) optically thin radio spectrum (Fig. radio_spect ). Based on the size of the radio emission ( 1.4 kpc, from the analysis of FIRST data) this object is a compact radio source. The steep radio spectrum and the lack of variability and polarization (as discussed in C14), instead, disfavor the hypothesis that the observed compactness is due to the orientation of a relativistic jet towards the observer, as in blazars. The compact morphology and the steep radio spectrum are characteristics remarkably similar to those observed in CSS sources.

gmw_00003700.name: Scl-MM-Dw1
gmw_00003700.overview: Scl-MM-Dw1 is a faint dwarf galaxy located at a projected distance of 65 kpc from the spiral galaxy NGC 253 [arXiv:1406.6687]. It hosts a complex stellar population containing both an old, metal-poor red giant branch and younger asymptotic giant branch stars [arXiv:1406.6687].

sources.summary: The paper reports Scl-MM-Dw1 as a newly discovered faint dwarf near NGC 253, likely at the same distance, with old metal-poor RGB stars plus younger AGB stars.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of a new faint dwarf galaxy, which we dub Scl-MM-Dw1, at a projected distance of 65 kpc from the spiral galaxy NGC 253. The discovery results from the Panoramic Imaging Survey of Centaurus and Sculptor (PISCeS), a program with the Magellan/Megacam imager to study faint substructure in resolved stellar light around massive galaxies outside of the Local Group. We measure a tip of the red giant branch distance to Scl-MM-Dw1 of D =3.9 0.5 Mpc, consistent with that of NGC 253, making their association likely. The new dwarf's stellar population is complex, with an old, metal poor red giant branch ( 10 Gyr, [ Fe/H ] 2), and an asymptotic giant branch with an age of 500 Myr. Scl-MM-Dw1 has a half-light radius of r_ h =340 50 pc and an absolute magnitude of M_ V = 10.3 0.6 mag, comparable to the Milky Way's satellites at the same luminosity.

We have presented the discovery of Scl-MM-Dw1, a faint dwarf galaxy found 65 kpc in projection from NGC 253. Its inferred distance (3.9 0.5 Mpc) matches NGC 253 itself, making a physical association likely. The stellar population of Scl-MM-Dw1 is complex, with an RGB consistent with an old, metal poor stellar population ( > 10 Gyr; [ Fe/H ] 2) and AGB stars with an age of 500 Myr.

gmw_00003701.name: giant arc seen in Abell 370
gmw_00003701.overview: The giant arc seen in Abell 370, also known as the Dragon arc, is a gravitationally lensed galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.725 [arXiv:2404.08033, arXiv:astro-ph/0008158]. It is lensed by the rich binary galaxy cluster Abell 370 [arXiv:2404.08033, arXiv:astro-ph/0008158] and exhibits surface brightness variability driven by stellar microlensing [arXiv:2404.08033, arXiv:astro-ph/0008158].

sources.summary: The paper uses the z=0.725 Dragon arc as a lensed-galaxy case study, modeling seven microlensing events to infer stellar luminosity functions and small-scale substructure.

quotes.quote: We study the particular case of seven microlensing events found in the Flashlights program in the Dragon arc ( z=0.725 ). A population of supergiant stars having a steep LF with =2.55^ +0.72 _ 0.56 fits the distribution of these events in the far and near regions. We also identify a small region of high density of microlensing events, and interpret it as evidence of a possible invisible substructure, for which we derive a mass of 1.3 10^8 (within its Einstein radius) in the galaxy cluster.
