In this work we pursue four goals: (i) study the macro+milli+micro lensing (``3M-lensing'') effect over stars at cosmological distances and near cluster CCs in order to provide context for recent and future discoveries of lensed stars where 3M-lensing is likely taking place, (ii) address the question of whether the millilensing effect from the numerous millilenses is sufficient to explain the transient events found at distances d>1'' from cluster CCs, (iii) study the relation between the number of observed microlensing events, the amount of substructure on small scales in the lens plane, and the luminosity function of the background population of high-redshift stars, and (iv) apply our results to recent observations, in particular to the case of the seven alleged microlensing events found by HST in the Dragon galaxy at z=0.725 as part of the Flashlights program Kelly2022 . This arc was originally known as the Giant Arc or A370 Arc01 Soucail1987,Soucail1988,Lynds1989,Grossman1989,Smail1993,Smail1996 , and rebranded as the Dragon arc after new images were obtained following the HST Servicing Mission 4 update of the ACS in 2009 https://www.newscientist.com/article/dn17765-upgraded-hubble-telescope-spies-cosmic-dragon/ .

Below we present a few useful numbers for the particular case of the Dragon arc, which holds the record for the number of transients discovered as part of the Flashlights program . The location of these events in relation to the CC is shown in Figure Fig_Dragon . This arc contains seven high-significance transients, with at least two of them found in the far region (see Figure Fig_Dragon ) and good candidates to be stars impacted by 3M-lensing Kelly2022 . For the particular case of the Dragon galaxy, the redshift of the lens is 0.375 (A370 cluster), and the redshift of the lensed galaxy is 0.725.

sources.summary: The paper uses the Abell 370 giant arc as a case study for cluster MACHO microlensing, adopting lensing parameters and predicting observable surface-brightness fluctuations in HST or NGST monitoring.

quotes.quote: As an illustration of the efficiency of microlensing as a mechanism for introducing variability, we present in section a370 a case study of the giant arc seen in the cluster Abell 370. This section also discusses the observational considerations for a monitoring program of giant arcs.

As an illustration of the efficacy of microlensing on introducing variability into surface brightness distributions, we now focus on the spectacular giant arc in Abell 370. At a redshift of z=0.37 , the rich galaxy cluster Abell 370 possesses several giant gravitationally lensed arcs (Soucail et al. 1987). Utilizing their spatial extent, detailed modeling of the mass distribution of Abell 370 has been undertaken (Kneib et al 1993; Abdelsalam et al. 1998), confirming the binary nature of the cluster. The most prominent giant arc is over 20 arcsecs in extent and has a spectroscopic redshift of z=0.724 (Soucail et al. 1988).

Examining the models for the mass distribution in Abell 370 in the vicinity of this giant arc, we choose a fiducial model with an optical depth of =0.6 and shear of =0.2 . This results in a mean magnification of _ th =8.33 .

sources.summary: The paper treats A370 HCM 6A as a lensed z=6.5 galaxy whose anomalous 4.5 micron flux complicates SED fitting, though simulated models otherwise give plausible physical parameters.

quotes.quote: A370 HCM 6A We have taken the photometric data and errors in the observed R,Z,J,H,K', and Spitzer /IRAC bands from hereafter CSE05 cha05 , and we have adopted their lensing magnification factor of 4.5. The measured 4.5 m flux is anomalously large; CSE05 interpret the excess over the inferred stellar continuum as H emission line flux and derive an SFR of 140 90 . In our simulations galaxies at z=6.5 that possess this object's 3.6 m flux are forming stars at 2--12 . Since such systems are not expected to produce H equivalent widths that would contribute significantly to the broad-band flux, we adopt the uncorrected 4.5 m measurement.

The fits that we obtain are very good, although we confirm that the 4.5 m measurement is difficult to interpret as purely stellar continuum emission. In fact, even without the anomalous 4.5 m flux the UV continuum is difficult to understand owing to the dip at K' sch05 , and the measured flux is difficult to reconcile with the observed luminosity function at z 6.5 mal04,kas06 . Thus, until more precise measurements of this object are available, any inferences regarding its physical properties should probably be regarded as suggestive.

sources.summary: A370 08 has the longest ionized gas tail in the sample, continuous from the galaxy, with a gas-depleted disk side, high tail velocity, and composite/star-forming line ratios.

quotes.quote: A370 08 is the galaxy with the longest tail of ionized gas ( 12 arcsec, i.e. 57 kpc). We carefully checked all the objects located along the tail and especially in the southmost part of the MUSE cubelet, but found no redshift correspondence with the ionized gas emission which shows a great level of continuity moving from the inner part of the galaxy to the extremes of the tail.

The galaxy itself shows a projected rotation of 200 , while the gas in the tail reaches 600 . The north-west side of the galaxy disk is already gas depleted, as is typical if the gas is removed due to ram-pressure stripping. The gas velocity dispersion is chaotic within the disk, with lower values at the compression front, and higher values at the opposite side and along the tail.

sources.summary: A370 02 is a small disturbed galaxy with a defined gas-tail region, low central gas dispersion, star-forming disk, and possible ionization-cone signatures.

quotes.quote: A370 02 is a small galaxy with signs of a disturbed morphology, especially towards the east side of the disk. The red galaxy covering large part of the cubelet is not interacting with A370 02, as MUSE spectra confirm. The kinematics show a rotation of 60 and following our definition of galaxy/tails, shown as black ellipse in Fig.

fig:a370_01_02 , a portion of it is already part of what we define a gas tail. The central gas velocity dispersion is low, while the beam smearing does not allow to clearly disentangle the stellar velocity/velocity dispersion which turn out to have odd values. The dust extinction is everywhere low, and the galaxy disk is dominated by the SF.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes A370p z1 as a double-peaked high-redshift LAE, estimating a smaller near-zone, predicted AGN X-ray luminosity, and possible self-ionized bubble.

quotes.quote: Recently, two other LAEs have also been reported to have double-peaked profiles: NEPLA-4, at z 6.54 songaila2018 , and A370p z1 in the parallel Frontier Field of Abell 370 meyer2020 .

The other two LAEs at z 6 , NEPLA-4 and A370p z1 can be analyzed by a similar procedure, with NEPLA-4's spectrum being nearly identical to that of COLA-1 due to the similar size of the near-zone, and that for A370p z1 being slightly smaller ( R_ , A370p z1 = 0.26 pMpc). We predict the intrinsic 0.5-7 keV emission from the AGN to be 10^ 44.17 and 10^ 44.09 ergs s ^ 1 respectively for these LAEs. While the stellar emission in A370 pz1 was found to be capable of self-ionizing its own HII bubble (with an escape fraction of f_ esc 0.9 for a fraction of its measured age of about 50 Myr), it was found that COLA-1 and NEPLA-4 need additional sources of ionization to keep their bubbles ionized given galaxy lifetimes of a few ten Myr meyer2020 .

We have studied the possible origin of the double-peaked Lyman-Alpha Emitters (LAEs), such as COLA-1, NEPLA4 and A370p z1= at z > 6.5 in the proximity zone of obscured AGN, whose ionizing emissivity leads to the emergence of the blue wing.

sources.summary: A370 01 is the most massive A370 sample galaxy, with a 38 kpc ionized gas tail, retained gas rotation, undisturbed stars, and composite/shocked tail spaxels.

quotes.quote: A370 01 is the most massive galaxy in the A370 sample ( (M_ /M_ )=10.62 ) and shows a ionized gas tail 8 arcsec long, i.e. 38 kpc (first and second panels in the first row of Fig. fig:a370_01_02 for the and [ OII ] emission, respectively). Part of the tail is visible from the HST images, but is outside the MUSE observed field. As in A2744 09 the gas rotation pattern is retained along the tail, that departs from a bright spiral arm in the galaxy. Again the stars are undisturbed, while the ionized gas component is, and within the disk they both display projected rotations of 150 . The BPT diagram allows to classify the stripped tail as composite, with some spaxels revealing possible shocks.

sources.summary: A370 03 is a clear RPS galaxy with undisturbed stellar kinematics, a long south-west ionized tail, high tail gas velocity, and star-forming disk emission.

quotes.quote: A370 03 is another clear example of a ram-pressure stripped galaxy: its stellar kinematics appears undisturbed, with a projected v_ rot 60 and a similar gaseous kinematics within the galaxy extent (black contours in Fig. fig:a370_03_06 . However, a very long tail of ionized gas appears toward the south-west direction (both in and in [ OII ] ). The gas velocity increases along the tail and reaches very high values (up to 600 ). No other neighbouring galaxies are visible in the HST images. The tail is characterized by high gas velocity dispersion and a combination of composite and star-forming regions, while the disk is all star-forming According to the BPT.

sources.summary: The paper notes A370 08 as ID 8006 with stripped clump CL49 and uses it as a disturbed-morphology example whose skewed light distribution increases centroid variance.

quotes.quote: Two of the galaxies are also noted in other MACS and Frontier Fields works, A370 01 is highlighted as an extreme case of RPS in Ebeling2019 , and A370 08 is noted in Lagattuta2019 as ID 8006 along with an associated clump of stripped material, ID CL49.

An example is shown in Figure fig:contours for A370 08, which shows the contours between 20 and 99 of the range of flux values, and the centroids of each contour as x markers colored accordingly. The skewed light distribution resulting from the disturbance pushes the centroids of the lower surface brightness emission towards the lower left of the figure relative to the central peak, resulting in an increased variance.

We further inspected the non-RPS sample objects and found that two of the blue objects in this region of the diagram are clumps which are close in location and velocity to A2744 01 and A370 08, strongly suggesting that they are clumps of material associated with those galaxies, which have been flagged as distinct objects by the source extraction but indeed are considered part of the jellyfish tail by our MUSE analysis Moretti2022 . The latter of those two objects, the clump to the south of A370 08, is likely to be the object identified as CL49 in Lagattuta2017,Lagattuta2019 which the authors also concluded was a clump of material detached from the main galaxy.

sources.summary: A370 06 is a rotation-dominated late-type galaxy with a 20 kpc ionized tail whose high-velocity, AGN-like line ratios suggest possible central-AGN contribution.

quotes.quote: A370 06 is clearly a rotation-dominated (with projected velocity of 150 ) late type galaxy, with a 20 kpc ionized gas tail extending toward the south east direction. Just outside the stellar contour traced by the ellipse another object is seen in projection, locate at a higher redshift, which is not responsible for the tail emission, nor is interacting with A370 06. The gas in the tail has a very high velocity ( 600 ), and as can be seen in the lowest right panel of Fig. fig:a370_03_06 , it is dominated by AGN-like line ratios, indicating the possible contribution by a central AGN in the development of this tail (possibly an outflow from the AGN).

gmw_00003702.name: NGC3613
gmw_00003702.overview: NGC 3613 is a bright elliptical galaxy that serves as the central galaxy of a group in a low-density environment [arXiv:2005.13370]. It is identified as a quasar remnant hosting a supermassive black hole [arXiv:0709.2574], and its surface photometry reveals a three-component profile with detectable substructure [arXiv:2005.13370].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 3613 as a bright E6 group-central elliptical with a bimodal globular-cluster system, low-density environment, three-component surface profile, and detectable substructure.

quotes.quote: We present the first photometric study of the globular cluster system (GCS) of the E galaxy NGC 3613 ( M_ V = -21.5 , d 30.1 Mpc), as well as the surface photometry of the host galaxy, based on Gemini/GMOS images. Being considered the central galaxy of a group, NGC 3613 inhabits a low-density environment although its intrinsic brightness is similar to the expected one for galaxies in the centre of clusters. The following characteristics are obtained for this GCS. The colour distribution is bimodal, with metal-poor globular clusters (GCs) getting slightly bluer with increasing radius. The radial and azimuthal projected distributions show that metal-rich GCs are more concentrated towards the host galaxy and trace its light distribution very precisely, while metal-poor GCs present a more extended and uniform distribution.

The GC luminosity function helps validate the adopted distance. The estimated total GC population of N_ tot = 2075 130 leads to a specific frequency S_N=5.2 0.7 , a value within the expected range for GCSs with host galaxies of similar luminosity. The surface photometry of NGC 3613 reveals a three-component profile and a noticeable substructure.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC3613 as a nearby quasar-remnant UHE cosmic-ray candidate, gives its black-hole mass and maximum cosmic-ray energy, and reports MAGIC observations with no significant excess.

quotes.quote: NGC3613 is a quasar remnant with a black hole of 1.6 10^ 8 M_ . The maximum cosmic ray energy is estimated to be 5.9 10^ 19 eV. This galaxy is located 0.7 ^ off the most energetic triplet event.

Following up with AGASA-HiRes quartet detection, we searched the possible UHE cosmic ray sources, nearby quasar remnants NGC3610 and NGC3613 and a starburst galaxy Arp299, for VHE emission. In 200 - 500 GeV energies, the upper limits on the gamma ray flux from each source is placed against each object and were 8 12 Crab flux at a 95 CL. Over the region observed, there are no positive signals for the VHE emission by effectively 15 hour observation.

gmw_00003703.name: KiDS J0224-3143
gmw_00003703.overview: KiDS J0224-3143 is a massive, high-redshift compact galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.3841 [arXiv:2011.05347]. It is characterized by old stellar populations that formed during a single star-formation burst at high redshift, exhibiting super-solar metallicity and a large stellar velocity dispersion [arXiv:2011.05347].

sources.summary: The INSPIRE pilot identifies KiDS J0224-3143 as the sample’s most massive UCMG, with high-redshift compact morphology, old stellar populations, and a star-formation history consistent with a relic galaxy.

quotes.quote: Observations for KiDS J0224-3143, KiDS J0314-3215, and KiDS J0847+0112, to which we gave priority to test our strategy, data reduction, and analysis routines, were completed in 2019. We highlight that these three objects are representative of the whole sample as they span a relatively wide range in redshift, stellar masses, sizes and magnitudes. In particular, KiDS J0224-3143 is the most massive system in our sample (M _ = 2.7 10^ 11 ), and among the galaxies with the highest redshift ( z=0.3841 0.0001 , corresponding to a Universe that is 9.5 Gyr old.

Considering the most plausible setups and assumptions, KiDS J0224-3143 and KiDS J0847+0112 are characterised, for at least 95 in mass, by stellar populations that are only 1-2 Gyr younger than the age of the Universe at their redshifts (at z=0.3841 , t_ Uni 9.5 Gyr and at z=0.1764 , t_ Uni 11.5 Gyr). They formed through only one star formation burst at very high redshifts ( z_ form, J0224 =10.65 ; z_ form, J0847 =9.20 , inferred from the mass-weighted median ages calculated by for the unregularised case and z_ form, J0224 =6.02 , z_ form, J0847 =6.04 for the regularised one) and have a large stellar velocity dispersion, a super-solar metallicity and super-solar abundances.

gmw_00003704.name: KiDS J0314-3215
gmw_00003704.overview: KiDS J0314-3215 is a compact galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.2874 [arXiv:2011.05347]. It has an effective radius of only 0.65 kpc and a smoother star-formation history that prevents it from being classified as a relic [arXiv:2011.05347].

sources.summary: The paper presents KiDS J0314-3215 as an extremely compact pilot target whose smoother, more extended star-formation history makes it a less extreme relic candidate than the other two galaxies.

quotes.quote: KiDS J0314-3215 is slightly less massive and closer ( z=0.2874 0.0001 , when the Universe was 10.4 Gyr old), but it is one of the smallest systems in the current sample, with an effective radius of only =0.65 kpc, corresponding roughly to 0.16" on the sky (a single pixel in XHS). Finally, KiDS J0847+0112 is brighter than the other two (mag _r = 18.41 mag), and it is one of the galaxies with the lowest redshifts ( z=0.1764 0.0001 , when the Universe was 11.5 Gyr old). The main morpho-photometric characteristics of these three objects are listed in Table tab:sample_previous .

Finally, for KiDS J0314-3215 (middle panel), in the case of an unregularised fit, one main star formation episode formed 80 of the stars 0.5 Gyr after the BB, and then the assembly was completed with another fast (1 Gyr long) episode that started 2 Gyr after the BB. Considering the unlikely configuration with [ /Fe ] =0 , the second star formation burst occurred instead only 4 Gyr after the BB. However, this galaxy has the highest MAX REGUL (600), and thus a much smoother SFH is compatible with its spectrum too. In the MAX REGUL case, the galaxy completed its assembly (i.e. 95 of the stellar mass in place) only 5 Gyr after the BB, and therefore we cannot classify it as a relic. The formation redshift of this object is also lower than that of the other two, in both the unregularised ( z_ form, J0314 = 4.65 ) and the regularised ( z_ form, J0314 = 2.64 ) configurations.

gmw_00003705.name: KiDS J0847+0112
gmw_00003705.overview: KiDS J0847+0112 is a bright relic galaxy located at a low redshift of z=0.1764 [arXiv:2011.05347]. It experienced an extreme early star formation history, assembling its entire stellar mass within 2 Gyr after the Big Bang over a very short timescale [arXiv:2011.05347].

sources.summary: The paper characterizes KiDS J0847+0112 as a low-redshift, bright pilot galaxy with an extreme, rapid early star-formation history and confirms it as a relic galaxy.

quotes.quote: Finally, KiDS J0847+0112 is brighter than the other two (mag _r = 18.41 mag), and it is one of the galaxies with the lowest redshifts ( z=0.1764 0.0001 , when the Universe was 11.5 Gyr old).

KiDS J0847+0112 has been targeted by the GAMA Survey. We downloaded the GAMA spectrum and calculated the velocity dispersion using with the same configuration. Also in this case, the result is in fair agreement and consistent within 2 errors.

From the figure, it is clear that KiDS J0847+0112 (bottom panel) has the most `extreme' star formation history, when considering all the possible sources of uncertainties due to the different configurations and setups. It formed the totality of its stellar mass at most 2 Gyr after the BB, most likely even before and over very short timescales ( 0.5 1.0 Gyr), and then it had no further star formation episodes.

gmw_00003706.name: RX J0911.4+0551
gmw_00003706.overview: RX J0911.4+0551 is a quadruply lensed quasar [arXiv:2009.01277, arXiv:2305.05173] at redshift 2.79 [arXiv:2009.01277, arXiv:1204.5614] that exhibits a molecular outflow [arXiv:1204.5614]. Its host galaxy is intrinsically low-mass [arXiv:1303.6110, arXiv:2305.05173] with compact dust emission and more extended molecular gas [arXiv:2009.01277].

sources.summary: The paper reports RX J0911+0551 as a cusp lens with DC and DA time-delay anomalies, noting that its cluster environment may contribute and that further timing is needed.

quotes.quote: We also find evidence for time delay anomalies in larger-separation image pairs in the fold lenses, B1608+656 and WFI 2033 4723, and the cusp lens RX J0911+0551.

Hjorth-0911-tdel report the time delays between each of the three cusp images (A, B, and C) and the fourth image (D) for 0911. We find clear evidence of time delay anomalies in the DC and DA image pairs, which are the two pairs with the largest separations. Since the lens galaxy in 0911 is part of a cluster 0911-cluster , it is possible that these anomalies are due to environmental effects.

Pinning down the origin of the anomalies (i.e., the environment of the lens, or substructure) will require that the time delays among the cusp images be precisely measured. Chartas-0911-xray and Morgan_1131 have measured time delays among close images in other lens systems, so we are hopeful that it will be possible to do so in 0911. Although the close time delays can be inferred from current data, the P-values they predict span the range (0, 1) when 3 measurement uncertainties are included. At this point we conclude that the 0911 time delays are very intriguing and warrant further study.

sources.summary: The paper initially recovers published RX J0911+0551 delays near 150 days, but finds secondary solutions and strong sensitivity to early monitoring points.

quotes.quote: RX J0911+0551 Data for this quadruply lensed quasar were made available by 2006yCat..34559001P , but had been previously treated and analysed by 2001PhDT.........4B and 2002ApJ...572L..11H , who proposed time delays of t_ BA = 150 6 days and t_ BA = 146 4 days respectively, where B is the leading image of the system and A the sum of the close components A1, A2, and A3. Using all data except the first point, which has too strong an influence on our slope parameter because of its isolation as can be seen in Fig. fig:RXJ0911 , we can at first sight confirm the published delays: the NMF method gives t_ BA = 150 2.6 days and the MD method results in t_ BA = 147.4 4.6 days. However, the histogram in Fig. RXJ0911+05 shows a secondary peak at t_ BA 157 days.

Investigating this peak further, we come to the conclusion that some points have a very strong influence on the delay: the first observing season, and especially the first ten points of the light curve, indicate a shorter time delay. According to 2001PhDT.........4B , these points were added to supplement the regular monitoring data of the Nordic Optical Telescope. However, the first three points of the regular NOT monitoring in the B curve are similarly crucial. Omitting these three points leads to larger error bars of t_ BA = 151.6 7.0 days using the NMF method. Finally, recalculating the time delay in the regular monitoring data only and without the first three points in the B curve, gives t_ BA = 159 2.4 days with the NMF method.

sources.summary: The paper models the lensed host galaxy of QSO RXJ0911, emphasizing how source size, caustic overlap, and relative image brightness affect interpretation of future resolved observations.

quotes.quote: After a brief reminder of the mechanism of gravitational lensing of extended sources, the particular case of the host galaxy of QSO RXJ0911, a high redshift ( z 2.8 ) quadruply imaged quasar, is explored. The non linearity of the problem, together with the proximity of the source to a cusp of the lens inner caustic, have important consequences on the dependence of the image appearance on the size and shape of the source. Their expected main features and their interpretation in terms of source extension and shape are investigated in a spirit of simplicity and in preparation for the analysis of high sensitivity and spatial resolution images that will soon be within reach with the completion of the Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array (ALMA). In particular, the information on source size carried by relative image brightness is discussed.

Recently, RXJ0911 has been observed using the Plateau de Bure Interferometer (Tuan Anh et al. ) tuned in the CO(7-6) region. At such wavelengths, the source is no longer the quasar, which can be considered as a point source, but its host galaxy. More precisely, the CO line is associated with the presence of molecular gas and the continuum with that of dust: measuring the source extension in each case would provide important information on the physics of early galaxies. The Plateau de Bure data resolve the source spatially on the CO line, suggesting a root mean square radius of several hundred parsecs ( 1 in the sky corresponds to 8 kpc in the source plane).

sources.summary: The paper resolves CO emission in the lensed quasar host RXJ0911.4+0551, estimating a compact molecular disk, low gas and dynamical masses, and a scaled-down high-redshift QSO host.

quotes.quote: We report on high angular resolution observations of the CO(7--6) line and millimeter continuum in the host galaxy of the gravitationally lensed (z 2.8) quasar RXJ0911.4+0551 using the Plateau de Bure Interferometer. Our CO observations resolve the molecular disk of the source. Using a lens model based on HST observations, we fit source models to the observed visibilities. We estimate a molecular disk radius of 1 0.2 kpc and an inclination of 69 6 , the continuum is more compact and is only marginally resolved by our observations.

However, the corresponding dynamical mass, M_ dyn =R v^2 G^ 1 i^ 2 =(3.9 0.9) 10^9 M _ , is a factor 2 larger than the molecular gas mass, implying a gas fraction of 50 , similar to the one observed in other high-z QSOs e.g., 2009ApJ...690..463R,2005ARA&A..43..677S . In other words, the line may be truly narrow (the line width corrected for inclination would be W=129 20 km s ^ 1 ) because this quasar host may lie on the 'light' end of the mass distribution of quasar hosts 2006AJ....131.2763C . This interpretation is also consistent with the X-ray luminosity, L_ X =1.4 10^ 44 erg s ^ 1 assuming the same magnification factor as for the FIR, i.e., 37.7 2009ApJ...690.1006F because according to the well-known M_ BH _ relation in local elliptical galaxies 2009ApJ...698..198G and following 2013MNRAS.429.3047B , the line width expected is W _ /1.5 135 km s ^ 1 for M_ BH =10^ 7.7 M _ . Thus, RXJ0911 appears to be a scaled-down version of the QSOs usually found at high- z , residing in a lighter halo, containing less gas, forming fewer stars and having a lighter central black hole.

sources.summary: RX J0911+0551 strongly affects the calibration result; the paper discusses its low likelihood, sensitivity to model assumptions, long time delay, and anomalous X-ray flux ratio.

quotes.quote: We have examined the effects of the individual systems and find that one system, RX J0911+0551, has a particularly strong influence on the final result. If we exclude it from the analysis the calibration factor F is smaller by a factor of 1.5. The pull of RX J0911+0551 on our principal result manifests itself in many of the above tests. For example, the effect of a change in the power law index is much smaller when RX J0911+0551 is eliminated from the sample.

Second, we carried out a second set of 999 simulations of RX J0911+0551 with an input calibration factor F = 1.189 . In only 2.5 of the cases simulated was the likelihood of the input value smaller than that computed using the observed X-ray flux ratios. Third, at 150 days, the time delay between the d image and the other three images is the longest for any of the systems in the sample, rendering it more vulnerable to intrinsic X-ray variability. The low likelihood for the present observations is explained by the fact that the ratio observed for the flux of image b to that of image d is 1.27, very much smaller than the 5.40 predicted by the lens model.

sources.summary: The paper treats RXJ0911+0551 separately because cluster-driven external shear makes its image distribution asymmetric and core-size prior broad, weakening constraints on lens-galaxy parameters.

quotes.quote: RXJ0911+0551 presents itself as an exception in our sample. The radial distribution of the 4 observed quad images is highly asymmetric, probably due to the presence of a nearby galaxy cluster in the field, providing external shear burud98,tortora04 . As a result, the core size distribution of our lens macro-models skews to larger values beyond the range of the other 6 lenses in our sample. Therefore, we treat this lens separately from the rest of the sample of 6 and derive independent constraints from it (see Table tab:indivconstraints and Section txt:rxj0911 ).

The influence of the external shear can be seen in the lens mass-model maps in Figure fig:RXJ0911 . Compared with the lens maps for RXJ1131-1231 (see Figure fig:RXJ1131all ), which is the system most similar to RXJ0911+0551 of the 6 we use, we can easily see that the region of the central images (green stars) is displaced from the center of the galaxy density map further in RXJ0911+0551 than in RXJ1131-1231. Since the VODKA distribution is with respect to the galaxy center, the large external shear and offset position of the source displace the maximum of _ Fermat further from the center, leading to small offset SMBH being able to produce extra images (this did not occur in the other lenses with centrally symmetric _ Fermat ). The constraints for this system are listed in Table tab:indivconstraints .

sources.summary: The paper treats RXJ0911+0551 as a complex time-delay lens with a possible second lens galaxy and strong nearby cluster perturbation, making H0 estimates model-dependent.

quotes.quote: The first complication is that some primary lenses have less massive satellite galaxies inside or near their Einstein rings. This includes two of the time delay lenses, RXJ0911+0551 and B1608+656. RXJ0911+0551 could simply be a projection effect, since neither lens galaxy shows irregular isophotes. Here the implication for models may simply be the need to include all the parameters (mass, position, ellipticity ) required to describe the second lens galaxy, and with more parameters we would expect greater uncertainties in H_0 .

The second complication occurs when the primary lens is a member of a more massive (X-ray) cluster, as in the time delay lenses RXJ0911+0551 (Morgan et al. 2001) and Q0957+561 (Chartas et al. 2002). The cluster model is critical to interpreting these systems (see sec:clusters ).

RXJ0911+0551 is very strongly perturbed by the nearby X-ray cluster (Morgan et al. 2001; Hjorth et al. 2002). Kochanek (2003b) found H_0=49 5 \ if the primary lens and its satellite were isothermal and H_0=67 5 \ if they had constant mass-to-light ratios. The higher value of H_0=71 4 \ obtained by Hjorth et al. (2002) can be understood by combining sec:theory and sec:clusters with the differences in the models.

sources.summary: The paper places RXJ0911+0551 in a cluster environment and compares it with HE1104 as a high-mass lens with low stellar-mass fraction and possible cluster mass contamination.

quotes.quote: RXJ0911 is located on the outskirts of a cluster MCM01 . Chandra observations of the cluster suggest a complex non-spherical cluster mass distribution at a temperature of roughly 2.3 keV.

The two high mass lenses RXJ0911 and HE1104 have a total stellar mass of 2 10^ 11 M_ . For comparison, the total mass profiles differ slightly for radii 1.5 and 2.5 . But it should be noted that RXJ0911 is located at the centre of a galaxy cluster which might lead to a lens mass estimate slightly larger than the actual virial mass of the lens galaxy. At 6.5 , i.e., 2 HE1104 has 12 less total mass than RXJ0911. At 3 , i.e. the difference is still 6 .

sources.summary: The paper observes the z=2.79 QSO host RXJ0911.4+0551, comparing molecular line profiles, detecting faint outflow wings, and using line redshifts to constrain fundamental-constant variation.

quotes.quote: On the Variations of Fundamental Constants and AGN feedback in the QSO host galaxy RXJ0911.4+0551 at z=2.79 A. \ Wei 1 , F. \ Walter 2 , D. \ Downes 3 , C. L. \ Carilli 4 , C. \ Henkel 1,5 , K. M. \ Menten 1 , P. \ Cox 3 1 Max-Planck Institut f ur Radioastronomie, Auf dem H ugel 69, 53121 Bonn, Germany 2 Max-Planck Institut f ur Astronomie, K onigstuhl 17, 69117 Heidelberg, Germany 3 Institut de Radio Astronomie Millimetrique, 300 Rue de la Piscine, Domaine Universitaire, 38406 Saint Martin d'H eres, France 4 National Radio Astronomy Observartory, P.O. Box O, Socorro, NM 87801, USA 5 Astronomy Department, King Abdulaziz University, P.O. Box 80203, Jeddah 21589, Saudi Arabia We report on sensitive observations of the \ and \ transitions in the z=2.79 QSO host galaxy \ using the IRAM Plateau de Bure interferometer (PdBI).

Our extremely high signal to noise spectra combined with the narrow CO line width of this source (FWHM = 120 ) allows us to estimate sensitive limits on the space-time variations of the fundamental constants using two emission lines. Our observations show that the \ and CO line shapes are in good agreement with each other but that the \ line profile is of order 10 narrower, presumably due to the lower opacity in the latter line. Both lines show faint wings with velocities up to 250 , indicative of a molecular outflow. As such the data provide direct evidence for negative feedback in the molecular gas phase at high redshift.

The redshift difference between the CO and \ lines is sensitive to variations of \ with F= ^2/ where is the fine structure constant and the proton-to-electron mass ratio. We find =6.9 3.7 10^ 6 at a lookback time of 11.3 Gyr, which within the uncertainties, is consistent with no variations of the fundamental constants.

sources.summary: The paper models RX J0911+0551 as a lensed quasar host with CO(5-4) ALMA imaging, compact reconstructed dust, and molecular gas more extended than dust.

quotes.quote: RX J0911+0551 RX J0911+0551 (RX J0911.4+0551) is a quasar at z_ s =2.79 that is lensed by a foreground galaxy at z_ l =0.77 Burud:1998,Kneib:2000 .

For our analysis, we use ALMA imaging at 145 GHz that also targeted the CO (5--4) emission line from RX J0911+0551. We detect thermal dust continuum for this system with a total flux density at 145 GHz of 1.4 mJy, consistent with the expectations from SED fitting (see Figs. fig:seds1 and fig:seds1 ). We achieve an rms noise of 7 Jy beam ^ 1 with natural weighting of the visibilties. Imaging of the dust shows compact emission that is resolved around the triplet images (see Fig. fig:0911_images ).
For HS 0810+2554 and RX J0911+0551, in which we observe CO (5--4) and CO (3--2), which are usually associated with star-forming regions, we find the gas to be more extended than the dust.

sources.summary: The paper treats RX J0911+0551 as a complicated cusp lens where differential extinction, octopole modes, and cluster shear affect but do not remove evidence for a flux-ratio anomaly.

quotes.quote: RX J0911+0551 RX J0911+0551 is the only cusp lens that shows significant evidence for differential extinction between the images. Attributing the color difference to dust in the lens galaxy, we estimate a differential extinction between A _1 and the A _2 /A _3 pair of (0.44,0.32,0.12) mag in (V,I,H) (following the analysis of Falco et al. \ 1999, using a redshifted R_V=3.1 extinction curve from Cardelli, Clayton Mathis 1989). Perhaps the most interesting point is that RX J0911+0551 is the only system where octopole perturbations affect the judgment about a flux ratio anomaly.

The reason we see such an effect here, but not in the other lenses we study, is that RX J0911+0551 is the only system where the source lies near a cusp on the minor axis of the galaxy. The analysis of RX J0911+0551 is actually even more complicated, because the lens galaxy lies in a cluster environment that contributes a large shear 0.3 to the lens potential (Kneib, Cohen, Hjorth 2000). We believe that the ``cluster shear'' case provides strong evidence for a flux ratio anomaly in RX J0911+0551.

sources.summary: RXJ0911+0554 is used to illustrate how a faint companion to the primary lensing galaxy can alter predicted time delays and complicate models.

quotes.quote: Consider the case of RXJ0911+0554, shown in figure fig:rxj0911 , where the primary lensing galaxy has a faint companion. Given its faintness and our aversion to adding additional parameters, we might be tempted to ignore it, as did in the model used by Courbin to construct figure fig:courbin . But allowing for a mass at the position of this dwarf companion changes the predicted time delay by 10 .

Though the smaller galaxy is a factor of 10 fainter than the primary lensing galaxy, its effect is to move the center of mass closer to the midpoint of the images, decreasing the differences in path length. There is an irony here in that the multiple lenses work to our advantage in producing (or adding to) the quadrupole moments that give us 4 images rather than 2. But at the same time they make modeling considerably more difficult.

sources.summary: The paper accounts for RX J0911's cluster environment, modifies its modeling strategy for external shear, and finds an intermediate posterior peaking near 10^7.

quotes.quote: The system RX J0911 is known to reside near a cluster of galaxies, and thus convergence from the cluster halo contributes to the mass within the Einstein radius. We approximate the contribution from the cluster convergence by noting that it should be approximately equal to the mean external shear we infer of 0.3. We then rescale the Einstein radius by 0.7 , since the stellar mass scales as R_ Ein ^2 and where we have used the fact that the mean convergence inside the Einstein radius is approximately equal to one for an isothermal deflector.

Lens-specific modeling for RX J0911+0551 and WGD 2038-4008 For system RX J0911, we alter the modeling strategy slightly to increase computational efficiency by allowing the external shear strength _ ext to vary freely while solving for macromodel parameters that fit the observed image positions. For the system WGD 2038, we widen the prior on the power-law slope of the macromodel as the posterior using the default range for _ macro between 1.95 - 2.2 is biased towards higher values of _ macro . For WGD 2038, the posterior peaks at _ macro 2.25 . In this section we present the results of our analysis. We begin in Section ssec:jointinf by showing dark matter halo convergence maps for some of the top-ranked realizations drawn in the forward model. We then display the posterior distributions for a few individual lenses, showing the simultaneous inference of parameters describing the macro lens model and the dark matter hyper-parameters. In Section ssec:mainresults we present the constraints on the abundance of substructure and dark matter warmth for the full sample of 11 quads. Top-ranked realizations and posteriors for individual lenses Minimizing the summary statistic in Equation eqn:summary_stat selects realizations that resemble the observed data as closely as possible. This guarantees that the set of accepted dark matter hyper-parameters yield an accurate approximation of the true posterior distribution for each individual lens with data : p | . For visualization purposes, and to reinforce the fact that the top-ranked realizations look like the data and satisfy S_ lens 0 (Equation eqn:summary_stat ), in Figure fig:best_realizations we display the dark matter halo effective multi-plane convergence maps for some of the top ranked realizations for a subset of quads in our sample. The effective multi-plane convergence is defined as half the divergence of the full deflection field _ effective 1 2 \ . This definition of the multi-plane convergence accounts for the non-linear effects present in multi-plane lensing, and satisfies the single-plane definition of convergence as second derivatives of a lensing potential in the absence of multiple lens planes. To visualize individual realizations of dark matter structure, we define _ effective(halo) _ effective _ macro , where _ macro is the convergence from the lens macromodel, including satellite galaxies and nearby deflectors. In the resulting convergence maps, halos located behind the main lens plane appear sheared tangentially around the Einstein radius due to coupling to the large deflections produced by the macromodel. In Figure fig:best_realizations , we show _ effective(halo) maps of randomly selected realizations of dark matter structure whose corresponding parameters were accepted in the final posterior on the basis of their summary statistic S_ lens . The specific realizations and the corresponding dark matter parameters correspond to a diverse set of substructure populations, warm and cold, which yield similarly good fits to the observed flux ratios satisfying S_ lens 0 . Some models, however, predict flux ratios that match the observed flux ratios more frequently than others. In terms of the Approximate Bayesian Computing algorithm described in Section sec:inference , the frequency with which one dark matter model relative to another predicts observables that resemble the data is a surrogate for the relative likelihood of the models. The probability of accepting a proposed based on the summary statistic in Equation eqn:summary_stat is therefore equal to the likelihood p | (Equation eqn:likelihood ), even though the form of this function is unknown and it is never directly evaluated. The top-ranked realizations for B1422 shown in Figure fig:best_realizations each have a relatively massive dark matter halo, or several smaller ones, located near the top left merging triplet image with (normalized) flux 0.88. This is in agreement with the analysis by Nierenberg++14 , who find that a blob of dark matter near this image brings the model-predicted flux ratios into agreement with a smooth lens model. Although not obvious from examining Figure fig:best_realizations , the underlying macromodels for each accepted realization are unique, with different external shears, power-law slopes, lens ellipticity, etc. We marginalize over different macromodel configurations by simultaneously sampling the macromodel parameters and the dark matter hyper-parameters in the forward model. To illustrate, in Figures fig:f , fig:f , and fig:f we show the posterior distributions for several parameters in the lens macromodel, along with the dark matter hyper-parameters _ sub and for HE0435, WFI 2033, and RX J0911. The system HE0435 generally favors models with low subhalo mass function normalizations (low _ sub ), or a turnover the mass function with higher _ sub . The system WFI 2033 is the opposite, with a posterior favoring CDM-like mass functions with many lens plane subhalos. The system RX J0911 lies somewhere in between, with a peak in the posterior distribution of near 10^ 7 .

sources.summary: The paper presents RX J0911.4+0551 as a quad lens with promising time-delay prospects, X-ray variability, and a large external shear aligned with a nearby galaxy cluster.

quotes.quote: RX J0911.4+0551 This quad, initially discovered as a triple bade97 , has unique geometrical properties and is a strong candidate for time delay determination, although no result has been published yet. Rapid variability has been detected in the X-ray regime, providing the possibility of a determination of all three time delays with unprecedented accuracy chartas01 . Our first models (elliptical potential plus shear, ^2 1 ) were presented in burud98 . The external shear in the best-fitting model is =0.32 and points almost exactly in the direction of a nearby cluster of galaxies. The redshift and velocity dispersion of this cluster was measured by kneib00 to z=0.769 and _v=(836 180 200 ) km s^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper resolves RX J0911.4+0551 into four quasar images, identifies an elongated lens galaxy, invokes lens ellipticity plus external shear, and notes a nearby candidate cluster.

quotes.quote: We report the detection of four images in the recently discovered lensed QSO RX J0911.4+0551. With a maximum angular separation of 3.1 , it is the quadruply imaged QSO with the widest known angular separation. Raw and deconvolved data reveal an elongated lens galaxy. The observed reddening in at least two of the four QSO images suggests differential extinction by this lensing galaxy. We show that both an ellipticity of the galaxy ( _ min =0.075 ) and an external shear ( _ min =0.15 ) from a nearby mass has to be included in the lensing potential in order to reproduce the complex geometry observed in RX J0911.4+0551.

sources.summary: The paper describes RX J0911.4+0551 as a lensed quasar with a nearly circular early-type lens in a massive cluster and a small northwest satellite galaxy.

quotes.quote: RX J0911.4+0551 () . This lensed quasar was discovered in Bade1997 by Bade1997 amongst the AGN candidates of the ROSAT All-Sky Survey (RASS). Its lensed nature was confirmed by Burud1998 . The lens is a nearly circular early-type galaxy, which belongs to a massive cluster, centered 38 south west of the lensed system. It is also known to have a small satellite galaxy in the north west direction Kneib2000 .

sources.summary: SDSS J091127.61+055054.1 is a quadruply lensed quasar with compact ALMA continuum and CO emission, unusually low molecular gas/FIR output, and an intrinsically lower-mass host.

quotes.quote: SDSS J091127.61+055054.1 (RXJ0911.4+0551) is also a quadruply lensed quasar at z =2.796 observed with ALMA in band 4, targeting also the CO (5-4) transition. Both continuum and CO emission were found to be similarly compact stacey21 . It was found to have a molecular-gas mass and far-infrared luminosity an order of magnitude lower compared to other quasars. A detailed analysis of the size and morphology of the galaxy anh13 showed that it was intrinsically a lower mass galaxy rather than an evolved galaxy that had depleted its gas reservoir.

sources.summary: The paper describes RX J0911+0551 as a quadruple quasar with no reliable microlensing detection; its long-term light-curve differences depend on interpolation and may be systematic.

quotes.quote: Quasars where no microlensing is confirmed The three quasars where no microlensing variability was reliably detected are the double quasar HE 2149 2745 observed at the Danish 1.5-m telescope, ESO-La Silla (October 1998 -- December 2000), the quadruple quasar RX J0911+0551 observed at the NOT (March 1997 -- April 2001), and the doubly imaged quasar B1600+434 observed at the NOT (April 1998 -- November 1999) (see Table table:1 ). Plots similar to Figs. 1 and 2 are shown in Figs. 3--5. For HE 2149 2745 (Fig. hev ) and RX J0911+0551 (Fig. ) we see some long term variations in the light curve difference but the analysis shows that the slopes differ depending on which of the two light curves are interpolated.

Thus, while we detect intriguing light curve differences and cannot rule out microlensing variability in these systems, this indicates that the detected magnitude changes may also be attributed to photometric or interpolation errors.

sources.summary: The paper studies RX J0911.4+0551 with VIMOS integral-field spectroscopy and interprets the A1 and A3 lensed images as likely microlensing-demagnified.

quotes.quote: We present VIMOS integral field observations of 27 27 \ fields of view around four lensed quasars: HE 0230-2130, RX J0911.4+0551, H 1413+117 and B 1359+154. Using the low, medium and high resolution modes, we study the quasar images and the quasar environments.

In HE 0230-2130, image D is likely to be affected by microlensing magnification; in RX J0911.4+0551, images A1 and A3 are likely to be modified by microlensing de-magnification and in 1413 at least image D is affected by microlensing.

gmw_00003707.overview: IRAS 09104+4109 is a hyper-luminous infrared cD galaxy [arXiv:1106.1647, arXiv:1110.0489] that hosts an obscured Type 2 quasar [arXiv:1301.3023] and resides in a rich galaxy cluster [arXiv:1106.1647]. The host galaxy exhibits ongoing star formation [arXiv:1606.05649], optical filaments [arXiv:1110.0489], and X-ray cavities aligned with radio hot spots [arXiv:1110.0489].

sources.summary: The paper studies IRAS 09104+4109 as an obscured hyperluminous quasar, using IR, X-ray, and optical data to model its AGN/starburst contributions and obscurer geometry.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: We adopt a position for \ of 09h13m45.49s, +40d56m28.22s (J2000) and assume H_ 0 = 70 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 , = 1 , _ = 0.7 .

quotes.quote: We have conducted a study of , an obscured hyperluminous quasar at z=0.442 , using X-ray data from , , \ and , infrared data from Spitzer and Herschel , and an optical spectrum from Palomar. We apply radiative transfer models to the infrared data to measure rates of ongoing star formation in the host galaxy, and to constrain the properties of the infrared obscurer around the AGN. We apply two types of models to the X-ray data -- a T + R (phenomenological) model and the / \ (geometrical) models -- to constrain the properties of the X-ray obscurer. We then fold in a distance constraint from the optical spectrum to construct a picture of the geometry of the structure around the AGN in this archetype object.

The infrared data can be reproduced by a combination of an AGN and a starburst. The total infrared (rest-frame 1-1000 m) luminosity is 6.76 0.20 10^ 46 erg s ^ 1 , with a contribution from the AGN of 5.94^ +0.26 _ 0.27 10^ 46 erg s ^ 1 . The starburst is required in the fit at 3.7 confidence, with a luminosity of 5.54 1.48 10^ 45 erg s ^ 1 , corresponding to a star formation rate of (110^ +35 _ 28 ) M _ yr ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper studies 09104 as a hyper-luminous infrared cD galaxy hosting a hidden quasar, arguing its X-ray and infrared data favor heavy but Compton-thin obscuration.

quotes.quote: 9104 \ (hereafter referred to as 09104) is a hyper-luminous infrared cD galaxy at z =0.442 residing in the core of a rich cluster of galaxies (Kleinmann et al. 1988). Its optical spectrum is characterized by the presence of narrow emission lines, although broad Balmer and MgII lines were observed in polarized light (Hines Wills 1993; Tran, Cohen Villar-Martin 2000). These remarkable results, based on optical observations, suggested the presence of a powerful quasar hidden in 09104. Subsequent \ observations confirmed this ``picture''.

The \ spectrum is in favour of a Compton-thin hypothesis, which has also been suggested by P07 from the analysis of \ data. Further support to the Compton-thin hypothesis comes from observations at longer wavelengths, where the obscuring matter (torus), responsible for all of the observed emission up to 60 , can be well parameterized, providing a (9.7) =3.0, consistent with the absorption measured through X-rays (using a Galactic gas-to-dust ratio conversion).

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS 09104+4109 as a hyper-luminous infrared cD galaxy hosting an obscured Type 2 AGN, with Fe K emission, X-ray cavities, radio hot spots, and optical filaments.

quotes.quote: MACS J0913.7+4056 The central galaxy in this cluster, more commonly known in the literature as IRAS 09104+4109, is a hyper-luminous infrared cD galaxy L_ IR 12 10^ 12 L_ ; Kle1988328 hosting a heavily obscured Type 2 AGN 1-5 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 ; Vig2011 . The Chandra X-ray spectrum of the nucleus shows that there is a prominent Fe K line at the redshift of the source see also Iwa2001321 . We find two cavities in this system. The cavity to the north-west was first noticed by Iwa2001321 , but the 9.1 ks Chandra observations were too faint to confirm whether this cavity was real. By using a much deeper observation of 70.4 ks, we confirm the existence of the first cavity and find evidence of a second located at a similar radius to the south-east.

Although larger, the cavities carved out in the X-ray image coincide with the location of the radio hot spots seen at 20 cm also see Fig. fig2 where we show the radio jet axis Hin1993415 , and the optical image shows a spectacular set of filaments spreading towards the north-east and south.

sources.summary: The paper presents Suzaku and Chandra X-ray analysis of hyperluminous infrared galaxy IRAS 09104+4109, arguing that it hosts an obscured Type 2 quasar with a Compton-thin absorber.

quotes.quote: We present the first long Suzaku observation of the hyperluminous infrared galaxy IRAS 09104+4109 which is dominated by a Type 2 AGN. The infrared to X-ray SED indicates that the source is an obscured quasar with a Compton-thin absorber. However, the 3 hard X-ray detection of the source with the BeppoSAX PDS suggested a reflection-dominated, Compton-thick view.

Unless IRAS 09104+4109 is a ``changing-look" quasar, we confirm that it is a Compton-thin AGN. Although the lack of detection of X-ray emission above 10 keV seems to favour the transmission scenario, we found that the two models offer fairly similar flux predictions over the X-ray band below 40 keV. We also found that the strong iron line shown in the Suzaku spectrum is in fact a blend of two emission lines, in which the 6.4 keV one is mostly contributed from the AGN and the 6.7 keV from the hot cluster gas.

sources.summary: The paper describes IRAS 09104+4109 as a high-redshift ultraluminous infrared quasar/galaxy with a steep X-ray to far-infrared SED and weak rest-frame optical-UV emission.

quotes.quote: Whether Compton thick AGN are common at high redshift is still debated (Fabian et al 2002) though the ultraluminous infrared quasars IRAS 09104 + 4109 at z=0.442 (Franceschini et al. 2000, Iwasawa et al. 2001) and F15307 at z=0.92 (Ogasaka et al. 1997) are good examples of mildly and heavily Compton thick AGN, respectively.

Indeed the steep rising of the X--ray and far--infrared spectrum towards high frequencies of the ultraluminous infrared galaxy IRAS 09104+4109 is very similar. Conversely, the weak rest--frame optical--UV emission is shifted in the R band explaining the extremely faint optical magnitudes. As a consequence the optical to X--ray flux ratio changes in a non--linear way.

The redshift tracks in the optical magnitude versus X--ray flux plane (Fig. 3) have been normalized to the observed X--ray flux and R magnitude of NGC 6240 and IRAS 09104 + 4109. The two objects are characterized by a similar SED but different X--ray luminosities (about 3 10^ 44 erg s ^ 1 and 10 ^ 46 erg s ^ 1 respectively).

gmw_00003708.name: NGC 2785
gmw_00003708.overview: NGC 2785 is a nearby disky galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.009 [arXiv:1106.1647]. It is classified as a Type 2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1106.1647, arXiv:1301.3023] that exhibits heavy extinction and a very red continuum [arXiv:1106.1647].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 2785 as a nearby disky Type 2 AGN likely responsible for BAT hard X-ray excess near 09104, with evidence for heavy extinction.

quotes.quote: Taking into account the positional uncertainty in the BAT map ( 6 , corresponding to the size of the circles in Fig. bat_map ), at the position of the BAT hard \ emission we found NGC 2785, a disky galaxy at z=0.009 , whose Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) spectrum is characterized by a very red continuum and a large H /H ratio ( 20.9 at face value, or 12.5 taking into account the correction for the average host galaxy absorption component), being highly suggestive of a heavily extincted object. The ``corrected'' H /H ratio is consistent with the value reported by Veilleux et al. (1995) using a different optical spectrum. Under reasonable assumptions, this line flux ratio translates into a E(B V) 1.5--1.9. Furthermore, the H / [ NII ] and H / [ OIII ] line ratios are consistent with this source being a Type 2 AGN. The association of the BAT excess with this source appears as the most likely possibility, as also suggested by their good spatial coincidence (see Fig. bat_map ).
Pointed and sensitive \ observations of NGC 2785 are required to shed light on all these issues.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2785 as a nearby Type 2 AGN whose hard X-ray emission may have contaminated earlier BeppoSAX measurements of IRAS 09104+4109.

quotes.quote: The high-energy detection was later found to be possibly contaminated by another Type 2 AGN, NGC 2785, which is only 17 arcmin away. Our new Suzaku observation offers simultaneous soft and hard X-ray coverage and excludes contamination from NGC 2785.

Moreover, they presented a 54-month Swift BAT map (15.0-30.0 keV band) which shows that the hard X-ray emission detected by BeppoSAX is probably associated with a z = 0.009 Type 2 AGN NGC 2785, which is only 17 arcmin away from IRAS 09104+4109.

Given that the field of views of Suzaku X-ray Imaging Spectrometer (XIS) and PIN are 17.8 arcmin 17.8 arcmin and 34 arcmin 34 arcmin, respectively, emission from the nearby AGN NGC 2785 is likely included in the PIN detector if the pointing is aimed at IRAS 09104+4109 (RA DEC = 09 13 45.49 40 56 28.2). The Suzaku PIN is a non-imaging instrument and emission from other sources cannot be simply excluded by region extraction. Therefore we shifted the pointing 6 arcmin west (new RA DEC = 09 13 21.49 40 56 28.2) and assigned a 90^ roll angle in our observation. The entire source still lies within the XIS detector after the change of pointing, and contribution from NGC 2785 should be excluded from the field of view of PIN.

gmw_00003709.name: J100544-0742.2
gmw_00003709.overview: J100544-0742.2 is a high-redshift extremely red galaxy candidate with an extended, irregular, and disturbed morphology [arXiv:astro-ph/9910163]. Spectral energy distribution fitting indicates that the system is affected by strong dust reddening, consistent with a dusty starburst galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/9910163].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J100544-0742.2 as a dusty ERG candidate with noisy spectra, strong-reddening SED fits, and extended disturbed morphology consistent with a dusty starburst.

quotes.quote: Due to its fainter and noisier spectra, the exception is J100544-0742.2 which has F_ lim < 4.5 10^ 16 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 and F_ lim < 3.5 10^ 16 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 in J and H , respectively. The J and H spectra of J100544-0742.2 were not used in the fitting analysis because too noisy.

(B) Galaxies with SEDs that require significant dust extinction. Two ERGs are present in this group: J100544-0742.2 and J124027-1131.0. Their SED fitting is characterized by a large degree of degeneracy among spectral templates, ages and amount of dust reddening. Nevertheless, the minimum amount of dust extinction required to provide acceptable fits ( E_ B-V >0.5 ) suggests that these two ERGs are systems affected by strong dust reddening. A third galaxy, J202807-2141.1, falls formally into this class because an acceptable fit is obtained only with E_ B-V 0.3 , with an age of 0.7 Gyr and with Z=2.5Z_ . However, we consider this case ambiguous because of the unusual set of parameters provided by the fit.

The I_ 814 band morphologies strengthen the indications given by the SED fitting analysis: J100544-0742.2 has an extended, irregular and disturbed morphology consistent with being a dusty starburst galaxy; J100551-0742.4 has a compact and elliptical-like morphology.

gmw_00003710.name: J124027-1131.0
gmw_00003710.overview: J124027-1131.0 is a dusty extremely red galaxy candidate [arXiv:astro-ph/9910163]. Its spectral energy distribution fits suggest the system is affected by strong dust reddening, while its morphology is compact and weakly disturbed [arXiv:astro-ph/9910163].

sources.summary: The paper treats J124027-1131.0 as a dusty ERG candidate whose acceptable SED fits require strong reddening, while its compact weakly disturbed morphology remains ambiguous.

quotes.quote: (B) Galaxies with SEDs that require significant dust extinction. Two ERGs are present in this group: J100544-0742.2 and J124027-1131.0. Their SED fitting is characterized by a large degree of degeneracy among spectral templates, ages and amount of dust reddening. Nevertheless, the minimum amount of dust extinction required to provide acceptable fits ( E_ B-V >0.5 ) suggests that these two ERGs are systems affected by strong dust reddening. A third galaxy, J202807-2141.1, falls formally into this class because an acceptable fit is obtained only with E_ B-V 0.3 , with an age of 0.7 Gyr and with Z=2.5Z_ . However, we consider this case ambiguous because of the unusual set of parameters provided by the fit.

The case of J124027-1131.0 is more ambiguous because its morphology is compact and weakly disturbed. Adopting z=z_ sphot , for each object we estimated the limits on the H luminosity L( H ) and on the star formation rate ( SFR ) through the relation SFR=7.9 10^ 42 L( H ) [ M _ yr ^ 1 ] (Kennicutt 1998).

gmw_00003711.name: J202807-2141.1
gmw_00003711.overview: J202807-2141.1 is classified as an extremely red galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/9910163]. Spectral energy distribution fitting suggests it is a dusty system with an estimated age of 0.7 Gyr, and it may belong to a galaxy cluster or group [arXiv:astro-ph/9910163].

sources.summary: The paper calls J202807-2141.1 a formally dusty but ambiguous ERG, requiring E(B-V) about 0.3 in SED fits and lacking J spectra for H-alpha SFR limits.

quotes.quote: (B) Galaxies with SEDs that require significant dust extinction. Two ERGs are present in this group: J100544-0742.2 and J124027-1131.0. Their SED fitting is characterized by a large degree of degeneracy among spectral templates, ages and amount of dust reddening. Nevertheless, the minimum amount of dust extinction required to provide acceptable fits ( E_ B-V >0.5 ) suggests that these two ERGs are systems affected by strong dust reddening. A third galaxy, J202807-2141.1, falls formally into this class because an acceptable fit is obtained only with E_ B-V 0.3 , with an age of 0.7 Gyr and with Z=2.5Z_ . However, we consider this case ambiguous because of the unusual set of parameters provided by the fit.

This was not possible for J202807-2141.1 and J202807-2140.8 because no J spectra were available to us. Finally, we noticed that the four ERGs in the J2027-217 field have all very similar z_ sphot (Fig.2), and may belong to a same cluster or group.

gmw_00003712.name: J090543.56+043347.3
gmw_00003712.overview: J090543.56+043347.3 is a luminous quasar host galaxy located at a redshift of z=1.3 [arXiv:1103.6026]. The system displays clear signs of an ongoing tidal interaction and hosts spatially extended star formation [arXiv:1103.6026].

sources.summary: The paper directly measures the quasar host dynamical mass, finds a near-local black-hole-to-host relation, and reports tidal interaction plus extended star formation.

quotes.quote: Here we report the first results of a new, complementary diagnostic route: we directly determine a dynamical host galaxy mass for the z=1.3 luminous quasar J090543.56+043347.3 through high-spatial-resolution ( 0.47^ , 4 kpc FWHM) observations of the host galaxy gas kinematics over 30 40 kpc using ESO/VLT/SINFONI with LGS/AO. Combining our result of M_ dyn = 2.05^ +1.68 _ 0.74 10^ 11 M_ (within a radius 5.25 1.05 kpc) with M_ BH, MgII = 9.02 1.43 10^8 M_ , M_ BH, H = 2.83^ +1.93 _ 1.13 10^8 M_ , we find that the ratio of black hole mass to host galaxy dynamical mass for J090543.56+043347.3 matches the present-day relation for M_ BH vs. \ M_ Bulge,Dyn , well within the IR scatter, deviating at most a factor of two from the mean. J090543.56+043347.3 displays clear signs of an ongoing tidal interaction and of spatially extended star formation at a rate of 50-100 M_ yr^ 1 , above the cosmic average for a galaxy of this mass and redshift. We argue that its subsequent evolution may move J090543.56+043347.3 even closer to the z=0 relation for M_ BH vs. \ M_ Bulge,Dyn .

gmw_00003713.name: TXS 0210+515
gmw_00003713.overview: TXS 0210+515 is an active galactic nucleus classified as a blazar [arXiv:2210.09797] and a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1911.06680, arXiv:2210.09797]. It is characterized as an extreme high-energy peaked BL Lac object with a hard TeV spectrum [arXiv:1911.06680] and a relatively low TeV luminosity [arXiv:2210.09797].

sources.summary: The paper selects TXS 0210+515 as a low-TeV-luminosity hard-TeV blazar, with pp-decay spectra improving the fit and exceeding WCDA sensitivity.

quotes.quote: TXS 0210+515, 1ES 2037+521, RGB J2042+244 Recently, Ref.  2020ApJS..247...16A reports a new sample of hard-TeV blazars, and collects their simultaneous multi-wavelength SEDs. Among them, three sources, i.e., TXS 0210+515, 1ES 2037+521 and RGB J2042+244, with relative low TeV luminosities are selected in our sample. Our fitting results are shown in Figure  hard .

Compared to the fitting results of the conical jet model, spine-layer model and proton synchrotron model that given in Ref.  2020ApJS..247...16A , the ray spectra generated in the ^0 decay improve the fitting of hard-TeV spectra of TXS 0210+515 and RGB J2042+244. For TXS 0210+515 and 1ES 2037+521, their ray emissions from ^0 decay also exceed the one-year sensitivity of WCDA of LHAASO.

sources.summary: MAGIC newly detects TXS 0210+515 as a hard-TeV EHBL; it has the hardest index among the new TeV detections and is recommended for deeper exposure.

quotes.quote: The analysis revealed firm VHE gamma-ray detection of three new sources, namely TXS 0210+515, RBS 0723, and 1ES 2037+521, and a hint of signal from RGB J2042+244.
Therefore, according to the MAGIC observations TXS 0210+515, whose intrinsic spectral index _ int is 1.6 0.3 , is a newly detected hard-TeV EHBL.
Among the new TeV-detected sources, TXS 0210+515 is the source with the hardest spectral index, making it a good target for deep exposure observations.

gmw_00003714.name: 1ES 2037+521
gmw_00003714.overview: 1ES 2037+521 is a hard-TeV blazar [arXiv:2210.09797, arXiv:1911.06680] characterized by a very bright host galaxy [arXiv:1911.06680]. The active galactic nucleus features a high synchrotron peak [arXiv:1911.06680] and a lower high-energy-hump peak luminosity than other hard-TeV sources [arXiv:2304.13893].

sources.summary: The paper selects 1ES 2037+521 among hard-TeV, low-luminosity blazars and finds its pp-decay ray emission can exceed the one-year WCDA sensitivity.

quotes.quote: TXS 0210+515, 1ES 2037+521, RGB J2042+244 Recently, Ref.  2020ApJS..247...16A reports a new sample of hard-TeV blazars, and collects their simultaneous multi-wavelength SEDs. Among them, three sources, i.e., TXS 0210+515, 1ES 2037+521 and RGB J2042+244, with relative low TeV luminosities are selected in our sample. Our fitting results are shown in Figure  hard .

In the modeling, the major contributors to multi-wavelength emission are leptonic processes. Compared to the fitting results of the conical jet model, spine-layer model and proton synchrotron model that given in Ref.  2020ApJS..247...16A , the ray spectra generated in the ^0 decay improve the fitting of hard-TeV spectra of TXS 0210+515 and RGB J2042+244. For TXS 0210+515 and 1ES 2037+521, their ray emissions from ^0 decay also exceed the one-year sensitivity of WCDA of LHAASO.

sources.summary: The paper models 1ES 2037+521 as a blazar with lower high-energy-hump peak luminosity than the hard-TeV cases, yielding larger allowed parameter space and a proton-synchrotron high-energy hump fit.

quotes.quote: In this energy range, we study 1ES 2037+521 and Mrk 421. The derived results are basically similar to those obtained in the range of 1 TeV 10 TeV. As shown in Table sample , since the high-energy hump's peak luminosities of 1ES 2037+521 and Mrk 421 are lower than those of 1ES 0229+200 and 1ES 1101-232, larger parameter spaces are derived. It can be seen in Figs. 2037 and 421-0.1T that parameter spaces can be found under the observational constraints when R=1 10^ 16 cm and R=1 10^ 17 cm . In the modeling, their high-energy humps are fitted by the proton synchrotron radiation as well.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 1ES 2037+521 as a newly detected MAGIC hard-TeV candidate with a bright host, a high hard-X-ray peak, and the sample highest synchrotron peak.

quotes.quote: Moreover, all the sources selected are present in the 2WHSP of high-synchrotron-peaked blazars except for 1ES 2037+521, whose very bright host galaxy is probably the cause of exclusion from the 2WHSP selection.
The analysis revealed firm VHE gamma-ray detection of three new sources, namely TXS 0210+515, RBS 0723, and 1ES 2037+521, and a hint of signal from RGB J2042+244.
Among the models presented in this paper, 1ES 2037+521 has the highest _ syn,pk , which is close to 100 keV.

gmw_00003715.name: RGB J2042+244
gmw_00003715.overview: RGB J2042+244 is a low-luminosity, hard-TeV blazar [arXiv:2210.09797] that is classified as a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:2210.09797, arXiv:1911.06680]. The source is considered a possible extreme high-energy peaked BL Lac object with a flat X-ray spectrum [arXiv:1911.06680].

sources.summary: The paper selects RGB J2042+244 as a hard-TeV, low-luminosity blazar and finds pp-decay ray spectra improve the hard-TeV fit, though with leptonic emission dominating.

quotes.quote: TXS 0210+515, 1ES 2037+521, RGB J2042+244 Recently, Ref.  2020ApJS..247...16A reports a new sample of hard-TeV blazars, and collects their simultaneous multi-wavelength SEDs. Among them, three sources, i.e., TXS 0210+515, 1ES 2037+521 and RGB J2042+244, with relative low TeV luminosities are selected in our sample. Our fitting results are shown in Figure  hard .

In the modeling, the major contributors to multi-wavelength emission are leptonic processes. Compared to the fitting results of the conical jet model, spine-layer model and proton synchrotron model that given in Ref.  2020ApJS..247...16A , the ray spectra generated in the ^0 decay improve the fitting of hard-TeV spectra of TXS 0210+515 and RGB J2042+244.

sources.summary: RGB J2042+244 is reported as a hint-of-signal source whose intrinsic index and modeled flat X-ray spectrum make it a possible hard-TeV EHBL.

quotes.quote: The analysis revealed firm VHE gamma-ray detection of three new sources, namely TXS 0210+515, RBS 0723, and 1ES 2037+521, and a hint of signal from RGB J2042+244.
The hint-of-signal source RGB J2042+244, _ int =1.7 0.6 , seems also a hard-TeV EHBL.

Assuming that the flat X-ray spectrum in RGB J2042+244 corresponds to the synchrotron peak, the spectrum is fitted adopting a relatively lower value for the maximum energy of electrons as shown in Figure fig:SEDs_detection (e).

gmw_00003716.name: RBS 0921
gmw_00003716.overview: RBS 0921 is a blazar classified as a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1911.06680, arXiv:2103.10861]. The source exhibits a changing-look nature [arXiv:2103.10861] and has shown only a weak hint of high-energy gamma-ray emission [arXiv:1911.06680].

sources.summary: RBS 0921 has only a hint of HE gamma-ray signal; its VHE upper limits do not constrain broadband modeling, unlike the better measured MAGIC sources.

quotes.quote: Interestingly, the analysis of more than 8 years of data from the source RBS 0921 indicates a TS of 23, corresponding to a significance of 4 , near the threshold used to define a source detected at HE.
The upper limits in the VHE range for RBS 0921 do not sufficiently constrain the model, therefore the modeling of the broadband spectrum is omitted in this case.

Except for RBS 0921, from which only a hint of HE gamma-ray signal has been observed by Fermi LAT, the spectral indices determined in time intervals centered on MAGIC observations range from 1.4 0.2 to 1.9 0.2 .

sources.summary: The paper says 5BZG J1056+0252 was cataloged as BZG, but LAMOST classifies it as BZB and the authors are confident about its changing-look nature.

quotes.quote: 5BZG J1056+0252 was reported by fischer98 and classified as a BZG in the Roma-BzCAT. We classified it as a BZB according to the LAMOST spectrum.

However, despite these sources of uncertainties, on the basis of our analysis we are confident about claiming the changing-look nature of J140244.51+155956.6, J132111.20+221612.1, and J105606.61+025213.4 being less confident for the remaining ones for the reasons previously described.

gmw_00003717.name: RGB J2313+147
gmw_00003717.overview: RGB J2313+147 is an active galaxy [arXiv:1004.3767] classified as a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1911.06680] at a redshift of z=1.319 [arXiv:1004.3767]. It is a relatively weak source [arXiv:1004.3767] with a synchrotron peak located below the threshold for extreme high-energy blazars [arXiv:1911.06680].

sources.summary: The paper presents BZU J2313+1444 as its highest-redshift source, a weak z=1.319 object without high-energy cutoff and useful for EBL evolution studies.

quotes.quote: BZU J2313+1444 is formally the highest redshift source in Table table1 , with z=1.319 . The source is relatively weak. Contrary to the other high-redshift sources, the significance of source detection in the 30-100 GeV band is less than 5 . From Fig. fig:BZU one can see that the source spectrum does not show a signature of high energy cut-off. This implies that the source might be an ideal candidate for the study of EBL evolution with redshift.

sources.summary: RGB J2313+147 is detected by Fermi over a one-year exposure, but its X-ray data and modeling indicate a synchrotron peak below the EHBL threshold.

quotes.quote: All the other sources are detected with a TS spanning from 34, for the source RGB J2313+147 (1 year exposure), to 94, for 1ES 1426+428 (1 year exposure) that is also the brightest source of the sample in X-ray.
The only exception is RGB J2313+147, whose X-ray data suggest a peak located below 10 ^ 17 Hz (see Sec. subsec:nustar ).

According to the model, 1ES 0229+200 remains the source of the sample with the most extreme synchrotron peak, while RGB J2313+147 and BZB J0809+3455 are non-EHBL sources, having their peak below the defined threshold of 10 ^ 17 Hz.

gmw_00003718.name: HS \ 2236+1344
gmw_00003718.overview: HS \ 2236+1344 is an extremely metal-poor blue compact dwarf galaxy located at a distance of 86.4 Mpc [arXiv:1405.7070, arXiv:1609.02358]. It hosts three giant H II regions [arXiv:1405.7070], a very young central starburst [arXiv:1405.7070], and localized He II emission [arXiv:1405.7070, arXiv:1309.6231].

sources.summary: The paper maps the extremely metal-poor blue compact dwarf HS \ 2236+1344, finding three giant H II regions, young central star formation, disturbed outskirts, velocity gradients, and localized He II emission.

quotes.quote: The main goal of this study is to carry out a spatially resolved investigation of the warm interstellar medium (ISM) in the extremely metal-poor Blue Compact Dwarf (BCD) galaxy HS \ 2236+1344 . Special emphasis is laid on the analysis of the spatial distribution of chemical abundances, emission-line ratios and kinematics of the ISM, and to the recent star-forming (SF) activity in this galaxy.

Emission-line maps and broad-band images obtained in this study indicate that HS \ 2236+1344 hosts three Giant H ii regions (GH ii Rs). Our data also reveal some faint curved features in the BCD periphery that might be due to tidal perturbations or expanding ionized-gas shells. The ISM velocity field shows systematic gradients along the major axis of the BCD, with its south-eastern and north-western half differing by 80 km/s in their recessional velocity. The H and H equivalent width distribution in the central part of HS \ 2236+1344 is consistent with a very young ( 3 Myr) burst.
The high-ionization He ii 4686 emission line is detected only in the central part of HS \ 2236+1344. Similar to many BCDs with He ii 4686 emission, HS \ 2236+1344 shows no Wolf-Rayet (WR) bump.

sources.summary: The paper identifies J2238+14 as the sample's lowest-metallicity galaxy and links two metallicity decrements to its two bright star-forming knots.

quotes.quote: Finally, Fig.  first_one portrays J2238+14 which clearly shows two metallicity decrements associated with the two bright knots of the galaxy -- compare the black solid line representing O/H with the H and continuum fluxes shown as histograms. The metallicity drop corresponds to 12+ ( O/H ) 0.5 . J2238+14 is the galaxy of lowest average metallicity in the sample (see the dashed line corresponding to O/H =( O/H )_ /10 , which is common to the three figures).

Two of them, J1647+21 and J2238+14 , show drops of metallicity ( 0.5 dex) associated with enhanced star-formation activity in central regions. Disk galaxies usually present a negative gradient, with the metallicity decreasing inside out. Therefore, a deficit of metallicity in the inner galaxy is strange, and attributed to the recent arrival of external metal-poor gas that has not yet mixed up with the pre-existing ISM (Sect.  introduction ; other alternatives are also examined in Sect.  rule_out ).

sources.summary: The paper identifies J2238+1400 as the sample's only undetected BCD in H I, places it at 86.4 Mpc, estimates a velocity width, and derives an H I mass upper limit.

quotes.quote: There is only one non-detection, the BCD J2238+1400 = HS 2236+1344.

We can use the measured RMS of 1.86 mJy of the spectrum of the non-detected galaxy J2238+1400, to derive an upper limit for its H i mass. This galaxy, at the distance of 86.4 Mpc, is the furthest object in our sample. We take its H i velocity width at zero intensity to be 192 km s ^ 1 , obtained by multiplying 155 km s ^ 1 , the largest v_ 20 in our sample (Table tab1 ), by 1.2, assuming that the velocity width at zero intensity is 20 wider than v_ 20 . Assuming a rectangular profile, we obtain a 5 upper limit M (H i ) 3.1 10 ^9 M _ .

sources.summary: The review identifies HS2236+1344 as an XMP BCD galaxy with GMOS-IFU H-alpha mapping, no localized oxygen overabundance, and He II emission considered against WR, HMXB, O-star, and AGN origins.

quotes.quote: In Figure fig2 we show the g-band acquisition image of the XMP BCD galaxy HS2236+1344, in which we indicated the total field of view of 4" 8" and the H map of the galaxy obtained from the composition of two different pointings with GMOS-IFU.

In any case, the absence of chemical overabundances in the ISM of UM 408, Tol 2146-391, Tol 0104-388 and HS2236+1344 and in the dwarf galaxies studied in the literature (e.g., ) indicates that the population of young star clusters is not producing localized oxygen overabundances. The most likely explanation for this is that metals formed in the current star-formation episode reside in a hot gas phase (T 10 ^ 7 K; ); thus, they are not observable in optical wavelengths.

We discuss, in , the possibility that the He ii 4686 emission line, in HS2236+1344, is associated with WR stars, high-mass X-ray binaries (HMXBs), O stars at low metallicities, and/or a low-luminosity Active Galactic Nucleus.

gmw_00003719.name: LBQS 1206+1052
gmw_00003719.overview: LBQS 1206+1052 is a bright broad-absorption line quasar [arXiv:1201.1054] located at a redshift of approximately 0.3955 [arXiv:1809.03114]. The system hosts two distinct absorption outflows [arXiv:1809.03114] and exhibits rare Balmer broad absorption lines [arXiv:1201.1054].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes two HST/COS epochs of the quasar LBQS 1206+1052, deriving outflow densities, distances, column densities, energetics, variability, and UV dust extinction effects.
