quotes.quote: We analyze new HST /COS spectra for two quasar absorption outflows seen in the quasi-stellar object LBQS 1206+1052. These data cover, for the first time, absorption troughs from S iv , Si ii , and P v . From the ratio of the S iv * to S iv column densities, we measure the electron number density of the higher-velocity ( 1400 km s ^ 1 , v1400) outflow to be log( n e ) = 4.23 ^ +0.09 _ 0.09 cm ^ 3 and constrain the lower-velocity ( 730 km s ^ 1 , v700) outflow to log( n e ) > 5.3 cm ^ 3 . The n e associated with the higher-velocity outflow is an order of magnitude larger than reported in prior work.

LBQS 1206+1052 (J2000: R.A. = 12 09 24.079, decl. = +10 36 12.06, z = 0.3955) was first observed by HST /COS in 2010 May (PID 11698) and again in 2017 July (PID 14777). The details of each observation are given in Table 1. The new observations cover a larger wavelength range, allowing for more density-sensitive diagnostics to be observed.

sources.summary: The paper identifies LBQS 1206+1052 as a bright LoBAL quasar with rare Balmer broad absorption, SDSS spectroscopy, and absorber constraints placing the BAL region between the accretion disk and BLR.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we report the new identification of Balmer BALs in LBQS 1206+1052, which was identified during the large bright quasar survey 1995AJ....109.1498H and recently classified as a LoBAL 2009ApJ...692..758G . It is in fact one of 20 Balmer-absorption quasars we recently identified during our systematic search in the quasar catalog from the 7th data release 2010AJ....139.2360S of Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS, 2000AJ....120.1579Y ). With i band PSF magnitude of 16.50, the high brightness of LBQS 1206+1052 makes it an excellent candidate for high resolution spectroscopic follow-up and for long-term variability campaign which are crucial for absorption line study.

Continuum and Emission Line Spectrum LBQS 1206+1052 was targeted as a quasar candidate for spectroscopy, and was observed by SDSS 2.5m telescope on 2003-03-24 with an exposure time of 3165 seconds. It is very bright with optical PSF magnitudes of 17.19, 16.63, 16.50, 16.34, 15.92 at u , g , , i , z bands. As discussed in 2.2, it is very likely that the BALR in LBQS 1206+1052 fully covers the accretion disk but does not cover the BLR.

gmw_00003720.name: SBS 1421+511
gmw_00003720.overview: SBS 1421+511 is a dual active galactic nucleus system consisting of a quasar host galaxy and a close companion galaxy [arXiv:1512.05877]. The companion galaxy is located at a projected distance of 12.5 kpc and exhibits Seyfert-like line ratios [arXiv:1512.05877].

sources.summary: Keck spectroscopy identifies SBS 1421+511 as a quasar plus close companion system, with a quasar host galaxy, a likely companion galaxy, and evidence that the pair may host dual AGN.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we report the results of the new Keck-II Echellette Spectrograph and Imager (ESI) long-slit spectrographic observation of the system SBS 1421+511, which took the spectra of the quasar and the extended source at the same time. We identified a host galaxy in the quasar position, and therefore the off-nuclear AGN character is not due to a recoiling black hole. The quasar BELs are well modeled as emissions from a circular disk with a blueshift velocity of 1400 relative to NELs, suggesting a super-massive black hole which is recoiling at a velocity of 1400 , and meanwhile other possible interpretations exist.

The spectrum of the extended source shows that it is an galaxy with a velocity difference relative to the quasar host galaxy of only 120 and a projected distance of only 12.5 kpc at the redshift. Therefore, the extended source is likely to be an companion galaxy and we refer to the extended source as ``the companion'' hereafter. The companion shows Seyfert-like line ratios and a high [ OIII ] luminosity, suggesting that SBS 1421+511 is a dual AGN system.

gmw_00003721.name: NGC 3877
gmw_00003721.overview: NGC 3877 is a spiral galaxy located in the Ursa Major cluster [arXiv:1907.09573]. It is classified as a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus candidate [arXiv:2202.13225] that exhibits nuclear radio and X-ray emission [arXiv:2202.13225], nuclear variability [arXiv:2202.13225], and hosts the supernova SN1998S [arXiv:astro-ph/0410623].

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 3877 as one of three Ursa Major spiral galaxies, fitting its observed rotation curve with a generalized Miyamoto-Nagai potential and analyzing possible regular or chaotic stellar orbits.

quotes.quote: In the present paper, using a generalization of the Miyamoto and Nagai potential we adjusted the observed rotation curves of three specific spiral galaxies to the analytical circular velocities. The observational data have been taken from a 21 cm-line synthesis imaging survey using the Westerbork Synthesis Radio Telescope, for three particular galaxies in the Ursa Major cluster: NGC 3726, NGC 3877 and NGC 4010. Accordingly, the dynamics of the system is analyzed in terms of the Poincar e sections method, finding that for larger values of the angular momentum of the test particle or lower values its total energy the dynamics is mainly regular, while on the opposite cases, the dynamics is mainly chaotic. Our toy model opens the possibility to find chaotic bounded orbits for stars in those particular galaxies.

In panels (a) of Figures 1, 2, and 3, we show the observational data (points) of the rotation curve with the corresponding velocity dispersions (error bars) as reported by for NGC 3726, NGC 3877 and NGC 4010. The solid lines correspond to the analytical expressions eq6 fitted to the rotation curves. As can be seen, in each case the model fits the observed data with good accuracy. Additionally, in panels (b) of Figures 1, 2, and 3, we plot the normalized mass-density distribution eq5 at z=0 for the three galaxies, as a function of the dimensionless radial coordinate R .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3877 as a new nuclear-source and LLAGN candidate with nuclear radio and X-ray emission, variability, a brightened C-band core, and flat spectral index.

quotes.quote: Three new nuclear sources have been identified from the radio cross-correlation: NGC 3877, NGC 4192, and NGC 5792 (Table t:new_AGNs ). NGC 3877 and NGC 4192 also have X-ray sources right at the nucleus. When compared to previous observations, we find that NGC 3877 is also variable.

Moreover, NGC 3877 appears to be variable, which is a strong argument for the presence of an AGN. ulv02 , in a search for weak AGNs in galaxies at C-band, did not detect a nuclear source in NGC 3877 to an rms limit of 70 Jy beam ^ 1 with a resolution of approximately one arcsec. We measure 2 mJy for the core of this galaxy (Table t:new_AGNs ) which is 30 of the Ulvestad Ho noise. If the flux of the nuclear source had been constant, would have easily detected this source. Therefore, between the time of the Ulvestad Ho observations (Oct. 28, 1999) and the CHANG-ES observations (April 4th 2012), the core of NGC 3877 has brightened at C-band by at least a factor of 6, if we take 5 as a detection limit.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3877 as containing two X-ray point sources with radio emission: SN1998S and a resolved near-nuclear source offset from the optical nucleus.

quotes.quote: NGC 3877 In the case of NGC 3877, there are two X-ray point sources associated with radio emission. One is well known, as it is SN1998S. The other is quite close to the nucleus, yet the mutually consistent radio and X-ray position is offset by several arcseconds from the optical nucleus in an HST image. The radio emission from SN1998S is unresolved, with S_ 4.86GHz = 1.12 0.10 mJy yielding an R_X of about 10^ 4 . The near-nuclear source is resolved, with a size of 4^ 2^ .

gmw_00003722.name: CEERS-1019
gmw_00003722.overview: CEERS-1019 is a high-redshift Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy at a redshift of approximately 8.68 [arXiv:2112.04505, arXiv:2301.07072] that features a multi-clump structure [arXiv:2301.07072]. It hosts an active galactic nucleus with a supermassive black hole [arXiv:2305.12504, arXiv:2309.07210] and displays nitrogen enrichment [arXiv:2402.03428, arXiv:2405.11235].

sources.summary: The paper describes EGSY8p7 as the alias of EGSY-2008532660, a z=8.68 LAE, then estimates its expected colors, Ly luminosity, and non-detectability in the NB1257 image.

quotes.quote: For instance, with 4.3 h of exposure time using the Multi-Object Spectrometer For Infra-Red Exploration (MOSFIRE) spectrograph at the Keck Observatory, confirmed the galaxy EGSY-2008532660 (also known as EGSY8p7) as a Lyman- emitter (LAE) at z = 8.68 with a total Ly flux of 1.0 2.5 10 ^ 17 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 .

By comparison with other studies, we included the expected location of three very high- z galaxies from the literature in our color-magnitude diagram (Fig. color_magnitude , triangles). The F125W and NB1257 magnitudes were computed using the published Ly fluxes and EWs. In particular, we derived the expected colors of the spectroscopically identified galaxies MACS1149-JD at z = 9.1096 2018Natur.557..392H,2018ApJ...854...39H,2020ARA&A..58..617O,2021MNRAS.505.3336L , EGSY8p7 at z = 8.683 2015ApJ...810L..12Z and EGS-zs8-1 at z = 7.7302 2015ApJ...804L..30O . We conclude that all these galaxies, even the two luminous galaxies in the EGS, are beyond the limiting magnitude of this work and therefore would be undetectable in our NB1257 image.

The estimated Ly luminosities at z = 9.34 of galaxies MACS1149-JD and EGSY8p7 were 4.7 10 ^ 42 erg s ^ 1 and 1.9 10 ^ 43 erg s ^ 1 , respectively. EGS-zs8-1 presents the same Ly flux as EGSY8p7 , hence the estimated Ly luminosity would be the same for both galaxies. As we already mentioned in Sect. finalsample , these galaxies are beyond the depth reached by our work and, in fact, only the surveys conducted by and were deep enough to be able to realistically detect LAEs with the same luminosity as the luminous galaxies of the EGS.

sources.summary: The paper describes CEERS-1019 as a bright multi-clump z=8.678 Ly emitter with a very young, highly ionizing stellar population and line ratios favoring star formation over AGN or shocks.

quotes.quote: This bright ( H_ 150 =24.9 ) galaxy was identified in Roberts-Borsani2016 and spectroscopically confirmed with Ly emission at z_ Ly =8.683 in Zitrin2015 . NIRCam imaging reveals it is a multi-component galaxy with three separate clumps, as is common for bright galaxies at these redshifts e.g., Chen2023 . The CEERS NIRSpec observations reveal numerous strong rest-frame optical lines in the rest-frame optical, confirming a systemic redshift of z_ sys =8.678 . The NIRCam SED indicates the galaxy is dominated by a very young stellar population ( 4.9 Myr) with powerful nebular emission ( [ O III ] +H EW =2599 ) and extremely efficient ionizing photon production ( [ _ ion / erg ^ 1 \ Hz ] =26.0 ).

A 4.6 detection of N V 1243 has been reported in Mainali2018 , suggesting a hard ionizing spectrum which might be powered by AGN or fast radiative shocks. In NIRSpec spectra neither N V 1238 or N V 1243 is detected with 3 upper limit of EW of <4.8 . Although the NIRSpec upper limit does not rule out the detection of N V 1243 , the UV line ratios (e.g., O III ] 1661 /He II >2.6 ) are consistent with those powered by a star-forming galaxy Feltre2016 . Therefore, we do not consider the AGN or shock interpretation of CEERS-1019 in this paper.

sources.summary: The paper models CEERS-1019 as a high-redshift AGN host whose stellar emission dominates, with a roughly 10^7 solar-mass black hole accreting sub-Eddington and possible evolutionary links to GNz11-like systems.

quotes.quote: In the EL model, the continuum is dominated by the emission from the host galaxy, which has a total stellar mass of log (M_ star /M_ ) = 9.4 . Here we have assumed that all stars formed in a single burst and a stellar age of 2 Myr, that is the typical time interval at z 8-9 in CAT. Despite this extreme assumption, some of the emitted lines are heavily contaminated by the accreting BH, which has a mass of log (M_ BH /M_ ) = 6.9 and Eddington ratio of _ Edd =0.45 . In the SE model, instead, the continuum and emitted lines are dominated by the BH emission, which has a mass of log (M_ BH /M_ ) = 6.8 and Eddington ratio of _ Edd =1.8 .

We find that the SE model, where the flux density is dominated by the accreting BH, provides a poor fit to the observed photometry of CEERS-1019. Our analysis supports the interpretation that the central point source of GNz11 could be powered by a 1.5 10^ 6 M_ super-Eddington accreting BH ( _ Edd 2 - 3 ), while CEERS-1019 harbours a more massive BH, with M_ BH 10^7 M_ , accreting sub-Eddington ( _ Edd 0.45 - 1 ), with a dominant emission from the host galaxy. We find that there might be an evolutionary link between systems with a mass similar to GNz11 at z = 10.6 and systems with a mass similar to CEERS-1019 at z = 8.7 .

sources.summary: The paper presents CEERS 1019 as a high-redshift nitrogen-enhanced galaxy whose observed CNO and Ne/O abundances constrain massive-star enrichment scenarios.

quotes.quote: Similarly, CEERS-1019, at a redshift of z=8.678 , displays strong nitrogen emission lines of N III and N IV, with the N IV ] 1486 being the most intense line in its rest-frame UV spectrum Tang2023 . This galaxy has an unusually high N/O abundance ratio of ( N/O )= -0.13 0.11 , which is approximately 5.6 times the solar ratio. However, its C/O and Ne/O ratios, with values of ( C/O )= -0.75 0.11 and ( Ne/O )= -0.63 0.07 respectively, are relatively normal for its metallicity Marques2023 .

We closely reproduced the observed Ne/O ratio in CEERS 1019 employing a model with several thousand solar masses and non-zero metallicity, and we projected a ^ 12 C/^ 13 C ratio of 7, substantially lower than the solar ratio of around 90. The significant nitrogen enrichment predicted by Pop III stars with a few thousand solar masses not only reinforces the argument for a heavy seed formation pathway for massive black holes at redshifts as high as z=10.6 but it also accentuates the need for deeper investigations into their complex nature and pivotal role in the early Universe.

Another high-z JWST galaxy discovered by the CEERS survey team - CEERS 1019 - displays similar abundances to GN-z11. Investigating CEERS 1019 Marques2023 found strong emission of NIII and NIV lines and obtained log (N/O) = -0.13 0.11, log (C/O) = -0.75 0.11, log (O/H)+12 = 7.78 0.18 and log (Ne/O) = -0.63 0.07.

sources.summary: The paper discusses CEERS-1019 as a nitrogen-rich high-redshift galaxy with low C/N, high N/O, low oxygen abundance, and abundance ratios matched by selected fast-rotating stellar populations.

quotes.quote: We present an analysis of the chemical compositions in high-redshift galaxies, with a focus on the nitrogen-enhanced galaxies GN-z11 and CEERS-1019. We use stellar models of massive stars with initial masses ranging from 9 to 120 M_ across various metallicities to deduce the chemical abundances of stellar ejecta for a few light elements (H, He, C, N, O). Our study reveals insights into the chemical processes and elemental synthesis in the early universe. We find that Population III stars, particularly at initial fast equatorial rotation and sampled from a top-heavy initial mass function, as well as stars at Z=10^ 5 with moderate rotation, align closely with observed abundance ratios in GN-z11 and CEERS-1019. These models demonstrate (N/O) = -0.38, (C/O) =-0.22 and (O/H) + 12 = 7.82 at dilution factors of f 20 - 100 , indicating a good match with observational data.

CEERS-1019's characteristics add further complexity to the narrative as highlighted by the works of Nakajima2023, Larson_2023 . Residing at a redshift of z=8.678 , its emission lines, particularly the prominent N IV ] 1486 , suggest advanced chemical processes for a galaxy of its era Tang2023 . Following a detailed analysis of UV spectra of galaxies at high redshifts, Isobe2023 found CEERS-1019 to exhibit extremely low C/N ( (C/N) 1 ) and high N/O ( (N/O) 0.5 ) ratios. Marques2023 found that CEERS-1019, similar to GN-z11, has a peculiar rest-frame UV spectrum which strongly hints to it being a N-emitter.

sources.summary: The paper reports Ly emission from EGSY-2008532660/EGSY8p7, a bright z=8.68 galaxy in the Extended Groth Strip with red IRAC color and likely modest foreground magnification.

quotes.quote: EGSY-2008532660 (hereafter EGSY8p7; RA=14:20:08.50, DEC=+52:53:26.60) is a H_ AB =25.26 galaxy with a photometric redshift of 8.57^ +0.22 _ 0.43 and a red IRAC [ 3.6 ] [ 4.5 ] color, recently discovered by . We discuss the likelihood that the line is Ly at a redshift z_ spec =8.68 making this the most distant spectroscopically-confirmed galaxy. Detectable Ly emission at a redshift well beyond z 8 raises several questions regarding both the validity of earlier claims for non-detections of Ly in fainter sources, and the physical nature of the luminous sources now being verified spectroscopically.

The galaxy EGSY8p7 was detected in the Extended Groth Strip (EGS; Davis2007EGS ) from deep ( 27.0 ) multi-band images in the CANDELS survey and first reported as one of four unusually bright ( H_ 160 <25.5 ) candidate z>7 galaxies by .

We note \ discussed the possibility that EGSY8p7 is magnified by foreground galaxies. Adopting the lens galaxy masses found in \ as input, and using an updated photometric redshift, we conclude EGSY8p7 is likely magnified by 20 , and less than a factor 2 .

sources.summary: The paper treats EGSY8p7 as an exceptionally bright z=8.68 galaxy whose unclustered environment is consistent with stochastic galaxy luminosity-halo modeling.

quotes.quote: The recent spectroscopic confirmation of galaxies EGS-zs8-1 2015ApJ...804L..30O and EGSY8p7 2015ApJ...810L..12Z at z = 7.73 and z= 8.68 present an ideal testbed to investigate the relation between brightness/mass and clustering properties. In fact, these two sources have exceptionally bright magnitudes ( M_ UV 22 , to be compared with the characteristic magnitude M^ * _ UV 20.2 at z 8 2015ApJ...803...34B ). Consequently, these galaxies are objects with number densities of the order 10^ 6 Mpc ^ 3 2015ApJ...803...34B , which are rare in cosmological simulations of volumes comparable to (or a few times larger than) that of the CANDELS survey 2016MNRAS.461L..51W .
Curiously, neither EGS-zs8-1 or EGSY8p7 show evidence of being located in significantly clustered regions based on current photometric data in the EGS fields.

The motivation for this study was to understand whether the lack of neighbors around EGS-zs8-1 and EGSY8p7 at similar redshift was creating tension with modeling of galaxy formation and evolution. Our results provide a natural explanation for the absence of a clustering signature in the data at the current depth.

sources.summary: The paper fits EGSY-2008532660 with spheroidal templates, deriving a z~8.6 mature galaxy with a 500 Myr age, active star formation, large stellar mass, and dust mass.

quotes.quote: The galaxy EGSY-2008532660 Fig.1 shows the best-fits of the galaxy EGSY-2008532660 and its ^2 \ minimization curves for the three parameters (mass, age,z) with the help of the spheroidal/elliptical scenario (E) templates with the star formation history of 1Gyr time-scale (see Fig  SFRz )built to fit the local SEDs. The most surprising result of this galaxy best-fit at the confirmed redshift is the high age of 500Myrs, mainly imposed by the amplitude of the spectral Balmer discontinuity (D4000), calibrated by the age and number of the giant stars. Moreover this age is coherent with the far-UV data fitted by the dust attenuated slope of massive but evolved stars.

The current star formation rate (180 M_ by year) is active, while the cumulated star formation history was particularly intense when compared to other scenarios. While the comparison with the SFH of an instantaneous starburst (SB) is proposed below, the spiral scenario predicts SEDs unable to fit observations, whatever ages. The best-fits fully reproduce the [ 3.6 ] [ 4.5 ] m IR excess with a population of evolved giant stars, and calibrated on observations, give respective masses of 6.8 10 ^ 10 M _ for live stars (the stellar mass) and 2 10 ^ 8 M _ for dust and 10 ^ 11 M _ for the global galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper uses EGSY8p7 as an intense-emission template at z=8.68 whose IRAC colors are dominated by strong nebular lines and remain uncertain without deeper spectroscopy.

quotes.quote: For the case of intense [ ] emission, we use a spectroscopic template fitted to the ground-based and HST / Spitzer photometry of EGSY8p7 at z =8.68 zitrin15 , one of the four IRAC-excess bright sources first identified in the CANDELS survey rb16 . The template and HST / Spitzer photometry used here are taken directly from the latter study and we refer the reader to that paper for details on the construction of the templates and derivation of the photometric data points.

The observed (rest-frame) EWs of the combined [ ] and H lines are EW _ obs ( [ ] +H ) 6690 \ (EW _ rest ( [ ] +H ) 770 ) and EW _ obs ( [ ] +H ) 3408 \ (EW _ rest ( [ ] +H ) 375 ) for EGSY8p7 and JD1, respectively.

Within the 7<z<9 redshift range, P egase models corresponding to younger ages closely track the evolution of the EGSY8p7 template, whose red colours are dominated by strong nebular emission lines, whilst evolved stellar ages are required to explain the evolution of the JD1 templates (both with and without emission lines), where the red colour is primarily due to a Balmer break. Considering next how the templates and synthesis models match the IRAC colours of 13 spectroscopically-confirmed z>7 galaxies, we can see that line emission in both \ and the EGSY8p7 (line emitting) template are required to explain the strong dip in [ 3.6 ] [ 4.5 ] colour at z 6.6-6.8 as indicated by smit18 ; as expected the JD1 template with masked emission lines has no dip. However, at higher redshift, where the [ ] +H lines enter the 4.5 m band, the red colour is initially more easily reproduced by cases with strong line emission. For SEDs with a flat continuum, as assumed by rb16 , such a colour remains relatively constant until the lines leave the band at z=9 . However, in the case of a moderate to strong Balmer break and reduced (but not absent) line emission, the IRAC excess increases from z 7.5 onwards as the Balmer break moves redward, thereby removing flux from the 3.6 m band whilst simultaneously providing a relatively constant amount of flux in the 4.5 m band. In the case where the rest-frame optical continuum is not flat, this impacts the slope of the [ 3.6 ] [ 4.5 ] colour evolution. The impact of the Balmer break is particularly evident when comparing the evolution of the masked JD1 spectrum to the older-aged \ synthesis models which, despite including (normal) emission lines display virtually identical [ 3.6 ] [ 4.5 ] evolution. Examining the actual data, one can reasonably securely conclude that the large IRAC excesses at 7 z 7.5 (e.g. ono12,finkelstein13,rb16,hashimoto19 ) are difficult to reproduce without an extreme [ ] +H contribution, as is clearly the case for IRAC colours for the sources at z 6.8 studied by e.g., sobral15 , smit18 , laporte17b and matthee19 . However, for the sources at 7.5 z 9 (e.g., watson15,hoag17,laporte17,tamura19 plus GN-z10-3 and EGSY8p7), the paucity of spectroscopic data makes it premature to conclude that the IRAC excess in galaxies beyond z 7 arises entirely from line emission.

sources.summary: The paper treats EGSY-z8p7 as a very distant z=8.683 Ly emitter with high-excitation UV emission, possible AGN contribution, and an 8.7-fold enhanced field.

quotes.quote: These are EGS-zs8-1 at z_ spec = 7.7302 0.0006 ( Oesch2015 ; see also Stark2017 ; Tilvi2020 ), EGS-zs8-2 at z_ spec = 7.4770 0.0008 Borsani2016,Stark2017 , and EGSY-z8p7 at z_ spec = 8.683^ +0.001 _ 0.004 ( Zitrin2015 ; see also Mainali18 ). All of these sources are expected to exhibit strong radiation fields due to high-equivalent width [ OIII ] +H emission lines identified through their Spitzer/IRAC photometry . Indeed, highly excited UV lines have been confirmed in the MOSFIRE spectra of EGS-zs8-1 ( C iii ; Stark2017 ) and EGSY-z8p7 ( N v ; Mainali18 ). Especially the detection of the N v line in EGSY-z8p7 points to a non-negligible, possible contribution from an AGN.

Specifically, the field around EGSY-z8p7 at z_ spec =8.683 is enhanced by a factor 8.7 , suggesting that this source might sit in one of the first overdensities and ionized bubbles in the Universe, only 500 Myr after the Big Bang.

sources.summary: CEERS 01019 is discussed as a z=8.679 galaxy and broad-line AGN candidate whose reported and newly measured broad H lines are consistent, though below final-sample significance.

quotes.quote: CEERS 01019 is reported as CEERS 1019 in 2023arXiv230308918L . The other galaxy is CEERS 01019 at z_ spec =8.679 , which was first confirmed spectroscopically with Ly by 2015ApJ...810L..12Z . This galaxy was reported to show a 4.3 Nv 1243 whose ionization potential is 77 eV 2018MNRAS.479.1180M , and recently, 2023arXiv230308918L report a broad H emission line with FWHM =1196 349 \ km \ s^ 1 with 2 ( 3.53/1.51=2.3 ), indicating that this galaxy is an AGN. Our analysis also shows a similar broad H line with FWHM =1664^ +475 _ 302 \ km \ s^ 1 with 3 , consistent with the results in 2023arXiv230308918L . Although these two galaxies are good candidates for broad-line AGNs, we do not include them in our final sample due to the moderate signal-to-noise ratio of the broad line.

sources.summary: The paper links CEERS-1019 to a black-hole population ending as bright z~6 quasars and models whether its black hole becomes overmassive or follows the local relation.

quotes.quote: Furthermore, the very distant quasar reported at z=8.68 by JWST/CEERS programs CEERS-1019; Larson_2023 and GN-z11 at z=10.6 by JWST/JADES programs Maiolino_2023 can be attributed to the BH population that ends up in typical bright quasars hosting 10^8 at z 6 . Recent theoretical studies provide a formation model of those BHs in CEERS-1019 and GN-z11 based on heavy seed BHs with 10^ 3 - 10^ 5 as shown in Figure 9 of Li_2023 (see also an alternative model presented in Schneider_2023 ).

To understand the subsequent evolution of the distant BH at z=8.68 Larson_2023 , which might be a luminous quasar at z 6 , we extrapolate the BH and stellar masses and predict their future evolution. We select two evolutionary tracks from Figure fig:Mevol that are closest to the BH of CEERS-1019 (note that the BH with 10^6 in GN-z11 recently reported by Maiolino_2023 is also located close to the two tracks). By adopting these BH growth tracks and extrapolating the stellar masses to z=6 , we find that this BH could either grow to be overmassive or maintain the BH-to-stellar mass ratio consistent with the local value as indicated by the two dashed curves Agarwal_2013,Natarajan_2017,Inayoshi_2022a,Hu_2022b,Scoggins_2023 .

sources.summary: The paper pinpoints CEERS 01019 as a z=8.68 high-redshift galaxy with low C/N, high N/O, AGN-like ionization signatures, and CNO-cycle processed gas.

quotes.quote: Among the 70 galaxies, we have pinpointed 2 galaxies, CEERS 01019 at z=8.68 and GLASS 150008 at z=6.23 , with extremely low C/N ( [ C/N ] 1 ), evidenced with ] 1907,1909, ] 1750, and ] 1483,1486, which show high N/O ratios ( [ N/O ] 0.5 ) comparable with the one of GN-z11. CEERS 01019 (GLASS 150008) has (does not have) a strong high ionization line that is explained by AGN (star-formation) photoionization models, which is (not) similar to GN-z11. Such low C/N and high N/O ratios found in CEERS 01019 and GLASS 150008 (additionally identified in GN-z11) are close to the equilibrium of the CNO cycle, suggesting that these 3 galaxies are enriched by metals processed by the CNO cycle.

Then, we revisit metallicity and N/O measurements of CEERS 01019 and GN-z11 with AGN radiations. We find that CEERS 01019 has the lower limit of ] / ] and the upper limit of /H simultaneously reproduced by the default AGN model.

sources.summary: CEERS-1019 is reanalyzed as a z=8.683 Ly-emitting galaxy with JWST spectroscopy, tentative N V emission, derived physical properties, and possible AGN-related broad H-alpha emission.

quotes.quote: CEERS-1019 ( , [ J2000 ] = 215.0354 ^ , 52.8907 ^ ) was initially identified as a z_ phot 8.6 dropout galaxy by and spectroscopically confirmed at z_ spec = 8.683 by through strong Ly emission (see also Mainali2018_NV1240 and Witten2023_lya ). It is one of the most distant Ly emitter known and is thought to reside in an over-dense region and ionized bubble boosting substantially its Ly transmission Larson2022_enviro, Leonova2022_enviro, Whitler2023_overdensityCEERS1019 . also report a tentative detection of N v 1240 ( 4.6 ), suggesting a hard ionizing spectrum of this source. Recently, much deeper spectroscopy of CEERS-1019 was reported and analyzed by , , and using NIRSpec, along with NIRCam and MIRI imaging. Although with some discrepancies, these works derived important physical properties of CEERS-1019 such as its stellar mass (log( M_ /M_ 8.7-10.1 ), gas-phase metallicities ( 7.6-8.0 ), ionizing indicators (e.g., O32 13-18 ), among others.

sources.summary: The paper treats CEERS 1019 as a z=8.7 JWST AGN candidate and compares its estimated SMBH mass, luminosity, Eddington ratio, and descendant halo mass with Trinity predictions.

quotes.quote: As of the writing of this paper, JWST has found at least two AGN candidates above z 8 , i.e., CEERS1019 at z=8.7 Larson2023 and GN-z11 at z=10.6 Maiolino2023 . CEERS1019 has an estimated SMBH mass M_ 10^7 M_ , and an Eddington ratio ( ) of 1.2 , whereas GN-z11 is estimated to be a M_ 10^6 M_ SMBH accreting at an Eddington ratio of 5 .

Given that both GN-z11 and CEERS1019 have bolometric luminosities of 10^ 45 erg/s, their masses ( M_ 6.2 and 6.94 ) and Eddington ratios ( _ rad 5.5 and 1.3 ) are consistent with Trinity 's predictions (shown in the solid and dashed vertical lines).

The typical descendant halo masses for GN-z11- and CEERS 1019-like objects are (1-3) 10^ 14 M_ , although the distribution is broad, due to the diversity in individual halo assembly histories. Similar to the z=0 descendants of z 6 massive black holes, the descendants of GN-z11- and CEERS 1019-like objects make up only 1 of all the haloes at similar mass scales.

sources.summary: The paper estimates CEERS-1019 radio flux from its black hole and host, finding ngVLA can detect it in 100 hours while SKA needs longer.

quotes.quote: The radio flux density from the BH in CEERS-1019 is 80 - 1000 nJy at 0.1 GHz and 2 - 300 nJy at 10 GHz. Its host galaxy has an SFR of 30 \ yr ^ 1 for which the expected flux from H II regions is about 40 nJy, still less than that of the BH. However, radio emission from SNe below 1 GHz is on par with that of the BH and even exceeds it at 0.1 GHz. NgVLA can detect CEERS-1019 with integration times of 100 hr but SKA would require longer times.

gmw_00003723.name: NGC 6921
gmw_00003723.overview: NGC 6921 is a spiral galaxy that exhibits weak CO and HCN emission [arXiv:1302.3526]. Millimeter-wave observations of the galaxy reveal three horn-like structures extending to the north, east, and south [arXiv:2309.02776], while formaldehyde absorption is detected against the cosmic microwave background [arXiv:1302.3526].

sources.summary: The paper reports significant H2CO absorption in NGC 6921 despite weak CO/HCN emission and argues the absorption is against the cosmic microwave background.

quotes.quote: NGC 6921: Gao2004a measure very weak CO and HCN J= 1-0 ( 10 and 1 mK, respectively) emission toward this spiral galaxy. We are not aware of any spectral line imaging measurements of this galaxy at radio wavelengths. Somewhat surprisingly, given the low intensity of the HCN J= 1-0 measurement, we detect significant H _2 CO 1_ 10 1_ 11 absorption toward this galaxy (Figure fig:ExgalFormSingles-OnePage ). As the 4.8 GHz continuum flux is measured to be only 28 mJy toward this galaxy, H _2 CO absorption of a strong background continuum source does not provide a mechanism for producing significant H _2 CO absorption. The H _2 CO 1_ 10 1_ 11 absorption we measure is clearly absorbing the cosmic microwave background.

sources.summary: The paper detects three horn-like mm-wave structures in NGC 6921 and says comparable-resolution images are lacking for interpreting the non-nuclear components.

quotes.quote: 87: NGC 6921 Three horn-like structures in the north, east, and south directions are seen in our mm-wave image constructed with a beam of 0 37 0 25. Images at comparable resolutions are not found. Additional observations are desired for discussing the non-nuclear components.

gmw_00003724.name: GHZ1
gmw_00003724.overview: GHZ1 is a bright, high-redshift Lyman-break galaxy candidate [arXiv:2210.08413, arXiv:2207.09436] located at a photometric redshift of approximately 10 to 11.5 [arXiv:2212.06666]. It is moderately magnified by gravitational lensing [arXiv:2212.06666] and exhibits active star formation [arXiv:2207.09436], though ALMA observations show no clear dust continuum or line emission [arXiv:2210.08413].

sources.summary: The paper reports ALMA observations of GHZ1/GLz11, a z~10.6 GLASS-JWST galaxy candidate, finding no clear line or continuum detection and inferring low dust and metal-poor/high-redshift-galaxy-like SFR limits.

quotes.quote: We report the ALMA observation of a z 10 galaxy candidate (GHZ1) discovered from the GLASS-JWST Early Release Science Program. Our ALMA program aims to detect the \ emission line at the rest-frame 3393.0062 GHz ( 88.36 m) and far-IR continuum emission with the spectral window setup seamlessly covering a 26.125 GHz frequency range ( 10.10<z<11.14 ). A total of 7 hours of on-source integration was employed, using four frequency settings to cover the full range (1.7 hours per setting), with 0 .7 angular resolution. No line or continuum is clearly detected, with a 5 upper limit of the line emission of 0.93 mJy beam ^ 1 at 25 km s ^ 1 channel ^ 1 and of the continuum emission of 30 Jy beam ^ 1 .

The galaxy candidate targeted for our ALMA observation (2021.A.00023.S, PI: Yoon) was first discovered by two independent groups (designated as GHZ1 by and as GLz11 by ) from the JWST ERS program, GLASS-JWST treu_etal_2022 with the estimated photometric redshift (photo- z ), z 10.6 as suggested by strong Lyman break ( >2 mag) feature at F115W and shorter. Multiple independent analyses confirm this galaxy as an ``ironclad'' candidate at z 10 donnan_etal_2022,harikane_etal_2022b .

The 5 upper limit of the \ line emission and the inferred SFR suggest that GHZ1 in the SFR- L_ [ OIII ] plane is consistent with the metal-poor dwarf galaxies and the several observed high- z galaxies. The SED modeling based on the 3 upper limit of the continuum emission and the JWST NIRCam photometry suggests that GHZ1 has a small fraction ( 10^ 5 of stellar mass) of hot dust ( T_d 90 K).

sources.summary: The paper uses GL-z10 as a massive z>9 candidate that could grow toward z=6 quasar hosts and whose expected MBH growth should not be suppressed by supernova feedback.

quotes.quote: Can these galaxies be progenitors of z=6 quasars? Let's take as an example GL-z10 and GL-z12 2022arXiv220709434N . The stellar masses are reported to be (M_ gal / )=9.6 and (M_ gal / )=9.1 respectively and the corresponding number density (given the survey volume) is 4.5 10^3 Gpc ^ 3 , which is about 3 orders of magnitude larger than the number density of luminous quasars at z 6-7 . So, of these exceptional young galaxies, only about one in a thousand is needed to be a progenitor of a z 6-7 quasar.

The mass of galaxies such as GL-z10 and SMACS z10c at the time of observation are already sufficiently massive that SN feedback should not hinder MBH growth. Assuming that these candidate galaxies host an MBH with mass (M_ gal )-4 , this leads to (M_ BH / )=5.6 and (M_ BH / )=5.7 . If the galaxies have a sufficiently regular morphology, MBHs of such mass should be able to remain close to the center 2019MNRAS.486..101P,2021MNRAS.508.1973M , and there is no reason to expect that such MBHs cannot grow.

sources.summary: The paper describes GHZ1/GLASSz10 as one of two bright z greater than 10 GLASS-JWST candidates, with consistent new photometry, moderate lensing, and likely placement at z 10-11.5.

quotes.quote: Among the various fields targeted by early JWST surveys, the first observations of a flanking field to the Hubble Frontier Field (HFF) cluster Abell 2744 A2744 hereafter; Lotz2014,Castellano2016b within the GLASS-JWST project delivered two of the brightest candidates at z>10 , dubbed GHZ1/GLASSz10 (at z 10.5 ) and GHZ2/GLASSz12 (at z 12.3 ) ( Castellano2022b 2022a, C22a hereafter; Naidu2022b,Bakx2022,Donnan2022,Harikane2022b,Yoon2022 ).

The two objects GHZ1 and GHZ4 from C22a fall in the region recently reobserved in parallel to NIRSpec. Reassuringly, their SEDs and photometric redshifts obtained from the new data are in excellent agreement with those measured in the first GLASS-JWST NIRCam observations, with the deeper data analyzed here reducing the likelihood of the secondary photometric redshift solution at z 2 for GHZ4. Candidate GHZ1 also falls in the area that we have removed from the UNCOVER field to avoid duplications. The availability of the lensing model extending to the GLASS-JWST region allows us to estimate that they are moderately magnified, such that the intrinsic M_ UV are 0.5 -0.6 mag fainter than the estimates reported in our previous paper.

In this respect, we note that GHZ9 has best-fit photometric redshift solutions at z 9.3-9.5 , compatible with the spectroscopic value measured for DHZ1, while objects GHZ1, GHZ7 and GHZ8 are more likely placed at 10 z 11.5 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies GHZ1 as a bright, robust z about 10.6 JWST Lyman-break galaxy candidate with no significant low-redshift solution and strong star formation.

quotes.quote: We highlight the detection of the two bright sources GHZ1 and GHZ2, at z 10.6 and z 12.2 , respectively. These objects are located in the color-color diagram fairly away from color selection borders, and have no significant low-redshift solution. Their P(z) is single peaked, the 68 confidence level photometric redshift range being z_ zphot = 9.68-11.15 ( z_ EAzY = 10.28-10.80) for GHZ1 and z_ zphot = 12.03-12.58 ( z_ EAzY = 11.85-12.39) for GHZ2. They are among the most robust candidates at z>9 ever detected, as demonstrated by the several recent works that have presented their selection with redshift and properties comparable to those discussed here Donnan2022,Harikane2022b,Naidu2022b . They are remarkably bright (M _ UV 21) and star-forming (SFR 20 M_ /yr).

sources.summary: The paper includes GHZ2/GLz13 in joint ALMA-SED analysis, noting its early JWST identification, lack of robust dust continuum, and favored high-redshift solution among z 11-17 candidates.

quotes.quote: The non-detection of the dust continuum from \ is reminiscent of recent ALMA results in other three UV-bright galaxy candidates at z 11 -13: GHZ1/GLz11 , GHZ2/GLz13, and HD1 harikane2022b, bakx2022, popping2022, yoon2022, kaasinen2022, yoon2022 . GHZ1/GLz11 and GHZ2/GLz13 were also identified in the early \ data from the GLASS field treu2022 from different teams e.g., castellano2022, naidu2022, donnan2022, harikane2023 . No robust dust continuum is detected in follow-up deep 1-mm observations with a total of >10 hrs observing time for both candidates bakx2022, popping2022, yoon2022 , while a tentative ( 2.6 ) detection is reported in GHZ1/GLz11 yoon2022 .

To further investigate the high- z ( z 11 ) and the lower- z scenarios for all these candidates, we perform SED fitting to the optical--mm photometry using cigale burgarella2005, noll2009, boquien2019 .
Owing to our and recent deep ALMA observations, the high- z solutions at z 11 -17 are all favored in the UV bright high- z candidates of S5-z17-1, GHZ1/GLz11, GHZ2/GLz13, and HD1 (Section sec:sed ).

sources.summary: The paper identifies GL-z11 as a luminous JWST high-redshift candidate and finds its inferred stellar mass larger than the most massive TNG galaxies at comparable redshift.

quotes.quote: Recently, Naidu_2022 reported the discovery of two luminous galaxy candidates labeled as GLASS-z11 (hereafter GL-z11) and GLASS-z13 (GL-z13) with _ 10 (M_ * /M_ ) = 9.4_ 0.3 ^ +0.3 and 9.0_ 0.4 ^ +0.3 located at photometric redshifts of z_ phot =10.9_ 0.4 ^ +0.5 and z_ phot = 13.1_ 0.7 ^ +0.8 , respectively (see their table 3).
GL-z11 at 10.9_ 0.4 ^ +0.5 , GL-z13 at 13.1_ 0.7 ^ +0.8 , and CEERS-1749 at 16.0_ 0.6 ^ +0.6 are compared with the simulations at z = 10.98 , 11.98 , and 14.99 , respectively.

At z = 11.98 and z=10.98 , the maximum stellar masses of subhalos are _ 10 (M_ * /M_ ) = 8.07 and 8.43 , respectively, which is less massive than GL-z13 with _ 10 (M_ * /M_ ) = 9.0_ 0.4 ^ +0.3 and GL-z11 with _ 10 (M_ * /M_ ) = 9.4_ 0.3 ^ +0.3 .

sources.summary: The paper describes GHZ1 as a GLASS-JWST high-redshift galaxy candidate whose ALMA OIII and dust-continuum search was unsuccessful except for a marginal nearby spectral feature.

quotes.quote: Another case is GHZ1, a galaxy candidate from the JWST ERS program, GLASS-JWST Treu22 with a photometric redshift z 10.6 , SFR = 36.3^ +54.5 _ 26.8 , and (M_ / )=9.1^ +0.3 _ 0.4 Santini22 . The ALMA search for 88 m and dust continuum emission from GHZ1 was unsuccessful, albeit a marginal spectral feature was reported within 0.17^ '' of the JWST position of GHZ1 Yoon22 . The reported 5 upper-limit of luminosity is 2.2 10^8 L_ .

gmw_00003725.name: NGC 2960
gmw_00003725.overview: NGC 2960 is a spiral [arXiv:2111.09316] or lenticular [arXiv:1602.06960] galaxy hosting an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2111.09316, arXiv:2207.06594] and a warped, highly inclined water-megamaser disk [arXiv:1205.4758, arXiv:2111.09316]. The galaxy exhibits a dusty, asymmetric inner spiral structure [arXiv:1602.06960] and outer tidal features that indicate a recent interaction [arXiv:1602.06960].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC2960 as a bulge-dominated galaxy with embedded nuclear and kiloparsec-scale disks, dusty asymmetric spiral structure, and outer tidal or shell-like evidence for recent interaction.

quotes.quote: NGC 2960 NGC2960 (Figures fig:NGC2960-1 and fig:NGC2960-2 ) is a bulge-dominated galaxy with a kpc-scale embedded disk. Its semi-major axis is oriented 150 East of North, and the average flattening is rather low, q 0.7 . The radial surface brightness profile appears to exhibit a bulge-disk structure of a typical lenticular galaxy, with a prominent steepening of the profile inside 13'' ( 4.5 ) and an exponential decline outside. However, two salient features distinguish NGC2960 visually from a simple lenticular: the increase of ellipticity towards the center, and an inner ( SMA 8'' / 2.7 ) flocculent and dusty disk. The residual image of a basic bulge+disk model (Figure fig:NGC2960-1 ) reinforces the impression of a dusty disk, and reveals embedded asymmetric spiral structure.

We thus might interpret this large-scale component as the bulge, despite its exponential profile. At the largest radii at which the light distribution can be traced, from 80'' ( 30 ) to 30'' ( 10 ), the profile again flattens and is marked by tidal or shell-like features that can be seen even in the science frames. These features indicate that NGC2960 has undergone a recent interaction.

sources.summary: The paper presents Mrk 1419 as a new water-megamaser disk galaxy, measuring its warped circumnuclear disk, black-hole mass, and an 81 Mpc angular-diameter distance.

quotes.quote: We present here the preliminary results from a new maser system, Mrk 1419. Through a model of the rotation from the systemic masers assuming a narrow ring, and combining these results with the acceleration measurement from the Green Bank Telescope, we determine a distance to Mrk 1419 of 81 10 Mpc. Given that the disk shows a significant warp that may not be entirely traced by our current observations, more sensitive observations and more sophisticated disk modeling will be essential to improve our distance estimation to this galaxy.

The megamaser in Mrk 1419 was discovered in 2002 by Henkel et al. with the 100 m Effelsberg telescope, and it was the first maser after NGC 4258 to display the characterestic ``disk signature'', but is ten times farther away. Through single dish monitoring with Effelsberg, the authors could already measure the secular acceleration of the systemic components, and therefore concluded that the maser emission in Mrk 1419 must arise from an almost edge-on circumnuclear disk. We observed Mrk 1419 with the global VLBI between May 2009 and January 2011, for a total of six epochs of 12 hours each.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2960/Mrk 1419 as an Sa water-megamaser galaxy with patchy H I emission, an observed velocity gradient and detected diameter, plus slightly extended radio continuum implying a jet.

quotes.quote: NGC 2960, also called Mrk 1419, is an Sa RC3 galaxy at 72.2 5.1 Mpc and also a water megamaser galaxy from Kuo11 . The systemic velocity from is 4939 km s ^ 1 . Our map of NGC 2960 has lower SNR (SNR =6 ). Although emission is only seen in discrete patches of the galaxy, an overall velocity gradient is observed (Fig fig:ngc2960 ). The detected diameter is 2 5 at the 1 level from the moment-0 map.
We find that the radio continuum of NGC 2960 is slightly extended, suggesting that there is a radio jet launched from the central black hole (Fig. fig:ngc2960_cont ).

sources.summary: The paper identifies Mrk 1419 as a clean maser-disk galaxy with weak radio extensions, notes disk warping, and compares its jet, maser disk, nuclear disk, and large-scale disk orientations.

quotes.quote: Mrk 1419 This galaxy poses a clean maser disk kuo2011 . sun2013 reported extended emission with a PA of 125 10 from 20 cm radio data. In our VLA maps we see a weak extension in the southeast-northwest direction with a PA of 117 84 . Gaussian deconvolution of our 5 GHz map shows a compact component that is not clearly resolved and may contain a weak extension with a PA of 159 2 . The outer part of the maser disk shows some degree of warping kuo2011 .

In Mrk 1419, which has likely experienced a merger or some interaction lasker2016 , the maser disk and the nuclear region with a size of 270 pc are aligned and the jet is 20 from the angular momentum direction of this disk, while the galaxy's large scale disk has a normal vector that is 29 away from the jet direction.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2960 as a nearby spiral hosting an obscured SMBH and inclined maser disk, with X-ray constraints and jet-maser geometry affecting inferred spin.

quotes.quote: NGC 2960 NGC 2960 is a nearby spiral galaxy z=0.01645 , 3rc91 whose nucleus hosts a SMBH of = 1.16 0.05 10^7 . The maser disk, discovered by henkel02 , is highly inclined kuo11, impellizzeri12 and likely responsible for the large obscuration affecting the nuclear emission. In the X-ray band, a short snapshot (20 ks) is presented by masini16 , pointing to high obscuration but inconclusive about the Compton-thick nature of the AGN. A re-analysis of the broadband ( 0.2-70 keV) + spectrum with the most up-to-date toroidal modeling gives a bolometric luminosity L_ bol 1.3^ +1.0 _ 0.5 10^ 43 .

Indeed, herrnstein05 show that the core radio emission at 22 GHz is perpendicular to the maser disk in NGC 4258, and greene13 show that the PA difference between the maser disk and the kpc-scale jets in UGC 3789 and NGC 2960 are both 80 90 . We note that kamali19 find that the jet in NGC 2960 is misaligned about 70 with respect to the masers: in this last case (i.e. assuming an inclination of 65 instead of 85 for NGC 2960), we find that its spin becomes consistent with lower values ( a<0.8 ).

sources.summary: Mrk 1419 is presented as a confirmed disk maser with 183 GHz systemic emission comparable to 22 GHz, while high-velocity masers are absent at 183 GHz.

quotes.quote: Mrk 1419 The 22 GHz maser system in Mrk 1419 was discovered by Henkel_2002 and VLBI observations presented in Kuo_2011 confirm that it is a disk maser system. Our 183 GHz spectrum of Mrk 1419 is shown in the lower lefthand panel of fig:hex1 , along with a 22 GHz spectrum reproduced from Pesce_2015 (see Fig. 1 in that paper). The 22 GHz spectrum is an average of multiple epochs of spectral monitoring observations taken with the GBT.

The only component seen in the 183 GHz spectrum of Mrk 1419 is the central set of systemic maser features, which span a velocity range of 100 around a central velocity of 4885 . The peak flux density of the systemic features is 23.6 mJy, which is comparable to the typical peak flux density of the 22 GHz systemic masers. However, no high-velocity maser features are detected at 183 GHz despite them being comparably prominent to the systemic features at 22 GHz; the high-velocity features must thus be at least 4 times fainter at 183 GHz than at 22 GHz.

sources.summary: The paper fits NGC 2960 as a horizontally aligned megamaser system, varying the black-hole vertical offset and deriving a near-origin fitted position with reduced chi-square.

quotes.quote: In this work we perform a Bayesian statistical fit to estimate the mass-to-distance ratio and the recessional redshift of 10 different black holes hosted at the centre of active galactic nuclei, namely the galaxies NGC 5765b, NGC 6323, UGC 3789, CGCG 074-064, ESO 558-G009, NGC 2960, NGC 6264, NGC 4388, J0437+2456 and NGC 2273. Our general relativistic method makes use of the positions in the sky and frequency shift observations of water megamasers circularly orbiting the central black hole on their accretion disks.

NGC 2960 The observations show that the maser features of this galaxy are horizontally aligned; nevertheless, it was not possible to statistically estimate the BH y_0 offset. By varying the y_0 position along the height of the disk, we found variations on M/D up to 4 with respect to the M/D ratio found with y_0 fixed at the origin (the geometric center of the systemic masers). By fitting three parameters in this galaxy, we obtained a x_0 position only 0.004 mas away from the origin with a reduced ^2 of 1.511 and a scattering angle = 15 °.

sources.summary: The paper reports a circumnuclear disk in NGC 2960/Mrk 1419, including a blue inner structure, a central PA change, and clear dust structure in HST maps.

quotes.quote: Circumnuclear structures and megamaser disks In three galaxies (NGC 2273, NGC 2960 or Mrk 1419, and UGC 3789) we see evidence for a circumnuclear disk.

We find a blue structure in the inner 3 \ of NGC 2960 with constant PA and ellipticity as the outer disk. Within the inner 1 , there is a PA change likely associated with the narrow-line region gas, as shown (Figure fig:disk ).
In the case of NGC 2273, NGC 2960, and IC 2560, the dust structure is clear.

sources.summary: The paper excludes NGC 2960 from the main megamaser AGN relation because a required parameter is poorly constrained, then reports a high torus-model value and low NuSTAR counts.

quotes.quote: In order to test the \ relationship for the megamaser AGN, we require good constraints on , thus we exclude sources where the uncertainty on is >0.25 , which excludes NGC 1386 and NGC 2960 from our sample.

Similarly, the torus model yields =2.6 0.4 for NGC 2960 ( fix in their fit with mytorus ). It is not clear whether these very high values of are related to the low-count nature of their spectra, or if they represent true outliers in the \ relationship for the megamasers.
We estimate that around 2000 counts at minimum in \ FPMA and FPMB are required for an accurate determination of in CTAGN, where NGC 1386 and NGC 2960 have less than 1000.

gmw_00003726.name: PGC 043900
gmw_00003726.overview: PGC 043900 is a galaxy that does not show any clear indication of net streaming motion within its observed field of view [arXiv:1802.02591]. Because it lacks rotation, its kinematic misalignment is reported only as an upper limit [arXiv:1802.02591].

sources.summary: The paper singles out PGC 043900 as the only M3G galaxy without clear net streaming, giving its kinematic misalignment only as an upper limit.

quotes.quote: While the maximum velocity amplitudes reached within the two effective radii covered by our MUSE observations are often low ( 30 -50 km/s), only one galaxy, e) PGC 043900, does not show a clear indication for net streaming motion within the field of view.

Note that while we report actual measurements for the kinematic and photometric position angles, the kinematic misalignment for PGC043900 is an upper limit, as there is no net streaming in this galaxy.
As mentioned before, another special case is e) PGC 043900, which does not have any rotation and its is not well defined. Therefore, it is plotted as an upper limit.

gmw_00003727.name: PGC 046832
gmw_00003727.overview: PGC 046832 is a brightest cluster galaxy located in a subcluster of the Shapley Supercluster [arXiv:2205.04165]. It exhibits a complex structure with a triaxiality that changes radially [arXiv:2205.04165], a highly complex velocity map [arXiv:1802.02591], and likely hosts a kinematically decoupled core [arXiv:1802.02591].

sources.summary: The paper reanalyzes PGC 046832, a complex brightest cluster galaxy, and finds corrected Schwarzschild orbit mirroring leaves its black-hole and orbit-distribution results essentially unchanged.

quotes.quote: PGC 046832 is the brightest cluster galaxy in one of the subclusters of the Shapley Supercluster, at a distance of 200 Mpc. Because of its complex structure, this galaxy poses a challenge for modellers, requiring several inversions in the direction of its angular momentum and a radial change in triaxiality. The dynamical modeling by denBrok2021 showed that a) the black hole mass determined with the triaxial Schwarzschild models was lower than the one determined using axisymmetric models and that b) the intrinsic shape of the galaxy changes from almost prolate in the centre to almost oblate in the outer parts.

To determine the direct influence of the orbit mirroring correction on the black hole mass of PGC 046832, we assume the same viewing angles as used by denBrok2021 and the same dark matter halo mass. We re-run the Schwarzschild models in the exact same setup as in denBrok2021 , but additionally supplement the grid with points at M/L between 2.9 and 3.0, as the ^2 contours imply a 4 lower M/L .
We have re-analysed the galaxy with the corrected triaxial Schwarzschild code, but obtained consistent results with denBrok2021 .

sources.summary: The paper places PGC 046832 among misaligned M3G galaxies but says its complex velocity map does not resemble prolate-like rotation and likely hosts a KDC.

quotes.quote: There are 4 galaxies with 15^ o < <75^ o : h) PGC 065588, p) PGC 047154, g) PGC 015524 and d) PGC 046832 (in order of decreasing ).

On the other hand, d) PGC 046832 exhibits a very complex velocity map with multiple changes between approaching and receding velocities, but its velocity map does not resemble prolate-like rotation, and its is significantly smaller than for other galaxies in this group.

More typical and standard size KDCs are found in a few M3G targets ( i) PGC019085 and d) PGC046832), but these will be discussed in more detail in a future paper devoted to the analysis of the high spatial resolution MUSE data cubes.

gmw_00003728.name: PGC 065588
gmw_00003728.overview: PGC 065588 is a brightest cluster galaxy located in the core of the northern subcluster of Abell 3716 [arXiv:1706.08533]. The galaxy exhibits a loss of coherent stellar motions with prolate-like rotation [arXiv:1802.02591], centrally concentrated ionized gas [arXiv:2012.07882], and an extended cold-gas structure [arXiv:1706.08533].

sources.summary: The paper describes PGC 065588 as a BCG with loss of coherent motions and prolate-like rotation spanning the observed field, included in the high-misalignment subset.

quotes.quote: This is especially noticeable among BCGs, where the velocity maps change orientation (e.g. b) PGC 048896), or there is a loss of coherent motions (e.g. h) PGC 065588).

There are 4 galaxies with 15^ o < <75^ o : h) PGC 065588, p) PGC 047154, g) PGC 015524 and d) PGC 046832 (in order of decreasing ). The first three have similar rotation pattern as other galaxies with prolate-like rotation. Strictly speaking the values are inconsistent with 90 , but their velocity maps resemble those of galaxies with prolate-like rotation. We will, therefore, also refer to them as having prolate-like rotation.

It can be present across the full observed field-of-view (approximately 2 effective radii), for example in n) PGC007748, h) PGC065588 and ) PGC047590, but most galaxies have it within a specific area, either outside the central region (but within one effective radius, s) PGC099188), or more typically covering the full half-light radius (e.g. k) PGC047752, f) PGC003342, ) PGC007300 or b) PGC048896).

sources.summary: The paper identifies ESO187-G026 as the BCG in Abell 3716 and reports an extended narrow Na D absorbing cold-gas structure projected against its stellar continuum.

quotes.quote: In this Letter we report the discovery of an extended cold gas structure in the z = 0.045 cluster Abell 3716, revealed through Na D absorption against the stellar continuum of its central galaxy ESO187-G026. X-ray observations show A3716 to be a non-cool-core cluster with two components of comparable mass, separated by 400 kpc in projection 2015ApJ...803..108A . ESO187-G026 lies at the core of the northern subcluster.

During an ongoing search for candidate lensed background sources, a visual inspection of the MUSE datacube for A3716 revealed an extended region of narrow Na D doublet absorption to the East and North of the BCG. The strongest part of the structure is already visible in a simple narrow-band image extracted in the wavelength region 6166--6179 \ (Figure fig:img a). To isolate the gas absorption signal from the BCG, which also has deep, but much broader, stellar Na D lines, we constructed a model datacube for the galaxy by interpolating the median counts in circular annuli, treating each wavelength channel independently.

sources.summary: The paper presents PGC 065588 as a BCG with centrally concentrated ionized gas, regular gas rotation, and a strong central velocity-dispersion peak reaching about 500.

quotes.quote: The ionised-gas of the first component is centrally concentrated in three of the BCGs (PGC 047202, PGC 049940 and PGC 065588) while extended with outward filaments in the remaining two (PGC 015524 and PGC 073000).
Concerning the velocity field of the BCGs, the gas of the first component is regularly rotating in PGC 049940 and PGC 065588 while not showing rotation in PGC 047202.

The velocity dispersion has a clear peak in the center of PGC 065588 (up to about 500 ), while it reaches values of about 300, 250 for PGC 047202 and PGC 049940, respectively, not showing a clear nuclear raise.

gmw_00003729.name: PGC 015524
gmw_00003729.overview: PGC 015524 is a brightest cluster cD galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0109387, arXiv:2012.07882] that hosts a kinematically decoupled core [arXiv:0808.1521] and exhibits prolate-like rotation [arXiv:1802.02591]. It features weak line emission [arXiv:1303.0884, arXiv:0808.1521] with ionized-gas filaments extending toward the northeast [arXiv:2012.07882] that closely trace its central dust lanes [arXiv:1303.0884].

sources.summary: The paper maps MCG-02-12-039 with GMOS IFU data, describing weak line emission, a compact radio source, dust-linked H emission, and decoupled stellar and gas kinematics.

quotes.quote: The central galaxy (MCG-02-12-039) of Abell 0496 is a fairly weak line emitter (Fabian et al. 1981; Hu, Cowie Wang 1985) and is host to the compact radio source MSH 04-112 (Markovic, Owen Eilek 2004). Abell 0496 is a relaxed cluster with a cool core with a central metal abundance enhancement (Tamura et al. 2001). At a redshift of 0.0329, the galaxy has a linear scale of 0.656 kpc arcsec ^ 1 (from Hatch et al. \ 2007). This cluster has an interesting H morphology (McDonald et al. \ 2010). There are at least 5 distinct filaments, with various shapes and directions.

Figure Haflux shows that the peak H emission is also where the dust (extinction) is the highest in Figure fig:MCG_extinct . This is similar to the finding of Hatch et al. \ (2007), where they found that the peak H + [ NII ] emission corresponds to the area where the three dust lanes meet in the galaxy centre. We confirm the observation by Hatch et al. \ (2007), that the line emission follows the general path of the dust (extinction) features, but is not as filamentary.

sources.summary: The paper includes PGC 015524 among high-misalignment prolate-like M3G galaxies and notes its BCG outskirts show symmetric velocity shifts rather than rotation.

quotes.quote: There are 4 galaxies with 15^ o < <75^ o : h) PGC 065588, p) PGC 047154, g) PGC 015524 and d) PGC 046832 (in order of decreasing ). The first three have similar rotation pattern as other galaxies with prolate-like rotation. Strictly speaking the values are inconsistent with 90 , but their velocity maps resemble those of galaxies with prolate-like rotation. We will, therefore, also refer to them as having prolate-like rotation.

They are, however, characterised by a spatially symmetric shift of velocities to larger values compared to the systemic velocity of the galaxy. Examples are the BCGs: m) PGC049940, i) PGC019085 and g) PGC015524.

sources.summary: The paper identifies MCG-02-12-039 as the central cD galaxy of Abell 496 and links metal-rich intracluster gas and excess iron near it to long-term Type Ia supernova enrichment.

quotes.quote: The X-ray emission is thus distributed azimuthally symmetrically centered on a cD galaxy, MCG-02-12-039.

The metal-rich gas produced in the cD galaxy is the most natural origin of the Fe increase as demonstrated by a comparison of clusters with and without a cD galaxy (e.g. Makishima et al. ; references therein; De Grandi and Molendi ). To compare the result obtained with the above idea quantitatively, we presume that the excess of the metal abundances is solely produced by SN Ia in the cD galaxy over the galaxy life-time.

Within the same area, the total blue luminosity of the cD galaxy is 4.1 10^ 10 L _ h^ 2 (Postman and Lauer ). These give an excess ( 1 2 of the total mass) iron mass-to-light ratio around the cD galaxy of 4 10^ 3 h^ 0.5 in the solar units . This is consistent with the value estimated by Renzini et al. ( ), who assumed the standard SN Ia rate in elliptical galaxies and 15 Gyr duration, within an uncertainty of the SN Ia rate (a factor of 4 ). Therefore we conclude that the excess Fe mass in the ICM around the cD galaxy can be supplied by the standard SN Ia frequency over a Hubble time.

sources.summary: The paper describes PGC 015524 as a BCG with multi-component emission lines and extended northeast ionized-gas filaments whose similar distributions suggest a common origin.

quotes.quote: This was the case of the emission lines in the central regions of PGC 015524 (BCG of Abell 0496), PGC 049940 (BCG of Abell 1836), PGC 073000 (BCG of Abell 4059) and PGC 047177 (satellite in Abell 3558).

The ionised-gas of the first component is centrally concentrated in three of the BCGs (PGC 047202, PGC 049940 and PGC 065588) while extended with outward filaments in the remaining two (PGC 015524 and PGC 073000). In particular, the filaments of PGC 015524 are stretched towards the North-East direction with respect to the center and their morphology is the same for all emission lines, even if with different spatial extents. \ and \ follow the ionised-gas distribution, even if they are less spatially extended.

Galaxies with extended gas and filaments PGC 015524 and PGC 073000 show extended emission with filamentary structures. In PGC 015524, the gas stretches in filaments with similar distribution suggesting a common origin, even if the filaments are less extended for \ and .

sources.summary: The paper maps PGC015524 with VEGAS and MUSE, estimating an 89 percent accreted fraction, transition radii inside the MUSE coverage, and a slightly decreasing color profile.

quotes.quote: In this paper we present the new deep images from the VEGAS survey of three massive ( M_ * 10^ 12 M _ ) galaxies from the MUSE Most Massive Galaxies (M3G) project, with distances in the range 151 D 183 Mpc: PGC007748, PGC015524 and PGC049940. The long integration time and the wide field of view of OmegaCam@VST allowed us to map the light and color distributions down to _g 30 mag/arcsec ^2 and out to 2R_e .

The higher accreted mass fraction estimated for PGC007748 and PGC015524 (86 and 89 , respectively), combined with the flat _R profiles suggest that a great majority of the mass has been acquired through major mergers, which have also shaped the shallower metallicity profiles observed at larger radii.

For PGC007748 and PGC015524 instead, both the first ( R_ tr1 ) and the second ( R_ tr2 ) transition radii are within 60 arcsec. The azimuthally averaged (g - ) color profiles show a similar behaviour up to the first transition radius ( R_ tr2 ) for all the three galaxies (bottom panels of Fig. PGC7748 , PGC015524 and PGC049940 ). They all have redder colors in the central regions. A gradient towards bluer colors is observed from the galaxies' centers out to the first transition radius ( R_ tr1 ). For R_ tr1 R R_ tr2 the color profile of PGC007748 remains almost flat, while for PGC015524 it is slightly decreasing.

sources.summary: The paper identifies MCG-02-12-039 as a weak line-emitting BCG with published surface-brightness and HST imaging, and detects a kinematically decoupled core.

quotes.quote: MCG-02-12-039: This BCG is a weak line-emitter. The surface brightness profile was published by Schombert (1986), and HST imaging by Laine et al. \ (2003). A KDC is detected in the radial kinematic profiles.

gmw_00003730.name: PGC 048896
gmw_00003730.overview: PGC 048896, also known as ESO 383-G076, is a highly elliptical brightest cluster galaxy [arXiv:1802.02591, arXiv:2304.11210]. The galaxy hosts one of the most populous massive globular cluster systems known [arXiv:2304.11210], and its internal velocity map exhibits a changing orientation with a central prolate-like rotation [arXiv:1802.02591].

sources.summary: The paper describes PGC 048896 as a BCG whose velocity map changes orientation and whose central prolate-like rotation shifts toward minor-axis rotation at larger radii.

quotes.quote: This is especially noticeable among BCGs, where the velocity maps change orientation (e.g. b) PGC 048896), or there is a loss of coherent motions (e.g. h) PGC 065588).

It can be present across the full observed field-of-view (approximately 2 effective radii), for example in n) PGC007748, h) PGC065588 and ) PGC047590, but most galaxies have it within a specific area, either outside the central region (but within one effective radius, s) PGC099188), or more typically covering the full half-light radius (e.g. k) PGC047752, f) PGC003342, ) PGC007300 or b) PGC048896). In these cases, the rotation at larger radii either disappears (e.g. t) PGC047197) or there is a change in the kinematic position angle and the rotation is consistent with being around the minor axis ( f) PGC003342, ) PGC073000, b) PGC048896). The change in the kinematic position angle is relatively abrupt and occurs over a small radial range. Therefore, such galaxies could even be characterised as having large-scale KDCs, with the central component exhibiting prolate-like rotation.

sources.summary: The paper notes ESO 383-G076 has high ellipticity and a GC density profile complicated by satellites, and identifies it as one of the two most populous massive GC systems in the sample.

quotes.quote: In Figure fig:dist_grid one can easily notice the high ellipticity of ESO 383-G076. Its GC density profile in Figure fig:dense_grid also does not fit a simple power law quite as accurately as most of the other targets. The quality of fit is not due solely to the higher ellipticity of the galaxy, since its distribution is still symmetric, but rather due to the many satellites concentrated on one side of its distribution.

The two most populous and massive GC systems in the high-mass galaxy sample of this study were found to be ESO 383-G076, and ESO 444-G046. Both are at the very top end of the most populous known GCSs, and comparable with NGC 6166, which was found to have N_ GC 39000 2000 within a 250 kpc radius Harris2016 .

gmw_00003731.name: SPT 2147
gmw_00003731.overview: SPT 2147 is a strongly lensed, massive, dusty star-forming galaxy at redshift z = 3.762 [arXiv:2404.01918]. It is classified as a barred spiral galaxy with a rotating disk [arXiv:2109.03450, arXiv:2404.01918], displaying high metallicity [arXiv:2307.10412] and potential active galactic nucleus emission [arXiv:2312.10465].

sources.summary: The paper models SPT2147-50 as a lensed dusty galaxy with an arc and counter-image, a simple Sersic source profile, velocity alignment, and rotating-disk evidence.

quotes.quote: SPT2147-50 exhibits an extended arc in the northeast and a compact counter-image in the southwest (see Sect. sec: lens_modelling ). The northeast arc is brighter in its most eastern part and there is a small gap with no detected continuum at the most northern part of the arc.

The best-fit value of the Sérsic index for the continuum is 1.36 0.05 (see Table tab:source params 2147 ). This index, close to 1, is compatible with the hypothesis that the object is a rotating disk (see also Sect. Sec: kinematics_2147 ). The source plane model (see Fig. fig:cont lens ) shows that part of this disk-like profile is near the inner caustic. Overall, the center of emission regions at the various velocities are well aligned. This provides an additional evidence that the source could a rotating disk.

sources.summary: The overview describes SPT2147 50 as a dusty lensed submillimeter TEMPLATES galaxy with JWST spectroscopy, spatially resolved metallicity, possible AGN emission, and ALMA data.

quotes.quote: For the two TEMPLATES targets that are submillimeter galaxies (SPT0418 47 and SPT2147 50), the NIRSpec, MIRI/MRS, and NIRCam data from TEMPLATES were partially analysed by Birkin.2023, Cathey.2023, Spilker:2023 . H and the [ N ii ] doublet are detected at high S/N in the integrated NIRSpec spectra; the [ N ii ] /H ratio, and thus the metallicity, are spatially resolved for these galaxies. Both sources show apparently near-solar metallicities, and SPT2147 50 in particular displays regions where [ N ii ] /H is greater than unity, which is interpreted as evidence for previously undetected AGN emission.

SPT0418 47 and SPT2147 50 SPT0418-47 and SPT2147-50 We provided reduced HST imaging data in WFC3-IR/F140W of both fields, described in Ma.2015 . ALMA data of both targets are described in Spilker.2016 . SPT0418 47 has continuum data at rest-frame 120 m, 160 m, and 380 m, and SPT2147 50 has data at rest-frame 160 m, 300 m, 380 m, and 450 m. Both also have extensive sub/millimeter spectroscopy from the same datasets.

sources.summary: The paper studies SPT 2147 as a strongly lensed z=3.762 dusty star-forming galaxy with a rotating disk, central bar, spiral arm, high star formation, and massive gas and stellar components.

quotes.quote: We examine the morphological and kinematical properties of SPT 2147, a strongly lensed, massive, dusty, star-forming galaxy at z = 3.762 . Combining data from JWST, HST, and ALMA, we study the galaxy's stellar emission, dust continuum and gas properties. The imaging reveals a central bar structure in the stars and gas embedded within an extended disc with a spiral arm-like feature. The kinematics confirm the presence of the bar and of the regularly rotating disc.

We find evidence from the stellar and gas morphologies that supports a scenario where SPT 2147 is a barred spiral galaxy, featuring at least one spiral arm originating from the edge of the bar. One spiral arm is clearly seen in H , east from the centre of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper observes SPT2147-50 with JWST/NIRSpec as a massive, highly star-forming lensed DSFG and finds especially supersolar metallicity regions amid strong enrichment.

quotes.quote: From TEMPLATES we utilise NIRSpec IFU G395M/F290LP observations of two SPT-selected DSFGs: SPT0418-47 and SPT2147-50. From spectral energy distribution (SED) fitting it is known that both galaxies are highly massive and highly star-forming (Cathey et al. in prep., and see Table tab:results ).

Empirically derived literature optical line calibrations are used to determine that the sources are highly metal rich, with both appearing to display regions of supersolar metallicity, particularly in SPT2147-50. While we cannot rule out shocks or AGN in these regions, we suggest that the two systems have already undergone significant enrichment as a result of their extremely high star-formation rates. Utilising ALMA rest-frame 380 m continuum and [ C i ] ( ^3 P _2 - ^3 P _1 ) line maps we compare metallicity and gas-to-dust ratio variations in the two galaxies, finding the two to be anticorrelated on highly resolved spatial scales, consistent with various literature studies of z 0 galaxies.

gmw_00003732.name: Leon X-1
gmw_00003732.overview: Leon X-1 is a Type-1, unobscured active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z=0.3207 [arXiv:astro-ph/0509053]. It exhibits strong X-ray emission and may host a massive black hole accreting near its Eddington limit [arXiv:astro-ph/0509053].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Leon X-1 as Chandra's brightest first-field X-ray source, a type-1 AGN at z=0.3207 with strong emission and a massive, near-Eddington black hole.

quotes.quote: 's Advanced CCD Imaging Spectrometer (ACIS) detected 15 X-ray sources, the brightest being dubbed ``Leon X-1'' to honor the \ Telescope Scientist, Leon Van Speybroeck. Based upon our analysis of the X-ray data and spectroscopy at the European Southern Observatory (ESO; La Silla, Chile), we find that Leon X-1 is a Type-1 (unobscured) active galactic nucleus (AGN) at a redshift z=0.3207 . Leon X-1 exhibits strong \ emission and a broad-line Balmer decrement that is unusually flat for an AGN. Within the context of the Eigenvector-1 correlation space, these properties suggest that Leon X-1 may be a massive ( 10^ 9 M_ ) black hole, accreting at a rate approaching its Eddington limit.

gmw_00003733.name: SDSS J083909.65 + 072431.5
gmw_00003733.overview: SDSS J083909.65 + 072431.5 is a low-mass active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2103.11935]. The galaxy exhibits variability, showing a decrease in its broad-line flux alongside a 0.3 magnitude variation in its W2 band light curve [arXiv:2103.11935].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J083909.65 + 072431.5 as a MagE low-mass AGN whose broad-line flux decreased while its W2 light curve varied by 0.3 mag.

quotes.quote: The six largely variable (low-mass) AGNs are the following: SDSS J083909.65 + 072431.5 (hereafter J0839 + 0724), SDSS J111349.83 + 000733.9 (hereafter J1113 + 0007), SDSS J125710.76 + 272417.6 (hereafter J1257 + 2724), SDSS J134245.69 + 243524.0 (hereafter J1342 + 2435), SDSS J141249.63 030720.9 (hereafter J1412 0307), and SDSS J144242.63 + 011911.2 (hereafter J1442 + 0119). They are a part of sources observed by MagE in 2017 March and July, with the initial goal of identifying IMBHs in broad-line AGNs (namely low-mass AGNs).
J0839 + 0724 decreased in broad- \ flux between the two epochs of the SDSS and MagE spectra, while the other five sources increased.

Third, according to the peak-to-valley changes measured from the binned light curves (corresponding to R_ max in Table table:lightcurve ), there are considerable optical or MIR continuum variabilities as follows (only listing those changing by >0.2 mag peak to valley): J0839 + 0724, 0.3 mag in the W2 band; J1113 + 0007, 0.4 mag in the W2 band; and the most dramatic one, J1412 0307, which is in fact the delimiter of this MagE sample (i.e., its broad- \ R_ max 1.3 ), 0.45 mag in the ASAS-SN V band, and 0.61 mag in the W2 band (see also subsubsec:MagEsample ).

gmw_00003734.name: SDSS J111349.83 + 000733.9
gmw_00003734.overview: SDSS J111349.83 + 000733.9 is a low-mass active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2103.11935]. The galaxy exhibits an increasing broad-line flux trend [arXiv:2103.11935] alongside a 0.4 magnitude continuum variability in the W2 band [arXiv:2103.11935].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J111349.83 + 000733.9 as a MagE low-mass AGN with increasing broad-line flux and notable W2-band continuum variability.

quotes.quote: The six largely variable (low-mass) AGNs are the following: SDSS J083909.65 + 072431.5 (hereafter J0839 + 0724), SDSS J111349.83 + 000733.9 (hereafter J1113 + 0007), SDSS J125710.76 + 272417.6 (hereafter J1257 + 2724), SDSS J134245.69 + 243524.0 (hereafter J1342 + 2435), SDSS J141249.63 030720.9 (hereafter J1412 0307), and SDSS J144242.63 + 011911.2 (hereafter J1442 + 0119). They are a part of sources observed by MagE in 2017 March and July, with the initial goal of identifying IMBHs in broad-line AGNs (namely low-mass AGNs).

Fortunately for J1113 + 0007, there are two SDSS spectra taken with a time separation of about one year; the later one gives a larger broad- \ flux larger by 1.6 times than the former one (albeit with a 2 significance only, according to the flux difference and errors listed in Table table:lineinfo ); this increasing trend is consistent with the MagE observation taken six additional years later.

Third, according to the peak-to-valley changes measured from the binned light curves (corresponding to R_ max in Table table:lightcurve ), there are considerable optical or MIR continuum variabilities as follows (only listing those changing by >0.2 mag peak to valley): J0839 + 0724, 0.3 mag in the W2 band; J1113 + 0007, 0.4 mag in the W2 band; and the most dramatic one, J1412 0307, which is in fact the delimiter of this MagE sample (i.e., its broad- \ R_ max 1.3 ), 0.45 mag in the ASAS-SN V band, and 0.61 mag in the W2 band (see also subsubsec:MagEsample ).

gmw_00003735.name: SDSS J125710.76 + 272417.6
gmw_00003735.overview: SDSS J125710.76 + 272417.6 is a low-mass active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2103.11935]. The galaxy exhibits large X-ray variability, with peak-to-valley flux changes of more than 20 times, alongside stable Balmer decrements [arXiv:2103.11935].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J125710.76 + 272417.6 as a MagE low-mass AGN showing large X-ray variability and stable Balmer decrements across four spectra.

quotes.quote: The six largely variable (low-mass) AGNs are the following: SDSS J083909.65 + 072431.5 (hereafter J0839 + 0724), SDSS J111349.83 + 000733.9 (hereafter J1113 + 0007), SDSS J125710.76 + 272417.6 (hereafter J1257 + 2724), SDSS J134245.69 + 243524.0 (hereafter J1342 + 2435), SDSS J141249.63 030720.9 (hereafter J1412 0307), and SDSS J144242.63 + 011911.2 (hereafter J1442 + 0119). They are a part of sources observed by MagE in 2017 March and July, with the initial goal of identifying IMBHs in broad-line AGNs (namely low-mass AGNs).
