Lastly, among the three sources having two- or multiepoch X-ray observations, the two-epoch fluxes of J1442 + 0119 change by 2.74 times, and J1257 + 2724 and J1412 0307 exhibit peak-to-valley changes by >20 times.
For J1257 + 2724, the \ ratios of the four spectra (ordered by increasing observing Date) are 3.58 0.63, 3.52 0.18, 3.71 0.17, and 3.79 0.34, respectively.

gmw_00003736.name: SDSS J134245.69 + 243524.0
gmw_00003736.overview: SDSS J134245.69 + 243524.0 is a low-mass active galactic nucleus classified as a type 1.2 source [arXiv:2103.11935]. The galaxy is strongly variable, showing a measured broad-line flux change without undergoing a type transition [arXiv:2103.11935].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J134245.69 + 243524.0 as a MagE low-mass AGN kept as strongly variable, with a measured broad-line flux change and no significant type transition.

quotes.quote: The six largely variable (low-mass) AGNs are the following: SDSS J083909.65 + 072431.5 (hereafter J0839 + 0724), SDSS J111349.83 + 000733.9 (hereafter J1113 + 0007), SDSS J125710.76 + 272417.6 (hereafter J1257 + 2724), SDSS J134245.69 + 243524.0 (hereafter J1342 + 2435), SDSS J141249.63 030720.9 (hereafter J1412 0307), and SDSS J144242.63 + 011911.2 (hereafter J1442 + 0119). They are a part of sources observed by MagE in 2017 March and July, with the initial goal of identifying IMBHs in broad-line AGNs (namely low-mass AGNs).
This number is higher than the broad- \ flux changes of three sources in our MagE sample, which are as follows: J0839 + 0724 (1.30), J1342 + 2435 (1.41), and J1412 0307 (1.32).
J0839 + 0724 (type 1.9), J1113 + 0007 (type 1.9 or 2), J1342 + 2435 (type 1.2) and J1442 + 0119 (type 1.5) do not show significant type transitions.

gmw_00003737.name: SDSS J141249.63 030720.9
gmw_00003737.overview: SDSS J141249.63 030720.9 is a low-mass active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2103.11935]. It is classified as a changing-look active galactic nucleus that exhibits spectral-type transitions alongside optical, mid-infrared, and soft X-ray variability [arXiv:2103.11935].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J141249.63 030720.9 as a MagE low-mass AGN and bona fide changing-look AGN with strong X-ray, optical, MIR, and spectral-type variability.

quotes.quote: The six largely variable (low-mass) AGNs are the following: SDSS J083909.65 + 072431.5 (hereafter J0839 + 0724), SDSS J111349.83 + 000733.9 (hereafter J1113 + 0007), SDSS J125710.76 + 272417.6 (hereafter J1257 + 2724), SDSS J134245.69 + 243524.0 (hereafter J1342 + 2435), SDSS J141249.63 030720.9 (hereafter J1412 0307), and SDSS J144242.63 + 011911.2 (hereafter J1442 + 0119). They are a part of sources observed by MagE in 2017 March and July, with the initial goal of identifying IMBHs in broad-line AGNs (namely low-mass AGNs).

Although belonging to the two sources least variable in broad , J1412 0307 has R_ max > 10 in soft X-ray, 1.75 (0.61mag) in the WISE W2-band light curve (binned every half years), and 1.51 (0.45 mag) even in the optical V band light curve (binned every 90 days) without removing the dominant starlight component.

J1412 0307 transited from type 1.9 to 1.5 during a timescale of 15 years, and then returned to type 1.9 recently. In its type 1.5 state, the broad- \ component is measured with S/N > 10 . As described above, J1412 0307 also showed significant continuum variabilities in the soft X-ray, optical, and MIR; in particular, the variability measures of its WISE W2 light curve are similar to those of typical CL AGNs see, e.g., 2017ApJ...846L...7S . Hence we regard it as a bona fide CL AGN.

gmw_00003738.name: SDSS J144242.63 + 011911.2
gmw_00003738.overview: SDSS J144242.63 + 011911.2 is a low-mass active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2103.11935]. Multi-epoch observations of the source reveal that its X-ray flux changes by a factor of 2.74, while its Balmer decrements remain stable [arXiv:2103.11935].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J144242.63 + 011911.2 as a MagE low-mass AGN with X-ray flux changes and stable Balmer decrements across multiepoch spectra.

quotes.quote: The six largely variable (low-mass) AGNs are the following: SDSS J083909.65 + 072431.5 (hereafter J0839 + 0724), SDSS J111349.83 + 000733.9 (hereafter J1113 + 0007), SDSS J125710.76 + 272417.6 (hereafter J1257 + 2724), SDSS J134245.69 + 243524.0 (hereafter J1342 + 2435), SDSS J141249.63 030720.9 (hereafter J1412 0307), and SDSS J144242.63 + 011911.2 (hereafter J1442 + 0119). They are a part of sources observed by MagE in 2017 March and July, with the initial goal of identifying IMBHs in broad-line AGNs (namely low-mass AGNs).

Lastly, among the three sources having two- or multiepoch X-ray observations, the two-epoch fluxes of J1442 + 0119 change by 2.74 times, and J1257 + 2724 and J1412 0307 exhibit peak-to-valley changes by >20 times.
For J1442 + 0119, the ratios are 6.23 1.79 , 5.88 0.44, and 6.78 1.01. All the three sources have insignificant variation in , well within 1 uncertainty, during those years.

gmw_00003739.name: 3C219
gmw_00003739.overview: 3C 219 is a Fanaroff-Riley class II radio galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0207671, arXiv:astro-ph/0302509] identified as an elliptical cD galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/9907126, arXiv:1710.02194] that shows weak signs of interaction with its numerous companion galaxies [arXiv:1710.02194]. The galaxy hosts a hidden active nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/9907126, arXiv:1508.03287] and features radio lobes that emit diffuse X-rays [arXiv:astro-ph/0207671, arXiv:astro-ph/0302509].

sources.summary: The review describes 3C 219 as a nearby powerful FRII radio galaxy whose Chandra data reveal non-thermal IC/CMB emission from radio lobes and energetically dominant particles.

quotes.quote: 3C 219 is a nearby (z=0.1744) powerful FRII radio galaxy extending for 180 arcsec corresponding to a projected total size of 690 kpc. The Chandra (17 ksec) 0.3--8 keV image is shown in Fig.9a superimposed on the radio contours from a deep 1.4 GHz VLA observation. Thanks to the arcsec resolution, it was possible to disentangle the nuclear emission (which affects only the innermost 3--5 arcsec) from the other components, and to identify the bright clump visible on the north--west with a background cluster at z=0.39. Diffuse emission coincident with the radio lobes also showing a brightness increment in the innermost part of the northern lobe is clearly detected.

The combined imaging and spectral analysis of this emission ( 400 net counts) point to a non--thermal, IC/CMB origin of the large scale diffuse emission. Following the procedure described in Section 3.5.2, the comparison between radio and X--ray IC emission yields a precise measurement of the magnetic field intensity (averaged over the total radio volume) which results a factor of 2.9 times lower than the equipartition value (assuming _ low =50 in Eq.( beq2 ). Under these conditions, the ratio between particle and field energy densities (Sect.3.5.2) is 64 . The derived energetics of the lobes of 3C 219 is 10^ 60 erg which results a factor 7 larger than that estimated with classical equipartition formulae (Sect.3.5.1).

sources.summary: The paper presents Chandra observations of 3C 219, interpreting its diffuse lobe X-rays as mostly inverse-Compton emission and inferring low magnetic fields and nuclear-photon contributions.

quotes.quote: In this paper we report on the Chandra observation of the nearby, powerful, double--lobed FRII radio galaxy 3C 219. The radio galaxy 3C 219 at z =0.1744 is a prototype of the FR--II class of powerful radio sources. Its projected angular size is about 180 arcsec (Clarke et al. 1992) corresponding to a projected liner size of about 680 kpc.

The relatively short (16.8 ksec) Chandra observation of the radio galaxy 3C 219 has successfully detected diffuse X--ray emission from the radio lobes and X--rays associated with the main radio jet. The morphology, extension and spectrum of the diffuse emission strongly support a non--thermal origin, most likely IC scattering of CMB photons. In addition, the net brightness increment observed in the innermost 70 kpc of the northern radio lobe (the far lobe) suggests an additional contribution from IC scattering of nuclear photons for a reasonable far--IR to optical isotropic luminosity of the hidden quasar of 6 10^ 45 erg s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper presents 3C 219 as a powerful FRII radio galaxy whose extended X-rays are best interpreted as inverse Compton emission from lobe electrons scattering hidden-quasar infrared photons.

quotes.quote: The detection of extended X-ray emission from 3C 219, likely due to Inverse Compton scattering of the IR photons from a hidden quasar with the relativistic electrons in the lobes, provides the first observational evidence supporting this conjecture. However, evidence that this might indeed be the case has been recently gathered by high angular resolution X-ray observations of 3C 219 with ROSAT-HRI (Brunetti et al., 1999; hereafter Paper II). 3C 219 is a well known powerful double-lobed FRII radio galaxy, identified with a cD galaxy at a redshift z = 0.174 (Clarke et al.

The X-ray image of 3C 219 is dominated by a point-like source coincident, within the positional errors, with the radio core (nucleus) of the galaxy, but a diffuse component extending up to the hundred kpc scale is also present. The nature of the emission of these components may soon be clarified by upcoming observations of 3C 219 with Chandra . The detection of extended X-ray emission from the FRII radio galaxy 3C 219 can be best interpreted as IC scattering of the IR radiation emitted by a quasar, hidden in the nucleus of the galaxy, with the relativistic electrons residing in the lobes.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes only the western jet of 3C 219 because the eastern lobe is deformed, estimating the central black-hole mass, lobe size, kinetic power, and age.

quotes.quote: In the case of 3C 219, we only analyze the jet on the western side, since the eastern lobe shows severe deformation (see Fig. radio ). We could not determine A_ h on the eastern side from its morphology. The central SMBH mass for 3C 219 is estimated by MCF04 as 6.3 10^ 8 M_ . r_ h and A_ h of the western lobe are measured as 210 kpc and 5000 kpc ^2 . From these values, the kinetic power and age are obtained as L_ j = (0.26 - 4.3) 10^ 47 ergs s ^ 1 and t_ age = 37 - 150 Myr, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper reports many companion galaxies, weak interaction signs, and a host best fit by a single bulge with possible extra extended component.

quotes.quote: 3C 219 (Fig. 11.3) --- \ \ There are more than 20 companion galaxies in the image. There seems to be a weak sign of interaction with companion galaxies. The host is well fit with a single bulge but an additional extended component slightly helps. The bulge magnitude would be 0.3 mag fainter if we use two components model. McLure et al. (1999) and Donzelli et al. (2007) classified the host galaxy as an elliptical galaxy.

sources.summary: This broad-line radio galaxy appears mostly point-like in ROSAT data, but shows excess counts possibly from a poor cluster or inverse-Compton emission from radio lobes.

quotes.quote: 3C 219 This broad-line radio galaxy (Laing \ 1994) shows largely point-like emission in the short archival PSPC observation, but there is some evidence of excess counts in the radial profile on scales of 40--100 arcsec. This may be related to the poor optical cluster known to be associated with 3C 219 (Macdonald, Kenderdine Neville 1968), though Brunetti \ (1998), using HRI observations not yet in the public archive, suggest instead that the extension is inverse-Compton emission associated with the radio lobes, while not ruling out a thermal origin for some or all of it. A point-source fit to the PSPC radial profile has ^2 = 17.3 with 9 degrees of freedom, a barely acceptable fit, while the addition of the model (Table profile ) improves the fit significantly. The point-like component dominates, contributing 75 7 per cent of the counts in the 2-arcmin source circle.

sources.summary: The paper models 3C 219 as one of four FR II radio sources with powerful jets, large cocoon energies, and substantial invisible energy components beyond minimum-energy estimates.

quotes.quote: We investigate the total kinetic powers ( L_ j ) and ages ( t_ age ) of powerful jets of four FR II radio sources (Cygnus A, 3C 223, 3C 284, and 3C 219) by the detail comparison of the dynamical model of expanding cocoons with observed ones. It is found that these sources have quite large kinetic powers with the ratio of L_ j to the Eddington luminosity ( L_ Edd ) resides in 0.02 <L_ j /L_ Edd <10 . Reflecting the large kinetic powers, we also find that the total energy stored in the cocoon ( E_ ) exceed the energy derived from the minimum energy condition ( E_ min ): 2< E_ /E_ min <160 . This implies that a large amount of kinetic power is carried by invisible components such as thermal leptons (electron and positron) and/or protons.

sources.summary: The paper describes 3C 219 as a HEG FRII source with strong AGN-emission evidence and MIR/FIR dust components.

quotes.quote: 3C 219. Like 3C192, this is a HEG, with XUV=1.7 . The radio source is of FR type II. The evidence for AGN emission is strong (see Sect. discussion ). MIR and FIR data are available, which can be fitted by a mix of dust with temperatures of 80 K ( M_d = 1.0 10^5 M_ ) and 310 K ( M_d = 1.5 10^2 M_ ).

sources.summary: The paper applies a total-cocoon-pressure method to 3C 219 as an FRII radio galaxy, finding positron-free proton-electron plasma implausible and requiring electron-positron mixtures independent of jet power and cocoon size.

quotes.quote: We apply this method to four FRII radio galaxies (Cygnus A, 3C 219, 3C 223 and 3C 284), for which the total cocoon pressures have been already evaluated. We find that the positron-free plasma comprising of protons and electrons is ruled out, when we consider plausible particle distribution functions. In other words, the mixture of positrons is required for all four FRII radio galaxies; the number density ratio of electrons/positrons to protons is larger than two. Thus, we find that the plasma composition is independent of the jet power and the size of cocoons.

sources.summary: The paper finds that both lobes of 3C219 produce two radio-continuum redshift probability peaks, with a high-redshift solution removed by imposing a lobe expansion velocity prior.

quotes.quote: However, the probability density function for both lobes of 3C219 have two peaks, one at z 0.2 and another at z 1.5 . The high-redshift peak is associated with lobe expansion rates at close to the speed of light and dissapears in the south lobe if the prior probability density function is modified to restrict velocities to be less than 0.5c . That is, our technique can still find the correct radio continuum redshift for this source.

gmw_00003740.name: host galaxy of AT 2020vdq
aliases_external: HG J1008 / VT J100853.44+424300.22 host galaxy
gmw_00003740.overview: The host galaxy of AT 2020vdq is a dwarf galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.045 [arXiv:2310.03782]. It is classified as a green-valley, post-starburst E+A galaxy [arXiv:2310.03795] with a low star formation rate [arXiv:2310.03795] and a low-mass central black hole [arXiv:2310.03782].

sources.summary: The paper identifies HG J1008 as the z=0.045 host galaxy of VT J1008, with low star formation, green-valley colors, and E+A spectral evidence.

quotes.quote: Host galaxy properties A PanSTARRS optical image of the host galaxy of VT J1008, HG J1008, is shown in the panel a of Figure fig:J1008_sum . It is at a redshift z=0.045 , or a luminosity distance d_L = 205 Mpc.

The host galaxy has a stellar mass M_*/M_ = 9.04^ +0.22 _ 0.16 , and the 3 upper limit on the star formation rate is SFR /(M_ yr ^ 1 ) < 1.47 . This host is consistent with the green valley. It is also consistent with being an E+A galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes the AT 2020vdq host as a dwarf galaxy at z = 0.045 with a nuclear transient, measured stellar velocity dispersion, and inferred low-mass black hole.

quotes.quote: Basic properties of the host galaxy of AT 2020vdq. We begin by describing the multiwavelength properties of the first flare from AT 2020vdq. These properties are described in detail by . AT 2020vdq was first detected as an optical transient by the Zwicky Transient Facility (ZTF) on Oct. 4 2020 (MJD 59126) using the selection described in implemented using the AMPEL filter 2019A&A...631A.147N . It was located at the nucleus of a dwarf galaxy at z = 0.045 with M_*/M_ = 9.25 . The stellar velocity dispersion of the nucleus host galaxy was measured to be _* = 44 3 km s ^ 1 from a high resolution spectrum obtained with ESI on the Keck II telescope Yao_tdes . This dispersion corresponds to a black hole mass of M_ BH, _* /M_ = 5.59 0.37 using the M_ BH _* relation from .

gmw_00003741.name: 1ES 1959+650
gmw_00003741.overview: 1ES 1959+650 is a high-frequency peaked BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1307.6772]. It is detected at TeV energies by VERITAS, exhibiting a low very-high-energy flux state that can be described by a leptonic synchrotron self-Compton model with an external Compton component [arXiv:1307.6772].

sources.summary: The paper reports multiwavelength monitoring of the BL Lac 1ES 1959+650 in a low VHE state, detects TeV emission, constructs SEDs, and models the source with SSC plus external Compton components.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: RA =19^h 59^m 59^s 20^s_ stat and Dec =65^ 9^ .25 0^ .34_ stat (J2000 coordinates)

quotes.quote: We report on the VERITAS observations of the high-frequency peaked BL Lac object 1ES 1959+650 in the period 2007-2011 . This source is detected at TeV energies by VERITAS at 16.4 standard deviation ( ) significance in 7.6 hours of observation in a low flux state. A multiwavelength spectral energy distribution (SED) is constructed from contemporaneous data from VERITAS, Fermi LAT, RXTE PCA, and Swift UVOT. Swift XRT data is not included in the SED due to a lack of simultaneous observations with VERITAS.

1ES 1959+650 is detected at 16.4 with an average flux of (3.97 0.37) 10^ 12 photons cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 (or (7.54 0.7) 10^ 12 ergs cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 , equivalent to 23 Crab Nebula flux) above 1 TeV. This corresponds to 268 excess rays at the source location at RA =19^h 59^m 59^s 20^s_ stat and Dec =65^ 9^ .25 0^ .34_ stat (J2000 coordinates). The observed VERITAS signal is consistent with a point source, and the source is designated VER J1959+651.

These observations show that 1ES 1959+650 can be reasonably well-described by a leptonic quasi-equilibrium SSC + EC model in a low VHE flux state, although it is clear that this model does not account for the flux observed at a few hundred MeV to 1 GeV and as such, does not provide an accurate representation of the inverse-Compton peak.

gmw_00003742.name: Lindsay-Shapley ring
gmw_00003742.overview: The Lindsay-Shapley ring is a double-ring collisional galaxy [arXiv:2402.13230] that was formed by an off-center satellite collision approximately 180 million years ago [arXiv:2402.13230, arXiv:astro-ph/0006084]. Its ring structure is dominated by atomic gas [arXiv:1003.4691] and exhibits active star formation [arXiv:2402.13230].

sources.summary: The paper describes the Lindsay-Shapley ring as an evolved ring galaxy with solar abundances, weak CO molecular arcs, atomic-dominated gas, broad profiles, and unexpectedly high star-formation efficiency.

quotes.quote: However L-S 's ring possesses solar abundances ( fma ), which together with its large angular size, made it an ideal target for the SEST ( higdon10 ). We observed 16 positions in L-S in ^ 12 CO(J=1-0) and ^ 12 CO(J=2-1) transitions: 14 on the ring and one each centered on the nucleus and enclosed disk. Figure 2 shows CO detections in 9/14 ring positions, defining two molecular arcs in the ring's north and southwest. The latter coincides with the galaxy's peak _ HI and _ H . L-S 's ring is dominated by atomic rather than molecular gas.

Detecting molecular gas in L-S 's ring allows the first estimate of SFE in a ring galaxy. We find SFE = -0.7 0.1 L _ /M _ , i.e., similar to an Irr and an order of magnitude higher than the (presumably Sa) progenitor. This result depends, of course, on our ability to reliably measure H _2 in the ring using ^ 12 CO emission.

sources.summary: The paper presents MUSE observations of AM 0644-741, a double-ring collisional galaxy, measuring H II-region abundances and linking its ring structure to a satellite collision.

quotes.quote: We present the analysis of archival Very Large Telescope (VLT) Multi-Unit Spectroscopic Explorer (MUSE) observations of 179 \ regions in the star-forming double-ring collisional galaxy AM 0644-741 at 98.6 Mpc. We determined ionic abundances of He, N, O and Fe using the direct method for the brightest \ region (ID 39); we report ( N O ) =-1.3 0.2 and 12+ ( O H ) =8.9 0.2 . We also find the so-called `blue-bump', broad , in the spectrum of this knot of massive star-formation; its luminosity being consistent with the presence of 430 Wolf-Rayet (WR) stars of the Nitrogen late-type. We determined the O abundances for 137 \ regions using the strong-line method; we report a median value of 12+ ( O H ) =8.5 0.8 .

Here we study the double-ringed galaxy AM 0644-741, a.k.a the Lindsay-Shapley (L-S) ring, located at a distance of 98.6 Mpc, assuming a Hubble constant H _ 0 =67.8 , _ m =0.308 , _ =0.692 (NASA NED The NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database (NED) is funded by the National Aeronautics and Space Administration and operated by the California Institute of Technology. ). AM 0644-741 is characterized by its striking double-ring of \ regions and star cluster complexes noticeable in optical bands, particularly in \ (see Fig. fig:muse_image ), but also in the near and far-UV, tracing recent bursts of star formation. The ring, presumably driven by a radially expanding density wave triggered by the collision of a satellite object nearly perpendicular to the plane of its disk and close to its centre Lynds1976,Higdon2011 , has an angular diameter of 97 arcsec, the second largest (after AM 1724-622 with 125 arcsec) reported in the Southern Catalogue of ring galaxies by Madore2009 , corresponding to a physical size of 46.4 kpc, using the mentioned distance. A slightly lower value for the major-axis is reported by see Tab. tab:parameters Higdon2011 , but differences are minor.

sources.summary: The paper identifies ESO 034--IG11 as the Lindsay-Shapley Ring, an off-center collision ring with an optical bridge, component nucleus coordinates, and an interaction age near 1.8e8 years.

quotes.quote: ESO 034--IG11 Also known as the Lindsay--Shapley Ring (Lindsay Shapley 1960). The asymmetric ring suggests an off--centre collision. It has been studied by Higdon Wallin (1997). They found an optical bridge from the ring to the `intruder' galaxy at RA = 06 ^h 43 ^m 26 ^s , Dec. = --74 ^ 15 ^ 29 ^ (J2000). The optical nucleus of the perturbed galaxy is at RA = 06 ^h 43 ^m 06.7 ^s , Dec.= --74 ^ 14 ^ 16 ^ (J2000).
From the expansion rate of the ring in ESO34--IG11 the interaction occurred about 1.8 10^8 yrs ago ( H_ 0 =75 km s^ 1 Mpc^ 1 ; Higdon Wallin 1997).

gmw_00003743.name: SGAS J152745.1+065219
gmw_00003743.overview: SGAS J152745.1+065219 is a gravitationally lensed Lyman-break galaxy located at a redshift of approximately z=2.76 [arXiv:1110.2833, arXiv:1003.0030]. It is characterized as an actively star-forming system with a young stellar population, low dust content, and broadly distributed stellar light across various ages and metallicities [arXiv:1110.2833, arXiv:1905.04314].

sources.summary: The paper treats SGAS J152745.1+065219 as a bright strongly lensed high-redshift galaxy whose SED and spectra indicate a young, lightly reddened, actively star-forming stellar population.

quotes.quote: We present a comprehensive analysis of the rest-frame UV to near-IR spectral energy distributions and rest-frame optical spectra of four of the brightest gravitationally lensed galaxies in the literature: RCSGA 032727-132609 at z=1.70 , MS1512-cB58 at z=2.73 , SGAS J152745.1+065219 at z=2.76 and SGAS J122651.3+215220 at z=2.92 . This includes new Spitzer imaging for RCSGA0327 as well as new spectra, near-IR imaging and Spitzer imaging for SGAS1527 and SGAS1226. Lensing magnifications of 3-4 magnitudes allow a detailed study of the stellar populations and physical conditions.

We compare star formation rates as measured from the SED fit, the H and [ O II ] 3727 emission lines, and the UV+IR bolometric luminosity where 24 m photometry is available. The SFR estimate from the SED fit is consistently higher than the other indicators, which suggests that the Calzetti dust extinction law used in the SED fitting is too flat for young star-forming galaxies at z 2 . Our analysis finds similar stellar population parameters for all four lensed galaxies: stellar masses 3-7 10^9 M _ , young ages 100 Myr, little dust content E(B-V) =0.10-0.25, and star formation rates around 20-100 M _ yr ^ 1 . Compared to typical values for the galaxy population at z 2 , this suggests we are looking at newly formed, starbursting systems that have only recently started the build-up of stellar mass.

sources.summary: S1527+0652 has broadly distributed stellar light across age and metallicity, lacks young-population P-Cygni signatures, and is modeled as a mixed-age 21 Myr stellar spectrum.

quotes.quote: All of the light from RCS Knot E comes from a very young, moderate metallicity stellar population (left panel); while the stellar light from S1527+0652 is broadly distributed across age and metallicity (right panel). We determined intrinsic stellar parameters from the fitted coefficients in eq:flux . The derived parameters are a weighted average of the total light at 1270 \ attributed to the individual stellar models ( X_i ).

The exceptions are the oldest stellar populations which do not show P-Cygni profiles in either the or the \ ionization state (e.g. S1527+0652). Weak and a non-detection of \ indicates that there is not currently a young (<8 Myr) stellar population in these older galaxies (see the discussion in cont ).

Alternatively, S1527+0652 has a mixed age stellar spectra with a light-weighted age of 21 3 Myr. Young stellar populations (<5 Myr) within S1225+2152 contribute 19 of the FUV light, while older stellar populations supply 81 of the FUV light (where the dominate ones are f_ 10 and f_ 40 with 31 and 44 , respectively). The age distribution of burst populations is relatively simple and clustered, but the light from mixed age populations is broadly distributed over many stellar ages.

sources.summary: The paper presents SGAS J152745.1+065219 as a z=2.7593 lensed Lyman-break galaxy with confirmed spectral features, limited magnification, and properties resembling an average LBG.

quotes.quote: The second, SGAS J152745.1+065219, is an LBG at z=2.7593 lensed by the foreground SDSS J1527+0652 at z=0.39 , with (g,,z) =( 20.90,20.52,20.58 ) and _ g,,z =( 25.15,24.52,24.12 ).

Moderate resolution spectroscopy confirms the redshifts suggested by photometric breaks, and shows both absorption and emission features typical of LBGs. Lens mass models derived from combined imaging and spectroscopy reveal that SGAS J122651.3+215220 is a highly magnified source ( M 40 ), while SGAS J152745.1+065219 is magnified by no more than M 15 . Compared to LBG survey results steidel03 , the luminosities and lensing-corrected magnitudes suggest that SGAS J122651.3+215220 is among the faintest 20 of LBGs in that sample. SGAS J152745.1+065219, on the other hand, appears to be more representative of the average LBG, similar to the ``Cosmic Eye''.

sources.summary: The paper flags CSWA 39 for blended absorption and uncertain H I covering, while also finding stronger Al III covering and adopting its systemic redshift from C III.

quotes.quote: 1402 in CSWA 128 and 1260 in CSWA 39 are blended with strong features arising from lower redshift systems (Figure fig:intervening ), and we therefore do not use these in subsequent analysis.

For CSWA 39 the uncertainty is large ( f_c = 0.71^ +0.29 _ 0.32 for ); adopting f_c 0.4 as for the low ions would increase N_ tot ( H i ) by 0.35 dex, within the uncertainty quoted in Table tab:transitions .
CSWA 39 We adopt the redshift of CSWA 39 measured from \ 1907,9 due to its relatively high SNR.

gmw_00003744.name: SGAS J122651.3+215220
gmw_00003744.overview: SGAS J122651.3+215220 is a strongly lensed Lyman-break galaxy at a redshift of approximately 2.92 [arXiv:1710.06857, arXiv:1003.0030]. It is a young, actively star-forming system with low dust attenuation [arXiv:1110.2833, arXiv:2312.10465] that features high-velocity galactic outflows [arXiv:1710.06857] and an extended circumgalactic halo [arXiv:2206.02949].

sources.summary: The paper focuses on this z=2.926 lensed galaxy, using high-SNR rest-UV absorption to study transitional outflow gas with high column density, high velocity, and two profile regimes.

quotes.quote: In a future paper, we will explore the outflow properties of the entire \ spectral data set, but here we focus on a single galaxy, SGAS J122651.3+215220 hereafter J1226+2152; koester , because the high signal-to-noise ratio ( 10 at 1032 ) allows us to clearly define and characterize the line profile. J1226+2152 has a redshift of 2.926 0.0002 , as measured from the [ C iii ] 1907 \ and C iii ] 1909 \ nebular emission doublet rigbya . The total integration time for J1226+2152 was 12.42 hr, spread over multiple observing runs, with a final spectral resolution, as measured from night sky emission lines, of R=4010 170 ( 75 3 ).

We then analyzed the interstellar absorption features in the spectrum of SGAS J122651.3+215220 (J1226+2152), a galaxy at a redshift of 2.926 0.0002 that has the highest signal-to-noise ratio near the 1032 \ interstellar absorption line. We used the line, which probes gas transitioning between warm (10 ^4 K) and hot (>10 ^7 K) temperatures, to study a seldom-probed phase of galactic outflows. The has an observed column density of 10 ^ 15.35 0.05 cm ^ 2 (although it is likely saturated at the bluest velocities) and a maximum outflow velocity near -530 . The maximum velocity is consistent with saturated low-ionization tracers like 1335 \ and 1260 \ ( tab:lines ). We found that the line profile has two regimes: a low-velocity portion that closely resembles weaker low-ionization lines, and a high-velocity portion that resembles the strong saturated lines.

sources.summary: The overview identifies SGAS1226 + 21 as a low-attenuation lensed TEMPLATES galaxy with HST and Spitzer products, lens models, and JWST diagnostics for star-formation studies.

quotes.quote: This paper gives an overview of TEMPLATES, a JWST Early Release Science program that targeted four extremely bright, gravitationally lensed galaxies: two extremely dusty, two with low attenuation, as templates for galaxy evolution studies with JWST. TEMPLATES obtains a common set of spectral diagnostics for these 1.3 z 4.2 galaxies, in particular H , Paschen , and the rest-frame optical and near-infrared continua. In addition, two of the four targets have JWST coverage of [ O III ] 5007 and H ; the other two targets have have JWST coverage of PAH 3.3 m and complementary ALMA data covering the [ C II ] 158 micron emission line. The science goals of TEMPLATES are to demonstrate attenuation-robust diagnostics of star formation, map the distribution of star formation, compare the young and old stellar populations, and measure the physical conditions of star formation and their spatial variation across the galaxies.

SGAS J1226 + 2152 SGAS J1226+2152 We delivered reduced HST imaging data in ACS/F606W, F814W; and WFC3-IR/F110W, F140W and F160W. The details of the observation and data reduction are given in Sharon.2022 , and a map of the HST mosaic of the three main cluster cores that make up the foreground lens is given in Figure 1 of that paper. The Channel 1 and Channel 2 Spitzer data reduction process is described in Florian.2021 . We provided two versions of strong lens model outputs for this cluster.

sources.summary: The paper treats SGAS J122651.3+215220 as a bright strongly lensed high-redshift galaxy whose SED and spectra indicate a young, lightly reddened, actively star-forming stellar population.

quotes.quote: We present a comprehensive analysis of the rest-frame UV to near-IR spectral energy distributions and rest-frame optical spectra of four of the brightest gravitationally lensed galaxies in the literature: RCSGA 032727-132609 at z=1.70 , MS1512-cB58 at z=2.73 , SGAS J152745.1+065219 at z=2.76 and SGAS J122651.3+215220 at z=2.92 . This includes new Spitzer imaging for RCSGA0327 as well as new spectra, near-IR imaging and Spitzer imaging for SGAS1527 and SGAS1226. Lensing magnifications of 3-4 magnitudes allow a detailed study of the stellar populations and physical conditions.

We compare star formation rates as measured from the SED fit, the H and [ O II ] 3727 emission lines, and the UV+IR bolometric luminosity where 24 m photometry is available. The SFR estimate from the SED fit is consistently higher than the other indicators, which suggests that the Calzetti dust extinction law used in the SED fitting is too flat for young star-forming galaxies at z 2 . Our analysis finds similar stellar population parameters for all four lensed galaxies: stellar masses 3-7 10^9 M _ , young ages 100 Myr, little dust content E(B-V) =0.10-0.25, and star formation rates around 20-100 M _ yr ^ 1 . Compared to typical values for the galaxy population at z 2 , this suggests we are looking at newly formed, starbursting systems that have only recently started the build-up of stellar mass.

sources.summary: S1226+2152 is described as an older stellar population with weak P-Cygni structure, small redshifted emission, declining ionizing continuum, and a relatively soft spectrum.

quotes.quote: Finally, S1226+2152 has an even older inferred light-weighted age; the P-Cygni feature has nearly disappeared and has been replaced by a flat feature with narrow, interstellar absorption. The derived light-weighted ages of each spectrum are consistent with the by-eye P-Cygni variation: Knot E, Knot G, and S1226+2152 have \ light-weighted ages of 2.5, 11, and 26 Myr, respectively. While the single age models reflect the profiles of the youngest populations, they do not describe all of the galaxy-to-galaxy P-Cygni variations in the older populations. The absorption from RCS Knot G is deeper at bluer velocities than the 8 Myr single age model, while S1226+2152 has a small amount of redshifted emission.

The ionizing continuum of RCS Knot E increases from 900 to 525 , the Sunburst Arc stays relatively flat from 900 to 525 , while the ionizing continua of S0108+0624 and S1226+2152 both decline with decreasing wavelength. The inferred shapes of the ionizing continua constrain the total number of ionizing photons produced by the stellar population and the hardness of the nebular emission spectra (see esc ). In this parlance, RCS Knot E has a particularly hard ionizing spectrum, while S1226+2152 has a relatively soft spectrum.

sources.summary: The paper identifies SGAS J122651.3+215220 as a z=2.9233 lensed Lyman-break galaxy whose spectroscopy confirms LBG features and whose lens model implies high magnification and faint intrinsic luminosity.

quotes.quote: The first, SGAS J122651.3+215220 at z=2.9233 is lensed by one of several sub-clusters, SDSS J1226+2152, in a complex massive cluster at z=0.43 . Its ( g,,i ) magnitudes are ( 21.14,20.60,20.51 ) which translate to surface brightnesses, _ g,,i , of ( 23.78,23.11,22.81 ).

Moderate resolution spectroscopy confirms the redshifts suggested by photometric breaks, and shows both absorption and emission features typical of LBGs. Lens mass models derived from combined imaging and spectroscopy reveal that SGAS J122651.3+215220 is a highly magnified source ( M 40 ), while SGAS J152745.1+065219 is magnified by no more than M 15 . Compared to LBG survey results steidel03 , the luminosities and lensing-corrected magnitudes suggest that SGAS J122651.3+215220 is among the faintest 20 of LBGs in that sample.

sources.summary: J1226 was targeted with PdBI to extend the molecular-gas sample at the high-redshift end; CO(3-2) was not detected and the paper reports a 0.16 Jy upper limit.

quotes.quote: In July-October 2011 we obtained CO(3-2) maps (rest frequency of 345.998 GHz) for three additional UV-bright lensed objects with the IRAM PdBI guilloteau92 . The three targets, J0900, J1137 and J1226, were chosen to extend the parameter space of objects with molecular gas measurements. Specifically, J0900 has the lowest metallicity of the sources with reliable Herschel flux measurements, and J1137 and J1226 are at the very low and very high ends of the redshift range of the sample, respectively.
The CO(3-2) line is clearly detected in J1137, but not in J0900 and J1226. Assuming 200 \ line widths, the 3 upper limit on the line flux is 0.16 Jy \ for J1226 and 0.44 Jy \ for J0900.

sources.summary: The paper studies SGASJ1226 as a strongly lensed interacting high-redshift galaxy pair with an extended halo, galactic winds, a faint companion, and possible fluorescence.

quotes.quote: In this paper we present MUSE observations of (hereafter ), a lensed, multiply imaged pair of SFGs at z=2.92 . The main arc was discovered as a u band dropout Koester2010ArcJ1226Discovery and thanks to its high apparent brightness ( =20.6 mag), it has been subject to several follow-up observations Wuyts2012StellarPopsLensed, Saintonge2013MolGasLensedGalaxies, Malhotra2017HerschelLensedCII, Gazagnes2018NeutralGasPropertiesLyC, Rigby2018MegaSauraI, Chisholm2019MegaSauraMetallicity, Solimano2021MolecularGasIntermediateMass , becoming one of the best-studied Lyman-break galaxies (LBGs). Here, we report the discovery of an LAH associated with this galaxy and its close companion, which thanks to the lensing effect spans ;;20 on the sky. We take advantage of its extreme magnification (between 10 and 100 across the whole system) to spatially sample the line on subkiloparsec scales, thus offering a unique view of the CGM.

Also, we did not expect a significant contribution of metagalactic ionizing photons from the cosmic UVB, since the latest observational constraints imply that the UVB produces profiles at z 3 with peaks at the 2e-20 SB level Gallego2021ConstrainingUVBwithMUSE , at least 3 orders of magnitude fainter than the observed SB peak of SGASJ1226 on halo scales---although its contribution to the profile can become relevant at large radii, in the interface with the IGM.

gmw_00003745.overview: NGC 1700 is an elliptical galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/9810046] that is considered a merger-remnant candidate [arXiv:1408.0750] with a coalescence age of approximately 3 Gyr [arXiv:1107.3280]. It features a younger, counter-rotating stellar core [arXiv:1103.3797, arXiv:1107.3280], boxy isophotes [arXiv:1408.0750], and no detected radio emission at 1.4 GHz [arXiv:1107.3280].

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 1700's photometry and stellar kinematics, finding near-axisymmetry, rejecting constant mass-to-light models, and presenting strong stellar-dynamical evidence for dark matter in an elliptical galaxy.

quotes.quote: A variety of modeling techniques is used with surface photometry from the literature and recently acquired high-accuracy stellar kinematic data to constrain the three-dimensional mass distribution in the luminous cuspy elliptical galaxy NGC 1700. First, we model the radial velocity field and photometry, and, using a Bayesian technique, estimate the triaxiality T and short-to-long axis ratio in five concentric annuli between approximately 1 and 3 effective radii. The results are completely consistent with T being constant inside about 2.5 r_e ( 36 ; 6.7 h^ 1 ).

Near axisymmetry permits us then to use axisymmetric models to constrain the radial mass profile. Using the Jeans (moment) equations, we demonstrate that 2-integral, constant- M/L models cannot fit the data; but a 2-integral model in which the cumulative enclosed M/L increases by a factor of 2 from the center out to 12 h^ 1 can. Three-integral models constructed by quadratic programming show that, in fact, no constant- M/L model is consistent with the kinematics. Anisotropic 3-integral models with variable M/L , while not uniquely establishing a minimum acceptable halo mass, imply, as do the moment models, a cumulative M/L_B 10 h at 12 h^ 1 . We conclude that NGC 1700 represents the best stellar dynamical evidence to date for dark matter in elliptical galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper studies 1700 as an early-type merger-remnant candidate, citing boxy isophotes, tails, fast rotation, a counterrotating core, and intermediate-age globular clusters consistent with its merger age.

quotes.quote: The elliptical galaxies 1700 and 2865 both show evidence for recent interactions or mergers and are therefore candidate ``young ellipticals.'' 1700 features boxy isophotes, a strong isophotal twist, and two broad arms or tails that have long suggested it may be the remnant of a major merger of two disk galaxies ( seitzer90 , esp. \ Fig. 1b; brown00 ). Its kinematics supports this hypothesis: it rotates fast statler96 , yet features a counterrotating core with a distinctly younger stellar population kleineberg11 . whitmore97 found that its GCs are consistent with an age of 4 Gyr, but do not necessarily form a population of obviously intermediate age.

The fits, done via Chi-square mapping of the parameter space, yield clear evidence for the presence of intermediate-age clusters in each galaxy. We find that the ages of 1 - 2 Gyr for these globular-cluster subpopulations are consistent with the previously estimated merger ages for the host galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 1700 has a younger, metal-poorer counter-rotating core with distinct stellar populations, favoring formation through ingestion of a small retrograde companion.

quotes.quote: We find a distinct stellar population in the counter-rotating and kinematically decoupled core of the isolated massive elliptical galaxy NGC 1700. Coinciding with the edge of this core we find a significant change in the slope of the gradient of various representative absorption line indices. Our age estimate for this core is markedly younger than the main body of the galaxy. We find lower values for the age, metallicity and Mg/Fe abundance ratio in the center of this galaxy when we compare them with other isolated elliptical galaxies with similar velocity dispersion. We discuss the different possible scenarios that might have lead to the formation of this younger kinematically decoupled structure and conclude that, in light of our findings, the ingestion of a small stellar companion on a retrograde orbit is the most favoured.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1700 as a protoelliptical merger remnant with a counter-rotating core, a roughly 3 Gyr coalescence age, and no detected NVSS radio emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 1700 -- A protoelliptical galaxy possesses a counter-rotating stellar core and other symptoms that it formed through merging of two spiral galaxies of similar mass (Brassington et al. ). The best age estimate for the coalescence event is about 3 Gyr (Brown et al. ). No radio emission of this galaxy was detected in the NVSS survey, so only the upper limit of radio emission at 1.4 GHz could be estimated (Table t:notreduced ).
However, for NGC 1700, which is perfectly an edge-on object (Table t:sample ) without detectable radio emission, we adopted the synchrotron pathlength L of 2 kpc (see Appendix s:formula ).

gmw_00003746.overview: NGC 7727 is a late-stage merger remnant [arXiv:1801.07254] located 27.4 Mpc away [arXiv:2111.14854, arXiv:1801.07254], featuring spiral-shaped tidal tails [arXiv:1408.0750] and intermediate-age globular clusters [arXiv:1408.0750]. The galaxy hosts a dual supermassive black hole system [arXiv:2111.14854] containing two active galactic nuclei separated by approximately 500 parsecs [arXiv:2311.14830].

sources.summary: The study dynamically confirms NGC 7727 as the nearest known sub-kpc dual SMBH system, with the offset nucleus interpreted as a stripped remnant of a merged galaxy.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of a dual SMBH system in NGC 7727. We detect a SMBH in the photometric center of the galaxy in Nucleus 1, with a mass of M_ SMBH =1.54^ +0.18 _ 0.15 10^ 8 M_ . In the second nucleus, which is 500 pc offset from the main nucleus, we also find a clear signal for a SMBH with a mass of M_ BH =6.33^ +3.32 _ 1.40 10^ 6 M_ . Both SMBHs are detected at high significance. The off-axis nature of Nucleus 2 makes modeling the system challenging; however, a number of robustness tests suggest that a black hole is required to explain the observed kinematics.

This confirms it as the surviving nuclear star cluster of a galaxy that has merged with NGC 7727. This discovery is the first dynamically confirmed dual SMBH system with a projected separation of less than a kiloparsec and the nearest dynamically confirmed dual SMBH at a distance of 27.4 Mpc.

sources.summary: The paper argues that the second nucleus of 7727 is a newly formed massive UCD, with compact size, post-starburst population, weak AGN activity, tidal debris, and elevated dynamical-to-stellar mass.

quotes.quote: The second nucleus of 7727, hereafter `` '' for short, was mistaken by many astronomers to be a Milky Way foreground star or was outright overlooked, including by Arp himself. Arp noted on the envelope of his plate PH-4002-A, a 25-min visual exposure of 7727 taken with the Hale 5-m telescope: ``Star in interior seems to be sitting on dark nebula --- investigate'' This foreground star lies 17 NNW of the primary nucleus, which---like --is not visible owing to overexposure of the central region; it is this plate that is reproduced in Arp's Atlas .

The second nucleus of 7727 is a fascinating object. As described above, it shares several properties with UCDs, but also features some that are unique for a UCD so far: it is very compact ( = 29 pc) and massive ( = 4.2 10^8 ), has an elevated ratio of / characteristic of massive UCDs, and features a unique post-starburst spectrum and BTS. In combination, these properties suggest that \ is the clearest case yet of a recently formed UCD , besides also being on a par in mass with the most massive UCD currently known, M59-UCD3 liu15b .

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 7727 as a nearby binary-black-hole AGN candidate where MUSE imaging resolves two nuclei separated by about 500 pc that will eventually merge.

quotes.quote: The first candidate for a binary-BH AGN is the nearby galaxy NGC 7727, which is located at a distance of only 27.4 Mpc. In the image taken in 2021 with the Very Large Telescope's multi-unit spectroscopic explorer (MUSE) at the European Southern Observatory, the two bright nuclei are clearly visible in the wide image (Fig. fig:example_1 d) and compact image (Figure fig:example_1 e). Special MUSE spectrometric equipment has made it possible to clearly identify the active galactic nucleus 1 (``Nucleus 1” in Figure fig:example_0 ), which is the photometric center of the ``main” galaxy NGC 7727, and active galactic nucleus 2 (``Nucleus 2” in Figure fig:example_0 ), which lies to northwest at an expected distance of 500 pc. Karina Voggel and her colleagues (Vogel et al., 2022) showed that a larger BH is located in the center of the galaxy NGC 7727, while the smaller BH is its satellite. Over time, they will merge into one giant black hole.
The masses of the two objects were determined by observing how the gravitational attraction of black holes affects the motion of the stars around them.

sources.summary: The paper describes 7727 as a tidally disturbed interacting galaxy and recent merger remnant with spiral-shaped tidal tails, bluish young star clusters, and intermediate-age globular-cluster populations.

quotes.quote: Finally, 7727 (= VV67 = Arp 222) is so obviously tidally disturbed that it was included by both vorontsov59 and arp66 in their catalogs of interacting galaxies. Because of its spiral-shaped tidal tails it was originally classified as a peculiar Sa galaxy devaucouleurs64,sandage81 , but was then reclassified as a recent merger remnant of age 1.3 Gyr by georgakakis00 . The presence of bluish young star clusters in it was noted by crabtree94 and described in some more detail by trancho04 .

The fits, done via Chi-square mapping of the parameter space, yield clear evidence for the presence of intermediate-age clusters in each galaxy. We find that the ages of 1 - 2 Gyr for these globular-cluster subpopulations are consistent with the previously estimated merger ages for the host galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper presents 7727 as a late-stage merger with tidal tails, disturbed structure, two nuclei, counterrotating central gas, and evidence for recent ingestion of a gas-rich companion.

quotes.quote: We present new observations of the late-stage merger galaxy 7727, including /WFPC2 images and long-slit spectra obtained with the Clay telescope. 7727 is relatively luminous ( = 21.7) and features two unequal tidal tails, various bluish arcs and star clusters, and two bright nuclei 480 pc apart in projection. These two nuclei have nearly identical redshifts, yet are strikingly different.

7727, also known as Arp 222, VV 67, and MCG 02-60-006, is the dominant member of a small group of three galaxies, the other two members being 7723 and 7724 (Group 1522 in croo07 ). Its primary nucleus, hereafter `` '' for short, is located at _ J 2000 = 23^ h 39^ m 53 80 , _ J 2000 = -12 17 34 0 (Section sec31 ) and has a recession velocity relative to the Local Group of = +2000 8 \ kara96 , which places the galaxy at a distance of D = 27.4 Mpc for H_0 = 73 \ Mpc ^ 1 free10 .

sources.summary: The paper describes Arp 222 as an advanced post-merger with optical and X-ray plumes, central radio emission, and weak total magnetic field strength.

quotes.quote: Arp 222 -- A post-merger galaxy is at a slightly more advanced stage of evolution than NGC 7252 (Brassington et al. ). The protrusions and plums observed in optical and X-ray emission indicate that the system has not relaxed to an elliptical galaxy yet. Our analysis of available VLA radio data of this system at 1.49 GHz provides the total emission from the central part of the system.

The mean values of total magnetic field strength B_ tot obtained for the interacting objects range from 5-6 G for post-mergers Arp 222 and NGC 1700 to 25-27 G for ongoing mergers NGC 3256 and Arp 220 (Table t:b ).

sources.summary: The paper reports Arp 222 has a stable center inside 20 arcseconds, low A1, A3, and A4, and a constant high A2 amplitude to about 30 arcseconds.

quotes.quote: Arp 222: The coordinates of the centre remain constant in the inner 20" and change beyond that. This galaxy has very low A _1 values. A _2 maintains a constant value of 0.3 from the center to about 30". The A _3 and A _4 values are both very small.

gmw_00003747.name: NGC 1097A
gmw_00003747.overview: NGC 1097A is a companion galaxy to NGC 1097, classified as a peculiar elliptical-like clump [arXiv:1505.03544]. Its colors and structure suggest that the system may represent a galaxy pair in an advanced stage of merging [arXiv:1505.03544].

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 1097A as NGC 1097's companion, describing it as a peculiar elliptical-like clump whose colors and structure suggest an advanced merging galaxy pair.

quotes.quote: This is likely due to a strong interaction with the companion NGC 1097A Note that NGC1097A cannot be seen in our online Atlas, since it has been masked out from our 3.6 m map. . This object is listed as a peculiar elliptical in the RC3 catalogue ( 3RC ), though with `uncertain' classification. In the GALEX Atlas ( GALEXAtlas ), NGC 1097A is clearly visible as a clump northwest of the prominent bar of NGC 1097, bright in NIR and NUV, faint in FUV and surrounded by an extended, FUV bright, disc-like structure, all properties common, in the GALEX Atlas, to the bulges of spiral galaxies. This is suggestive that the whole system may be a galaxy pair in an advanced stage of merging.

We also noted that NGC 1097 is the object with the largest derived disc scalelength (6.32 kpc) and we verified that this result is not changed if we exclude from the exponential fit the whole radial range occupied by NGC 1097A.

gmw_00003748.name: Segue 2
gmw_00003748.overview: Segue 2 is an ultra-faint dwarf spheroidal galaxy orbiting the Milky Way [arXiv:0903.0818, arXiv:1805.01839]. It is classified as a galaxy rather than a star cluster due to its wide stellar metallicity spread [arXiv:1304.6080, arXiv:1901.05465], which indicates an extended star formation history lasting at least 100 million years [arXiv:1304.6080].

sources.summary: The paper treats Segue 2 as the only DR2 sample with unclear kinematic coherence, rejects stream contaminants, and finds a relatively round orbit with 11 bright members.

quotes.quote: The only system whose DR2 proper motions do not form an obvious kinematically coherent group is Segue 2 (see Figure  segue2_pm ). Two spectroscopic members of the belokurov09 stream have proper motions (which are different from each other) above 10  , so I reject them as being associated with Segue 2. Four stars form a separate tight group with 3   , 3   while the others are within or marginally outside the distribution of Segue 2 stars. These stars may be Segue 2 stars or field stars, but do not appear likely to be members of a stream given their significantly different proper motions. To be conservative, I exclude all of the stream candidates from the Segue 2 proper motion sample.
By combining the available data sets, we find a total of 11 spectroscopically confirmed Segue 2 members and 280 confirmed non-members brighter than g=20 .

Among the galaxies for which the binding energy is well-behaved, the rocha12 relation suggests infall times of 7  Gyr for Tri II, Segue 2, and Willman 1, 8  Gyr for Draco II and Tuc III, and >12  Gyr for Ret II. Only Willman 1 is robustly on a circular orbit; the orbit of Segue 2 is consistent with being relatively round but with large uncertainties.

sources.summary: The paper announces Segue 2 as an ultra-faint Milky Way satellite, measuring low luminosity, compact size, velocity dispersion, possible stream association, and a satellites-of-satellites origin.

quotes.quote: We announce the discovery of a new Milky Way satellite found in the data of Sloan Extension for Galactic Understanding and Exploration (SEGUE). We followed this up with deeper imaging and spectroscopy on the Multiple Mirror Telescope (MMT). From this, we derive a luminosity of M_v = -2.5 , a half-light radius of 34 pc and a systemic velocity of 40 kms ^ 1 . Our data also provides evidence for a stream around at a similar heliocentric velocity, and the SEGUE data show that it is also present in neighbouring fields.

A search in the high Galactic latitude ( |b| > 20^ ) area covered by SEGUE imaging has revealed another new satellite. has a half-light radius of 30 pc and an absolute magnitude of M_v = -2.5 . The photometry and spectroscopy suggest that the metallicity [ Fe/H ] 2 . is similar in structure, size, luminosity and velocity dispersion to three other recent discoveries, namely Segue 1, Boo II and Coma.

sources.summary: The review describes Segue 2 as immersed in Triangulum-Andromeda debris, with stream-like velocities matching the satellite and newer spectroscopy lowering its inferred mass.

quotes.quote: The evidence of such an accretion event is even more dramatic in the case of Segue 2. Taking into account the observations reported in Majewski2004,Rocha2004 , Segue 2 is immersed in the debris of the Triangulum-Andromeda stream, which is interpreted as the distant (at 30 kpc compared to 34 kpc for Segue 2) counter-part of the Monoceros stream and part of the larger Galactic Anti-Center Stellar Structure. As published by Rocha2004 , the velocities of M giant members of Tri-And structure are 0 < V_ GSR < 60 in the range of longitudes 160^ < l < 130^ at the Galactic latitudes slightly lower than that of Segue 2. This velocity distribution can be modeled as a Gaussian that peaks around V_ GSR 30 km s ^ 1 which is a good match to the measurement of the satellites line-of-sight velocity V_ GSR 40 km s ^ 1 .

Most recently, Kirby2013 re-evaluated the spectroscopic properties of Segue 2 albeit with a different observational setup and a smaller field of view as compared to the original study of Belokurov2010 . They claim no detection of the stream signal, which is perhaps not surprising given the targeting strategy and the area of the sky surveyed. Intriguingly, they measure much lower velocity dispersion (essentially consistent with zero), thus markedly reducing the central mass of the satellite.

sources.summary: The paper finds Segue 2 radial-velocity-only proper motions biased by small-number statistics, derives a different Gaia DR2 proper motion, and notes its peculiar VPD spread.

quotes.quote: We present mean absolute proper motion measurements for seven ultra-faint dwarf galaxies orbiting the Milky Way, namely Bo o tes III, Carina II, Grus II, Reticulum II, Sagittarius II, Segue 2 and Tucana IV. For four of these dwarfs our proper motion estimate is the first ever provided. The adopted astrometric data come from the second data release of the Gaia mission. We determine the mean proper motion for each galaxy starting from an initial guess of likely members, based either on radial velocity measurements or using stars on the Horizontal Branch identified in the Gaia ( G_ BP G_ RP , G) colour-magnitude diagram in the field of view towards the UFD.

The step-wise procedure we follow thus constitutes a big improvement, and the case of Segue 2 highlights another reason why. For this system the distribution of spectroscopic members in the VPD is significantly shifted towards larger _ and larger _ in comparison to the rest of the stars we identify as members. This results in a small discrepancy between and our own measurement: our derived mean proper motion is ( _ , _ ) = ( 1.27 0.11 , 0.10 0.15 ) , which is 1.5 away from the value quoted in .

sources.summary: The paper identifies Segue 2 as the least massive known galaxy, with metallicity spread and declining alpha ratios indicating extended star formation, and argues it may be stripped or born in a tiny halo.

quotes.quote: Segue 2 is the least massive galaxy known. We identify Segue 2 as a galaxy rather than a star cluster based on the wide dispersion in [ Fe/H ] (from to ) among the member stars. The stars' [ /Fe ] ratios decline with increasing [ Fe/H ] , indicating that Segue 2 retained Type Ia supernova ejecta despite its presently small mass and that star formation lasted for at least 100 Myr. The mean metallicity, [ Fe/H ] = (about the same as the Ursa Minor galaxy, 330 times more luminous than Segue 2), is higher than expected from the luminosity--metallicity relation defined by more luminous dwarf galaxy satellites of the Milky Way. Segue 2 may be the barest remnant of a tidally stripped, Ursa Minor-sized galaxy.
Alternatively, Segue 2 could have been born in a very low-mass dark matter subhalo ( v_ max < 10 km s ^ 1 ), below the atomic hydrogen cooling limit.

sources.summary: The paper studies a Segue 2 red giant, finding normal alpha and iron-group patterns, low neutron-capture abundances, and evidence that the ultra-faint dwarf was enriched by multiple supernovae and formerly more massive.

quotes.quote: We present the first high resolution spectroscopic observations of one red giant star in the ultra-faint dwarf galaxy Segue 2, which has the lowest total mass (including dark matter) estimated for any known galaxy.
This adds to the growing body of evidence indicating that Segue 2 may have been substantially more massive in the past.

Metal Production in Segue 2 The [ Mg/Fe ] , [ Ca/Fe ] , and possibly [ Ti/Fe ] ratios in \ are enhanced relative to the solar ratios and are not subsolar like those found in the more metal-rich stars of classical Local Group dwarf galaxies. Neither the intermediate odd- Z elements (Na, Al, K) nor the iron group elements show any significant deviations from the abundance patterns commonly found in field stars or dwarf galaxies. Ratios among the neutron-capture elements and iron ( [ Sr/Fe ] , [ Ba/Fe ] ) are subsolar by more than 1 dex, but these deficiencies are common in the ultra-faint dwarf galaxy populations and lie at the low ends of the halo star distributions.

sources.summary: Segue2 was an ultra-faint dSph target; its brightest FoV source and high-resolution bounds enter the analysis, and with BootesII it gives the strongest constraints.

quotes.quote: The targets included three classical dSphs (CDS), Carina, Fornax, and Sculptor, and three ultra-faint dSphs (UDS), BootesII, Hercules and Segue2, for a total of 123 hours of observing time.
Indeed, except for the two sources in the Carina FoV and for the brightest source in the Segue2 Fov, the other cases reported in Table tab:sources do not appear in the catalogue presented in Paper I.

For the specific case of Fig. fig:ann1 a, Fornax and Segue2 bounds come from the high resolution maps, while the other bounds arise from the low resolution ones. As expected, the combined analysis slightly improves the bound obtained in the most constraining case. The closest dSphs (Segue2 and BootesII) provide the strongest bounds.

sources.summary: The paper uses Segue 2's compact size, velocity-dispersion upper limit, and old stellar population as part of a two-galaxy UFD constraint on fuzzy dark matter.

quotes.quote: Using simulations of FDM halos, and existing measurements of sizes and stellar radial velocities in Segue 1 and Segue 2 UFDs, we derive a lower limit on the dark matter particle mass of > 3 10^ 19 eV at 99 confidence, marginalized over host halo circular velocity.

Two examples of such systems are the nearby ultra-faint dwarfs Segue 1 and Segue 2 , which are among the smallest and darkest galaxies known. The projected (2D) half light radii of Segue 1 and Segue 2 are =24.2 2.8 pc and =40.5 3.0 pc, respectively.

Spectroscopic surveys of stars in these galaxies indicate extremely low velocity dispersions, with _v = 3.9 0.8 km/s in Segue 1 , and only an upper limit _v < 2.6 km/s (95 confidence) in Segue 2 . The stellar populations in UFDs are generically quite old, with ages of 10 Gyr . For Segue 1, population modelling indicates that most stars were formed at z>7 , while modeling of Segue 2 suggests a star formation history similar to that of the dwarf Ursa Minor, with typical age of 9-10 Gyr .

sources.summary: The paper uses certain Kirby members for Segue 2, finds no clear proper-motion clump, and contrasts its systemic motion with Simon and Massari.

quotes.quote: Segue 2 We use only the stars classified as certain members in Kirby_13 . After our cuts, we are left with 10 stars, which however do not form a clear clump in proper motion space. Our systemic PM agrees in R.A. with the motion of Simon_18 but differs by 2 in Dec, while it differs by 3.1/3 (in R.A./Dec.) from the determination of Massari_18 . In contrast to us, the latter work includes also stars w/o spectroscopy.

sources.summary: The review says Segue 2 lacks a measured velocity dispersion and confirmed dark matter, but its broad metallicity spread still supports classification as a galaxy.

quotes.quote: Despite a comprehensive analysis of the kinematics of Segue 2, kirby13 were unable to measure its velocity dispersion, finding < 2.6 \ at 95 confidence. With only an upper limit on the dynamical mass and mass-to-light ratio, it cannot be confirmed that Segue 2 contains dark matter. However, kirby13 also showed that the stars of Segue 2 span a large range of metallicities, from = -2.9 to = -1.3 , with a dispersion of 0.43 dex. Segue 2 can therefore still be classified as a galaxy rather than a globular cluster on the basis of its chemical enrichment.

sources.summary: The paper classifies Segue 2 as a dwarf galaxy, following prior metallicity-spread evidence and the authors' finding that its member stars do not show significant mass segregation.

quotes.quote: kirbyetal2013 identified Segue 2 as a dwarf galaxy based on the wide spread in metallicity that they found among the member stars, even though they were not able to resolve its velocity dispersion. Our mass segregation data is compatible with this assumption since we do not detect significant mass segregation among the member stars ( P_ Mseg =4.5 ). We therefore classify Segue 2 as a dwarf galaxy.

gmw_00003749.name: COSMOS 27289
gmw_00003749.overview: COSMOS 27289 is a massive, compact star-forming galaxy located at a redshift of z=2.234 [arXiv:1908.02294]. It exhibits a low molecular gas fraction and extremely short central depletion times, indicating that inside-out quenching of its rapid star formation is imminent [arXiv:1908.02294].

sources.summary: The paper studies COSMOS 27289 as a massive compact z=2.234 star-forming galaxy whose central molecular gas depletion implies imminent inside-out quenching.

quotes.quote: Here we present high-resolution VLA observations of CO(1--0) in one such compact SFG, COSMOS 27289. Typical of this population of galaxies, COSMOS 27289 has a stellar mass 1.3 10^ 11 , SFR 400 /yr, and a compact size 2.3 kpc. The galaxy is undetected in X-ray imaging. Our previous lower-resolution VLA observations of this object found a very low gas fraction 0.1 and extremely short depletion time 30 Myr ( spilker16b , hereafter spilker16b ).

We find extremely short depletion times in the central regions of COSMOS 27289, 10-15 Myr, rising by a factor of 2 at radii of 3 kpc. At even larger radii, the low resolution and low S/N of the data prevent robust constraints on the depletion time. Nevertheless, this Figure does indicate that quenching of the central kiloparsecs (and indeed, the galaxy as a whole) is imminent, as the galaxy will be unable to sustain its current rapid star formation without substantial gas accretion.

gmw_00003750.name: A68-HLS115
gmw_00003750.overview: A68-HLS115 is a strongly lensed galaxy at a redshift of approximately 1.58 [arXiv:1408.0816, arXiv:1809.01162] that likely belongs to the same group as A68/C0 [arXiv:1310.2655]. It features a clumpy, rotation-dominated disk with a high molecular gas fraction [arXiv:1408.0816, arXiv:1809.01162] and has experienced a recent starburst [arXiv:1310.2655].

sources.summary: The paper reports strong CO(2-1) detection in A68-HLS115, showing clumpy UV-bright unresolved structure, a Gaussian CO line, matched CO/H redshift, and very high gas fraction.

quotes.quote: A68-HLS115 The CO(2--1) line is also very well detected in A68-HLS115 as it can be seen in Figs. fig:COmaps and fig:COspectra , with its intensity peaking at 14 . The CO(2--1) emission is nicely centred on the HST images to within 0.5'' . The HST images show that this z_ H = 1.5869 galaxy has a very clumpy structure. The clumps are bright in the rest-frame UV, betraying intense star formation.

Unfortunately they are unresolved in our CO observations, but would be of high interest for a deeper CO study at high spatial resolution. The CO(2--1) spectrum reveals a strong emission line very well fitted by a single Gaussian function with a full width half maximum FWHM_ CO = 267 km s^ 1 and an observed integrated flux F_ CO = 2.00 0.30 Jy km s^ 1 . The measured CO redshift perfectly agrees with the H redshift to within z = (z_ H z_ CO ) = 0.001 . The derived lensing-corrected molecular gas fraction f_ gas = 0.75 is very high; A68-HLS115 is among the gas-richer z>1 SFGs known (see Sect. sect:fgas ).

sources.summary: The paper analyzes the lensed z=1.5858 galaxy A68-HLS115, finding rotation-dominated but marginally stable kinematics, high gas fraction, strong turbulence, and three star-forming clumps.

quotes.quote: In this work, we present an analysis of the clumpy galaxy A68-HLS115 at z=1.5858 , located behind the cluster Abell 68, but strongly lensed by a cluster galaxy member. Resolved observations with SINFONI/VLT in the near-infrared (NIR) show H , H , [ NII ] , and [ OIII ] emission lines. Combined with images covering the B band to the far-infrared (FIR) and CO(2-1) observations, this makes this galaxy one of the only sources for which such multi-band observations are available and for which it is possible to study the properties of resolved star-forming clumps and to perform a detailed analysis of the integrated properties, kinematics, and metallicity. We obtain a stability of _ rot / _0 = 2.73 by modeling the kinematics, which means that the galaxy is dominated by rotation, but this ratio also indicates that the disk is marginally stable. We find a high intrinsic velocity dispersion of 80 10 km s ^ 1 that could be explained by the high gas fraction of f_ gas =0.75 0.15 observed in this galaxy.

Finally, we identify three clumps in the H map which have similar velocity dispersions, metallicities, and seem to be embedded in the rotating disk. These three clumps contribute together to 40 on the SFR _ H of the galaxy and show a star formation rate density about 100 times higher than HII regions in the local Universe.

sources.summary: The paper describes HLS115 as a strongly lensed z=1.5859 galaxy near A68/C0, clumpy rather than spiral, with SED fits favouring a young recent-starburst population.

quotes.quote: A68/HLS115: HLS115 is a galaxy lensed by both the cluster itself and a cluster member elliptical galaxy for a total estimated magnification of = 15 . We have detected H from this galaxy with LBT/LUCIFER, and CO with the IRAM/PdB interferometer (Dessauges-Zavadsky et al., in prep. ), from which we infer a redshift of z = 1.5859 . This redshift is nearly identical to that of A68/C0. The two galaxies, therefore, most likely belong to the same group. Contrary to A68-C0, however, HLS115 does not show a well-defined spiral structure, but rather consists of a series of clumps.
The physical properties of HLS115 with the aforementioned model (see Table selected_model_table ) describe it as a young galaxy having passed through a recent starburst.

gmw_00003751.name: GALEX J02535.8 001357
gmw_00003751.overview: GALEX J02535.8 001357 is a galaxy that exhibits a large region of extended X-ray emission [arXiv:1907.00225]. Within this system, there are two identified areas of high counts alongside a separate, discrete point source located away from the extended emission [arXiv:1907.00225].

sources.summary: The paper identifies GALEX J02535.8 001357 as galaxy 3 and describes extended X-ray emission, two high-count regions, and a separated discrete point source.

quotes.quote: In the case of GALEX J02535.8 001357 (galaxy 3), wavdetect found a large region of extended emission. A circular aperture of a size matching the area used by srcflux for point sources was manually moved around the region of extended emission to find areas of high counts. The coordinates found for these high count areas were then run as point sources in srcflux . Two such regions were identified.

The third region in GALEX J02535.8 001357 is simply a discrete point source separated from the area of extended emission. The sum of the sources for galaxy 3 in Table 3 is the combination of the extended region and the separated source. The arrangement of the galaxy D25, extended emission region, and three sources can be seen in figure 1.

gmw_00003752.name: GALEX J100712.2 + 065736
gmw_00003752.overview: GALEX J100712.2 + 065736 is a galaxy characterized by a slightly extended X-ray source [arXiv:1907.00225]. A second source within the galaxy is physically separated from this region of extended emission [arXiv:1907.00225].

sources.summary: The paper identifies GALEX J100712.2 + 065736 as galaxy 7, describing a slightly extended X-ray source, its coordinate adjustment, and a second separated source.

quotes.quote: The source in GALEX J100712.2 + 065736 (galaxy 7) was also found to be slightly extended, and the same procedure outlined above for GALEX J025325.8 001357 was followed to find a central coordinate corresponding to maximum counts. This slight adjustment to the central coordinate for the extended region only resulted in a single additional count for the source when run as a point source in srcflux , versus running srcflux with the central coordinate of the extended region. The second source in this galaxy is separated from the area of extended emission.

gmw_00003753.name: NGC 4220
gmw_00003753.overview: NGC 4220 is an almost edge-on spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0603161, arXiv:2004.11402] that forms a non-interacting pair with NGC 4218 [arXiv:astro-ph/0603161]. The galaxy features a boxy bulge [arXiv:astro-ph/0603161], a northeast dust lane containing younger stellar populations [arXiv:astro-ph/0603161, arXiv:0704.2839], and a younger stellar ring [arXiv:0704.2839].

sources.summary: NGC 4220 is described as an almost edge-on galaxy in a non-interacting pair, with a prominent off-center dust lane, low line-ratio values there, and a boxy bulge.

quotes.quote: NGC 4220 is an almost edge-on galaxy ( i 85 ) that forms a non-interacting pair with NGC 4218 at 15 gmcp00 . Our unsharp-masked image displays a prominent dust lane at a distance of 5 \ north-east of the centre, which is probably part of a more extended dust ring. The presence of the dust lane is also evident in the / \ map, where the ratio is very low at that location. The \ reconstructed intensity image reveals a clear boxy bulge component ldp00 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies a younger stellar ring in NGC 4220, says inclination affects its line-strength-map appearance, and notes a prominent dust lane northeast of the center.

quotes.quote: Starting with the second category: we find rings of younger stars in NGC 2844, 4220, 4245, 4274, 4314, 5953 and 7742. The appearance of the line strength maps in e.g. NGC 2844 and 4220 is different from those in e.g. NGC 4314, because of the effects of inclination.
NGC 4220 displays a prominent dust lane at a distance of 5 \ north-east of the centre, with younger stellar populations in the dust lane.

sources.summary: The paper notes NGC 4220 was added to the DNGS sample from an already observed field and is smaller than all stellar-mass-matched TNG100 galaxies.

quotes.quote: We note that, in an effort to take advantage of Dragonfly's large field of view, we also searched for additional spiral galaxies above our magnitude limit (effectively just relaxing the proximity requirement) in fields that we had already observed; this lead to the inclusion of NGC 4220 in our sample.

On the other hand, as noted previously in Sections sec:metrics:masses and sec:discussion:matching , some of the DNGS galaxies (NGC 3351, NGC 3368, and NGC 4220) are have smaller sizes (2D half-mass radii) than every one of their stellar mass-matched TNG100 samples.

Taken together, we conclude that the small sizes of NGC 3351, NGC 3368, and NGC 4220 may act to increase any existing differences relative to their mass-matched samples, particularly when measuring stellar halos outside of 20 kpc.

gmw_00003754.name: J1332 3146a
gmw_00003754.overview: J1332 3146a is a radio galaxy associated with the brightest galaxy in the SC 1329-313 group [arXiv:astro-ph/0505614, arXiv:astro-ph/0311251]. It features a low-brightness extended radio tail [arXiv:astro-ph/0311251] and rotating central gas that is misaligned with its stellar rotation [arXiv:2012.07882].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1332--3146a as a peripheral radio galaxy with steep extended emission aligned toward A3562, possibly connected to the cluster radio halo by faint bridge emission.

quotes.quote: The diffuse source J1332--3146a is visible in the bottom--right part of Figure fig:halo327 . This source is associated with the brightest galaxy in SC 1329--313. Its extended emission at 332 MHz is strongly elongated to the North--East direction and points toward the centre of A3562.

The full resolution images at 240 MHz and 332 MHz of the region of the J1332--3146a source (not presented here) show no sign of the compact component associated with the optical galaxy nucleus and detected at 610 MHz (image not shown here) and at higher frequency (V00, G04). This suggests that the radio nucleus of the optical counterpart is self--absorbed at low frequencies, and that the extended radio emission is not connected to the present activity of the AGN. We also note that the radio spectrum of J1332--3146a is steep, with a spectral index between 240 MHz and 1.4 GHz (G04) _ 240MHz ^ 1.4GHz =1.2 0.1 .

The radio images suggest that the western filament and the extension of J1332--3146a are the ``peaks'' of a bridge of very low brightness extended radio emission, which falls below the sensitivity limits of the current radio interferometers.

sources.summary: The paper describes J1332 3146a as the brightest SC 1329--313 galaxy with a very low-brightness extended radio tail, large projected extent, modest radio power, and location at the X-ray border.

quotes.quote: A remarkable feature of the radio emission in A3558--C is the very low brightness extended tail associated with the radio galaxy J1332 3146a. This source is identified with the cluster galaxy 11744 ( b_J = 14.96 and velocity v = 13107 km s ^ 1 , see Table 4), the brightest galaxy in the group SC 1329--313. The projected angular size of the radio emission is 6^ 4^ , corresponding to 240 160 kpc. The radio power is logP _ 1.4GHz (W Hz ^ 1 ) = 22.52 and its surface brightness is 0.16 mJy b ^ 1 . We note that the X--ray emission in this region is elongated in the direction of A3562, and compressed towards SC 1327--312, and J1332--3146a is located at the border of the X--ray emission of the SC 1329 group, as clear from Figure fig:J1332-3146a .

sources.summary: The paper finds PGC 047590 has rotating central gas whose orientation is misaligned with both stellar rotation and the isophotal major axis, while its line ratios rule out AGN/LINER contamination.

quotes.quote: In satellites, the gas is clearly rotating in PGC 047590, it seems to be in rotation in PGC 097958 and PGC 047197, while it does not show any rotation in PGC 046860 .
On the contrary, the gas rotation in PGC 047273 and PGC 047590 show misalignments of the same amount with respect to both the stellar rotation and isophotal major axis.

For PGC 047273 and PGC 047590, the ratio varies within the resolved maps (up to about 0.3 and 0.4, respectively, see Fig. fig:NIIHA ) but it definitely remains under 0.6, ruling out AGN/LINER contamination.

gmw_00003755.name: NGC 5371
gmw_00003755.overview: NGC 5371 is a grand-design, barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:0909.1816, arXiv:2405.14483] classified as the brightest Sb system in its group [arXiv:0802.1737]. It hosts a LINER nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0409622, arXiv:0909.1816] and exhibits multiple spiral modes with a bar that is coupled to the spiral pattern [arXiv:2405.10673, arXiv:2405.14483].

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 5371 among five galaxies with multiple spiral modes, consistent corotation-resonance distributions, available rotation curves, and short winding times.

quotes.quote: To investigate this aspect, we focus on five objects from our dataset that could have multiple spiral modes: IC 342, M 58, M 74, NGC 3583 and NGC 5371.
