The R_c distributions of these galaxies have consistent measurements and demonstrate the evident existence of several corotation resonances. In addition, the rotation velocity profiles for these objects are also publicly available.

Subsequently, we obtained rotation curve data v_c(R) for these five objects from the following sources:  Crosthwaite2000 (IC 342),  Lang2020 (M 58),  Walter2008 (M 74),  Noordermeer2005 (NGC 3583),  Sanders1996 (NGC 5371). Analogous phenomena have been observed in other selected galaxies as well. The figure illustrates that, for all the galaxies in our sample, the winding up process of the spiral structure takes approximately two or three galactic years.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 5371 as the group’s brightest Sb spiral, notes its projected position from the core, and finds no noteworthy dwarf-galaxy enhancement around it.

quotes.quote: The most luminous galaxy in the group is the Sb system NGC 5371 with M_R=-22.3 at our assumed distance of 29 Mpc. We chose to give attention to a region around the luminous spiral galaxy NGC 5371 which, as we finally constitute the group, contains 12 galaxies of type Sab and later and 10 galaxies of type Sa and earlier with M_R<-18 .

The most luminous galaxy in the group is the Sb system NGC 5371 at 230 kpc in projection to the ENE (outside Fig. centralknot ). NGC 5353 and NGC 5354 are the second and third most luminous galaxies and the sum of their luminosities exceeds that of NGC 5371.
In particular, there is no noteworthy enhancement of dwarfs around NGC 5371, the giant spiral that is the brightest galaxy in the group.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC5371 as a barred spiral in the six-galaxy resonance-coupling sample, noting imperfect rotation-curve data, bar-spiral coupling, and supportive H i velocity residuals.

quotes.quote: According to the literature, only NGC3344 and NGC5371 contain a noticeable bar. Secondly, in NGC5371, data points do not agree perfectly with each other resulting in what is probably a larger v_0 value than in the fit, but all this does not greatly affect the colored areas shown in Fig. fig:rotvels . It is easy to see that all 6 galaxies demonstrate the apparent pattern coupling, visible in this Figure. Finally, if we also mark the bar size, which is 25.5 arcsec for NGC3344 and 19.3 arcsec for NGC5371 2015A&A...582A..86H , and taking into account that the bar ends near its CR, we find that the bar is coupled with ILR of spiral pattern for both galaxies. For NGC5371, we find that the residuals of the velocity field for the HI data in 1987PhDT.......209B are consistent with Fig. fig:crs , when we apply the 2011ApJ...741L..14F method.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5371 as a barred LINER with poor-seeing velocity data limited to bright spiral-arm regions and uncertain circumnuclear line-profile separation.

quotes.quote: This galaxy possesses a modest bar of about 20 30 length aligned with the minor axis of the galactic disk. The bar is barely seen on our continuum image (Fig. ngc5371 a), but reveals itself rather well on the other optical images (e.g. Gonzalez et al. 1996). Unfortunately the IFP observations of this galaxy were made under poor seeing conditions during a non-photometric night. Due to this, we present velocity measurements only for the brightest emission regions in the spiral arms (Fig. ngc5371 ). In the circumnuclear region the H emission line is strongly broadened, and the intense [ NII ] emission line from the neighboring interference order is overlaid (the [ NII ] emission is abnormally strong because NGC 5371 is classified as a LINER (Rush et al. 1993)).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5371 as a grand-design, possibly post-starburst LINER spiral whose weak bar H-alpha emission leads the analysis to trace the spiral-arm pattern speed.

quotes.quote: NGC 5371 is a grand design, possibly post starburst, spiral galaxy with a LINER nucleus Rushetal1993, Koornneef1993, Elfhagetal1996 . It hosts a prominent 2 kpc stellar bar 45 degrees from the orientation of the outer disk major-axis with no significant change when comparing B and H band images Eskridgeetal2002 , and displays a star formation rate of 0.9 \ GonzalezDelgado1997 . The rotation curve was measured by ZasovSilchenko1987 from which they derived an exponential main disk scale length of 8.7 kpc, and a massive bulge ( M_ bulge M_ disk ). Although the outer parts of their rotation curve are not well determined, the inner regions agree well with our \ rotation curve.

sources.summary: The paper says MOND cannot acceptably fit NGC 5371, overestimating inner rotation velocities; adding an external field gives only a slightly better fit.

quotes.quote: (15) NGC 5371 : This is one of the galaxies for which MOND could not produce an acceptable fit to the observed rotation curve. MOND overestimates the rotation velocities in the inner parts of the rotation curve which leads to a high reduced chi-squared of ^2=10.49 . A slightly better fit is achieved by introducing the external field effect into the fit. The best-fit is obtained by a_e= 0.330^ +0.080 _ 0.085 .

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC5371 among reliable cored rotation curves, but notes baryon-dominated inner regions and imperfect profile fits, with the cored model fitting better overall.

quotes.quote: Cored RCs Among the 21 most reliable RCs, there are 10 RCs - NGC6503, NGC2403, F563-I, UGC05005, F574-I, NGC1090, F583-I, UGC05253, NGC5371, and IC4202 - which exhibit a cored nature with > 1 .

In the case of NGC5371, the inner region (within < 5, kpc ) is predominantly influenced by the baryonic component ( = 2.8 ), lacking a clearly defined rising part. Both cuspy and cored profile fittings for NGC5371 fall short of accurately matching the inner data points, yet the cored profile provides a relatively better overall fit to the RC compared to the cuspy profile, especially when considering the CDM prior. With the cored profile fit, the obtained c_ 200 for NGC5371 is 4.8 higher than the mean MCR, whereas the cuspy profile fit results in an even higher c_ 200 (5.5 ).

gmw_00003756.name: NGC 1366
gmw_00003756.overview: NGC 1366 is a lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1701.07631] located in the Fornax cluster [arXiv:1701.07631] that hosts a counter-rotating stellar component [arXiv:1701.07631, arXiv:0806.2988]. This galaxy also exhibits a bright nucleus [arXiv:1702.02013] and ionized gas with a disordered distribution and disturbed kinematics [arXiv:1701.07631].

sources.summary: NGC 1366 is analyzed as an S0 galaxy with counter-rotating stellar components; the younger, less alpha-enhanced component and disturbed gas suggest ongoing external acquisition.

quotes.quote: The S0 NGC 1366 in the Fornax cluster is known to host a stellar component that is kinematically decoupled from the main body of the galaxy. Here we successfully separated the two counter-rotating stellar components to independently measure the kinematics and properties of their stellar populations. We performed a spectroscopic decomposition of the spectrum obtained along the galaxy major axis and separated the relative contribution of the two counter-rotating stellar components and of the ionized-gas component.

We found that the counter-rotating stellar component is younger, has nearly the same metallicity, and is less / Fe enhanced than the corotating component. Unlike most of the counter-rotating galaxies, the ionized gas detected in NGC 1366 is neither associated with the counter-rotating stellar component nor with the main galaxy body. On the contrary, it has a disordered distribution and a disturbed kinematics with multiple velocity components observed along the minor axis of the galaxy. The different properties of the counter-rotating stellar components and the kinematic peculiarities of the ionized gas suggest that NGC 1366 is at an intermediate stage of the acquisition process, building the counter-rotating components with some gas clouds still falling onto the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 1366 as having peculiar bulge gradients and a younger counter-rotating nuclear stellar disk likely formed from enriched material acquired by interaction or minor merging.

quotes.quote: NGC 1366 shows a steep positive age gradient (from about 5 to 11 Gyr) a strong negative metallicity gradient (from about 0.5 to 0 dex) within 5" from the centre. In this region a sharp rotation is measured while immediately further out the galaxy is almost not rotating. Moreover, the rotation in the innermost regions is opposite to that observed at large radii (Fig. fig:kinplot ). Even if a disc-bulge decoupling bertetal99,sarzetal04,matdeg04 could give a similar kinematical signature the stellar population analysis is more suggestive of the presence counter-rotating nuclear stellar disc similar to those already observed in both elliptical e.g., moreetal04 and spiral galaxies e.g., corsetal99,pizzetal02,krajaf03,emsetal04,mcdetal06 . The nuclear disc is younger than the rest of host bulge and formed by enriched material probably acquired via interaction or minor merging.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 1366 has low NUV Sersic indices consistent with disk structure, a bright nucleus, boxy and disky isophotes, and bluer outer colours.

quotes.quote: The n values (Figure NGC1366-sb bottom panel) are lower than 2 in all the NUV bands, consistent with the presence of a disk structure. Very similar values are also measured in the U and V bands while the B band gives a larger value of n=2.55 0.03 (Figure analysis-1 ). The nuclear part of the galaxy is brighter than the S ersic model in all the NUV bands. The a_4/a ratio in M2 (Figure a4M2 ) shows a boxy nucleus and disky outskirts. The ( M2-V ) color profile of this galaxy (Figure color-M2-V ) is bluer in the outer parts relative to the inner ones.

gmw_00003757.name: EON 10.477 41.954
gmw_00003757.overview: EON 10.477 41.954 is classified as a spiral galaxy [arXiv:1909.00550]. Radiative-transfer modeling confirms that the galaxy belongs to a group characterized by having substantial extraplanar dust [arXiv:1909.00550].

sources.summary: The paper revisits EON 10.477 41.954 with MCMC radiative-transfer modeling, finding much larger parameter uncertainties but unchanged classification among galaxies with substantial extraplanar dust.

quotes.quote: We have revisited the target EON 10.477 41.954 in order to determine more accurately the uncertainties in the model parameters that are important for target classification (i.e., galaxies with or without substantial extraplanar dust) . We performed a Markov-Chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) analysis for the fifteen parameters of the three-dimensional radiative-transfer galaxy model we used previously for target classification. To investigate the convergence of the MCMC sampling , --which is usually neglected in the literature but should not be--- we monitored the integrated autocorrelation time ( _ int ), and we achieved effective sample sizes >5,650 for all the model parameters.

The final confidence intervals are not equal to --and not even constant multiples of--- --but 10-100 times larger than--- 5-100 times larger than the nominal uncertainties used in our previous study (the standard deviation of three best-fit results). Thus, those nominal uncertainties are not good proxies for the model-parameter uncertainties. However, although Although the position of EON 10.477 41.954 in the target-classification plot (the scale-height to diameter ratio of dust vs that of light-source) decreases by about 20-30 when compared to our previous study, its membership in the ``high-group''---i.e., among galaxies with substantial extraplanar dust---nevertheless remains unchanged.

gmw_00003758.overview: The galaxy 0820+4847 is a disk galaxy that acts as a strong gravitational lens [arXiv:1201.1677]. Its lensing system features an arc with very little curvature, an elliptical source, and a very flat deflector [arXiv:1201.1677].

sources.summary: The paper models 0820+4847 as a SWELLS lens with little arc curvature, no definite counter-image, an elliptical source, and a very flat inferred deflector.

quotes.quote: 0820+4847 Very little curvature is present in the arc. No definite counter-image is detected. An elliptical source is required, and the deflector is inferred to be very flat.

gmw_00003759.overview: The system 0841+3824 is a disk galaxy that acts as a strong gravitational lens [arXiv:1201.1677]. Modeling of this system requires two source components and has a consistent lens model [arXiv:1201.1677].

sources.summary: The paper says 0841+3824 needs two source components, has a bimodal posterior with one usable mode, and already has a consistent lens model.

quotes.quote: 0841+3824 Two source components are required. Whereas a bimodal posterior PDF is found, one mode chosen because of unrealistic small axis ratios for the other modes. A consistent lens model for this system has also been presented by B08.

gmw_00003760.overview: The system 1103+5322 is a disk galaxy that acts as a strong gravitational lens [arXiv:1201.1677]. Its arc is produced by a single circular source that is imaged four times [arXiv:1201.1677].

sources.summary: The paper describes 1103+5322 as a four-image lens of a circular source with tightly constrained parameters and a lens model consistent with B08.

quotes.quote: 1103+5322 Arc can be explained by a single circular source imaged four times. Lens model parameters are tightly constrained. A consistent lens model for this system has also been presented by B08.

gmw_00003761.overview: The object 1135+3720 is a galaxy system characterized by a complex gravitational arc structure [arXiv:1201.1677]. Modeling this arc requires two source components, though the position of its counterimage remains uncertain and the lens parameter constraints are relatively loose [arXiv:1201.1677].

sources.summary: The paper describes 1135+3720 as a complex arc requiring two source components, with uncertain counterimage position and relatively loose lens-parameter constraints.

quotes.quote: 1135+3720 The arc structure is complex, requiring two components for a good fit. Position of the counterimage is uncertain. Lens parameter constraints are not particularly tight.

gmw_00003762.overview: The galaxy 1422+4134 is a disk galaxy that acts as a strong gravitational lens [arXiv:1201.1677]. Its two compact images are possibly double images of a background QSO, and the system's outlier stellar mass fraction may be due to its dust content or a potential misidentification of the source redshift [arXiv:1201.1677].

sources.summary: The paper obtains a plausible model for grade-B lens 1422+4134, but notes possible QSO double images, weak constraints, outlier stellar fractions, and exclusion from IMF analysis.

quotes.quote: 1422+4134 This is the only grade 'B' lens system for which a plausible model has currently been obtained. The two compact images are possibly double images of a background QSO. This configuration yields weak constraints on the lens model, and the inferred stellar mass fractions place this system as a significant outlier from the relation in Figure correlation1 , possibly due to its significant dust content or a potential misidentification of the source redshift. Hence, this system has been excluded from the IMF analysis of Section imf .

gmw_00003763.overview: The galaxy 2141-0001 is a high-inclination, disk-dominated S0 spiral galaxy [arXiv:2102.12614, arXiv:1201.1692]. It acts as a strong gravitational lens, producing a deflected arc from a background source that appears split by dust lanes [arXiv:2102.12614, arXiv:1201.1677].

sources.summary: SDSSJ2141-001 is described as a strong-lensing S0 spiral whose disk-dominated mass distribution is reconstructed with rotation-curve and lensing constraints.

quotes.quote: The galaxies SDSSJ2141-001(J2141) and SDSSJ1331+3628(J1331) are systems that show strong lensing effect, its rotation velocities data were given by Dutton et.al. ; for these mass reconstructions the profiles of Miyamoto-Nagai, Exponential Disk, and NFW were taken into account . Regarding the GLE, in the case of J2141 an extended circular source was modeled considering that the deflected image is an arc, while for J1331 it was considered a punctual source in which four images are produced in the lens plane. As to the mass distribution of these galaxies, other authors have reported a high contribution of baryonic matter and this coincides with the obtained results in the use of Galrotpy and Gallenspy .

Galaxy J2141 J2141 is a type S0 spiral galaxy, with a dominant gravitational contribution coming from its disk . This object was initially observed in 2006 by means of the Hubble Spatial Telescope(HST) , with an ACS camera in a F814 filter and an exposure time of 420 seconds. In 2009 the Keck telescope took again images of these object with a NIR camera and K filter. From these images the GLE in this galaxy was evidenced, with the formation of an arc belonging to a source with a redshift different to J2141 ( z_ L =0.3180 , z_ s =0.7127 ) , for this reason it was considered important to study the mass distribution of this galaxy. The rotational velocity data of this galaxy was derived from spectral lines Mg b 5177, Fe II5270, Na D 5896, O II 3727 and H_ 6563, obtained from the Keck telescope.

sources.summary: The paper models SDSS J2141 0001 as a high-inclination disk-dominated spiral lens, combining lensing, gas rotation, and stellar kinematics to infer its stellar mass, dark matter fraction, and halo shape.

quotes.quote: One of the most promising spiral lens systems for a joint lensing and dynamics analysis is SDSS J2141 0001 (hereafter simply referred to as for brevity), at redshift = 0.1380 , which is a disk-dominated galaxy (it has a disk K'-band light fraction of 80 ) at high inclination ( i 78^ ). In addition to the discovery data from the SLACS survey, a wealth of imaging and kinematic data are available from the SWELLS project (Paper I and II). A joint strong lensing and gas kinematics (rotation curve) analysis of conducted in Paper II yielded a gravitational stellar mass of _ 10 ( / ) = 10.99^ +0.11 _ 0.25 (consistent with that from a stellar population analysis assuming a Chabrier IMF), a dark matter fraction at 2.2 disk scale lengths of = 0.55^ +0.20 _ 0.15 , and a dark matter halo flattening of = 0.91^ +0.15 _ 0.13 .

We construct a fully self-consistent mass model for the lens galaxy at redshift 0.14 , and use it to improve on previous studies by modelling its gravitational lensing effect, gas rotation curve and stellar kinematics simultaneously. We adopt a very flexible axisymmetric mass model constituted by a generalized NFW dark matter halo and a stellar mass distribution obtained by deprojecting the multi-Gaussian expansion (MGE) fit to the high-resolution K'-band Laser Guide Star Adaptive Optics (LGSAO) imaging data of the galaxy, with the (spatially constant) mass-to-light ratio as a free parameter. We model the stellar kinematics by solving the anisotropic Jeans equations.

sources.summary: The paper says 2141-0001 has an arc apparently split by dust lanes, explainable by one circular source, with posterior models allowing split or continuous arcs.

quotes.quote: 2141-0001 As discussed in paper II, the arc appears to be split into three images, but this is likely due to the presence of two dust lanes. The arc morphology can be explained by a single circular source. The posterior distribution includes models where the arc is split into distinct images, as well as models where the arc is continuous.

gmw_00003764.name: ID.01
gmw_00003764.overview: ID.01 is an emission line galaxy candidate that exhibits a possible double-peaked line profile [arXiv:1312.6364]. The galaxy lacks an optical or near-infrared counterpart, which makes a higher-redshift interpretation of z=1.8799 more likely than a lower-redshift alternative [arXiv:1312.6364].

sources.summary: ID.01 is treated as a prominent high-quality CO line candidate with a possible double-peaked profile, no optical/NIR counterpart, and a favored higher-redshift interpretation.

quotes.quote: ID.01 ( =80.05 0.03 GHz) is a prominent line candidate (the third most significant line, in terms of SNR _ spec ). The line profile may be indicative of two peaks separated by 300 , but the SNR is too low for a proper line profile characterization. The lack of a second line at the same position suggests that the source is at z=0.440 (if the line were identified as CO(1-0)) or z=1.8799 (if the line were CO(2-1)). The source has no optical/NIR counterpart, which makes the lower- z interpretation unlikely.

gmw_00003765.name: ID.02
gmw_00003765.overview: ID.02 is a high-redshift emission line galaxy candidate [arXiv:1312.6364]. Its observed line is interpreted as either CO(1-0) emission at a redshift of z=0.41 or CO(2-1) emission at z=1.82, with spectral energy distribution fitting favoring the latter scenario [arXiv:1312.6364].

sources.summary: ID.02 is a line candidate with two possible counterparts; the SED fit favors CO(2-1) at z=1.82 and a dustier, likely brighter CO counterpart.

quotes.quote: ID.02 ( =82.07 0.02 GHz) is at the edge of the primary beam area in one of the lowest frequency setups in the scan. It has two possible counterparts (see Fig. fig_sed ), with z_ phot =0.92 and 1.12 in soto99 . The line can be intepreted as CO(1-0) at z=0.41 or as CO(2-1) at z=1.82 . The SED fit favors the latter scenario for both counterparts. The first of the two galaxies seems to be the most plausible counterpart, since it is best fitted assuming higher dust extinction in the optical bands than in the companion, implying brighter FIR emission (and therefore, likely brighter CO fluxes).

gmw_00003766.name: ID.05
gmw_00003766.overview: ID.05 is a high-redshift emission line galaxy [arXiv:1312.6364]. Because it lacks any optical or near-infrared counterpart, its single detected emission line is most likely CO(3-2) at a redshift of z=2.8471 [arXiv:1312.6364].

sources.summary: ID.05 has no second line or optical/NIR counterpart, making low-redshift CO(1-0) unlikely and favoring a CO(3-2) interpretation at z=2.8471.

quotes.quote: ID.05 ( = 89.89 0.04 GHz) shows no second line at the same spatial position, suggesting z_ CO <3.016 . The lack of any optical/NIR counterpart makes an identification as CO(1-0) ( z_ CO =0.2824 ) unlikely. The two remaining options are CO(2-1) at z=1.5648 or CO(3-2) at z=2.8471 . The latter is preferred due to the lack of any optical counterparts.

gmw_00003767.name: ID.10
gmw_00003767.overview: ID.10 is a high-redshift emission line galaxy [arXiv:1312.6364]. Based on its lack of optical and near-infrared counterparts, the galaxy is interpreted to be at a redshift of z=2.329 [arXiv:1312.6364].

sources.summary: ID.10 is interpreted as CO(3-2) at z=2.329 because it lacks optical/NIR counterparts, though the paper notes a possible z~1.2 overdensity caveat.

quotes.quote: ID.10 ( = 103.88 0.03 GHz) can be identified as CO(2-1) at z=1.219 or CO(3-2) at z=2.329 . In our analysis, we adopt the latter (based on the lack of optical/NIR counterparts). We note however that a few other galaxies with optical/NIR spectroscopic redshift z 1.2 are observed in the field, tentatively pointing towards a galaxy overdensity at this redshift. If that was indeed the case, the redshift assignment for ID.10 should be re--considered.

gmw_00003768.name: ID.12
gmw_00003768.overview: ID.12 is a high-redshift galaxy [arXiv:1312.6364] that is spatially consistent with a pair of optical and near-infrared sources [arXiv:1312.6364]. It has a blue spectral energy distribution [arXiv:1312.6364] and a tentative Herschel/PACS detection [arXiv:1312.6364].

sources.summary: ID.12 is spatially consistent with two optical/NIR galaxies; the second galaxy has a photometric redshift matching CO(3-2), blue SED, and tentative PACS emission.

quotes.quote: ID.12 ( = 108.43 0.04 GHz) is spatially consistent with a pair of galaxies detected at optical/NIR wavelengths. One of them has a redshift z=0.9614 barger08 , which is not consistent with any CO identification of the line detected in our 3 mm scan. On the other hand, the second galaxy has a photometric redshift of z=2.08 soto99 , consistent with the identification of this line as CO(3-2) at z=2.1891 . The SED of this galaxy suggests that this is a rather blue galaxy with L_ dust 8 10^9 . The Herschel/PACS 100 m observation of the field reveals a tentative detection ( 1 mJy, close to the 3- sensitivity limit) spatially consistent with this source.

gmw_00003769.name: ID.15
gmw_00003769.overview: ID.15 is a high-redshift emission line galaxy that lacks an optical or near-infrared counterpart [arXiv:1312.6364]. Its detected emission line is identified as CO(3-2) at a redshift of z=2.150, or alternatively as CO(2-1) at z=1.100 [arXiv:1312.6364].

sources.summary: ID.15 lacks an optical/NIR counterpart and is identified primarily as CO(3-2) at z=2.150, with CO(2-1) at z=1.100 left as an alternative.

quotes.quote: ID.15 ( = 110.55 0.04 GHz) has no counterpart (the closest galaxy in the HST/NICMOS H-band observations is >3'' away). We identify the line as CO(3-2) at z=2.150 . Alternatively, the line could be CO(2-1) at z=1.100 .

gmw_00003770.name: ID.18
gmw_00003770.overview: ID.18 is a high-redshift emission line galaxy with a likely redshift of z=2.071 [arXiv:1312.6364]. This identification is supported by a CO(3-2) line detection at 112.63 GHz and a lack of optical, near-infrared, or mid-infrared counterparts [arXiv:1312.6364].

sources.summary: ID.18 is the second most significant line detection; repeat data splits and Bayesian analysis support reality, while absent counterparts favor CO(3-2) at z=2.071.

quotes.quote: ID.18 ( = 112.63 0.04 GHz) is the second most significant line detection in our survey. We verified that the line is likely real by splitting the data taken in this frequency setup into 4 independent blocks, and searching for the line in each of them. This test retrieves the line at the expected significance in each setup. The probability of this line being a real astrophysical source is 95 in the analysis by Lentati et al. (in prep.). The line flux is 0.58 0.11 .

On the other hand, no galaxy counterpart is observed at any optical/NIR/MIR wavelengths. The lack of counterparts likely rules out a low-- z interpretation (excluding anything below z 1.5 , down to stellar luminosities comparable with the Large Magellanic Cloud). This suggests that the line is most likely CO(3-2) at z=2.071 .

gmw_00003771.name: ID.19
gmw_00003771.overview: ID.19 is a massive galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 2.05 [arXiv:1312.6364]. It exhibits a specific star formation rate below that of an average main-sequence galaxy at its redshift [arXiv:1312.6364] and possesses a relatively low molecular gas mass [arXiv:2302.10323].

sources.summary: ID.19 is a secure CO(3-2) source associated with a galaxy at z~2.05; its SED implies a massive, below-main-sequence galaxy with uncertain gas mass.

quotes.quote: ID.19 ( 113.45 0.04 GHz) is associated with a galaxy at RA=12:36:51.61, Dec=+62:12:17.3, for which grism spectroscopic redshift z_ grism =2.044 0.002 is available through AGHAST (Weiner et al. \ 2013, in prep., see Fig. fig_id19 ). This redshift is in excellent agreement with the redshift inferred from the CO line detection in our study ( z=2.0474 0.0015 ), under the assumption that the line is identified with CO(3-2). The available photometry of this source samples a broad range of wavelengths, up to MIPS 24 m. The source is formally not detected in Herschel maps, although a bright, nearby foreground ( z=0.300 ) galaxy prevents us from directly measuring the FIR emission in the counterpart of ID.19. From the SED fit, we infer the following properties of the counterpart of ID.19: SFR= 7.9_ 1.4 ^ +3.5 yr ^ 1 ; M_*=(1.9 0.3) 10^ 10 ; L_ dust = (8_ 1 ^ +11 ) 10^ 10 ; M_ dust = (9_ 7 ^ +50 ) 10^ 6 .

The galaxy is thus quite massive and has a specific star formation rate of sSFR 0.4 Gyr ^ 1 , below the sSFR of an average `main--sequence' galaxy at z 2.05 e.g. elbaz11 . We measure a line luminosity L'_ CO(3-2) = (9.9 2.6) 10^9 . Assuming a Milky-Way-like CO excitation, the corresponding CO(1-0) luminosity would be L'_ CO(1-0) 3.7 10^ 10 .

sources.summary: The paper treats ID.19 as a CO-selected SMG with a newly detected but relatively poor overdensity and among the lowest molecular gas masses in the sample.

quotes.quote: We note that two SMGs, namely, ID.03 and ID.19, have not been selected through submillimeter surveys but they were found directly through a CO blind survey Decarli14 . These three overdensities are those around ID.19 (in the case of both B19 and L18 catalogs) and GN20.2a (B19), and for our analysis we consider them altogether with the others.

ID.03, as well as ID.19 for which we detect a relatively poor 2 overdensity, have the lowest gas masses M_ H_2 (1-2) 10^ 10 M_ among those reported in Table tab:mol_gas_properties for our SMG sample. These relatively low H _2 gas masses imply that the two sources may not be true SMGs, that is, with strong dust-continuum emission and high molecular gas content. Indeed, ID.19 has been detected in CO(3 2) only Decarli14 , while ID.03 has relatively low-J detections by Decarli14 and Boogaard2023 in CO(1 0) (tentative), CO(2 1), and CO(4 3).

sources.summary: The paper confirms Z1 as a significant directed-search detection, corresponding to D14 ID19, with high probability once position and redshift priors are included.

quotes.quote: Applying the same rescaling of probabilities to the results of the directed search as in the blind survey we find only Z1 is a significant detection in the data with a probability of 99 . Z1 corresponds to ID19 in D14, the second source assigned a secure rating in D14 which we therefore confirm with the directed Bayesian analysis. One can immediately see the benefit of including the additional position and redshift information in the priors for the search, as in the context of a blind survey, Z1 had a probability of less than 50 and thus was not significant enough a detection to warrant inclusion in the final list of candidates.

gmw_00003772.name: ID.20
gmw_00003772.overview: ID.20 is a high-redshift galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 2.05 [arXiv:1312.6364]. It is characterized by a low stellar mass of 1.21 billion solar masses, a dust luminosity of 56 billion solar luminosities, and a star formation rate of 1.0 solar mass per year [arXiv:1312.6364].

sources.summary: ID.20 is associated with a galaxy at z~2.05, with photometry-supported CO(3-2), low stellar mass, dust luminosity, star formation, and inferred CO luminosity.

quotes.quote: ID.20 ( 113.45 0.05 GHz) is associated with a galaxy at RA=12:36:50.396, Dec=+62:12:05.12. Our CO observations suggest that the source is CO(3-2) at z=2.05 , a redshift identification that is supported by the observed photometry (see Fig. fig_sed ). Our SED fit yields a stellar mass M_*=(1.21 0.07) 10^9 , a dust luminosity of L_ dust =(5.6 1.2) 10^ 10 , and an associated SFR= 1.0 0.3 yr ^ 1 . The CO(3-2) line luminosity is L'_ CO(3-2) =(8.8 2.5) 10^9 . Under the same assumptions used for ID.19, we derive L'_ CO(1-0) 3.2 10^ 10 .

gmw_00003773.name: Fornax UFD1
gmw_00003773.overview: Fornax UFD1 is an ultra-faint dwarf galaxy located approximately 19.0 Mpc away in the outskirts of the giant galaxy NGC 1316 [arXiv:1701.03465]. It is likely a member of the Fornax cluster and is composed primarily of metal-poor red giant branch stars [arXiv:1701.03465].

sources.summary: The paper reports Fornax UFD1 as a resolved, metal-poor ultra-faint dwarf near NGC 1316 at 19.0 Mpc, with small size and luminosity comparable to Virgo UFD1.

quotes.quote: We report a serendipitous discovery of an UFD, Fornax UFD1, in the outskirts of NGC 1316, a giant galaxy in the Fornax cluster. The new galaxy is located at a projected radius of 55 kpc in the south-east of NGC 1316. This UFD is found as a small group of resolved stars in the Hubble Space Telescope images of a halo field of NGC 1316, obtained as part of the Carnegie-Chicago Hubble Program. Resolved stars in this galaxy are consistent with being mostly metal-poor red giant branch (RGB) stars.

Applying the tip of the RGB method to the mean magnitude of the two brightest RGB stars, we estimate the distance to this galaxy, 19.0 1.3 Mpc. Fornax UFD1 is probably a member of the Fornax cluster. The color-magnitude diagram of these stars is matched by a 12 Gyr isochrone with low metallicity ( [ Fe/H ] 2.4 ). Total magnitude and effective radius of Fornax UFD1 are M_V 7.6 0.2 mag and r_ eff = 146 9 pc, which are similar to those of Virgo UFD1 that was discovered recently in the intracluster field of Virgo by Jang Lee (2014).

gmw_00003774.name: NGC 6744
gmw_00003774.overview: NGC 6744 is a giant, Milky-Way-like spiral galaxy [arXiv:1807.02604, arXiv:2304.13484] featuring a barred ringed structure [arXiv:2405.13499] and active star formation along its spiral arms [arXiv:2102.11565]. It hosts a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1807.02604, arXiv:2405.13499] and shows evidence of a merger that occurred approximately one billion years ago [arXiv:1807.02604].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 6744 as a nearby Milky-Way-like spiral hosting a LINER/AGN nucleus, circumnuclear emitting regions, merger evidence, and a misaligned gaseous disk.

quotes.quote: NGC 6744 is the nearest and brightest south-hemisphere galaxy with a morphological type similar to that of the Milky Way. Using data obtained with the Integral Field Unit of the Gemini South Multi-Object Spectrograph, we found that this galaxy has a nucleus with LINER (Low Ionization Nuclear Emission Line Region) surrounded by three line emitting regions. The analysis of the Hubble Space Telescope archival images revealed that the nucleus is associated with a blue compact source, probably corresponding to the active galactic nucleus (AGN). The circumnuclear emission seems to be part of the extended narrow line region of the AGN. One of these regions, located 1 southeast of the nucleus, seems to be associated with the ionization cone of the AGN.

Spectral synthesis shows evidence that this galaxy may have gone through a merger about one billion years ago. On the basis of the kinematic behavior, we found a gaseous rotating disk, not co-aligned with the stellar disk. Given the relative degree of ionization and luminosities of the nuclear and circumnuclear regions, we suggest that the AGN was more luminous in the past and that the current circumnuclear emissions are echoes of that phase.

sources.summary: The paper demonstrates getsf on GALEX FUV imaging of NGC 6744, extracting many star-forming sources and filamentary spiral-arm structures from the galaxy.

quotes.quote: Star-forming galaxy NGC 6744 NGC 6744 was observed with GALEX in a far-ultraviolet (FUV) waveband ( 1350 1750 ) centered at 0.15 m Lee_etal2011 . The 0.4 0.4 image https://archive.stsci.edu/missions-and-data/galex/ in Fig. galaxy with an angular resolution of 4 shows the spiral galaxy, which is considered to be similar to our own Galaxy. Despite noisiness of the FUV image, it displays the spiral arms with many hundreds of unresolved emission sources. These are the regions of ongoing star formation, heated by the embedded young massive stars.

The source catalog contains measurements of 1169 sources, 1130 of which are selected as acceptably good by Eq. ( acceptable ). Most of the sources likely correspond to the star-forming regions along the galactic spiral arms; many of them overlap with each other, hence they required deblending for accurate measurements of their fluxes. The filaments extracted in the F _ component represent the spiral arms and their branches. The 147 skeletons trace the simple, non-branching segments of the filamentary network (Sect. fildetection ).

sources.summary: The paper adds NGC 6744 as an intermediate spiral comparison galaxy, rebinned from 93 to 1113 pc, and finds larger-scale metallicity correlations than in NGC 5236.

quotes.quote: To ensure these results are valid for a range of galaxies, we also include data from two other galaxies in the TYPHOON survey, NGC 6744 and NGC 7793 (Seibert et al. in prep.).

For this, we expand our analysis to two other galaxies -- NGC 6744, an intermediate spiral galaxy at a distance of 11.6 Mpc, and NGC 7793, a flocculent spiral galaxy 3.6 Mpc away. Each of these galaxies was binned to increasingly coarser resolutions, using values of f=1,2,4,6,8,10, and 12 . This corresponds to physical resolutions ranging from 93 to 1113 pc ( 0.012-0.14R_e ) for NGC 6744, and 28 to 346 pc ( 0.016-0.19R_e ) for NGC 7793.

For NGC 6744, at native resolution, the most likely value of is 1.30^ +0.15 _ 0.12 kpc -- much larger than the value of found for NGC 5236, even when NGC 5236 was binned to a resolution coarser than NGC 6744's resolution. This indicates that correlations between metallicities exist on much larger scales in this galaxy than in NGC 5236.

sources.summary: The paper compares targeted MPIPAF observations of NGC 6744 with archival HIPASS data, integrating the beams covering the H I disk and finding agreement in spectral shape and flux density.

quotes.quote: We present results of targeted observations of two HIPASS sources, J1413-65 (Circinus) and J1909-63A (NGC 6744) from August 3 and September 4, respectively, observed with the MPIPAF, in addition to two HIPASS sources we detected in the GAMA G23 field, J2242-30 (NGC 7361) and J2309-30 (ESO 469-G015). We obtain HIPASS spectra from the archival HIPASS data cubes from the first HIPASS data release Meyer2004 .

Although the NGC 6744 observation utilized all 16 MPIPAF beams, not all 16 beams lie upon the galaxy. NGC 6744 has an angular radial size in H i of 15 arcmin 30 arcmin in diameter, Ryder1999 , while 13 of the MPIPAF beams have angular separations >30 arcmin from the centre of NGC 6744. We integrated the flux from the three beams with angular separations <24 arcmin. We also calculated the total integrated flux density from the archival HIPASS cube within a 13 13 pixel box centred on NGC 6744 (maximum angular separation 24 arcmin, matching the separation of the MPIPAF beams). The spectral shape and flux density of the integrated MPIPAF spectrum agrees with the HIPASS spectrum (lower panel of Figure fig:hipass_specs ).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6744 as a Milky-Way-like spiral and adopts a deeper PHANGS-HST TRGB distance of 9.39 Mpc.

quotes.quote: NGC 6744 is a Milky-Way like spiral and the brightest member of its group. The CMDs/TRGB catalog measurement from data taken by SNAP-12546 (PI: R. Tully) shows D = 9.50 0.63 Mpc. The PHANGS-HST parallel is deeper, and when limited to regions with fewer Pop I stars, provides a higher confidence measurement of m_ TRGB = 25.91 0.05 mag, resulting in a distance of D = 9.39 0.43 Mpc. We adopt the new PHANGS distance measurement for NGC 6744.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC6744 as a giant Local Volume spiral whose angular momentum sharply changes the cumulative spin vector; it lies near the Local Supercluster equator and has an extended stellar and H I disk.

quotes.quote: Then, the total angular momentum falls sharply at =9.5 Mpc due to the contribution of the giant spiral galaxy NGC6744. It is located near the equator of the Local Supercluster (sgb 10^ ), and its spin lies almost in the plane of the Local Supercluster (sgb 7^ ). The galaxy NGC6744 is the most extended stellar system in LV with a diameter of A_ 26 =60 kpc and an angular momentum of about 2 times J_ MW . The dimensions of the galaxy reach 100 kpc in the neutral hydrogen line Ryder_Walsh_Malin_1999 .

sources.summary: The Euclid showcase treats NGC 6744 as a large barred ringed spiral whose satellite system, gas morphology, low-luminosity nucleus, star-formation evidence, and dwarf companions are examined.

quotes.quote: NGC 6744 is one of the physically largest spirals beyond the Local Group, and the largest angular-extent barred ringed spiral in the southern sky devaucouleurs63 . An extensive multi-frequency study by yew18 found several point sources detected in both X-rays and radio, likely supernovae remnants, and a luminous nuclear X-ray source thought to be associated with a super-massive black hole. This central source is optically characterized as a very low-luminosity active galactic nucleus dasilva18 . In 2005, a Type Ic SN exploded in the disk of NGC 6744 kankare14 , adding evidence for a past star formation episode.

gmw_00003775.name: NGC 2434
gmw_00003775.overview: NGC 2434 is an early-type, elliptical field galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/9601044, arXiv:astro-ph/0505448]. Kinematic modeling indicates the galaxy is embedded in a dark matter halo [arXiv:astro-ph/9601044], with observations showing a rapidly rising circular-velocity curve [arXiv:astro-ph/0012381] and a single-peaked globular cluster color distribution [arXiv:astro-ph/0505448].

sources.summary: The paper applies scale-free dynamical distribution-function models to NGC 2434, finding its velocity-profile data can be fit with either flattened or spherical dark-halo models.

quotes.quote: The distribution functions are used to model the galaxies NGC 2434 (E1) and NGC 3706 (E4), for which stellar kinematical measurements out to two effective radii indicate the presence of dark halos (Carollo et al.). The velocity profile shapes of both galaxies can be well fit by radially anisotropic case II models with a spherical logarithmic potential. This contrasts with the f(E,L_z) models studied previously, which require flattened dark halos to fit the data. galaxies: elliptical and lenticular, cD -- galaxies: individual: NGC 2434 and NGC 3706 -- galaxies: kinematics and dynamics -- galaxies: structure -- line: profiles.

The families of DFs presented here can be used to further interpret the observations for NGC 2434 and NGC 3706. The model parameter is chosen to fit the observed surface brightness slope at large radii, and q_ p is chosen equal to the average apparent flattening of the isophotes outside half the effective radius. This yields = 2.94 and q_ p =0.92 for NGC 2434, and = 3.36 and q_ p =0.65 for NGC 3706. The potential is chosen to be logarithmic ( = 0 ), since it has already been demonstrated that both galaxies must be embedded in a dark halo.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 2434 as an early-type field host galaxy and finds its globular-cluster colours consistent with a broad single-peaked distribution.

quotes.quote: The host galaxies (NGC 1380, NGC 2434, NGC 3115, NGC 3379, NGC 3585, NGC 5846, and NGC 7192) were selected to sample a significant range in environmental density at intermediate galaxy luminosity and velocity dispersion in the range 19.2 M_B 21.2 and 184 264 km s ^ 1 , respectively. All galaxies are of early type ( T<-2 ) according to the RC3 galaxy catalog RC3 . Our sample includes five elliptical and two lenticular galaxies (see Tab. tab:galdat ).
The colour distributions of globular clusters in NGC 2434, 3379, 5846, and 7192 are consistent with a broad single-peak distribution.
Three galaxies of our sample (NGC 1380, NGC 3379, and NGC 5846) are assigned group/cluster membership, while the remaining four galaxies are considered field members.

sources.summary: The paper places NGC 2434 among ellipticals with rapidly rising circular-velocity curves and strong dark-halo evidence, including compact, dense halo parameters and low luminous M/L in halo models.

quotes.quote: In the other group the CVC rises rapidly until 0.2 R_e and reaches a peak at 1 R_e after which it remains nearly flat (NGC 2434, NGC 7145, NGC 7192, NGC 7507).

Note that some of the galaxies with the best evidence for dark halos (NGC 2434, 7507, 7626) are among the smallest halo core radii and largest halo density points with respect to the mean fit lines in Fig. rcLum .
On the other hand, the best-determined galaxies near the lower boundary with the best evidence for additional dark matter, NGC 2434, 7507, 7626, have (r_ ch /R_e) =(-0.15:-0.35) .

gmw_00003776.name: NGC 7192
gmw_00003776.overview: NGC 7192 is an early-type field galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0505448] classified as either an elliptical [arXiv:1702.02013, arXiv:astro-ph/0505448] or a lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1702.02013, arXiv:astro-ph/0505448]. Its structure contains a bulge, two lenses, and a disk [arXiv:1702.02013], while its globular cluster colors show a broad single-peaked distribution [arXiv:astro-ph/0505448].

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 7192 as probably S0 rather than elliptical, with multi-component models, shallow NUV Sersic fits, and an under-luminous nucleus.

quotes.quote: NGC 7192 The galaxy is classified S0 in the RSA and elliptical (.E+..*.) in the RC3. Huang13 suggest that the galaxy is an S0 on the basis of a multiple S ersic fit. Their four component model recognizes a bulge ( n=1.7 ), two lenses ( n =0.4 and n =0.5), and a disk ( n=0.9 ). The presence of two lenses in addition to an outer exponential disk has been previously revealed by Laurikainen11 . The Huang13 model and that of Laurikainen11 converge in indicating that this galaxy is an S0 rather than an E Huang13 .

Our fits of the NUV luminosity profiles (Figure NGC7192-sb bottom panel) provide values of n slightly shallower ( n 3 ) than the ^ 1/4 law. Similar values are derived in the optical and NIR bands (Figure analysis-1 ). The nucleus is under-luminous respect to the S ersic fit both in the W2 and M2 bands.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 7192 as an early-type field host galaxy and finds its globular-cluster colours consistent with a broad single-peaked distribution.

quotes.quote: The host galaxies (NGC 1380, NGC 2434, NGC 3115, NGC 3379, NGC 3585, NGC 5846, and NGC 7192) were selected to sample a significant range in environmental density at intermediate galaxy luminosity and velocity dispersion in the range 19.2 M_B 21.2 and 184 264 km s ^ 1 , respectively. All galaxies are of early type ( T<-2 ) according to the RC3 galaxy catalog RC3 . Our sample includes five elliptical and two lenticular galaxies (see Tab. tab:galdat ).
The colour distributions of globular clusters in NGC 2434, 3379, 5846, and 7192 are consistent with a broad single-peak distribution.
Three galaxies of our sample (NGC 1380, NGC 3379, and NGC 5846) are assigned group/cluster membership, while the remaining four galaxies are considered field members.

gmw_00003777.name: NGC 1543
gmw_00003777.overview: NGC 1543 is a barred lenticular galaxy in the Dorado group [arXiv:2405.15363] that features a LINER-like nucleus [arXiv:2009.14731] and a nuclear bar [arXiv:1004.5393]. The galaxy contains an outer ring visible in optical, ultraviolet, and atomic gas emissions [arXiv:1811.11386] that hosts active star formation [arXiv:2405.15363, arXiv:1811.11386].

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 1543 as a Dorado-group barred lenticular with decoupled gas-star kinematics, UV/H-alpha rings, an HI ring, and a rejuvenated nucleus.

quotes.quote: We have fulfilled a detailed long-slit spectroscopic analysis for two SB0 galaxies -- NGC 1533 and NGC 1543, -- belonging to the Dorado group. Our spectral data reveal asymmetric decoupled kinematics of the stars and ionised gas in these barred lenticular galaxies that give evidences for external origin of the gas in the rings. We have calculated the star formation rates in the rings by using the ultraviolet fluxes of the rings corrected for the foreground and intrinsic absorption; and we have estimated parameters of the stellar populations in the inner parts of the galaxies confirming that they are old -- except the nucleus of NGC 1543, which demonstrates signatures of re-juvenation less than 5 Gyr ago.

NGC 1543 looks quite isolated, while NGC 1533 has two close dwarf satellites, IC 2038 and IC 2039, in some 60 kpc from it. Ultraviolet (UV) features of recent star formation were found in NGC 1533 and NGC 1543 long ago. Over this field great efforts were applied by Roberto Rampazzo with co-authors. Firstly, they have used UV data of the spacecraft Swift Trinchieri_Swift,Rampazzo_Swift to discover outer UV-bright rings in these SB0 galaxies -- with the radius of up to R_ ring 50^ in NGC 1533 and up to R_ ring 3^ in NGC 1543. Secondly, narrow-band photometry was undertaken, with the filter including the emission-line complex H + [ NII ] 6583, and the ring structures of NGC 1533 and NGC 1543 were traced with another indicator of recent star formation; the integrated star formation rates (SFR) were estimated as 0.4 per every galaxy, NGC 1533 and NGC 1543 Rampazzo2020 . The cold gas reservoirs feeding this star formation were also found -- by RyanWeber2004 in NGC 1533 and by Murugeshan2019 in NGC 1543, in the latter galaxy the HI ring being coincident with the UV-ring.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1543 as a barred S0 with ring-linked H-alpha complexes, outer-ring emission, and a LINER-like nucleus dominated by non-star-formation processes.

quotes.quote: NGC 1543 The galaxy is considered a S0 with bar and a ring (Type=-2.0 0.8) in HyperLeda . The \ emission follows the bar in the galaxy central region (see Figure NGC1543 ). The \ emission is concentrated in some \ complexes, labeled A, B and C in the figure, along the South East edge of the outer ring. These star formation areas are clearly visible in FUV GALEX observations Marino2011a,Marino2011b,Rampazzo2017 .

\ regions are detected in the outer ring in NGC 1543. In the central 1.5 of NGC 1543, Parkash2019 measured a ratio [ NII ] / =2.004 confirming that, as in the case of NGC 1553, the dominant phenomenon at least in the nucleus, is not SF. Indeed, the galaxy nucleus is classified as a LINER.

sources.summary: The paper portrays NGC 1543 as a barred S0 whose NUV imaging reveals an outer filamentary arm or ring, complex profiles, and lower-than-three NUV Sersic indices.

quotes.quote: Starting from U to W2 a filamentary arm-like/ring structure emerges at the outer edge of this ring (Figure NGC1543-sb top right panel). The NUV luminosity profiles and the values of S ersic fit are shown in the bottom panels of Figure NGC1543-sb . We exclude from the single S ersic fit the ring/arm-like structure in the galaxy outskirts. Notwithstanding, the fit is poor due to the complex structure of the galaxy out to its outskirts (see also the a_4/a profile in Figure a4M2 ). The NUV S ersic indices are lower than 3 and the galaxy nucleus results brighter than the S ersic model in the NUV filters.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1543 as an S0 ring galaxy with optical, UV, and atomic-gas ring emission, likely produced by backsplash passage and collision with a dwarf galaxy.

quotes.quote: A peculiar galaxy in the sample is NGC 1543, where we observe a ring of 1 gas surrounding the central nucleus (see Fig. fig:galaxy-maps ). The ring is also observed in the optical as well as the UV, indicating current star formation. The origin of such a symmetric ring is unclear, however, NGC 1543 is at the very edges of the Dorado group and as such, it is possible that it is a ``backsplash" galaxy, meaning it has passed through the group at least once and along the way collided with a dwarf galaxy, driving the formation of the ring. Simulations support this scenario and indicate that an interaction via collision or drop-through is the most common way such ring galaxies are formed (see for example Elagali et al. elagali18 ). NGC 1543 is classified as an S0 galaxy and does not seem to possess a normal disc.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 1543 as a pseudobulge galaxy, citing There is a prominent nuclear bar; n_b=1.5 , and the bulges is not actively forming stars with M_ 3.6 M_ 8.0 =-1.2 mag.

quotes.quote: NGC 1543 RLBS0../RSB0/a Pseudobulge. There is a prominent nuclear bar. The decomposition yields n_b=1.5 , and the bulges is not actively forming stars with M_ 3.6 M_ 8.0 =-1.2 mag.

gmw_00003778.name: NGC 1559
gmw_00003778.overview: NGC 1559 is a barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0207201] classified as a pseudobulge galaxy with a flocculent spiral structure extending to its center [arXiv:1004.5393]. Located approximately 19 Mpc away [arXiv:1504.02404, arXiv:2401.09581], it features massive spiral arms with strong star formation [arXiv:astro-ph/0207201] and has hosted multiple supernovae [arXiv:1504.02404].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1559 as the host of multiple supernovae and derives a combined distance of about 19.8 Mpc from SN and Tully-Fisher methods.

quotes.quote: SN 2009ib was discovered by the Chilean Automatic Supernova Search (CHASE) project on August 6.30 UT 2009 in NGC 1559 2009ib_felfed . This galaxy have hosted three other SNe, 1984J type II 1984j_discovery , 1986L type II-P 1986l_discovery and 2005df type Ia 2005df_discovery .

The distance to the host galaxy of SN 2009ib, NGC 1559, has been measured several times. The results, however, show a high scatter, falling in the range between 12.6 and 22.0 Mpc (see Sect. sec_ave ).

Considering the TF distance, that of SN 2005df, our EPM result for SN 1986L, and the distance to SN 2009ib determined with EPM and SCM (Table ave_dist ), we conclude the mean of these five results, D=19.8 2.8 Mpc as the distance to NGC 1559.

sources.summary: The paper uses Mira variables in the Type Ia supernova host NGC 1559 to estimate its distance modulus and infer a Mira-based Hubble constant.

quotes.quote: Currently the only SNIa host with observed Mira variables is NGC 1559 Huang2020 so Mira-based Hubble constant measurements are limited primarily by the uncertainty on the properties of the single supernova.

NGC 1559 hosted the Type Ia supernova SN 2005df with peak magnitude m_B=(12.14 0.11) mag Scolnic2018 . Given a distance modulus to NGC 1559, _ 1559 , the Hubble constant is estimated as _ 10 H_0 = 1 5 (m_B+5a_B+25) - 1 5 _ 1559 , where a_B=(0.71273 0.00176) is the SNIa magnitude-redshift intercept as measured by .

Using period--luminosity relations derived from the Gaia DR3 data, the Mira variable sample in the SNIa host galaxy NGC 1559 Huang2020 has been used to measure the distance modulus to this galaxy and in turn estimate the Hubble constant from the SNIa analysis of . The level of C-rich contamination of the NGC 1559 Mira variable sample is significant and leads to fainter zeropoints (higher H_0 ) but its strength can be constrained from the modelling due to the effect increasing C-rich contamination has on flattening the period--luminosity relation.

sources.summary: The atlas describes NGC 1559 as a strong-star-forming spiral with a small east-west bar, very strong bar and disc radio emission, asymmetric polarization, and extended bar-perpendicular magnetic alignment.

quotes.quote: NGC 1559 (Fig. 19) possesses massive spiral arms with strong star formation. The small bar of about 40 \ length is oriented almost east-west. Very strong radio emission originates in the bar and the disc. Polarized emission is strongly asymmetric with peaks near the ends of the bar and magnetic field lines at large angles to the bar (Fig. 26). The region where the magnetic field is strongly aligned and almost perpendicular to the bar's major axis is larger than in NGC 1097.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 1559 as a pseudobulge galaxy, citing A flocculent spiral extends all the way to the center of the galaxy; n_b=.7 , and the bulges is actively forming stars with M_ 3.6 M_ 8.0 =5.4 mag.

quotes.quote: NGC 1559 .SBS6../SBc(s) Pseudobulge. A flocculent spiral extends all the way to the center of the galaxy. The decomposition yields n_b=.7 , and the bulges is actively forming stars with M_ 3.6 M_ 8.0 =5.4 mag.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1559 as the SN 2005df host with a Mira distance modulus, the most distant galaxy where Miras had been studied.

quotes.quote: NGC 1559 determined the distance modulus to NGC 1559, the host of SN 2005df to be _ N1559 = 31.41 0.050 (stat) 0.060 (sys) using a sample of 115 Miras.
At 19.1 1.1 Mpc, NGC 1559 is the most distant galaxy in which Miras have been studied.
Data used for the NGC 1559 study consisted of 10 epochs of WFC3/IR channel F160W observations (GO-15145; PI: Riess) with roughly monthly spacing and a baseline slightly longer than 1 year.

gmw_00003779.name: NGC 1617
gmw_00003779.overview: NGC 1617 is a classical-bulge galaxy classified as Sa(s) [arXiv:1004.5393]. Its bulge is not forming stars [arXiv:1004.5393], but submillimetre observations reveal cold, off-nucleus dust blobs along its major axis [arXiv:1507.08778, arXiv:1601.01206].

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 1617 as a classical-bulge galaxy and reports its central morphology, Sersic index, and mid-infrared star-formation state.

quotes.quote: NGC 1617 .SBS1../Sa(s) Classical Bulge. There is a mild (non-spiral) dust lane slightly off center from the bulge center, otherwise the bulge has a smooth E-like morphology. The decomposition yields n_b=2.1 , and the bulges is not forming stars with M_ 3.6 M_ 8.0 =-1.0 mag.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC1617 as a cool galaxy in the ADF-S field whose submillimetre emission includes dust blobs along its major axis.

quotes.quote: A further galaxy of interest, NGC1617, classified as cool in Table 2, was observed as part of the HerMES survey (Oliver et al., 2012) since it lies in the Akari Deep Field South (ADF-S, Matsuura et al 2011). Figure 9L shows 250 m contours superposed on an -band image (from Hameed and Devereux 1999), while Fig 9R shows 250 m and -band intensity profiles. In this case a significant fraction of the submillimetre emission arises from two dust blobs that lie along the major axis to the north-west.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC1617 as an ADF-S example, noting far-infrared SPIRE emission offset from the nucleus that suggests cold off-nucleus dust missed at shorter wavelengths.

quotes.quote: An idea of what can be achieved by the combination of this data can be seen in Fig. fig:ngc1617 which shows a close-up image of the nearby galaxy NGC1617, visible as the bright blob to the bottom right of the field in Fig. fig:adfs . One thing that is readily apparent from this comparison is the presence of a blob of bright emission to the right of the galaxy's nucleus in the SPIRE images that is not discernible in the AKARI or Spitzer data at shorter wavelengths. This suggests the presence of a region of cold, off nucleus dust that would be missed in observations at wavelengths shorter than 160 m.

gmw_00003780.name: NGC 1637
gmw_00003780.overview: NGC 1637 is a barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:2101.04697] that hosts a pseudobulge [arXiv:1004.5393] and exhibits well-defined spiral arms [arXiv:2101.04697]. It is exceptionally lopsided in both young and old stellar populations [arXiv:0803.0109], with active star formation occurring in its central region [arXiv:1004.5393].

sources.summary: The review calls NGC 1637 an exceptionally lopsided spiral whose optical asymmetry has a corresponding H I velocity anomaly and possible tidal origin.

quotes.quote: One of the most striking cases is that of the spiral galaxy NGC 1637 (Fig. \ f_n1637 ), which is exceptionally lopsided in blue light as well as in the near infrared , indicating that the lopsidedness is present not only in the young stellar population but also in the old stellar disk. In this galaxy there is clear evidence for a kinematical anomaly associated with the morphological asymmetry. Indeed, \ observations with the VLA reveal a peculiarity in the velocity structure in correspondence with the northern anomalous arm. This shows up as a step in the position-velocity diagram along the major axis (at about 60'' in the p-v diagram in Fig. \ f_n1637 ) implying a local deviation of at least 50 from circular motion. This peculiar feature closely resembles the strong kinematic anomaly and the strong \ arm in the southern part of M 101 .

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 1637 as a pseudobulge galaxy and reports its central morphology, Sersic index, and mid-infrared star-formation state.

quotes.quote: NGC 1637 .SXT5../SBc(s) Pseudobulge. A flocculent spiral extends all the way to the central arcsecond, where it becomes elongated and clumpy. The decomposition yields n_b=1.6 , and the bulges is actively forming stars with M_ 3.6 M_ 8.0 =3.4 mag.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 1637 among barred galaxies with well-defined spiral arms and reports a milder but significant increase in molecular-cloud virial parameter within its inner effective radius.

quotes.quote: Bar - Six of our 10 galaxies have stellar bars (NGC 1637, NGC 2903, NGC 3627, NGC 5068, NGC 5643, NGC 6300).
Spiral Arm - These clouds are located within a spiral arm of the galaxy with well defined arms (NGC 0628, NGC 1637, NGC 3627, NGC 5643).
NGC 1637, NGC 2903, NGC 3627, NGC 3621, and NGC 5643 show milder but still significant increased _ vir in the inner 0.5 1 R_e and all of these are barred systems except NGC 5643.

gmw_00003781.name: NGC 1744
gmw_00003781.overview: NGC 1744 is a late-type barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:2212.10751] that lacks a classical bulge [arXiv:1004.5393] and features open, grand-design spiral arms [arXiv:0912.4266]. Active star formation occurs throughout the galaxy [arXiv:0912.4266], including within its long-lived central bar structure [arXiv:2212.10751].

sources.summary: NGC 1744 shows a steep-shallow metallicity gradient with inner-bar spaxels above a single fit; the authors connect this to enhanced bar-region star formation in a long-lived bar.

quotes.quote: In NGC 1744, we fit a single component to measure the global metallicity gradient of 0.021 0.002 dex/kpc (left column of Figure fig:Ograd2 ). All spaxels within the R _ bar region (inner 1.07 kpc) lie above the single linear fit, highlighting the inability for a single fit in describing the metallicity gradient of NGC 1744.

As a result, this gives rise to an enhanced metallicity within the inner region of NGC 1365 and NGC 1744. Similar to NGC 1365, the steep-shallow metallicity gradient for NGC 1744 may also result from enhanced star formation within the bar.

NGC 1744 NGC 1744 is a late-type barred galaxy with a higher inclination 69.9 ; Makarov_2014 than the other galaxies in the study, thus it is difficult to trace the spiral arms. The HII regions are located across the bar as well as the northern and western spiral arms Ryder_1993 . report that NGC 1744 shows no evidence of recent interaction with an unperturbed velocity field. This indicates that the bar in NGC 1744 is not a newly-formed but long-lived structure.

sources.summary: NGC 1744 has FUV/NUV images similar to R-band and H-alpha data, a bar with open grand-design arms, visible star-forming regions, and M33-like radial color trends.

quotes.quote: Both the FUV and NUV images of NGC 1744 are very similar to the R-band and H _ images in Meurer06 . The galaxy has a bar from which grand design, open spiral arms depart. Star forming regions are visible in all parts of the galaxy.

The general trend of the (FUV-NUV) radial profiles in LGG 127 spirals, in particular that of NGC 1744 and NGC 1792, is not very dissimilar from that of M33. A possible signature of past interaction events is the presence of a bar structure, seen in LGG 127's, NGC 1744 and ESO305-009 which show in addition open arms, and multiple arms, as NGC 1808 in the same group.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 1744 as a no-bulge/pseudobulge case and reports its central morphology, Sersic index, and mid-infrared star-formation state.

quotes.quote: NGC 1744 .SBS7../SBcd(s) No bulge/Pseudobulge. The ``bulge'' is probably better described as a nuclear star cluster. Nonetheless, a decomposition yields n_b=0.5 , and the bulges is actively forming stars with M_ 3.6 M_ 8.0 =5.2 mag.

gmw_00003782.name: NGC 2500
gmw_00003782.overview: NGC 2500 is a low-surface-brightness spiral galaxy [arXiv:1905.09020] that belongs to a galaxy quartet [arXiv:1509.07094]. It features a short bar [arXiv:1509.07094], a pseudobulge [arXiv:1004.5393], and active star formation concentrated in its nuclear region and northern spiral arm [arXiv:1509.07094, arXiv:1004.5393].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2500 as a quartet member with diffuse emission, a short bar, nuclear and northern-arm star formation, and observable vertical-velocity changes across spiral arms.

quotes.quote: NGC 2500 belongs to a quartet of galaxies 1994cag..book.....S . 2008MNRAS.390..466E find diffuse \ emission in their observed \ maps, in agreement with 2004A&A...414...23J , where its short bar is almost aligned with its minor kinematical axis. There are no individual works for this galaxy, mainly found as part of surveys, e.g. 2008MNRAS.390..466E or 2004A&A...414...23J . In the HST/WFPC2+F450W image, Figure fig1 , it is observed that the star formation is mainly concentrated in the nuclear region and its well-defined, northern spiral arm. The southern arm is fainter and, globally, the southern region of this galaxy appears more flocculent.

Some examples of such patterns are found, for example in NGC 2500, between 2.5 and 2 kpc at PA = 24^ , V_Z goes from 10 to 20 . Again, just after the spiral arm, the gas flows up to nearly 30 . At PA = 40.7^ , in the central part of the slit, V_Z are more pronounced but they are also noisier due to the faint .

sources.summary: The paper uses HST imaging of NGC 2500 to search for ULX X-1 counterparts, describing the galaxy as a low-surface-brightness spiral at 10 Mpc with two catalogued ULXs.

quotes.quote: In this work, we present the results of a search for optical identification of one of the ULXs (X-1) of the galaxy NGC 2500 by using the archival images of HST /WFC3/UVIS camera with various filters. NGC 2500 is a spiral galaxy (type SB(rs)d) with a low surface brightness at a distance of 10 Mpc ( 1988ngc..book.....T ). Two ULXs were catalogued by in this galaxy. X-1 is located 52 ^ away from the center with a Chandra coordinates, R.A. = 08^ h 01^ m 48^ s .10 , Dec. = + 50^ 43 54 60 having an unabsorbed X-ray luminosity of 5 10 ^ 39 erg s ^ 1 in the 0.3 10 keV energy band.

Archival data of NGC 2500 from HST /WFC3/UVIS were utilized to search for the optical properties of ULX X-1. The observation log given in Table tab1 . The three color SDSS image of the NGC 2500 with the approximate position of ULX X-1 are shown in Figure Aksaker_fig1 .

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 2500 as a pseudobulge galaxy and reports its central morphology, Sersic index, and mid-infrared star-formation state.

quotes.quote: NGC 2500 .SBT7../Sc(s) Pseudobulge. The bulge is comprised of several clumps of stars, reminiscent of a very late-type disk galaxy. The decomposition yields n_b=1.7 , and the bulges is actively forming stars with M_ 3.6 M_ 8.0 =0.4 mag.

gmw_00003783.name: ESO 509-IG066
gmw_00003783.overview: ESO 509-IG066 is a merging galaxy pair at a redshift of 0.034 with a nuclear separation of 11 kpc [arXiv:1710.03233]. The system hosts a dual active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1710.03233], where the eastern nucleus exhibits a cone-shaped outflow and a surrounding star-forming ring [arXiv:2402.02107].

sources.summary: The paper studies ESO 509-IG066 as an 11 kpc dual-AGN galaxy pair, finding one nucleus faded dramatically and merger-driven gas kinematics may affect black-hole accretion.

quotes.quote: We analyze the evolution of the dual AGN in ESO 509-IG066, a galaxy pair located at z=0.034 whose nuclei are separated by 11 kpc. Previous observations with \ on this dual AGN found evidence for two moderately obscured ( 10^ 22 ) X-ray luminous ( 10^ 43 ) nuclear sources. We present an analysis of subsequent , \ and \ observations that show one source has dropped in flux by a factor of 10 between 2004 and 2011, which could be explained by either an increase in the absorbing column or an intrinsic fading of the central engine possibly due to a decrease in mass accretion. Both of these scenarios are predicted by galaxy merger simulations.

However, new Keck/LRIS optical spectroscopy reveals a previously unreported broad \ line which is highly unlikely to be visible under the extreme absorption scenario. We therefore conclude that the black hole in this nucleus has undergone a dramatic drop in accretion rate. From AO-assisted near-infrared integral-field spectroscopy of the other nucleus, we find evidence that the galaxy merger is having a direct effect on the kinematics of the gas close to the nucleus of the galaxy, providing a direct observational link between the galaxy merger and the mass accretion rate on to the black hole.

sources.summary: The paper flags ESO509-IG066 as AGN-dominated in WISE color, masks its western nucleus, and finds UV-deficient eastern emission with a possible outflow-fed star-forming ring.

quotes.quote: We found that only MRK 739 and the western galaxy of the ESO 509-IG066 system are above the W1 - W2 cutoff of 0.5. As the AGN is not resolved in our images, we have created an aperture with PSF size with a radius of 3 pixels and then masked both the nuclei of MRK 739 and the western nucleus of ESO 509-IG066 using photutlies library in Python.

carlos2020 have detected a cone-shaped [ O III ] outflow in the eastern nuclei of ESO509-IG066. Our FUV image shows UV deficiency in the same nucleus and SFCs distributed in a tentative ring surrounding the nucleus (Figure eso_ring ). It is possible that outflowing materials is forming stars in the ring.

gmw_00003784.name: Mrk 1
gmw_00003784.overview: Mrk 1 is a Seyfert galaxy [arXiv:0905.3349] that hosts a double-peaked water megamaser [arXiv:2202.03437] with both disk and outflow components [arXiv:1910.14314]. It forms a pair with NGC 451 [arXiv:1905.12693] and exhibits a radio jet misaligned with its maser disk [arXiv:1902.05545].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 449 as a maser Seyfert with extended collimated emission, misaligned ionization species, circular H velocity, and central stellar-population structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 449 (Mrk 1) The galaxy is well known to possess a prominent water maser in its nucleus Braatz94 . No evidence of a hidden Seyfert 1 nucleus was found, in either polarised light Kay94 or in the infrared VeillIR . MulObs reported [ O iii ] emission extending to 3 kpc, co-aligned with both the galaxy major axis, PA =83 and 20 cm radio emission on the scale of 30 pc Kukula99 . Stellar-population synthesis modelling of long-slit data of 3 A resolution performed by Raimann03 revealed mostly intermediate-age stellar populations ( 1 Gyr) in the central kpc and an increasingly dominant old population ( 10 Gyr) outside this radius.

We confirm strongly collimated emission in [ O iii ] , H ii , and [ N ii ] extending to radii of at least 3 kpc (Fig. Mrk1maps ). In the other observed lines, the S/N is insufficient to obtain reliable data to these distances. We found a misalignment between the emission in [ O iii ] ( PA 65 ) and the low-ionisation species ( PA 85 ). The velocity map in H suggests circular rotation.

sources.summary: The paper finds an extended ionized cloud near Mkn 1, associates it with the Mkn 1-NGC 451 pair, and argues the cloud is photoionized by the Seyfert AGN.

quotes.quote: Of 24 Seyfert galaxies, we find one extended emission feature, a discrete cloud projected 12 kpc from the center of Mkn 1 and spanning a transverse extent of 8 kpc. In addition, we identify several potential ``emission-line dots" (ELdots), compact objects which may be outlying, relatively isolated star-forming regions. Optical spectroscopy of the Mkn 1 cloud confirms its redshift association with the Mkn 1 - NGC 451 galaxy pair, shows it to closely match the kinematics of nearby H I, and reveals emission-line ratios requiring photoionization by the AGN at roughly the direct observed luminosity of the nucleus..

Relation to circumnuclear gas and H I present integral-field spectroscopy of the central regions of Mkn 1, showing resolved emission lines across a region spanning about 20 along the projected major axis of the inner disc of Mkn 1. Using the [ O III ] /H ratio to identify potential ionization cones shows narrow high-ionization regions to NE and SW. Outside the nuclear emission extending to about =4 \ , the possible ionization cones are seen between position angles 50--85 ^ with the highest ionization along PA 65 ^ , slightly misaligned with the stellar distribution.

sources.summary: The paper discusses Mrk 1 as a double-peaked maser galaxy whose asymmetric red and blue maser kinematics resemble simulated binary-black-hole signatures.

quotes.quote: Some maser spectra show the presence of a double peak instead of a triple peak, whose physical nature is still not completely understood. In some double-peaked systems such as Mrk 1, the red- and blue-shifted masers show very different kinematics, with the kinematics of the redshifted masers being far more complicated and inconsistent with Keplerian rotation, and their speeds being systematically lower than those of the blueshifted masers. Both these properties are difficult to explain by simple models.

It was recently found that kuo2020 the galaxy Mrk 1 exhibits a double peaked structure of masing particles. Depending on the choice of the gravitational centre, the blue-shifted masers may be consistent with a Keplerian curve, with the red-shifted ones showing evidence of an offset and also being scattered around lower values of line-of-sight velocities. To explore this effect in the simulations, we split the masing particles into red and blue subsets to match the criterion adopted in the observations of Mrk 1, i.e. v_ LOS > 40 km/s and v_ LOS < -120 km/s respectively.

sources.summary: The paper reports a centrally peaked [ FeII ] line-to-continuum profile in Mrk 1, co-rotating disk gas, and marked species-dependent velocity-dispersion differences across its central kiloparsec.

quotes.quote: The H _2 line-to-continuum ratio is constant or drops significantly towards the nucleus in most galaxies, except NGC 2273, NGC 5273, and NGC 7450. For Br , in contrast, the ratio peaks towards the nucleus in many galaxies, consistent with stronger ionization mechanisms towards the active nucleus. These two results both explain the observed central drop of the H _2 /Br ratio. Of the three emission lines, the [ FeII ] line-to-continuum ratio shows the most distinct radial variations. Its profile is centrally peaked in Mrk 1, Mrk 3, NGC 262, NGC 1320, NGC 2273, NGC 3081, NGC 5273, and NGC 7450. Several sources show two maxima (or a central drop, e.g. NGC 1667, NGC 3185, NGC 4151, NGC 4253, NGC 4388, and NGC 5506). Finally, Mrk 1066, NGC 931, NGC 2110, NGC 2992, and NGC 4051 show peculiar distributions, e.g. the distribution in NGC 2110 shows three separate peaks.

However, the gas velocity curves are similar to those of the stars, which define the galaxy disk. This is the case in Mrk 1, Mrk 1066, NGC 262, NGC 931, NGC 1667, NGC 2110, NGC 2273, NGC 3081, NGC 3185, NGC 4388, NGC 5273, and NGC 5506. In these galaxies, all three emission lines display a velocity curve that follows that of the stars and is reminiscent of rotation, thus strongly implying that the line-emitting gas is located in the disk of the galaxy and rotating with the disk. Other galaxies (Mrk 3, NGC 1320, NGC 2992, NGC 4051, NGC 4151, NGC 4253, and NGC 7450) display velocity curves different from the stellar velocity curve in one or more emission lines, which may be a signature of non-rotational motion, either in- or outflows or other non-planar motion of the gas.

The radial dispersion profiles in Mrk 3, Mrk 1066, NGC 2110, NGC 2273, NGC 4051, NGC 4151, NGC 4253, NGC 5273, and NGC 5506 have a central peak, which indicates a higher kinetic gas temperature in the nucleus. Either the ISM is more turbulent (possibly caused by a jet- or wind-driven outflow), and/or more intensely heated by the incident radiation field. In either case, a peaked dispersion distribution is a strong indication for the AGN governing the physical state of the gas at the nucleus. The dispersion peak is most often present in [ FeII ] (indicating dust destruction close to the nucleus), and/or in Br (indicating a strong radiation field), while the H _2 line shows the least-pronounced central rise. Examples for flat velocity dispersion profiles are NGC 3081, NGC 3185, and NGC 4388, which show no significant change over up to 1.2 kpc in diameter. The velocity dispersion in all three sources is of order 100 km/s, well within the range observed for star forming regions, which is consistent with star formation being the dominant the gas excitation mechanism. Finally, the dispersion profiles in Mrk 1, NGC 262, NGC 931, NGC 1320, NGC 1667, NGC 2992, and NGC 7450 show marked differences between the three emission lines, with variations of 100 km/s or more at a given radius. This indicates that the ionized atomic and molecular gas is responding differently to the central AGN in these sources, perhaps due to the presences of asymmetric energy input (a jet or shocks) together with a stratified medium, e.g. a thin molecular disk in a thick ionized medium.

sources.summary: The paper detects compact and extended radio continuum in Mrk 0001, compares its jet direction with the maser disk, and finds the maser and continuum positions consistent within uncertainties.

quotes.quote: Five out of 18 galaxies observed were detected at 8 or higher levels (Mrk 0001, Mrk 1210, Mrk 1419, NGC 2273 and UGC 3193). While all these sources are known to have maser disks, four of these five sources exhibit a maser disk with known orientation.

Mrk 0001 We observed compact radio emission in our 33 GHz radio maps with a PA of 120 2 . In the C-band VLBA data we see extended emission, also in the northwest-southeast direction. The maser disk has a PA of 6.4 10 and its rotation axis is by 17 11 misaligned from the jet propagation direction.

sources.summary: The paper treats Mrk 1 as a double-peaked water-megamaser active galaxy with VLBI kinematics too disturbed for robust black-hole mass measurement and with both disk and outflow components.

quotes.quote: We present VLBI images and kinematics of water maser emission in three active galaxies: NGC 5728, Mrk 1, and IRAS 08452 0011.

NGC 5728 and Mrk 1 display double-peaked spectra and VLBI imaging reveal complicated gas kinematics that do not allow for a robust determination of black hole mass. We show evidence that the masers in NGC 5728 are in a wind while the Mrk 1 maser system has both disk and outflow components.

For Mrk 1, we observed four IF pairs in the phase-referencing observation and two IF pairs in the self-calibration observation. Two IF bands were used to cover the blueshifted and redshifted masers, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper presents VLA and VLBI radio imaging of Mrk 1, finding an unresolved arcsecond source, a somewhat resolved mas-scale component, weak southern emission, and a maximum radio extent of 30 pc.

quotes.quote: Mrk 1 (0113+328) Y : Mrk 1 is unresolved. The total flux density is similar to that presented by and is less than the value of 38 mJy found by . suggest that, along with an unresolved component, there is a faint ( 2 mJy) extension 0.4 arcsec to the south. This faint extension is not detected in our map. Since do not show a map for this source, it is difficult to judge whether we should have detected it.

gmw_00003785.name: NGC 7682
gmw_00003785.overview: NGC 7682 is a Seyfert 2 galaxy [arXiv:1002.3152] that is interacting with NGC 7683 [arXiv:1002.3152].
