Its active nucleus shows no X-ray variability [arXiv:2007.02616], while VLBI observations reveal extended radio structure to the south and southeast [arXiv:astro-ph/0406597].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7682 as an interacting Seyfert 2 with kiloparsec ionized gas, optical and near-infrared coronal lines, and asymmetric extended [Ne V] emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 7682 NGC 7682 is a Seyfert 2 galaxy (Huchra Burg 1992) in interaction with NGC 7683 (Arp 1966). Ionised gas in H + [ N ii ] and [ O iii ] is detected on scales of kiloparsecs in this galaxy (Brodie et al. 1987; Durret 1994). In the NIR, only K-band spectroscopy was previously reported by Imanishi Alonso-Herrero (2004).

The only optical coronal line observed in the STIS spectrum of NGC 7682 is [ Ne v ] . No iron lines or previous reports in the literature of higher ionisation lines in this wavelength interval were found. In the NIR, however, NGC 7682 shows [ Si vi ] 1.963 m and [ S viii ] 0.991 m (Riffel et al. 2007), whose ionisation potential is 167 and 281 eV, respectively. Though weak, the presence of these latter features indicates that, in a photoionisation scenario, high-energy photons reach the NLR. In addition to the unresolved nuclear [ Ne v ] emission, this coronal line displays a highly asymmetric extended morphology, observed up to 150 pc NE and 50 pc SW.

sources.summary: The paper fits NGC 7682 spectra from XMM-Newton and NuSTAR, finds no variability, and reports consistent Compton-thin line-of-sight column densities.

quotes.quote: NGC 7682: We fit the \ and \ spectra of this source, and found no indication of variability. The source is detected out to 7 keV in the \ observation. We fit the spectra using all four models. All models result in consistent and Cth column densities in our LOS. Both FPMA and FPMB spectra show an excess at 9 keV that we modeled using a Gaussian emission line (with a best-fit energy of 9.5 0.3 keV ).

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 7682 unresolved on arcsecond scales with its radio peak at the optical nucleus, while VLBI shows southern and southeastern extended structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 7682 (2326+032) Y : Our map is unresolved, as was also true of the image of . Combining our flux density with the 8.4 GHz flux density given by (obtained in June 1991) yields a spectral index _ 5 GHz ^ 8.4 GHz 1.1 . The radio peak coincides with the optical nucleus Clements83 within the errors. G : The image shows clear evidence for extended structure to the south (P.A. 180 ^ ) and south-east (P.A. 120 ^ ).

gmw_00003786.name: VIK J2318 3113
gmw_00003786.overview: VIK J2318 3113 is a radio-loud quasar at a redshift of z = 6.44 [arXiv:2101.11371, arXiv:2205.05859]. Its radio emission is dominated by a compact, parsec-scale active galactic nucleus core [arXiv:2205.05859] powered by a young relativistic jet [arXiv:2405.03741] that may exhibit flux density variability [arXiv:2101.11371, arXiv:2203.08142].

sources.summary: The paper reports VIK J2318 3113 as a z=6.44 radio-loud quasar, the most distant known at the time, with possible variability suggesting a jet or blazar nature.

quotes.quote: We report the 888 MHz radio detection in the Rapid ASKAP Continuum Survey (RACS) of VIK J2318 3113, a z =6.44 quasar. Its radio luminosity (1.2 10^ 26 W Hz ^ 1 at 5 GHz) compared to the optical luminosity (1.8 10^ 24 W Hz ^ 1 at 4400 ) makes it the most distant radio-loud quasar observed so far, with a radio loudness R 70 (R =L_ 5GHz /L_ 4400 ). Moreover, the high bolometric luminosity of the source (L _ bol =7.4 10^ 46 erg s ^ 1 ) suggests the presence of a supermassive black hole with a high mass ( 6 10 ^8 M _ ) at a time when the Universe was younger than a billion years. Combining the new radio data from RACS with previous ASKAP observations at the same frequency, we found that the flux density of the source may have varied by a factor of 2, which could suggest the presence of a relativistic jet oriented towards the line of sight, that is, a blazar nature. However, currently available radio data do not allow us to firmly characterise the orientation of the source.

In this Letter we report the radio detection of the z =6.444 0.005 QSO VIKING J231818.35 311346.3 (hereafter VIK J2318 3113; Decarli2018 ). With a relatively bright radio flux density ( 1.4 mJy at 888 MHz), this source is the most distant RL QSO observed to date. VIK J2318 3113 was discovered from the near-IR (NIR) VIKING survey with the dropout technique, and its redshift was confirmed with both X-Shooter in the NIR and the Atacama Large Millimetre/submillimetre Array (ALMA) in the submillimetre Decarli2018, Yang2020b .

sources.summary: The paper presents first VLBI imaging of VIK J2318-3113, finding a z=6.44 radio-loud quasar dominated by compact parsec-scale AGN core emission.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The source peak position is at RA = 23^ h 18^ m 18.3684^ s (with an estimated uncertainty of 0.0004^ ), Dec= 31^ 13^ 46.4030^ (uncertainty 0.0008^ ).

quotes.quote: \ is a recently discovered quasar at z = 6.44 that had not been imaged with VLBI before the current work. Here we present the first VLBI imaging results of this high-redshift quasar, with the aim of corroborating its high-resolution appearance with the physical model of the object. We carried out VLBI phase-referencing observations of \ using the Very Long Baseline Array at two frequencies, 1.6 and 4.7 GHz, and obtained the first view at the radio structure on the milliarcsecond scale.

The source was clearly detected at 1.6 GHz. We found that almost all of its radio emission comes from the parsec-scale core region. Our dual-frequency observations constrain the spectral index and brightness temperature of the radio core. Its properties are similar to those of other known high-redshift radio-loud AGNs.
The source peak position is at RA = 23^ h 18^ m 18.3684^ s (with an estimated uncertainty of 0.0004^ ), Dec= 31^ 13^ 46.4030^ (uncertainty 0.0008^ ).

sources.summary: The paper presents VIK J2318 31 as a z=6.44 radio-loud QSO with broad radio coverage indicating a compact, very young jet and a near-Eddington SMBH.

quotes.quote: In particular, in , we were able to uncover the RL nature of the z=6.44 QSO VIKING J231818.3 311346 (hereafter VIK J2318 31) thanks to the first data release of the RACS-low survey, at 888 MHz McConnell2020 . Being one of the most distant RL QSOs currently known, the characterisation of VIK J2318 31 and the other few similar sources in this redshift range can provide more stringent constraints on the evolution of SMBHs hosted in RL systems than the majority of the other sources in this class. Moreover, in , by combining radio observations of the Galaxy And Mass Assembly (GAMA; Driver2011 ) 23 ^ h field, where VIK J2318 31 is located, we constrained its radio spectrum over two orders of magnitudes in frequency ( 0.1--10 GHz, observed, corresponding to 0.7--75 GHz in the rest frame). Based on the shape of the radio spectrum and on its variability at 888 MHz, we found that this system is likely hosting a young radio jet with a projected size of the order of milli-arcsec (mas; 5--10 pc).

As described in the previous sections, VIK J2318 31 hosts a 8 10^8 M_ SMBH accreting close to the Eddington limit. The radio luminosity and spectral shape of this source suggests that the radio emission is powered by young relativistic jets (see also the discussion in Ighina2022b ). Indeed, the compact radio morphology Zhang2022 together with the presence of a turnover around 650 MHz (observed frame) in the radio spectrum are typical sings of young radio jets (see, e.g., Odea2021 ). Even though it is not possible to confirm the origin of the turnover in the radio spectrum of VIK J2318 31, due to the lack of information at very low frequencies ( 200 MHz), it seems unlikely to be generated by SSA, since it would require an emitting region orders of magnitude smaller than what is typically found in radio peaked sources e.g., Orienti2008,Orienti2014 .

sources.summary: The paper constrains the z=6.44 radio-loud QSO VIKING J231818.3 311346, finding a steep high-frequency radio spectrum, flatter low-frequency behavior, young radio jets, and marginal variability.

quotes.quote: The recent detection of the quasi-stellar object (QSO) VIKING J231818.3 311346 (hereafter ) at redshift z=6.44 in the Rapid ASKAP Continuum Survey (RACS) uncovered its radio-loud nature, making it one of the most distant known to date in this class. By using data from several radio surveys of the Galaxy and Mass Assembly 23 ^ h field and from dedicated follow-up, we were able to constrain the radio spectrum of in the observed range 0.1--10 GHz. At high frequencies (0.888--5.5 GHz in the observed frame) the QSO presents a steep spectrum ( _ =1.24, with S_ ^ _ ), while at lower frequencies (0.4--0.888 GHz in the observed frame) it is nearly flat. The overall spectrum can be modelled by either a curved function with a rest-frame turnover around 5 GHz, or with a smoothly varying double power law that is flat below a rest-frame break frequency of about 20 GHz and which significantly steepens above it.

gmw_00003787.name: HS 1442+4250
gmw_00003787.overview: HS 1442+4250 is a star-forming galaxy characterized by metal-poor gas [arXiv:2111.11508]. It is a high-equivalent-width nebular C IV emitter that displays clear stellar absorption from massive O stars [arXiv:2111.11508].

sources.summary: The paper includes HS 1442+4250 as a high-equivalent-width nebular C IV emitter with metal-poor gas, clear stellar absorption, and one of the sample's highest inferred photospheric metallicities.

quotes.quote: In particular, we selected our sample to include the two highest equivalent width nebular emitters in this doublet J104457 at 11 and HS 1442+4250 at 4 ; bergCarbonOxygenAbundances2016,senchynaExtremelyMetalpoorGalaxies2019 ; two of the systems with the highest gas-phase metallicities still powering nebular emission in C 4 SB 2 and 82, at 12+ O/H 7.8 - 7.9 ; senchynaUltravioletSpectraExtreme2017 ; and two metal-poor systems with extremely high equivalent width optical line emission yet relatively modest C 4 equivalent widths <4 J082555 and J120202; bergCarbonOxygenAbundances2016,bergChemicalEvolutionCarbon2019 . These systems were targeted with G160M/1533 spectroscopy with allocations of 5--10 orbits each over the course of two programs (GO:15646 and GO:15881, PI: Stark) which we describe in Section sec:uvobs . The basic properties of this sample of emitters are summarized in Table tab:basicprop , and described in the remainder of this section.

In particular, consider the strongest nebular emitter in our sample and known locally among star-forming galaxies at all, J104457. This galaxy is clearly more metal-poor in both stars (at Z 3.1 ) and gas (at 12+ O/H =7.5 ) than the next-strongest emitter in our sample, HS 1442+4250 ( Z 2.9 , 12+ O/H =7.7 ). This is consistent with this picture in which a harder stellar ionizing radiation field (and potentially hotter gas temperature) serve to boost this emission at lower metallicities. However, the other two lowest-O/H galaxies in our sample J082555 and J120202 present nearly identical O/H and H equivalent widths to J104457 (Table tab:basicprop ), yet power at only 2.1 and 1.5 ; 5 times smaller than J104457.

Strong stellar P-Cygni absorption is detected in SB 82 at 2.4 equivalent width in depth and in SB 2 and HS 1442+4250 with equivalent widths of 0.5 (Table tab:uvmeas ; integrated from 1530--1550 after subtracting Gaussian fits to the ISM/MW and nebular lines and with linear continuum determined in the windows [ 1490, 1520 ] and [ 1560, 1590 ] ). The other three systems with lower gas-phase metallicities are not formally detected via this full absorption trough integration at their achieved S/N (Table tab:uvmeas ), but hints of broad stellar absorption at <1 EW in depth are still visible in their spectra (see also Fig. fig:cbs99_wind ). This high-velocity P-Cygni feature is produced by outflowing gas in the expanding atmospheres of the massive O stars powering the UV continuum in these galaxies.

gmw_00003788.name: SB 2
gmw_00003788.overview: SB 2 is an extended blue compact dwarf galaxy located approximately 18.7 Mpc away [arXiv:1706.00881]. The galaxy is dominated by four star-forming clumps in a linear configuration [arXiv:1909.10574, arXiv:1706.00881], with its brightest central region hosting an accreting intermediate-mass black hole [arXiv:2211.13179] and a coincident X-ray source [arXiv:1909.10574].

sources.summary: The paper describes SB 2 as one of the higher gas-metallicity systems still powering nebular C IV, with detected P-Cygni absorption and among the highest photospheric metallicities in the sample.

quotes.quote: In particular, we selected our sample to include the two highest equivalent width nebular emitters in this doublet J104457 at 11 and HS 1442+4250 at 4 ; bergCarbonOxygenAbundances2016,senchynaExtremelyMetalpoorGalaxies2019 ; two of the systems with the highest gas-phase metallicities still powering nebular emission in C 4 SB 2 and 82, at 12+ O/H 7.8 - 7.9 ; senchynaUltravioletSpectraExtreme2017 ; and two metal-poor systems with extremely high equivalent width optical line emission yet relatively modest C 4 equivalent widths <4 J082555 and J120202; bergCarbonOxygenAbundances2016,bergChemicalEvolutionCarbon2019 . These systems were targeted with G160M/1533 spectroscopy with allocations of 5--10 orbits each over the course of two programs (GO:15646 and GO:15881, PI: Stark) which we describe in Section sec:uvobs . The basic properties of this sample of emitters are summarized in Table tab:basicprop , and described in the remainder of this section.

Strong stellar P-Cygni absorption is detected in SB 82 at 2.4 equivalent width in depth and in SB 2 and HS 1442+4250 with equivalent widths of 0.5 (Table tab:uvmeas ; integrated from 1530--1550 after subtracting Gaussian fits to the ISM/MW and nebular lines and with linear continuum determined in the windows [ 1490, 1520 ] and [ 1560, 1590 ] ). The other three systems with lower gas-phase metallicities are not formally detected via this full absorption trough integration at their achieved S/N (Table tab:uvmeas ), but hints of broad stellar absorption at <1 EW in depth are still visible in their spectra (see also Fig. fig:cbs99_wind ). This high-velocity P-Cygni feature is produced by outflowing gas in the expanding atmospheres of the massive O stars powering the UV continuum in these galaxies.

The highest photospheric metallicities we infer in our sample occur in SB 2 ( Z = -2.93^ +0.19 _ 0.15 ), HS 1442+4250 ( 2.94^ +0.10 _ 0.15 ), and SB 82 ( 3.00^ +0.05 _ 0.04 ). These systems reveal the clearest detections of photospheric absorption lines (Fig. fig:photo1450comp , fig:cbs99_photo ), with inferred model `wiggles' notably more prominent than those in the three other systems. These systems also reveal the three highest abundances of gas-phase O/H; with the other three lower-O/H galaxies all constrained to confidently (at the 84 confidence level) Z<-3.0 ( <6 solar).

sources.summary: The paper maps nuclear coronal-line emission in the metal-poor dwarf galaxy SDSS J094401.87 003832.1 and interprets the persistent emission as evidence for an accreting intermediate-mass black hole.

quotes.quote: Using MUSE/VLT observations, we report the detection of [ Fe 10 ] 6374 coronal line emission and a broad H line in the nucleus of SDSS J094401.87 003832.1, a nearby ( z=0.0049 ) metal poor dwarf galaxy almost 500 times less massive than the LMC. Unlike the emission from the lower ionization nebular lines, the [ Fe 10 ] 6374 emission is compact and centered on the brightest nuclear source, with a spatial extent of 100 pc, similar to that seen in well-known AGNs. The [ Fe 10 ] luminosity is 10^ 37  erg s ^ 1 , within the range seen in previously identified AGNs in the dwarf galaxy population.

The [ Fe 10 ] emission has persisted over the roughly 19 year time period between the SDSS and MUSE observations, ruling out supernovae as the origin for the emission. The FWHM of the broad component of the H line is 446 17  km s ^ 1 and its luminosity is  1.5 10^ 38  erg s ^ 1 , corresponding to a black hole mass of  3150  M _ , in line with its stellar mass if virial mass relations and black hole-galaxy scaling relations apply in this mass regime . These observations, together with previously reported multi-wavelength observations, can most plausibly be explained by the presence of an accreting intermediate mass black hole in a primordial galaxy analog.
J0944 0038, also known as MCG+00-25-010, is a metal deficient blue compact galaxy that has previously been reported to display signatures of high ionization emission.

sources.summary: The paper studies CGCG007-025 as a compact dwarf undergoing a galaxy-wide starburst, mapping MUSE gas chemistry, oxygen abundance, and elevated central density.

quotes.quote: This work focuses on the gas chemical composition of CGCG007-025. This compact dwarf is undergoing a galaxy wide star forming burst, whose spatial behaviour has been observed by VLT/MUSE.

The 8 ionic abundances analyzed show a spatial normal distribution with a 0.1 dex , where only half this value can be explained by the uncertainty in the measurements. The oxygen abundance distribution is 12+log(O/H)=7.88 0.11 . The T_ e [ SIII ] and ne [ SII ] are also normally distributed. However, in the central and brightest region, the ne [ SII ] is almost thrice the mean galaxy value.

sources.summary: The paper describes SB 2 as a clumpy dwarf whose brightest region shows nebular emission and a coincident X-ray source, while another source is offset.

quotes.quote: SB 2 is a dwarf galaxy at a distance of 19 Mpc dominated by four star-forming clumps. SDSS and Keck/ESI spectroscopy of the brightest of these clumps reveals prominent nebular emission in the 4686 line, with /H =0.0130 0.0006 senchynaUltravioletSpectraExtreme2017 and a star formation rate from the Balmer lines of 10^ 1.61 M_ /year . This system was observed with a 15 ks observation (ObsID: 19463, PI: Mezcua, not yet published), revealing an X-ray source coincident with the optical clump observed with ESI ( r_ sep =0.3'' ) with a computed luminosity of =4.0 1.7 38 erg/s.

The optical clump to the southeast of the spectroscopically-targeted region also has an associated X-ray detection, with an even higher luminosity =5.3 0.5 39 erg/s; but since this second source is 3.2 '' away, we conclude it cannot contribute to the observed nebular flux and ignore it in this analysis. This X-ray luminosity corresponds to a production efficiency of 1.6 0.7 40 erg/s per unit M_ /year .

sources.summary: The appendix describes SB 2 as an extended blue compact dwarf with multiple bright linear components; the SDSS and COS apertures target the central component at a distance of 18.7 Mpc.

quotes.quote: SB 2: (SDSS J094401.87-003832.1, MCG+00-25-010, CGCG 007-025, SDSS Plate-MJD-Fiber 266-51630-100) An extended BCD with at least four bright components in a linear configuration in SDSS imaging. The SDSS and COS spectral apertures were centred on the brightest central object. We compute a distance of 18.7 Mpc. At this distance, the aperture radius corresponds to 113 pc.

gmw_00003789.name: SB 82
gmw_00003789.overview: SB 82 is a starburst galaxy that hosts very metal-poor gas [arXiv:2111.11508]. It is characterized by nebular C IV emission, strong P-Cygni absorption, and clear detections of photospheric absorption lines [arXiv:2111.11508].

sources.summary: The paper describes SB 82 as the highest gas-metallicity C IV emitter in the sample, with strong P-Cygni absorption and one of the clearest photospheric absorption detections.

quotes.quote: In particular, we selected our sample to include the two highest equivalent width nebular emitters in this doublet J104457 at 11 and HS 1442+4250 at 4 ; bergCarbonOxygenAbundances2016,senchynaExtremelyMetalpoorGalaxies2019 ; two of the systems with the highest gas-phase metallicities still powering nebular emission in C 4 SB 2 and 82, at 12+ O/H 7.8 - 7.9 ; senchynaUltravioletSpectraExtreme2017 ; and two metal-poor systems with extremely high equivalent width optical line emission yet relatively modest C 4 equivalent widths <4 J082555 and J120202; bergCarbonOxygenAbundances2016,bergChemicalEvolutionCarbon2019 . These systems were targeted with G160M/1533 spectroscopy with allocations of 5--10 orbits each over the course of two programs (GO:15646 and GO:15881, PI: Stark) which we describe in Section sec:uvobs . The basic properties of this sample of emitters are summarized in Table tab:basicprop , and described in the remainder of this section.

The resulting direct- T_e oxygen abundances (Table tab:basicprop ) confirm that these emitters harbor very metal-poor gas. The values range from 12+ O/H =7.44 in the lowest-metallicity case (J082555) up to 7.93 in the highest (SB 82). This corresponds to a range of 5--15 the solar photospheric oxygen value, with all but SB 2 and SB 82 falling below 10 and thus in the realm of designated extremely metal-poor galaxies (XMPs).

The highest photospheric metallicities we infer in our sample occur in SB 2 ( Z = -2.93^ +0.19 _ 0.15 ), HS 1442+4250 ( 2.94^ +0.10 _ 0.15 ), and SB 82 ( 3.00^ +0.05 _ 0.04 ). These systems reveal the clearest detections of photospheric absorption lines (Fig. fig:photo1450comp , fig:cbs99_photo ), with inferred model `wiggles' notably more prominent than those in the three other systems. These systems also reveal the three highest abundances of gas-phase O/H; with the other three lower-O/H galaxies all constrained to confidently (at the 84 confidence level) Z<-3.0 ( <6 solar).

sources.summary: The paper examines Mrk 193 as a high-optical-depth object whose helium-abundance likelihood has double minima; adding the O III temperature removes the unphysical solution.

quotes.quote: As an example of the degeneracies for the large optical depth regime, we examine Mrk 193. Shown in figure 193-y , the T(O III) prior clearly distinguishes the physical from the unphysical minimum. Before including the O III measurement, the two minima are troublingly similar in their ^2 values, yet dramatically different in their physical environment. The slightly higher minimum with ^2=4.3 , has a lower abundance, lower temperature, and higher density than the one with ^2=4.0 . The parameter values of the higher minimum are y ^ + = 0.07271, n _ e = 5675 cm^ 3 , and T = 6,655 K.

By including the [ O III ] temperature ( T_ OIII =15,561 K ), the unphysical minimum is completely removed, and the galaxy produces a well-defined solution: y ^ + = 0.08619, n _ e = 1 cm^ 3 , and T = 15,226 K. This new minimum is shifted 0.5 higher, but this variance is insignificant in comparison with the overall uncertainty of 5 .

sources.summary: The paper discusses SBS 1152 + 579 as a BCD with molecular hydrogen detections, possible excitation-diagram discrepancies, and lower molecular column density than other secure-temperature objects.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:h2excitation shows the resulting excitation diagrams for the four BCDs which have at least one detection at the 3 level or greater in a low-order transition (except for SBS 1152 + 579 for which we have used 2.5 detections), and at least two other significant ( 3 ) detections in higher-order lines.

The temperatures derived from mixed-parity \ transitions for the BCDs in our sample are generally consistent, indicating no departure from thermalization for most objects, although Fig. fig:h2excitation does show some sign of a discrepancy for SBS 1152 + 579.

At a higher temperature of 340 K, SBS 1152 + 579 has a much lower molecular column density with \ 3 10^ 18 (0.04 ). With a warm/total \ fraction of 5 rigopoulou02,roussel07 , the total molecular column density in SBS 1152 + 579 would be 1 .

gmw_00003790.name: J120202
gmw_00003790.overview: J120202 is a metal-poor dwarf galaxy that exhibits extremely high equivalent width optical line emission [arXiv:2111.11508]. It is classified as an extremely metal-poor galaxy with relatively modest ultraviolet C IV equivalent widths [arXiv:2111.11508].

sources.summary: The paper treats J120202 as a metal-poor C IV emitter with modest equivalent width, deep G160M follow-up, and low-S/N UV constraints approaching the model metallicity floor.

quotes.quote: In particular, we selected our sample to include the two highest equivalent width nebular emitters in this doublet J104457 at 11 and HS 1442+4250 at 4 ; bergCarbonOxygenAbundances2016,senchynaExtremelyMetalpoorGalaxies2019 ; two of the systems with the highest gas-phase metallicities still powering nebular emission in C 4 SB 2 and 82, at 12+ O/H 7.8 - 7.9 ; senchynaUltravioletSpectraExtreme2017 ; and two metal-poor systems with extremely high equivalent width optical line emission yet relatively modest C 4 equivalent widths <4 J082555 and J120202; bergCarbonOxygenAbundances2016,bergChemicalEvolutionCarbon2019 . These systems were targeted with G160M/1533 spectroscopy with allocations of 5--10 orbits each over the course of two programs (GO:15646 and GO:15881, PI: Stark) which we describe in Section sec:uvobs . The basic properties of this sample of emitters are summarized in Table tab:basicprop , and described in the remainder of this section.

The resulting direct- T_e oxygen abundances (Table tab:basicprop ) confirm that these emitters harbor very metal-poor gas. The values range from 12+ O/H =7.44 in the lowest-metallicity case (J082555) up to 7.93 in the highest (SB 82). This corresponds to a range of 5--15 the solar photospheric oxygen value, with all but SB 2 and SB 82 falling below 10 and thus in the realm of designated extremely metal-poor galaxies (XMPs).

In particular, J082555 and J120202 were previously only observed with the low-resolution COS/G140L grating in the FUV which affords nearly an order of magnitude lower spectral resolution than G160M, precluding clear disentanglement of the nebular emission profile from interstellar absorption. In addition, the broad ( >1000 km/s) and metallicity-dependent stellar wind contribution to this complex was generally too weak to be confidently detected in the single-orbit spectra obtained previously for this metal-poor galaxy sample though see senchynaExtremelyMetalpoorGalaxies2019 , leaving us with minimal information about the massive stars underlying this emission.

gmw_00003791.name: Southern Galaxy
aliases_external: Southern Galaxy (SG) in the Lyra complex
gmw_00003791.overview: The Southern Galaxy is an elliptical galaxy that is considered the remnant of a galaxy group [arXiv:1908.07527]. It features a corona surrounded by hotter gas, showing evidence of ram pressure stripping and past tidal interactions with the cluster RXCJ1825 [arXiv:1908.07527].

sources.summary: The paper treats the Southern Galaxy as an elliptical group remnant near RXCJ1825, linking its corona and surrounding thermal/radio extensions to stripping or tidal interaction with the cluster.

quotes.quote: In addition to this main merger, a minor collision also occurred in the SW periphery of RXCJ1825, where an extension of the X-ray emission is found between RXCJ1825 and the Southern Galaxy (SG in Fig.  fig:view , right panel). This is an elliptical galaxy showing evidence for a 1 \ ``corona'' surrounded by slightly hotter gas ( 2 ), suggesting that this is the remnant of a group of galaxies that was ram pressure stripped by the interaction with RXCJ1825 and caused the X-ray surface brightness extension in the SW clavico19arx .

Fig. fig:overlay (right panel) shows the \ very low-resolution contours where the extension of the radio halo towards the Southern Galaxy in the SW is detected at 3 . The linear extent of the diffuse radio emission in this direction is up to 1.8 Mpc.

The indication of the low surface brightness radio extension following the X-ray extension towards the SW direction suggests past interaction between RXCJ1825 and the galaxy group which hosted the Southern Galaxy.

gmw_00003792.name: DES J214923.66 25815.28
gmw_00003792.overview: DES J214923.66 25815.28 is a star-forming host galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.4755 [arXiv:2005.13160]. Spectroscopic observations show nebular emission lines indicating ongoing star formation [arXiv:2005.13160], while ALMA observations yielded no CO detection, placing an upper limit on its molecular gas [arXiv:2304.10377].

sources.summary: The paper reports no CO detection for HG20181112A, sets an upper limit, and notes a nearby submillimeter galaxy that could contribute to the FRB dispersion measure if foreground.

quotes.quote: HG20181112A and HG20190102C were not detected, and so we placed 3- upper limits on the emission using spectra that were extracted in the same manner as the detections but from the expected position of the host galaxies. A bright sub-mm galaxy (SMG) was detected 6'' west of the expected position of HG20181112A, distinct from a previously detected bright foreground galaxy 5'' north of the host found in g band FORS2 imaging by Prochaska2019 , but the SMG found here is absent in that data. Likewise, the northern foreground galaxy is not detected in the ALMA data. If the SMG is foreground to HG20181112A, its baryonic halo could contribute to the detected dispersion measure (DM) of the FRB. However, bright SMGs in Band 6 tend to correspond to higher redshift galaxies Chen2023 , and since we detect the SMG in continuum emission rather than line emission, we cannot constrain the redshift of the galaxy well, and we do not study the source further.

emission from HG20181112A and HG20190102C was not detected, but we placed 3- molecular gas upper limits of 1.29 10^9 M_ and 4.34 10^9 M_ using the same method above, where is the RMS measured in the spectra as described in Section sec:ALMAobs .

sources.summary: DES J214923.66 25815.28 is identified as the FRB 181112 host; spectroscopy gives z=0.4755 from nebular lines indicating star formation, and radio follow-up found no continuum emission.

quotes.quote: It is associated with the galaxy DES J214923.66 25815.28. On UT 2018 Dec 5, observations of the host of FRB 181112 with the VLT/FORS2 spectrograph established the redshift of the galaxy to be z=0.4755 from nebular emission lines such as , , H and H indicating ongoing star-formation.
We found no continuum emission from anywhere within the host galaxies of FRB 180924, FRB 181112, or FRB 190102 above a 20 Jy flux density limit (3 ).

gmw_00003793.name: host galaxy of FRB 190102
gmw_00003793.overview: The host galaxy of FRB 190102 is a star-forming galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.29 [arXiv:2005.13160, arXiv:2211.02279]. It exhibits a modest star formation rate [arXiv:2005.13160, arXiv:2211.02279], falls into the composite region of the BPT diagram [arXiv:2211.02279], and has no detected CO emission [arXiv:2211.02279, arXiv:2304.10377].

sources.summary: The host galaxy of FRB 190102 was found in deep VLT imaging, has z=0.29 from Magellan and VLT spectroscopy, and shows modest star formation and stellar mass.

quotes.quote: A host was identified in deep FORS2 imaging performed in g and I band with the VLT on 2019 January 12 UT. On UT 2019 March 12, we observed the host galaxy with the Magellan/MagE spectrograph and obtained a redshift of z=0.29 from the \ doublet (resolved) and other nebular emission lines, which was also confirmed in our VLT/FORS2 spectrograph observations on 2019 March 25, from H emission. Its photometry and nebular line emission indicate a star-forming galaxy with a modest star-formation rate and stellar mass (Table tab:host ).

We observed a single epoch for FRB 180924, FRB 181112 and FRB 190608 and two epochs for FRB 190102 (See Appendix: Table tab:radio ). We combined the data from the two IF bands, and also combined the two epochs for FRB 190102, to perform a deep search of radio continuum emission at 6.5 GHz from the position of the FRB host galaxies. We found no continuum emission from anywhere within the host galaxies of FRB 180924, FRB 181112, or FRB 190102 above a 20 Jy flux density limit (3 ).

sources.summary: The paper describes the FRB 20190102C host as a z=0.291 galaxy with measured stellar mass, SFR, and metallicity, a composite BPT classification, and a CO non-detection.

quotes.quote: FRB 20190102C was detected on 2019 January 2 at 05:38:43.49184 UTC with ASKAP and was localised to a host galaxy at z = 0.291 macq20 . The host has the stellar mass of (3.39 1.02) 10^9 M_ , the SFR of 0.86 0.26 M_ yr ^ 1 , and the metallicity of 12 + ( O/H) = 8.70^ +0.08 _ 0.07 hein20a . The host is located in the ``composite'' region close to the boundary of star-forming galaxies and active galactic nuclei (AGNs) on the BPT diagram bhan20 .

The CO observations resulted in the detection of CO(3--2) emission in the FRB 20180924B host and non-detections of CO(3--2) and CO(2--1) emission in the hosts of FRB 20190102C and FRB 20190711A, respectively. The derived molecular gas mass and 3 upper limit is (2.4 0.2) 10^9 M_ , < 3.8 10^8 M_ , and < 6.7 10^9 M_ for the hosts of FRB 20180924B, FRB 20190102C, and FRB 20190711A, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper reports no CO detection for HG20190102C, uses its low metallicity to choose a conservative conversion prescription, and sets a molecular-gas upper limit.

quotes.quote: HG20181112A and HG20190102C were not detected, and so we placed 3- upper limits on the emission using spectra that were extracted in the same manner as the detections but from the expected position of the host galaxies.

Following , we take the mean of two prescriptions for (Z) except for HG20190102C, which has a log (Z/Z_ )<-0.27 , placing it in the metallicity regime for which the two prescriptions differ significantly. Since this host was not detected, we select the (Z) relationship in Equation 7 of genzel2015 which results in a higher, and thus more conservative, upper molecular gas mass limit.

emission from HG20181112A and HG20190102C was not detected, but we placed 3- molecular gas upper limits of 1.29 10^9 M_ and 4.34 10^9 M_ using the same method above, where is the RMS measured in the spectra as described in Section sec:ALMAobs .

gmw_00003794.name: SDSS J221604.90 075355.9
gmw_00003794.overview: SDSS J221604.90 075355.9 is a massive grand-design spiral galaxy [arXiv:2307.03766, arXiv:2005.13160] that hosts the fast radio burst FRB 20190608B [arXiv:2307.03766]. The galaxy contains a Type I Seyfert active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2307.03766, arXiv:2005.13160] and exhibits a post-starburst star formation history [arXiv:2307.03766].

sources.summary: The host of FRB 20190608B is described as a massive grand-design galaxy with post-starburst history and luminous nuclear X-ray emission consistent with a Type I Seyfert AGN.

quotes.quote: The host galaxy of FRB 20190608B (SDSS J221604.90-075355.9) is a grand design SB() galaxy and is among the most massive ( 3.6 10^ 10 \ M_ ) FRB hosts discovered to date Gordon2023 . While the FRB location is coincident with a knot of UV emission that is likely star formation Chittidi2021 , model fits to the full spectral energy distribution (SED) indicate it is one of two FRB hosts with a post-starburst star formation history in which the galaxy has undergone a recent quenching event Gordon2023 . Such galaxies commonly host supermassive black holes which are believed to regulate star formation via AGN-driven feedback MartnNavarro2018 .

Here, we report the first detection of luminous X-ray emission spatially coincident with the center of the host of FRB 20190608B. An AGN origin is consistent with the previous detection of broad H emission in the optical spectrum indicative of a Type I Seyfert galaxy Jonathan2012,Chittidi2021 . As such, the host of FRB 20190608B is the only one known to harbor a Type I AGN.

sources.summary: SDSS J221604.90 075355.9 is the z=0.118 FRB 190608 host, a star-forming, Type 1 AGN galaxy whose deeper VLT imaging revealed faint spiral arms.

quotes.quote: It was localized to the star-forming galaxy SDSS J221604.90 075355.9 at a redshift z=0.118 , obtained from SDSS data release 9 Ahn2012 . This is a relatively well-studied galaxy, which is known to host a Type 1 (broad-line) AGN Stern2012a . Our derived SFR for this galaxy (1.24 M _ yr ^ 1 , as listed in Table tab:host ) agrees reasonably well with the SDSS DR12 sdss12 value of 1.7 0.2 M _ yr ^ 1 . On UT 2019 Aug 21, we performed deep g-band imaging of this host with the X-shooter instrument at the VLT. These observations show spiral arms which were too faint to be observed in the SDSS data.

gmw_00003795.name: IRAS 00397-1312
gmw_00003795.overview: IRAS 00397-1312 is an ultraluminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:1103.3212, arXiv:2205.00011] that represents an old merger stage [arXiv:1103.3212]. It is classified as a composite starburst-active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0908.4271] containing a buried active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1103.3212] and displaying deep silicate absorption [arXiv:2205.00011].

sources.summary: IRAS 00397 1312 is presented as a luminous composite ULIRG and old merger whose mid-IR buried AGN signatures contrast with X-rays consistent mainly with starburst emission.

quotes.quote: IRAS 00397 1312 As mentioned above, IRAS 00397 1312 is one of the most striking objects in the local Universe for many reasons. Owing to its huge luminosity and composite nature, it is an ideal source for a case study of the AGN/SB connection in extreme environments. This ULIRG does not show clear morphological indications of a recent interaction, and likely represents an old merger stage (Veilleux, Kim Sanders 2002). Despite its optical classification as H ii region, the mid-IR spectral shape reveals all the unambiguous signatures of a buried AGN (Imanishi 2009), first and foremost the outstanding CO absorption profile around 4.65 m. The X-ray spectrum of IRAS 00397 1312 is shown in Fig. f2 : the only detected X-ray emission is well reproduced by means of an absorbed power law, resulting in a C stat of 45.0 for 52 degrees of freedom (d.o.f.).

The unabsorbed 0.5--2 and 2--10 keV fluxes are, respectively, 9 and 7 10^ 15 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 , and are both in good agreement with the SB X-ray flux predicted from our mid-IR analysis and the Ranalli et al. (2003) relations for star-forming galaxies (Table t2 ). The steepness of the power law, whose photon index is 2.3^ +1.1 _ 0.6 , is itself consistent with pure SB emission with no AGN contribution; for the latter, an upper limit has been evaluated by adding a pexrav component (Magdziarz Zdziarski 1995) to account for possible AGN reflection.

sources.summary: The paper classifies FSC 0039-13 as a composite starburst-AGN ULIRG and measures low-ionization outflow absorption, including systemic absorption attributed to its host interstellar medium.

quotes.quote: The more recent Kewley (2006) division of these optical-emission-line ratios into excitation types classifies FSC 0039-13 as a composite starburst-AGN, FSC 1009+47 as a Seyfert 2, FSC 1407+05 at the Seyfert/LINER boundary, FSC 1630+15 at the LINER/Composite boundary, and FSC 2349+24 as a Seyfert 2.
The spectrum of FSC 0039-13 has the best SNR near the \ 2853, \ 2600, and \ 2587 lines; but these transitions are detected in all the spectra except for FSC 2349+24.

Some He I emission is present near the doublet in all the spectra and is most prominent in FSC 0039-13. Spectra of A, F, and G stars present low-ionization, metal lines; but any photospheric contribution to the total equivalent width in our spectra is small. The velocity offset of the absorption troughs require a non-stellar absorption component; and the troughs for four of the galaxies show little or no absorption at the systemic velocity. Only the FSC 0039-13 spectrum presents absorption at v=0 . We attribute it to the interstellar medium in the host galaxy, likely a result of high inclination relative to our sightline.

sources.summary: IRAS 00397-1312 is one of the extreme HERUS ULIRGs whose corrected luminosity agrees across several models; it is predicted hyperluminous and classed with deep silicate absorption.

quotes.quote: In the most extreme objects, IRAS 00397-1312 and IRAS 08572+3915 the corrected luminosities predicted by the CYGNUS, FR06 and SKIRTOR combinations agree very well.

These are IRAS 00397-1312, IRAS 10378+1109 and IRAS 11095-0238 which show very similar SEDs to IRAS 08572+3915. IRAS 00397-1312 belongs to class 3A of spo07 which are associated with a deep silicate absorption feature and weak PAH emission.

gmw_00003796.name: MCG +09-27-087
gmw_00003796.overview: MCG +09-27-087 is a nearly face-on ring galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.04866 [arXiv:1509.09062]. It has an estimated integrated oxygen abundance of 12 + log(O/H) = 9.08 dex and hosted the transient ASASSN-15ed [arXiv:1509.09062].

sources.summary: The paper describes MCG +09-27-087 as the nearly face-on ring host of ASASSN-15ed, deriving its redshift, luminosity distance, extinction contribution, absolute magnitude, and oxygen abundance.

quotes.quote: Very little information is available on the galaxy hosting ASASSN-15ed, MCG +09-27-087 (see Figure fig1 ). It is a nearly face-on ring galaxy with total apparent magnitude g = 15.42 0.01 and colours u - g = 1.79 0.03 , g - = 0.74 0.01 , i = 0.36 0.01 and i - z = 0.27 0.01 by Sloan Digital Sky Survey Data Release 6 as obtained on 2007 September 17, ade08 . In the late-time spectra of ASASSN-15ed, a weak and unresolved H line in emission was detected, from which we infer a redshift z = 0.04866 0.00017 for the host galaxy. Adopting a standard cosmology with H _0 = 73 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 , _M = 0.27, _ = 0.73, we obtain a luminosity distance of 207.5 13.7 Mpc, which provides a distance modulus = 36.59 0.14 mag.

We can safely detect the Na ID feature at the redshift of MCG +09-27-087 in our spectra, with an equivalent width of 0.8 0.1 . Following , we obtain a colour excess E( B-V ) = 0.12 mag due to the host galaxy extinction.

As the total B band magnitude of MCG +09-27-087 is B = 16.03, we obtain an absolute magnitude of M_B = 20.67, corrected for Galactic and internal extinction following the prescriptions of the Hyperleda database http://leda.univ-lyon1.fr/ ; mak14 . . Following tre04 , we estimate an integrated oxygen abundance of 12 + log(O/H) = 9.08 (dex).

gmw_00003797.name: Mrk 739W
gmw_00003797.overview: Mrk 739W is a galaxy that hosts a weakly absorbed, low-luminosity active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2210.08791]. Its optical spectrum shows no evidence of broad emission lines, which may be contaminated by strong starburst components [arXiv:2210.08791].

sources.summary: The paper treats Mrk 739W as the fainter dual-AGN component, finding weak absorption, low intrinsic X-ray luminosity, no broad optical lines, and a spectrum modeled with absorbed power-law plus torus reflection.

quotes.quote: On the basis of the results with XCLUMPY, we find that the AGNs in Mrk 739E and Mrk 739W have intrinsic 2--10 keV luminosities of 1.0 10^ 43 and 7.5 10^ 41 \ erg \ s ^ 1 absorbed by hydrogen column densities of N_ H < 6.5 10^ 19 \ cm ^ 2 and N_ H = 6.9^ +3.2 _ 1.7 10^ 21 \ cm ^ 2 , respectively.

Koss_2011a suggest that Mrk 739W also contains an AGN on the basis of the hard photon index in the soft X-ray (0.5--10 keV) band. The optical spectra of Mrk 739W show no evidence for broad emission lines, which may be contaminated by strong starburst components Tubin_2021 .

The X-ray spectrum of Mrk 739W can be modelled by an absorbed power-law and its reflection from the torus. We have estimated the line of sight column densities by cold matter to be N_ H < 6.5 10^ 19 \ cm ^ 2 and N_ H =6.9^ +3.2 _ 1.7 10^ 21 \ cm ^ 2 and the intrinsic X-ray luminosities to be 1.0 10^ 43 and 7.5 10^ 41 \ erg \ s ^ 1 for Mrk 739E and Mrk 739W, respectively. This confirms that Mrk 739E is an unabsorbed moderately-luminous AGN, whereas Mrk 739W is a weakly absorbed, low-luminosity AGN.

gmw_00003798.name: galaxy N1
gmw_00003798.overview: Galaxy N1 is a member of the Abell 3827 cluster [arXiv:1505.05470]. It exhibits a persistent spatial offset between its stars and its dark matter halo [arXiv:1701.04412, arXiv:1505.05470], which may be skewed in a direction consistent with self-interacting dark matter [arXiv:1701.04412].

sources.summary: The paper finds a persistent offset between galaxy N1's stars and dark matter and tentative skewness in the dark matter distribution, consistent with a self-interacting dark-matter interpretation.

quotes.quote: Galaxy N1 The previously-detected offset between galaxy N1's stars and dark matter persists at >3 in our new analysis. Adding two free parameters for the asymmetry of its dark matter slightly increases uncertainty in its position. The modal offset is x, y = 0.22 ^ +0.25 _ 0.14 , -0.81 ^ +0.16 _ 0.17 for an unskewed model, and x, y = 0.29 ^ +0.25 _ 0.14 , -0.71 ^ +0.30 _ 0.16 if skewness is allowed. The consistency between these suggests that the measured position of the density peak is robust against the skew parameter probed here.

We also find (at much lower statistical significance) that galaxy N1's dark matter is skewed in a direction consistent with the SIDM interpretation of its offset. This could be signs of a tail of scattered DM particles and would favour high momentum transfer scattering. However, the weak statistical significance of our result makes it impossible to rule out the low momentum transfer case. Figure fig:gal_1_skews shows the posterior PDF of the skew onto the vector pointing from the DM peak to the stellar luminosity in the fiducial model, such that a positive skew corresponds to the direction predicted by SIDM.

sources.summary: The paper describes galaxy N1 in Abell 3827 as having low mass at its stellar position but an offset dark-matter peak; projection and simulated astrophysical effects fail to explain the observed offset.

quotes.quote: In Abell 3827, projection effects do not appear to be an issue: of the four galaxies at the centre of the cluster three have a total mass appropriate for their stellar mass, while the fourth (galaxy N1) has a low mass at the location of the stars, but a similarly appropriate total mass slightly offset. Had this been a chance projection, there would be mass at the location of N1 (because of its own, non-offset dark matter) plus a second mass peak (and probably a luminous source). These are not seen.

Astrophysical effects, as modelled in the simulation, produce no feasible analogue, for the galaxy observed in Abell 3827. Taking the best-fitting value for its observed offset, this galaxy would be a > 3 outlier in a universe with collisionless dark matter.

gmw_00003799.name: galaxy N4
gmw_00003799.overview: Galaxy N4 is an elliptical galaxy that exhibits an intriguing offset between its dark matter and its stars [arXiv:1701.04412]. Despite this measured offset, the galaxy shows no signs of skewness in its dark matter distribution [arXiv:1701.04412].

sources.summary: The paper reports an intriguing possible offset between galaxy N4's stars and dark matter, but no skewness; it suggests systematics or environmental tidal effects could explain the difference from N1.

quotes.quote: Galaxy N4 The measured position of N4's dark matter is intriguing. Accounting purely for statistical error bars, thanks to the confirmed positions of demagnified images, we find that galaxy N4 is offset from the galaxy's stars at the 3 level. However, the offset position is mildly degenerate with the position of the cluster-scale dark matter (Figure fig:positions_corner ), thus a flat prior on the cluster scale halo could lead to a different offset measurement. Furthermore, the measured ellipticity of the galaxy light is contaminated by light from an adjacent Milky Way star, and its position may also be.

Despite the measured offset of dark matter from galaxy N4, it shows no sign of skewness. If the offset is spurious, as discussed above, then a tail is not expected. If the offset is real, the lack of skew favours low momentum transfer scattering and (at very low S/N) is in mild tension with the skew detected in galaxy N1.

gmw_00003800.name: GAMA64646
gmw_00003800.overview: GAMA64646 is an early-type galaxy classified as S0/a [arXiv:1811.02967] that exhibits weak active galactic nucleus activity [arXiv:2210.11101]. It features an extended, rotating molecular gas reservoir [arXiv:1811.02967, arXiv:2210.11101], a faint tidal tail [arXiv:2210.11101], and a star-forming ring likely caused by a tidal disturbance [arXiv:2210.11101].

sources.summary: GAMA64646 is classified as S0/a and has extended rotating CO emission; its molecular gas reservoir is unusually large, while its ALMA continuum is likely resolved out.

quotes.quote: Optical and H-ATLAS images of our 5 target galaxies are shown in Figure 1. These optical images show generally smooth light distributions typical of elliptical and lenticular galaxies. There are no strong dust lanes seen in absorption in these optical images. Three were classified as S0/a (GAMA64646, GAMA622305, GAMA622429) and two as E (GAMA177186, GAMA272990) in the GAMA survey, by Kelvin et al. (2014), based on SDSS images.

The CO emission is extended where detected in GAMA64646, 272990 and 622429. There is clear rotation in these cases. In the case of GAMA64646 the extent of CO emission exceeds the LAS per channel for our ALMA observations.
In one case (GAMA64646) the continuum emission has been resolved out because the full extent of detected CO is more than 18 (see Figure A1).

sources.summary: The paper finds GAMA64646 has an extended molecular gas reservoir, weak AGN activity, a faint tidal tail, and evidence for tidal disturbance and a star-forming ring.

quotes.quote: Two of the ETGs (GAMA64646, 622305) have faint spiral structure, which is apparent in the deeper KiDS images but not in Sloan Digital Sky Survey SDSS, York00 images used originally for morphological classification. In addition, optical line emission diagnostics cidfernandes10,cidfernandes11 derived from more recent GAMA DR3 catalogues Baldry18 show that GAMA622429 is associated with strong AGN activity (Figure fig:WHAN ). Nonetheless, these galaxies are of interest in this study to examine evolutionary mechanisms at work to form ETGs. GAMA64646 and 272990 are shown in Figure fig:WHAN as having weak AGN activity, but this has not affected the detection of widely-distributed massive molecular gas reservoirs. None of the five targets are in the star-forming region of Figure fig:WHAN . Figure fig:r_gas_dust_overlay also shows that GAMA64646 has a faint tidal tail to the left, which is not associated with galaxy GAMA64647 (redshift 0.12) at the apparent end of the tail.
GAMA64646 appears to have undergone a tidal disturbance, probably leading to a star-forming ring in the SAMI observation and a tidal tail in optical images.

gmw_00003801.name: GAMA622305
gmw_00003801.overview: GAMA622305 is an early-type lenticular galaxy classified as S0/a [arXiv:1811.02967]. It exhibits optical asymmetry and a distorted stellar distribution, suggesting it experienced a recent tidal interaction that may have formed a star-forming molecular gas ring [arXiv:2210.11101].

sources.summary: GAMA622305 is an S0/a target with no centered ALMA CO or continuum detection; the paper considers and largely rejects contamination from nearby weak or serendipitous sources.

quotes.quote: Optical and H-ATLAS images of our 5 target galaxies are shown in Figure 1. These optical images show generally smooth light distributions typical of elliptical and lenticular galaxies. There are no strong dust lanes seen in absorption in these optical images. Three were classified as S0/a (GAMA64646, GAMA622305, GAMA622429) and two as E (GAMA177186, GAMA272990) in the GAMA survey, by Kelvin et al. (2014), based on SDSS images.

For GAMA622305 there is no ALMA CO or continuum emission centred on the target, but a weak offset CO detection is only 2.9 away. Might undeteced dust, associated with this offset source, have contaminated the H-ATLAS data for GAMA622305? Very little contamination would be expected from such a weak CO source, unless the dust-to-gas ratio is unexpectedly high. Two other serendipitous continuum detections at 13 away might contribute some contaminating flux to the H-ATLAS detection of GAMA622305, however, if they dominated the H-ATLAS fluxes then an offset of 13 would be expected between the optical and H-ATLAS centres, which is not seen (see Figure 1).

sources.summary: The paper says GAMA622305 shows optical asymmetry and off-center molecular gas, likely from a recent tidal disturbance that formed or disturbed a star-forming inner ring.

quotes.quote: The optical image of GAMA622305 (Figure fig:r_gas_dust_overlay ) shows asymmetry, with the stellar distribution extended towards the lower left of the image. This ETG is therefore likely to have experienced a recent interaction that distorted it. New molecular gas could have been acquired during this event or existing molecular gas could have been disturbed, leading to the formation of a molecular gas ring with star formation as discussed previously. Formation of the ring by purely secular means Buta96 cannot be ruled out, but this ETG has been disturbed.

gmw_00003802.name: GAMA622429
gmw_00003802.overview: GAMA622429 is an early-type S0/a galaxy [arXiv:1811.02967, arXiv:2210.11101] classified as a radio-quiet Seyfert 2 [arXiv:1811.02967]. Its rotating molecular gas is kinematically disturbed [arXiv:1811.02967] and exhibits a bright central nuclear ring, a bar, and faint spiral arm structures [arXiv:2210.11101].

sources.summary: GAMA622429 is an S0/a galaxy with disturbed central morphology, a dust lane, rotating CO, a central continuum source, and evidence that its gas may have been recently accreted.

quotes.quote: For GAMA622429 a point source is detected, which may be associated with dust around the centre of this galaxy, where there is compact radio emission and star formation (Best Heckman 2012). This galaxy is classified as a Seyfert 2 in NED, which had been missed in our previous emission line analysis of the GAMA data (Agius et al. 2013). It is listed as a radio-quiet (i.e. no strong radio jets) in G u rkan et al. (2015). GAMA622429 shows signs of a dust lane in the KiDS image (Figure 1) and the detected continuum emission may be part of this more extended, but weaker dust distribution.
The CO emission is extended where detected in GAMA64646, 272990 and 622429. There is clear rotation in these cases.
In the case of GAMA177186, GAMA622305 and GAMA622429 it might be that the gas has recently been accreted externally, because the CO is kinematically and morphologicallly disturbed.

sources.summary: The paper models GAMA622429 as a disturbed, strongly AGN-associated ETG whose molecular gas includes bar, nuclear-ring, and spiral-arm-like structures, consistent with a minor merger.

quotes.quote: GAMA622429 Initial attempts at kinematic modelling for GAMA622429 using a single disc with a S ersic surface brightness profile were not successful, with the the model fitting a bright bar-like feature as a disc viewed edge-on. This model is not considered to be representative of features within the ALMA observation, due to the unlikely edge-on orientation relative to the host galaxy. The zeroth order moment map for GAMA622429 (Figure fig:Zeroth_Order_Maps ) shows a bright central feature resembing a nuclear ring, a bar and faint spiral arm structure (Figure fig:r_gas_dust_overlay ). These were modelled by positioning point sources to create these structures, using the inClouds functionality in KinMSpy .

gmw_00003803.name: GAMA177186
gmw_00003803.overview: GAMA177186 is classified as an elliptical galaxy with a smooth optical light distribution and no strong dust lanes [arXiv:1811.02967]. A nearby, brighter continuum source located 4.4 arcseconds away may contaminate its infrared flux measurements [arXiv:1811.02967].

sources.summary: GAMA177186 is classified as an elliptical target; ALMA resolves a nearby brighter continuum source, implying that Herschel blended fluxes and dust parameters may be contaminated.

quotes.quote: Optical and H-ATLAS images of our 5 target galaxies are shown in Figure 1. These optical images show generally smooth light distributions typical of elliptical and lenticular galaxies. There are no strong dust lanes seen in absorption in these optical images. Three were classified as S0/a (GAMA64646, GAMA622305, GAMA622429) and two as E (GAMA177186, GAMA272990) in the GAMA survey, by Kelvin et al. (2014), based on SDSS images.

For GAMA177186 there is a brighter continuum source only 4.4 away, which could have contaminated the H-ATLAS flux measurements. In the ALMA continuum data the offset source is four times as bright as the point source centred on GAMA177186 (see Figure 5 and measured fluxes in Table 2). If we include the offset source flux with the GAMA177186 flux at , then the total continuum flux is consistent (within errors) with our predicted ALMA value (shown as an orange point in the second panel of Figure 6). Therefore we estimate that this offset source may have contributed 80 percent of the H-ATLAS flux, depending on the temperature of the offset source.

gmw_00003804.name: GAMA272990
gmw_00003804.overview: GAMA272990 is an elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1811.02967] that hosts a rotating, asymmetric molecular gas disk [arXiv:1811.02967, arXiv:2210.11101]. This disk exhibits undisturbed kinematics [arXiv:1811.02967], though it also displays an irregular emission region to its north [arXiv:2210.11101].

sources.summary: GAMA272990 is classified as an elliptical target with extended, rotating CO emission; its undisturbed gas kinematics suggest in-situ formation or accretion more than a few hundred Myr ago.

quotes.quote: Optical and H-ATLAS images of our 5 target galaxies are shown in Figure 1. These optical images show generally smooth light distributions typical of elliptical and lenticular galaxies. There are no strong dust lanes seen in absorption in these optical images. Three were classified as S0/a (GAMA64646, GAMA622305, GAMA622429) and two as E (GAMA177186, GAMA272990) in the GAMA survey, by Kelvin et al. (2014), based on SDSS images.
The CO emission is extended where detected in GAMA64646, 272990 and 622429. There is clear rotation in these cases.

Considering possible origins for the molecular gas, the kinematics of two of the galaxies looks undisturbed (GAMA64646, GAMA272990), indicating that either the gas is formed in-situ from stellar mass loss, or the gas was accreted more than a few hundred Myrs ago.

sources.summary: The paper describes GAMA272990 as an elliptical-like galaxy with an asymmetric embedded molecular gas disc, ring-like structures, weak AGN activity, and likely recent disturbance.

quotes.quote: GAMA272990 For GAMA272990, a disc of molecular gas with an asymmetric distribution between North and South is apparent in the zeroth order moment map (Figure fig:Zeroth_Order_Maps ), with more emission in the lower part of the image than the upper. The spectrum (Figure fig:Diagnostic_Plots ) shows a noticeable tilt, with flux density increasing from negative to positive velocity, consisent with the spatially asymmetric emission. There is also an irregular emission region at the North of the molecular gas disc, apparent in the zeroth order moment map. The PV diagram (Figure fig:Diagnostic_Plots ) shows that the model with the arctangent-based circular velocity profile (Equation eq:velprof ) fits the data well.

gmw_00003805.name: MACS0647-3754
gmw_00003805.overview: MACS0647-3754 is a high-redshift galaxy that is strongly lensed [arXiv:2309.02504]. Based on the glafic lens model, the galaxy has an estimated magnification of 6.3 [arXiv:2309.02504].

sources.summary: The paper identifies MACS0647-3754 as a strongly lensed high-redshift galaxy whose weak Balmer break remains below simulations even after applying the glafic magnification estimate.

quotes.quote: The galaxy MACS0647-3754 is also strongly lensed as indicated in imaging. Here, we estimate a magnification 6.3 ^ +0.5 _ 0.4 using the glafic lens model. The remaining galaxies from the MACS0647 field were estimated to have smaller magnifications, found to be in the range 1.1 2.5 with errors in magnification of 0.1 . This introduces wider error bars in the calculated restframe far ultraviolet luminosity ( L_ FUV ), but will not be large enough (for the weaker lensing estimates) to impact our conclusions regarding the agreement between observations and the simulated distributions, shown in figures fig_BBRvsLfuv_z6_z7 fig_BBRvsLfuv_z9_z10 .

Since MACS0647-3754 is gravitationally lensed with resulting large errors in L_ FUV , this outlier can potentially be alleviated, such that it falls into the distribution if sufficiently lensed. Using one lens model (glafic), we estimate that MACS0647-3754 is lensed by 6.3 0.5 , which is just slightly too low to place it within the simulated distribution.

gmw_00003806.name: JADES-10058975
gmw_00003806.overview: JADES-10058975 is a high-redshift galaxy at z ~ 9 that exhibits a weak Balmer break [arXiv:2309.02504]. It hosts a type-2 active galactic nucleus with strong ionizing radiation, a low stellar mass, and likely starburst activity [arXiv:2311.18731].

sources.summary: The paper highlights JADES-GS+53.11243-27.77461 as its most distant type-2 AGN, identified by UV lines, with strong ionizing radiation, low stellar mass, high SFR, and likely starburst activity.

quotes.quote: The most distant type 2 AGN in our sample is ID 10058975 (JADES-GS+53.11243-27.77461), which was identified using two separate methods: 1) the detection of 2422 UV emission line; 2) by identifying 1640, and emission line ratios diagnostic. 1640 is redshifted to the 1.71 m, which is covered by both Band-1 and Band-2 R1000 grating observations from 1210 observations and Band-1 observations in 3215 observations. We showed the final stacked spectrum from all three observations in Figure fig:94HeII . The 1640 emission line is detected in all three sets of observations, however, we stacked the three spectra for more robust detections (SNR=6.5). The 2424 emission is detected at 4 , and we show the spectrum in Figure fig:High_ion , indicating the presence of strong ionising radiation.

As this object is well detected in all gratings and PRISM observations, we were able to fit the full 1-5 m spectrum with BEAGLE. We estimated the stellar mass of the AGN host galaxy to be 1.5 10^ 8 M _ with an SFR of 6.6 M _ yr ^ 1 (SFR/M _ * = 40 Gyr ^ 1 ). The object has a UV magnitude of M _ UV =-20 and is most likely going through a starburst.

sources.summary: The paper singles out JADES-10058975 as a z 9 JADES galaxy with a weak Balmer break that deviates by more than 3 sigma from the simulated distribution.

quotes.quote: However, we find three clear candidates (JADES-18846 and 10058975, MACS0647-3754) which show Balmer breaks that deviate significantly from the simulated data such that they are consistently lower than those predicted by the simulations, even when considering the 2.2-97.8th percentile variations from the median in FLARES and the measurement uncertainties of the break strength.

However, an exception arises in the case of JADES-10058975 (see left panel fig. fig_z9_outliers for spectra), showing a weak Balmer break strength of 0.74 0.06 at z 9 . Considering the relatively minor uncertainty associated with this measurement, it deviates significantly from the corresponding simulations by more than 3 from the 97.8 significance interval. The galaxies in the MACS0647 and WHL0137 clusters also show good consistency with the simulations.

gmw_00003807.name: B 0925+504
gmw_00003807.overview: B 0925+504 is a compact BL Lacertae object located at a redshift of 0.37 [arXiv:1804.09665, arXiv:1705.00124]. It features a core-dominated radio structure with a southeast-directed jet [arXiv:1705.00124, arXiv:0711.1158] and exhibits rapid, quasi-periodic radio and optical variability [arXiv:1804.09665, arXiv:1705.00124].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 0925+504 as a redshift 0.37 BL Lac object with compact core-jet emission and rapid radio variability, arguing its 4.85 GHz IDV is caused mainly by a nearby scattering screen.

quotes.quote: The radio source 0925+504 is a BL Lac object Plotkin2008 at redshift z =0.37 Healey2008 . The 5 GHz VLBI image shows a compact core-jet structure Xu1995 . The direction of radio ejection is very close to the line of sight with a viewing angle of 3.1^ , which leads to a large Doppler factor of 9.2 Wu2007 .

Analysis of rapid variability at 4.85 GHz for the BL BLac object 0925+504 is presented and discussed. The structure functions (SF) are investigated with both refractive and weak interstellar scintillation (RISS/WISS) models analytically. Parameters obtained with these models are quantitatively compared, suggesting that the emission region of IDV is remarkably compact and the responsible interstellar scintillation medium (ISM) lies very close to the observer. Furthermore possible evidence of annual modulation of the variability timescales is detected in this source. Our findings indicate that the observed rapid variability in 0925+504 is predominantly caused by a scattering screen located along the line of sight to the source, at a distance of 110 pc to the observer.

sources.summary: The paper describes B 0925+504 as a compact ISP BL Lac host with VLBI jet, previous rapid radio IDV, prominent radio/optical variations, and quasi-periodic radio variability.

quotes.quote: B 0925+504 , 2FGL J 0929.5+5009 (ISP BL) The optical R-band image of this ISP BL Lac object at redshift 0.37 shows a very compact host galaxy that can be fitted with the de Vaucouleurs model nils03 . In the radio band, it is not resolved at 5 GHz with the VLA taylor96 but is resolved with the VLBI, showing a core jet to the southeast. Rapid radio IDVs have previously been detected towards this source at 5 GHz koay11,liu15 . Our results show prominent intra- and inter-day variations with similar time scales of 0.3 0.1 day in the radio and optical bands (but the optical time scale is tentative because the data are from only a single site), and exhibit a remarkable quasi-periodic radio variability with P 0.5 0.1 day estimated from the light curve (see Fig. fig4 and Table tab1 ).

sources.summary: The paper reports that 0925+504 is core-dominated in VLBI but has a southeast jet aligned with VLA structure, plus a highly polarized core and unpolarized jet.

quotes.quote: 0925+504 The 1.4 GHz VLA image of Giroletti04a shows a faint jet-like extension to the southeast, while the 8.4 GHz VLA map of Patnaik92 shows only the unresolved core. The source is quite core-dominated in our VLBI image (Fig.  fig:0929 ), but also shows a clear jet to the southeast, well aligned with the structure observed by Giroletti04a . The VLBI core displays the very high fractional polarization of m_c=15 , while no polarization was detected in the jet.

sources.summary: The paper describes WISE J092915.43 + 501336.0 as a BL Lac object with fast IDV in both sessions, increasing variability strength, and previous annual-modulation fits.

quotes.quote: WISE J092915.43 + 501336.0 in Figure fig1 , two counterexamples---B2 1239 + 376 and 1633 + 382 with no sign of variability in Figure fig2 . The same as our previous work , in order to ascertain whether or not an IDV is present, we applied a ^2 test to each data set. We adopt the criterium that a data set with `a probability of 0.1 to be constant' is considered to be variable (i.e., the `null hypothesis' probability < 1.0 10^ 3 in Table tab1 or Table tab2 ), which is equivalent to the confidence level of 99.9 to define the IDV. According to this definition, there are 14 out of 20 targets show IDV in session 1, all 17 targets show IDV in session 2. In Tables tab1 and tab2 , we find there are a couple of prominent IDV sources from the two observing sessions, e.g., those with the modulation index m>3 m_ 0 , the m_ 0 is the average modulation index of the calibrators, which is 0.64 for session 1, and 0.45 for session 2. The modified modulation index m' represents a variability strength, but not a real variability amplitude. The parameter Y is more like a variability amplitude, which ranges from a few per cent to 22 for the prominent IDV sources in Tables tab1 and tab2 . We note that there are 11 common targets appeared in both session 1 and session 2, all of them have shown IDV in the two sessions except the quasar 1156 + 295 which only shows IDV in session 2, we give a short discussion for this in Section sec:discuss . Some of the IDV sources show relatively fast variability, e.g., the source J1128 + 5925, WISE J092915.43 + 501336.0 exhibit a short timescale of a few hours and some of them e.g., the source 1357 + 769 shows a relatively longer timescale. A full analysis on the IDV timescales along with the source structure and brightness temperature derived from the RA space VLBI measurements will be presented in a forthcoming paper. paracol 2 Comment on Individual Sources We comment briefly on some prominent IDV sources which showed a modulation index m>3m_ 0 in one or two observing sessions, in the following. We note that all the target sources are blazars with core-dominated radio structures in milliarcsecond scales from the VLBI images (e.g., ). PKS 0528 + 134 : the source shows IDV in both sessions, see Tables tab1 and tab2 , the IDV strength is slightly increased from session 1 to session 2, while the mean flux density of 1.710 0.034 Jy in session 1 decreases to 1.525 0.034 Jy, at 4.8 GHz. 0716 + 714 : this BL Lac object is a well known IDV source, although its IDV strength is moderate with relatively longer IDV timescales from previous observations and its IDV timescales can be fitted with the annual modulated ISS model .

gmw_00003808.name: the stream
gmw_00003808.overview: The stream is a highly elongated, S-shaped stellar stream located approximately 10 kpc southeast of the galaxy NGC 4449 [arXiv:1112.2154]. It is classified as a dwarf spheroidal galaxy undergoing tidal disruption, characterized by an absence of star formation and cold gas [arXiv:1112.2154].

sources.summary: The paper designates a stellar stream near NGC 4449, resolves it into old RGB stars, interprets it as an elongated dwarf spheroidal being tidally disrupted, and locates a possible remnant nucleus.

quotes.quote: Clearly visible near the minor axis of NGC 4449, 10 kpc to the southeast, is a very elongated, S-shaped feature of dimensions 1.5 7 kpc, which we designate ``the stream''. The stream's position does not overlap with any of the complex HI-gas features surrounding NGC 4449 1998ApJ...495L..47H . Also, it is on the opposite side of the galaxy with respect to an interesting star cluster that may be linked to another past accretion event 2012ApJ...745L...1A .

The right-hand panel shows a Suprime-Cam zoom-in of the stream. It is clearly resolved into stars and has the general appearance of a dwarf spheroidal (dSph) galaxy that is elongated by tides. This classification (defined by a spheroidal morphology, stellar mass M_ < 10^8 M_ , and the absence of star formation and cold gas) is also supported by the HI non-detection of 2009A&A...506..677H .

The central stellar clump is visible in Figure fig.map (d), with a position slightly offset from the stream's main ridgeline ( _ J2000 = 12 28 43.32, _ J2000 = +43 58 30.0 ; positional uncertainty 6^ ).

gmw_00003809.name: NGC 5949
gmw_00003809.overview: NGC 5949 is a nearby dwarf disk galaxy [arXiv:1405.4854, arXiv:1911.05836] that exhibits a flocculent morphology with poorly delineated spiral arms [arXiv:1608.06264]. The galaxy hosts a nuclear star cluster [arXiv:1405.4854], and its dark matter halo features a relatively steep inner density slope [arXiv:1911.05836].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 5949 in the dwarf sample, noting its nuclear star cluster, lack of resolved HI data, and a more cuspy stellar-traced model than gas-traced result.

quotes.quote: We present new constraints on the density profiles of dark matter (DM) halos in seven nearby dwarf galaxies from measurements of their integrated stellar light and gas kinematics. The gas kinematics of low mass galaxies frequently suggest that they contain constant density DM cores, while N-body simulations instead predict a cuspy profile. We present a data set of high resolution integral field spectroscopy on seven galaxies and measure the stellar and gas kinematics simultaneously.

Two galaxies are outliers beyond 1- significance, which is expected from normal statistics alone. NGC 5949 looks more cuspy from the stellar-traced models. NGC 5949, however, does not have evidence for a bar from the gas kinematics field and the discrepancy is harder to explain.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 5949 in its dark-matter slope sample and finds its inner slope consistent with both gas-traced and stellar-traced literature estimates.

quotes.quote: Two galaxies overlap with our sample, NGC 2976 and NGC 5949. For NGC 5949, determines an inner slope of 0.88, which again agrees well with our value of ^* = 0.84 .

We observe three of the same galaxies, allowing us to compare the individual slopes for those cases. NGC 2976 agrees well: finds a gas-traced ^* of 0.44 0.18 , while we find 0.33 0.05 . We find a slope of 0.82 0.08 for NGC 5949, which is consistent with the gas-traced slope of 0.65 0.14 from , as well as with their stellar-traced slope of 0.87 0.11 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5949 as a flocculent disk whose H-alpha rotation curve agrees across studies and whose MOND rotation-curve fit is good.

quotes.quote: NGC 5949 has a more flocculent morphology, with less delineated spiral arms.
spa08 also measured the H rotation curve of NGC 5949 and obtained an almost identical rotation curve as S05.
We see that the fit is good for NGC 3621 and NGC 5949, acceptable for NGC 6689, but poor for NGC 4605, 5963 and 6503.

gmw_00003810.name: NGC 4096
gmw_00003810.overview: NGC 4096 is a barred spiral galaxy of type SABc that is viewed almost edge-on [arXiv:2108.10909]. The galaxy contains compact star-forming regions in its central and northern areas, which exhibit flatter radio spectra than the rest of the disk [arXiv:2108.10909].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4096 as an almost edge-on SABc galaxy, measuring 0.33 and 1.4 GHz fluxes and finding flatter spectra in central and northern compact star-forming regions.

quotes.quote: (iii) NGC 4096: It is a SABc type galaxy seen almost edge-on. Its flux density measured by Condon1987ApJS at 20 cm was 52.2 mJy. More recent observations with JVLA have shown its flux density to be 57.1 1.1 and 16.3 0.3 mJy at 19 and 5 cm respectively Wiegert2015AJ . From our analysis, measured flux density for this galaxy is 174 10 and 56.2 1.5 mJy from 0.33 GHz GMRT and 1.4 GHz JVLA archival data respectively. Galaxy integrated spectrum is 0.78 0.06 between 1.4 and 0.33 GHz.

Emission from the central region has a spectral index of 0.64 0.02 , while that of the Northern compact source (Fig. 4) in the plane of the galaxy is 0.5 0.02 (Fig. 13). Their spectrum is flatter than rest of the galaxy, indicating association of star forming regions with these discrete emissions.

gmw_00003811.name: host galaxy of GRB 020305
gmw_00003811.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 020305 is an extended galaxy with a Vega R-band magnitude of 25.17 [arXiv:astro-ph/0504050]. Hubble Space Telescope imaging indicates that this galaxy may be part of an interacting system [arXiv:astro-ph/0504050].

sources.summary: The paper describes the GRB 020305 host as an extended R=25.17 galaxy coincident with the afterglow, with STIS photometry and possible membership in an interacting system.

quotes.quote: The HST observations revealed an extended galaxy ( 1.3^ 0.6^ ) coincident with the OA position (see Fig. hst ). The integrated CL and LP magnitudes (measured in the third epoch observations) of the host are m^ AB _ CL = 25.38 0.05 and m^ AB _ LP = 25.18 0.12 , corresponding to a Vega R band magnitude of R=25.17 0.14 . The photometry was performed with SExtractor using the automatic aperture magnitude ( MAG AUTO ), which is suitable to measure the total flux of extended objects (Bertin Arnouts ). The STIS host magnitudes correspond to a spectral index = -0.45^ +0.52 _ 0.43 (dereddened for Galactic extinction).
The HST imaging revealed an extended host galaxy with R=25.17 0.14 , which might be part of an interacting system.

gmw_00003812.name: NGC 1357
gmw_00003812.overview: NGC 1357 is a non-barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1907.10188]. It features a central bulge, a ring-like spiral-arm region, an inner blue disk containing well-defined spiral arms, and an outer red disk that lacks spiral arms [arXiv:1907.10188].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1357 as a non-barred spiral whose curvature profile separates a bulge, ring-like spiral-arm region, inner disk, and outer disk.

quotes.quote: NGC 1357 is a non-barred spiral galaxy classified as SA(s)ab RC3_1991 . considered that the galaxy has two disks: an inner blue disk which contains well defined spiral arms, and an outer red disk with no spiral arms. fig:ngc1357 shows the curvature for NGC 1357. The bulge is indicated by the region of high curvature (1); the valley at (2) is related to the tight shape of the spiral arms, having a ring-like shape, pointed out by .

However, all spiral structure is contained inside region 2 which is the inner disk. The transition between the inner and the outer disk is underpinned by the decrease in the oscillations after the local peak at R/R_p 0.65 , therefore region 3 corresponds to the outer disk with no spiral arms.

gmw_00003813.name: NGC 936
gmw_00003813.overview: NGC 936 is a luminous, nearly face-on barred lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1507.02691, arXiv:1209.4873] containing a strong bar [arXiv:1209.4873], a barlens [arXiv:1507.02691], and a pseudobulge with a nuclear ring [arXiv:1907.10188]. The galaxy's disk exhibits a broken-exponential profile with a distinct down-bending break [arXiv:1312.3384, arXiv:0709.3505].

sources.summary: NGC 936 is treated as a barred lenticular case with complicated, rotating isophotes from its bulge, disk, and barlens, showing the new model handles non-edge-on structure.

quotes.quote: To begin with, the interesting barred lenticular (SB0) galaxy NGC 936 was the first candidate to be modelled. Due to this galaxy's orientation (slightly inclined but close to face-on view) and components (bulge, disk and barlens ; see Laurikainen_barlens2011 ), its isophote structure is relatively complicated. The data was retrieved from the publicly available archive of the Spitzer Survey of Stellar Structure in Galaxies (S4G http://irsa.ipac.caltech.edu/data/SPITZER/S4G/ ), and consisted of an infrared (3.6 ) Spitzer observation (IRAC 1 instrument, PI: K. Sheth). Figure fig:n936 displays the results: the isophote shape starts off close to elliptical in the central regions, then it transitions to lemon-shaped and parallelogram-shaped (when capturing the barlens) and finally returns to a shape close to elliptical, when the barlens no longer dominates and the disk takes over.

Because of all these strong deviations from ellipticity, the 2D model built with fails to reproduce the light distribution in this galaxy and after its subtraction from the image, a residual map remains that is heavily contaminated by twisting features, both positive and negative.
