The much more realistic 2D model built with (top right) and the associate residual map (bottom right) demonstrate that the formalism proposed here is not limited to edge-on systems, but can competently handle any type of galaxy. With such good quality results, it is now possible to make stronger claims about galaxy morphology and also gain better insight into the substructure than before.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 936 as a barred lenticular with bulge, bar, disk, nuclear ring, and a curvature pattern suggesting a pseudobulge.

quotes.quote: NGC936 is classified as barred lenticular galaxy SB0 wozniak1991a , formed by a structure of bulge+bar+disk. In fig:pgc0009359rkur we infer from that the bulge dominates region 1, while the bar becomes dominant in region 2 -- highlighted by the straight valley and negative . The bar ends close to R/R_p 0.5 . Its properties were also extracted by , regarding the bar length, they provide 50 arcsec .The peak in at R/R_p 0.5 ( 54 arcsec) indicating the end of the bar seems to be in agreement The pixel scale of EFIGI (SDSS DR4) is 0.396"/ pix and our R_p = 273 px, therefore R_p [ arcsec ] = 108 ''. Then our bar length value gives 0.5Rp 54 arcsec, in agreement with . with the outer limit found by the authors (see their figure 3).

The bar of NGC 936 was also studied by together with a nuclear ring inside the bar region. Returning back to the inner component, is nearly constant and 1 (small value compared to the maximum amplitude of 10 ). This suggest that the inner region is not a classical bulge, but rather being a pseudobulge -- which also may contain a nuclear ring.

sources.summary: The review uses NGC 936 as a nearly face-on barred early-type and prototypical barlens galaxy, noting bar-end flux enhancements and ansae.

quotes.quote: In the same figure we show also a typical barred early-type galaxy, NGC 936, in almost face-on view. Also this galaxy has flux enhancements at the two ends of the bar, and it is tempting to argue that they are the same features as in ESO 443-042.

Two prototypical barlens galaxies, NGC 936 and NGC 4314, are shown in Figures 4 and 10. In the images barlenses can be easily mixed with the classical bulges. However, in the surface brightness profiles barlenses appear as nearly exponential, flat sub-sections, both along the major and the minor axis of the bar. Characteristic morphological features for barlens galaxies are the ansae (or handles), which appear at the two ends of the bar (see NGC 936 in Fig. 4).

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 936 as a barred radio-ejecting galaxy paired with quasars, with elongated radio isophotes and X-ray/radio evidence used for comparison to NGC 613.

quotes.quote: In separation, redshifts and other properties there is an almost exact duplicate of NGC 613 in the pair of z 2 quasars paired across the radio ejecting, barrred galaxy NGC 936. NGC 941, a companion to NGC 936, has a quasar apparently connected to it by a radio bridge which shows structure at high resolution. One of the pair across NGC 936, a strong Parks quasar, is also accurately aligned with a strong Parks quasar further away.
Is NGC 936 an active galaxy? Fig. 5 shows an NVSS map of NGC 936. It is apparent that the galaxy has radio isophotes elongated along the direction of the closeby pair of z 2 quasars.

sources.summary: The paper applies a velocity-field recovery method to NGC 936, describing it as a luminous barred S0 whose strong bar and SAURON data make large non-circular motions measurable.

quotes.quote: NGC 936 is a barred S0 galaxy, whose morphology and kinematics have been studied in detail in the pre-IFU era (Kormendy 1983, 1984; Kent 1987; Kent Glaudell 1989, Merrifield Kuijken 1995). It is very luminous and hence massive, which helps maximizing the measured LOS velocities. Because of the strong bar present in NGC 936, the amplitude of non-circular motions is expected to be large as well. The bar in NGC 936 is not nearly aligned with either principal axis, which minimizes errors caused by adopting inaccurate geometry, as analyzed in Sect.3. Here, we apply the method described in Sect.2 in order to recover the radial and tangential velocities from the integral-field data of NGC 936 observed with the SAURON integral-field spectrograph (Bacon et al. 2001) mounted on the William Herschel telescope in La Palma.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 936 as a model-fit example, describing its bulge, bar, down-bending disk break, separate disk scale lengths, and flat bar profile.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:res presents a summary page of the result for NGC 936 that has a bulge, and a bar, and show a disk break with a down-bending surface brightness profile. Thus the bulge, bar, and disk break are included in the model fit, and the result has different inner and outer disk scale lengths and an interpolated central surface brightness. From this figure, one can evaluate the resulting fit.

A striking feature in these cuts is that some bars (e.g., NGC 936, NGC 1350, and NGC 5750) are better fitted with a flat surface brightness profile with S ersic index of about 0.5 or less. These bars indeed have a flat profile all the way to the central regions when the other components such as bulge and disk component are removed.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 936 as a barred-galaxy comparison where simulation gtr116 closely matches the bar region, showing that its central barlens morphology could be mistaken for a classical bulge.

quotes.quote: The left-hand panel shows an image of the barred galaxy NGC 936, obtained by combining the g, and i images from the SDSS. The right-hand panel displays gtr116 at time t = 6 Gyr, viewed from angles such that it mimics best NGC 936. This simulation was not specifically run so as to model NGC 936, yet the bar regions in the observations and in the simulations have a very similar morphology. The most important thing to note, however, is that this simple visual inspection of the central region of both NGC 936 and the simulation could lead to the conclusion that both have a classical bulge, while at least for the simulation we are sure, by construction, that it has no such component. This shows that a barlens component may be mistaken for a classical bulge, if morphology is the only available information.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 936 as a strong probable OLR case: a Type II broken-exponential S0 profile with a break at about twice the bar radius and supporting prior decompositions.

quotes.quote: This is one of the stronger cases of a probable OLR system: there is no visible outer ring in this S0 galaxy, but the break is at about twice the bar radius. The Type II (broken-exponential) nature of the profile is not always visible in shallower exposures e.g., kent89,laurikainen05 , but has previously been noted by kormendy84 and wozniak91 . bba98 fit their major-axis profile using a disk with a large hole, another indication of a Type II profile; their disk scale length (23.8 ) is a reasonably good match to our h_ o = 27.4 . The break -- and the relatively shallow inner slope -- also agree with the ``outer lens'' classification by kormendy79 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 936 as an example structural fit, modeling its disc alongside bulge and bar components and noting a disc break near 100 arcsec.

quotes.quote: In Fig. fig:schematic , we show as an example the fit of NGC 936. For simplicity, we only plot the model of the disc component even though we fit the galaxy also with a bulge and a bar component simultaneously. The disc of NGC 936 has a break at R_ br 100 arcsec, and the slope of the disc profiles inside and outside of the disc break are different, i.e. their disc scale lengths are different.

gmw_00003814.name: NGC 2767
gmw_00003814.overview: NGC 2767 is a galaxy with a complex structure that has been classified as both an elliptical and a lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1907.10188]. Analysis of its profile suggests it contains a pseudobulge, a possible central bar, and a type-II disk structure [arXiv:1907.10188].

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 2767 difficult to classify, using curvature to infer at least three components: bulge, possible bar, type-II disk, and likely pseudobulge.

quotes.quote: NGC 2767 is classified as an elliptical RC3_1991 and as a S0 nair2010 . However, the structure of this galaxy is not trivial to be resolved. comment that the PA twist in the centre of the galaxy may imply the existence of a bar and a dust disk. Examining in cut_NGC2767_f160w_kur shows the existence of two peaks and a narrow deep valley, revealing that the galaxy has at least three components (regions 1, 2 and 3).

The region 3 seems to be of a disk structure of type II, very similar to NGC 936 (note that after R/R_p 1.0 , the curvature is very small). A final note is that, photometrically, we suggest that the bulge of this galaxy is indicated to be pseudobulge due to the behaviour of in the inner part of the profile -- it takes some distance from the centre to increase significantly until the transition region at R/R_p 0.4 . Summarizing, in agreement with , the curvature entail that the galaxy may have a bulge+bar+disk structure.

gmw_00003815.name: NGC 4267
gmw_00003815.overview: NGC 4267 is a lenticular galaxy that has been historically classified as either an S0 or SB0 galaxy [arXiv:1907.10188]. Photometric analysis reveals no evidence of a bar component, showing instead a classical bulge that transitions into a disk [arXiv:1907.10188].

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 4267 classifications and concludes its curvature shows a classical bulge-disk transition but no photometric evidence for a bar.

quotes.quote: In earlier catalogues nilson1973 NGC 4267 was classified as S0, but the presence of a bar is also suggested sandage1981,wozniak1991a,jungwiert1997,gadotti2006,erwin2008 - SB0. The shape of is shown in fig:pgc0039710rcomponentsgray . In comparison to NGC 2767, NGC 4267 exhibit an akin radial profile, however is considerably different. Region 1 around R/R_p 0.3 refers to the bulge while the disk takes part in the remaining of the profile in region 2 (lower below zero). In respect to the bar, the curvature does not trace a sign of it because the pattern of a narrow valley is not exhibited.

Also combining together the curvature and the residual image (middle panel of fig:pgc0039710rcomponentsgray ), shows no evidence of a photometric bar component. In relation the bulge and the disk, the transition of both is highlighted by the peak in at R/R_p 0.3 . The inner region shows an abrupt increase in from zero to the transition region with the disk, therefore this points that the bulge is a classical bulge.

gmw_00003816.name: NGC 7723
gmw_00003816.overview: NGC 7723 is a barred spiral galaxy classified as SBb [arXiv:1907.10188, arXiv:0806.0471]. Its structure features a central boxy bulge, a bar, an inner pseudoring, and a multiple-armed spiral pattern [arXiv:1907.10188, arXiv:0806.0471].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 7723 as a barred spiral whose curvature separates bulge, bar/pseudoring, spiral arms, and a low-curvature disk-like region.

quotes.quote: NGC 7723 is classified as SB()b/SB(rs) RC3_1991,comeron2013 . fig:ngc7723kur shows the result of the curvature for NGC 7723. The bulge is contained inside the region 1 delimited by R/R_p 0.2 . argue that the central part of this galaxy is composed by a boxy bulge with a symmetric nucleus embedded in. Analysing the valley in region 2 together with the residual image we see that it is related to a bar and a ring.

In fact, this component is considered to be a pseudoring formed by the way the spiral arms emerges from the bar delrio1998,delrio1999,prieto2001b,eskridge2002 . In they mentioned the ring structure but in their structural decomposition they have not taken it into account. The spiral arms begin at R/R_p 0.35 - and manifests in oscillations of .

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 7723 inner pseudoring and multiple-armed spiral, preferring R 1.3 while noting an alternative R 1.9 fit.

quotes.quote: NGC 7723 : the inner pseudoring and multiple-armed spiral of this SBb ( T=3 ) galaxy are best reproduced by a model giving R 1.3 . There is an alternative model, with R 1.9 , which gives about as good fit with the spiral structure. For comparison see Fig. coro7723 .

gmw_00003817.name: NGC 6056
gmw_00003817.overview: NGC 6056 is a galaxy with disputed classifications, described as both a barred spiral with an internal bulge and a non-barred system [arXiv:1907.10188]. Its structure features a possible pseudobulge component and a transition from a bar to an outer disk or spiral [arXiv:1907.10188].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6056 as a disputed barred or non-barred system where curvature indicates a bulge, bar, outer disk or spiral, and possible pseudobulge.

quotes.quote: This galaxy is considered to be an barred spiral with an internal bulge (SB(s)0) RC3_1991,prieto2001b , and also non-barred (S0/a) tarenghi1994 . In of fig:ngc6056rkur , the bulge is within R/R_p 0.18 (first peak). The valley in 2 is the bar (the same behaviour in as the other galaxies), and confirmed by .

The transition of the bar with the outer disk (spiral) is indicated by the local peak at R/R_p 0.6 . A pseudobulge component was pointed by and they found a Sérsic fit of n=1.1 in the band. But the pseudobulge part does not shows the same behaviour in as the other galaxies.

gmw_00003818.name: NGC 472
gmw_00003818.overview: NGC 472 is classified as a compact elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1907.10188]. Its structure is well represented by a single high-index Sérsic component, though it exhibits some minor perturbations in its inner regions [arXiv:1907.10188].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 472 as a compact elliptical whose smooth decreasing curvature is well represented by a single high-index Sérsic component.

quotes.quote: NGC 472 is a compact elliptical galaxy yildrim2017 . Similar to NGC 1270, it does show some perturbations in the inner regions -- the two local peaks are attributed to inhomogeneities -- but the shape of follows an overall shape resembling a pure component, which is smooth and decreases from inner to outer regions. The great difference between NGC 472 and NGC 1270 is that for NGC 1270 the increasing of in the innermost part is more subtle than NGC 472. Lastly, a single Sérsic fit n=5.8 returned a robust representative model for NGC 472, since there is no residual structures in the outer regions.

gmw_00003819.name: NGC 384
gmw_00003819.overview: NGC 384 is classified in literature as either an elliptical [arXiv:1907.10188] or a lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1907.10188]. Its structure exhibits curvature oscillations that reveal an internal, oval-shaped disk [arXiv:1907.10188].

sources.summary: The paper contrasts NGC 384 with other elliptical-like galaxies, finding curvature oscillations and residual structure consistent with an internal oval disk.

quotes.quote: NGC 384 is classified in literature as E RC3_1991,yildrim2017 but also S0 nilson1973 . In comparison to NGC 472 and NGC 1270, (R) is quite different ( fig:cut_NGC0384_f160w_kur ) due to the emergence of the substantial oscillations in the region R/R_p 0.5 . These delineates some internal and small structure. In the residual image of fig:cut_NGC0384_f160w_kur it is readily seem such structure with a disk oval shape.

gmw_00003820.name: NEPLA4
gmw_00003820.overview: NEPLA4 is an ultraluminous Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy [arXiv:1805.00490] located at a redshift of approximately 6.54 [arXiv:2012.00014, arXiv:2004.14496]. It features a complex double-peaked Lyman-alpha profile [arXiv:1805.00490, arXiv:2012.00014] that may require ionizing radiation from an obscured active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2012.00014].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NEPLA4 as a z=6.6 ultraluminous LAE with a complex double-peaked Ly-alpha profile, strong blue side, and no lower-redshift [O II] interpretation.

quotes.quote: NEPLA4 has a complex profile with red and blue peaks and bears a strong resemblance to COLA1. In Figure  contours , we compare the two-dimensional spectra of COLA1 and NEPLA4. In NEPLA4, the blue component is at a more negative velocity, and the red wing is more extended. In both cases, the spatial profile of the blue wing along the slit matches that of the red wing, suggesting we are seeing light from a single source rather than from two overlapping galaxies at slightly separated redshifts.
NEPLA4 shows the same characteristic double peak structure as the lower redshift sources, with a strong dip between the peaks.
We conclude that NEPLA4 is not a lower redshift [ O 2 ] emitter.

sources.summary: The paper treats NEPLA-4 as a z>6 double-peaked Ly-alpha emitter whose near-zone resembles COLA-1's and may require additional ionization from an obscured AGN.

quotes.quote: Recently, two other LAEs have also been reported to have double-peaked profiles: NEPLA-4, at z 6.54 songaila2018 , and A370p z1 in the parallel Frontier Field of Abell 370 meyer2020 .

The other two LAEs at z 6 , NEPLA-4 and A370p z1 can be analyzed by a similar procedure, with NEPLA-4's spectrum being nearly identical to that of COLA-1 due to the similar size of the near-zone, and that for A370p z1 being slightly smaller ( R_ , A370p z1 = 0.26 pMpc). We predict the intrinsic 0.5-7 keV emission from the AGN to be 10^ 44.17 and 10^ 44.09 ergs s ^ 1 respectively for these LAEs. While the stellar emission in A370 pz1 was found to be capable of self-ionizing its own HII bubble (with an escape fraction of f_ esc 0.9 for a fraction of its measured age of about 50 Myr), it was found that COLA-1 and NEPLA-4 need additional sources of ionization to keep their bubbles ionized given galaxy lifetimes of a few ten Myr meyer2020 .

We have studied the possible origin of the double-peaked Lyman-Alpha Emitters (LAEs), such as COLA-1, NEPLA4 and A370p z1= at z > 6.5 in the proximity zone of obscured AGN, whose ionizing emissivity leads to the emergence of the blue wing.

sources.summary: The paper treats NEPLA4 as a well-studied z~6.54 Ly-alpha emitter with a COLA1-like line shape, unknown rest-frame UV magnitude, and small red-blue peak separation.

quotes.quote: `NEPLA4' is a emitter at z 6.54 with a line shape resembling that of `COLA1'. Since its location is in the currently less-studied NEP field the rest-frame UV magnitude is, unfortunately, unknown.

Unlike COLA1 and NEPLA4, the velocity separation between the red and blue peak of the line is large in Aerith B ( v = (580 80) ), indicating the galaxy is unlikely a significant Lyman continuum leaker 2016ApJ...828...71D,2016MNRAS.461.3683I .

gmw_00003821.name: AC dwarf I
gmw_00003821.overview: AC dwarf I is a newly discovered dwarf companion galaxy of NGC 3203 [arXiv:1202.4790]. The galaxy is rich in neutral hydrogen gas that covers its entire stellar body and exhibits a disky morphology [arXiv:1202.4790].

sources.summary: The paper names AC dwarf I as a newly discovered H I-rich dwarf near NGC 3203, with disky gas morphology suggesting capture by the primary galaxy.

quotes.quote: We do not find any other H i gas in this field and name the newly discovered H i rich dwarf ``AC dwarf I''.
In the case of the dwarf companion of NGC 3203, AC dwarf I, the H i covers its entire stellar body.
AC dwarf I appears to be disky, and the H i cloud is enlongated in the same direction as its disc, the major axis being perpendicular to that of NGC 3203.

gmw_00003822.name: NGC 7339
gmw_00003822.overview: NGC 7339 is a nearly edge-on spiral galaxy classified as Sb or Sbc [arXiv:1009.4438, arXiv:astro-ph/0402225]. It forms a close pair with NGC 7332, and a tidal interaction between them is likely stripping gas from NGC 7339's rich neutral hydrogen reservoir [arXiv:1009.4438, arXiv:1202.4790].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 7339 as the gas-rich Sbc member of the NGC 7332 pair, noting prior H i measurements and possible interaction-related gas near NGC 7332.

quotes.quote: NGC 7332 and NGC 7339 form a close pair (5.2 arc minutes separation) with velocities of 1172 and 1313 km s ^ 1 respectively. They lie at a distance of 23 Mpc (Tonry et al. 2001, from surface-brightness fluctuations), giving a projected separation of 35 kpc. Biermann, Clarke Fricke (1979), using the same receiver, also see this signal but attribute it to side-lobe contamination from NGC 7339.

They did not detect any gas at the position of NGC 7332 but did detect the gas in NGC 7339 and also found a cloud with an H i mass of 6 10^6 M_ around 3 arc minutes from NGC 7332, in the direction of NGC 7339, which they attribute to an interaction between the two galaxies. NGC 7339 is a nearly edge-on Sbc galaxy. It has been observed a number of times in the H i line. Springob et al. (2005) find a neutral hydrogen mass of 1.3 10^9 M_ after correction for the size of the galaxy, which is consistent with the mass of 1.2 0.2 10^9 M_ found by Staveley-Smith Davies (1987) with the Lovell Telescope at Jodrell Bank.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 7339 as the likely interacting neighbor responsible for NGC 7332's accreted gas, linking its disk and velocity to a high-velocity clump in NGC 7332.

quotes.quote: The neighbour galaxy NGC 7339, a Sb galaxy placed 5 \ away from NGC 7332, is most likely the one responsible for such an event. The position of this galaxy is such that the offplane gas clump found in NGC 7332 could well be a direct continuation of the west side of NGC 7339's disk. Additionally, the maximum receding velocity measured on the west side of this galaxy is 1525 courteau97 , which is strikingly similar to the radial velocity of the high velocity clump in NGC 7332 ( 1550 ). This picture leads to the suggestion that this clump is linked to NGC 7339.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 7339 as NGC 7332's gas-rich neighbour, with an H I disc or envelope likely tidally interacting with and feeding NGC 7332.

quotes.quote: Instead, NGC 7339, a neighbour of a comparable size at about 10 kpc, is found with 8.9 10^8 M_ of H i gas.
More recently, minchin10 have discovered a large H i structure covering both NGC 7339 and NGC 7332, with the H i peak coinciding with the center of NGC 7339.
If the H i in NGC 7339 was more extended than the stellar disk, as is often seen in spirals, then a tidal interaction would have stripped the gas before the stars are pulled out.

gmw_00003823.name: GRB 120815A host galaxy
gmw_00003823.overview: The GRB 120815A host galaxy is a high-redshift, strongly star-forming elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1407.6905, arXiv:1903.01353] located at a redshift of approximately 2.36 [arXiv:1407.6905, arXiv:2310.15967]. It is characterized by a young age of 10 to 15 million years [arXiv:1407.6905, arXiv:1903.01353] and contains a substantial amount of molecular hydrogen [arXiv:1304.7003].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the z=2.36 GRB 120815 host as a likely massive proto-spheroid with strong star formation, high dust along the sightline, and an estimated age of 15 Myr.

quotes.quote: Finally, in Figure 120815 , we report the results for the host of GRB 120815,at z=2.36 observed by Kruhler et al. (2013); the elements analyzed here are Si, S, Ni and Zn and all seem to indicate again a massive spheroid with strong SF. In fact, to best fit the abundances in this host we adopted model E01 with SFE=20 Gyr^ 1 , which is the maximum value we explored. It is interesting to note the high value of [ Zn/Fe ] which means that a higher quantity of dust is present along the line of sight of this GRB. In our analysis, although many uncertainties are still present in the adopted stellar yields and in the observational data and no firm conclusions on the GRB hosts can be drawn, two of the three GRB host galaxies taken into account seem to favor either a spiral or a spheroidal host, while the third GRB (GRB120815) seems to be more confidently hosted by a proto-spheroid.

Applying the method to the three GRBs analyzed in this paper, we find an age of 50 Myr for the galaxy hosting the GRB 120327A, 0.32 Gyr for the GRB081008 host and 15Myr for the GRB120815 host. These young ages indicate that we find GRBs to be "young" galaxies, namely galaxies in which the star formation is very active, probably suffering their first major star formation episode, but they are not necessary typical of the main population of galaxies that could have started forming stars already much before.

The result of GRB120815 is particularly important since it is the first time that abundances measured in hosts of GRBs indicate early type galaxies. Our result, if confirmed by more precise data, suggest that GRBs can be hosted in any type of galaxy suffering active star formation.

sources.summary: The paper finds the selected GRB 120815A target was not the host, detects an intervening z=1.539 galaxy in the spectra, and cites prior X-shooter host emission lines.

quotes.quote: In a late time observation of the field of GRB 120815A taken with the High Acuity Wide field K-band Imager (HAWK-I) a source is detected 1 1 north-west of the GRB afterglow position, and this was considered to be the host galaxy. However, the NIRSpec data show the detection of two sources spatially offset by 1 2, neither of which correspond to a galaxy at the redshift of GRB 120815A, at z=2.358 . The spectrum of the trace at the centre of the 2D spectrum resembles the Rayleigh-Jeans tail of a black body spectrum, implying that the targeted source is in fact a foreground star (see Fig. fig:grb120327A-grb120815A_spec , bottom panel). Precise astrometry was performed on the available imaging data of the GRB fields, and the lack of host galaxy detections in the case of GRB 120327A and GRB 120815A was therefore the result of the incorrect target being identified as the host galaxy in our imaging data, rather than due to an error in the positioning of the NIRSpec fixed slit.

The second trace detected in the 2D spectra taken for GRB 120815A shows a bright continuum with strong Balmer and metal emission lines at observer wavelengths consistent with a galaxy at z=1.539 . This galaxy is therefore likely the emission counterpart to a strong intervening absorption line system that was detected in the GRB afterglow spectrum at the same redshift klf+13 .

Emission lines from the host galaxy of GRB 120815A were previously detected with X-shooter kmf+15 , and the lack of detection in our NIRSpec data is therefore due to a mistake in the target that we selected to observe rather than a lack of sensitivity.

sources.summary: The paper classifies the GRB 120815A host as a massive, strongly star-forming elliptical galaxy whose high alpha ratios imply an early-type system about 10 Myr old.

quotes.quote: GRB 120815A In Figure f:120815 are compared the abundance patterns for the reference models with the observed data for this host. The [ /Fe ] ratios (where are Si and S ) are very high: this feature is an indicator of an early type galaxy. Such hypothesis tends to be confirmed by the [ Zn/Fe ] observed ratio, due to the claimed Zn correlation (see GRB 050820). The massive ( 10^ 12 M_ ) and strongly star forming ( =25 Gyr^ 1 ) elliptical galaxy model (E m ), in fact, was resulted to be the best in explaining this GRB host galaxy, though the observed [ S/Fe ] remains still too high than what predicted. In this scenario, the host age was found to be 10 Myr .

sources.summary: The paper uses GRB 120815A to probe a high-redshift star-forming host galaxy, finding a strong DLA with substantial molecular hydrogen, representative metallicity, dust depletion, and extinction.

quotes.quote: The galactic environment of GRB 120815A is characterized by a strong DLA with (N ( )/cm^ 2 ) = 21.95 0.10 , prominent H _2 absorption in the Lyman-Werner bands ( (N (H_2)/cm^ 2 ) = 20.54 0.13 ) and thus a molecular gas fraction f( H _2)=-1.14 0.15 .

The bright afterglow emission, observed through efficient instruments at large telescopes, provides a detailed probe of the physical properties of the inter-stellar medium in a high-redshift star-forming galaxy.

The sightline towards GRB 120815A is characterized by a strong DLA with (N( )/ cm ^ 2 ) = 21.95 0.10 and substantial amount of molecular hydrogen with a molecular fraction f of 7 ( f( H _2)=-1.14 0.15 ), characteristic of Galactic diffuse clouds.

gmw_00003824.name: VUCD7
gmw_00003824.overview: VUCD7 is an ultracompact dwarf galaxy characterized by a dual, core-plus-halo structure [arXiv:1408.2558]. A single Sersic fit measures its size at approximately 100 parsecs, whereas its core size is about 10 parsecs [arXiv:1408.2558].

sources.summary: The paper uses VUCD7 as an example where a core-plus-halo structure can inflate a single-component size and apparent dynamical-to-stellar mass ratio.

quotes.quote: For example, VUCD7 is known to have a dual, core plus halo, structure. From a single Sersic fit its size is measured to be R _e 100 pc (Evstigneeva et al. 2007) and we calculate an apparent mass ratio of 19 (not shown in Figure 7). However, if we were to use the core size of R _e 10 pc and the stellar mass of the core, then its mass ratio would reduce to around 6. We note that our stellar population fit to the colours of VUCD7 are poor and therefore the dynamical-to-stellar mass ratio is uncertain.

gmw_00003825.name: NGC 3963
gmw_00003825.overview: NGC 3963 is a grand-design spiral galaxy that hosts numerous H II regions [arXiv:2105.06203]. The galaxy has a distorted outer southern spiral arm, which may be influenced by its nearby companion galaxy NGC 3958 [arXiv:2105.06203].

sources.summary: The paper studies H II regions in NGC 3963, describing it as a distorted grand-design spiral with abundance peculiarities attributed to external gas accretion.

quotes.quote: We selected for spectroscopic study the Milky Way-type galaxy NGC 3963 and NGC 7292 which is similar to Large Magellanic Cloud by size and morphology (Fig. figure:map , Table table:sample ). Both galaxies host numerous H ii regions. NGC 3963 is a rather symmetric grand-design galaxy with distorted outer part of southern spiral arm. It has a companion of comparable mass, NGC 3958, which is located at the distance of 7.7 arcmin (110 kpc) to the south from NGC 3963. Tidal distortions have been found in both galaxies of the pair moorsel1983 .

H ii regions in the outer part of southern spiral arm of NGC 3963 at distances >0.7R_ 25 show constant, slightly higher oxygen and nitrogen abundances. This can be explained by the inflow of metal-enriched gas into the south-western part of NGC 3963.

gmw_00003826.name: NGC 7292
gmw_00003826.overview: NGC 7292 is a barred irregular galaxy of the Large Magellanic Cloud type [arXiv:2309.02360, arXiv:2105.06203]. Its bar-centered kinematic center is offset from its photometric center [arXiv:2309.02360], and its flat chemical abundance gradients suggest a past major merger [arXiv:2105.06203].

sources.summary: The paper focuses on NGC 7292, finding a bar-centered kinematic center offset from the photometric center, significant bar-driven radial motions, and complex star-forming bar/disk structure.

quotes.quote: The paper presents results of studying the kinematics of the ionized gas in the galaxy of the Large Magellanic Cloud type NGC 7292 obtained with the 2.5-m telescope of the Caucasian Mountain Observatory (CMO SAI MSU) and the 6-m BTA telescope of the Special Astrophysical Observatory (SAO RAS). Analysis of the velocity ﬁelds of the ionized and neutral hydrogen showed that the kinematic center of NGC 7292 located at the center of the bar, northwest of the photometric center of the galaxy (the southeastern end of the bar) previously taken as the center of NGC 7292. In addition to the circular rotation of the gas, the radial motions associated with the bar play a significant role in the kinematics of the disk.

One of these star systems is a nearby but relatively poorly-studied galaxy of the IBm type (according to the NED http://ned.ipac.caltech.edu database) NGC 7292 (Fig. fig:map1 ). The galaxy is prominent with a heavy bar displaced from the disk. At the end of the bar, large star-forming regions are located, which are the brightest areas in the short-wavelength optical bands ( U and B ) and in the H line (Fig. fig:map1 ). The southeastern part of the bar is usually taken as the galaxy's center (see, for example, the NED database), although, , based on multicolour surface photometry data, assumed that the center of the bar is the center of NGC 7292.

sources.summary: The paper studies H II regions in NGC 7292, describing it as a barred irregular galaxy with flat abundance gradients interpreted as a major-merger signature.

quotes.quote: NGC 7292 is an irregular galaxy with a strong bright bar. The galaxy looks like a flower with three petals with the bar between two of them (Fig. figure:map ).

Chemical elements abundances in NGC 7292 are constant and do not depend on the galactocentric distance. These peculiarities can be explained in terms of external gas accretion in the case of NGC 3963 and major merging for NGC 7292.

Oxygen and nitrogen abundances do not decrease with galactocentric distance in NGC 7292. However, the nitrogen-to-oxygen ratio seems to decrease along the major axis from the eastern to the western part of the galaxy.

gmw_00003827.name: ID 9992
gmw_00003827.overview: ID 9992 is an ambiguous possible double source consisting of two components separated by only 0.15 arcseconds [arXiv:2306.02467]. One component is photometrically consistent with a high-redshift dropout galaxy at z ~ 10, while the other is more consistent with a lower redshift solution at z = 1.962 [arXiv:2306.02467].

sources.summary: The paper treats ID 9992 as an ambiguous possible double source, with low-redshift emission lines and a possible second dropout galaxy at z about 10.

quotes.quote: ID 9992 -- possible double source This object was targeted as a z>8.5 candidate (Class 3). The Prism/CLEAR spectrum reveals a number of emission lines. The two most significant emission features are consistent with [ O iii ] and H at z=1.962 .

However, an emission line at 5.1 m could be H or [ O iii ] 5007 at z>9 , and this would be consistent with a tentative spectral break observed at 1.25 m being a Lyman- break. The NIRCam photometry reveals two components separated by only 0 15 (Figure fig:postage_mosaic ). One of these is photometrically consistent with a drop-out galaxy at z 10 , while the other (over which the central shutter is better placed) is more consistent with lower redshift solutions. We do not consider our high redshift solution from the spectrum to be highly robust, and Table tab:targets reports the low-redshift solution.

gmw_00003828.name: J1359+4011
gmw_00003828.overview: J1359+4011 is a flat-spectrum radio quasar and blazar located at a redshift of z = 0.407 [arXiv:1309.1158, arXiv:2104.04124]. It hosts a black hole with a high Eddington accretion rate and exhibits a persistent 120-to-150-day quasi-periodic oscillation in its radio flux [arXiv:1309.1158, arXiv:2104.04124].

sources.summary: The paper reports a persistent 15 GHz quasi-periodic oscillation from the FSRQ J1359+4011, identifies it as a z=0.407 source with a high-Eddington black hole, and describes unusual flux modulation.

quotes.quote: In this letter we describe the discovery of a remarkable and persistent quasi-periodic oscillation in the 15 GHz light curve of the flat-spectrum radio quasar CGRABS J1359+4011. The source J1359+4011 ( 13^ h 59^ m 38.1 s , +40^ d 11^ m 38.3 s J2000) is a high Galactic latitude ( b = 70.8^ ) flat-spectrum radio quasar (FSRQ) 2001MNRAS.323..757L,2005ApJ...626...95S at a redshift of z = 0.407 0.001 . It has been detected at X-ray energies using ROSAT 2001MNRAS.323..757L , but it is not visible at ray energies and has not been included in either of the Fermi catalogs 2010ApJS..188..405A,2012ApJS..199...31N . J1359+4011 is thought to contain a 10^8 M_ black hole with high Eddington ratio accretion (Table  tab:properties_of_J1359+4011 ).

The object has been observed slightly more often than once a week, on average, with occasional gaps in the light curve due to telescope maintenance or poor weather. A modulation of about 25 of the flux density is visible. This is the only source in the OVRO catalogue to show such a strong and persistent modulation.
We conclude that the modulation seen in the J1359+4011 light curve is intrinsic to the source.

sources.summary: The paper compares J1359+4011 as a blazar whose 120-150 day radio QPO resembles J0849+5108's period and has been explained by Lightman-Eardley secular instability.

quotes.quote: In CGRABS J1359+4011, a QPO exhibiting 150 day period was reported by King2013 .
The 176-day period is similar to that of the QPO in the blazar J1359+4011, determined to be 120 -150 days King2013 .
The Lightman-Eardley secular instability Lightman1974 , applicable to the black hole systems with high Eddington accretion rates, was suggested to explain the QPO case of J1359+4011.

gmw_00003829.name: NGC 5792
gmw_00003829.overview: NGC 5792 is a barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:2108.04461] that contains a pseudobulge [arXiv:2108.04461] and is highly inclined with respect to the plane of the sky [arXiv:1405.5876]. The galaxy hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2202.13225] and has been identified with a radio nuclear source [arXiv:2202.13225].

sources.summary: The paper highlights NGC 5792 as the most highly inclined sample galaxy with a measurable pitch angle, possible only because its image resolution is unusually high.

quotes.quote: NGC 5792 has the highest inclination angle amongst the galaxies with measurable pitch angles from the volume-limited sample, with an inclination angle i = 80.44^ Calculated as the arccosine of the axial ratio of the minor to major axes of the galaxy. with respect to the plane of the sky. It is important to note that this is an extreme case, and that pitch angle recovery is usually not possible for galaxies with this inclination. Only galaxies with very high resolution images, like NGC 5792, can hope to have their pitch angles determined when they are so highly inclined.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC5792 as a barred galaxy without reported velocity dispersion, uses low n and B/T plus bar evidence, and classifies its bulge as a pseudobulge.

quotes.quote: In addition, the galaxies M83 and NGC5792, lacking of reported velocity dispersion data, do not fit into either of the two bulge categories. These galaxies have n<2 and do follow the KR, but to assign a classification, we considered less established properties of pseudobulges, such as B/T ratio and the presence of bars within the bulge region Fisher-Drory_2008 , since M83 (SAB) and NGC5792 (SB) are barred galaxies and have B/T 0.20 . Hence, our final classification is that the bulges in M83 and NGC5792 are pseudobulges.

sources.summary: The paper lists NGC 5792 among three newly identified radio nuclear-source galaxies, notes no X-ray data for it, and cites independent evidence that it harbours an AGN.

quotes.quote: Three new nuclear sources have been identified from the radio cross-correlation: NGC 3877, NGC 4192, and NGC 5792 (Table t:new_AGNs ).

The two nuclear sources that were not previously identified as AGN candidates are NGC 4192 and NGC 3877, both of which were identified as `nuclear' in Table t:new_AGNs from radio data alone The third source in that table, NGC 5792, had no X-ray data and so does not appear in Table t:cross_LCX . .
However, recently, have independently argued that the galaxy, NGC 5792, harbours an AGN.

gmw_00003830.name: IC 4290
gmw_00003830.overview: IC 4290 is a barred galaxy characterized by an inner, oval-shaped ring [arXiv:astro-ph/0309583]. This ring features non-smooth angular isophotes with distinct breaks or corners, and the galaxy displays a visible T-shaped structure associated with its bar [arXiv:astro-ph/0309583].

sources.summary: The paper presents IC 4290 as an inner-ring barred galaxy whose oval ring has breaks or corners, non-smooth angular isophotes, and a visible T-like bar-related structure.

quotes.quote: In Fig. rgal b we show IC 4290 which has an oval ring with characteristic breaks or corners. These are indicated with arrows on the overplotted isophotes. The outermost isophote is again oval-shaped and even slightly pointed close to the major axis. The oval, however, is not smooth and forms, or gives the impression of, angles. One can also discern a structure in the form of an `` T '' with the vertical part of the symbol being the bar.

gmw_00003831.name: NGC 3367
gmw_00003831.overview: NGC 3367 is a barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1901.11197] that hosts a rare pentagonal inner pseudo-ring formed by segments of its spiral arms [arXiv:astro-ph/0309583]. Its star formation efficiency is highest in the central region, followed by the bar, and is lowest within the spiral arms [arXiv:1901.11197].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3367 as a representative barred spiral whose central region has the highest SFE, with bar SFE below the center but above spiral arms.

quotes.quote: In order to investigate the physical origin of the high SFE within the inner radii ( /r_ 25 < 0.3 ) further, we examine SFE maps for two representative SB galaxies in our sample, NGC 3367 and NGC 7479, as shown in figure 12. The ellipse of /r_ 25 = 0.3 seems to cover not only the central region but also a part of the disk, mainly the bar. For NGC 3367, we found that SFEs are (5 -- 8) 10^ 9 yr ^ 1 in the central region, (2 -- 4) 10^ 9 yr ^ 1 at /r_ 25 of 0.1 -- 0.3 (corresponding to the bar), and (1 -- 2) 10^ 9 yr ^ 1 at /r_ 25 > 0.3 (corresponding to spiral arms) with the typical uncertainty of 0.5 10^ 9 yr ^ 1 . This indicates that SFE in the bar is lower than that in the central region, but higher than in spiral arms.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3367 as a rare pentagonal inner pseudo-ring case, describing its ring as made of spiral-arm segments and explaining why such pentagonal rings should be uncommon.

quotes.quote: The ring in Fig. rgal has an almost pentagonal geometry. It is observed in an image of NGC 3367. It can be described as a pseudo-ring, since the ring appears to be made of segments of spiral arms.
For these two reasons pentagonal rings like in the case of NGC 3367 should be rare.

gmw_00003832.name: NGC 7020
gmw_00003832.overview: NGC 7020 is an early-type ringed galaxy classified as type (R)SAB(?)0/a [arXiv:1004.5063]. It features a bright, stellar inner hexagonal ring with major-axis cusps [arXiv:1004.5063, arXiv:astro-ph/0309583], as well as a detached outer ring that coincides with a ring of neutral hydrogen gas [arXiv:1004.5063, arXiv:2304.00515].

sources.summary: A striking early-type ringed galaxy whose outer ring emits H-alpha, while its inner hexagonal zone and ansae are mainly stellar dynamical features.

quotes.quote: NGC 7020 (Figure img6 , top row). This galaxy is classified as type (R)SAB(?)0/a. Although exceedingly regular and well-defined, the interpretation of its bright inner hexagonal zone as an inner ring and/or a bar is uncertain (Buta 1990b; Buta et al. 2007). The B band image shows that the large outer ring is completely detached from the inner hexagonal zone of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 7020 as the notable hexagonal inner-ring case, with major-axis cusps and corners, and links the rarity of such rings to tangent-bifurcation orbit families.

quotes.quote: Finally in Fig. rgal d we give a deprojected image of NGC 7020 bu90 , which has a type of hexagonal ring with cusps on the major axis of the bar and two sides parallel to it. The ``corners'' are indicated with arrows.

The rarity of inner rings of the hexagonal type seen in NGC 7020 bu90 reflects the way family s is introduced in the system through the tangent bifurcation. The stable branch of this bifurcation is associated with the formation of ovals, while the unstable one is associated with `NGC 7020-type' hexagons.

sources.summary: The paper reports that NGC 7020 has inner and outer optical rings, an HI ring coincident with the outer ring, and fairly ordered rotation.

quotes.quote: NGC 7020: The top left panels in Fig. fig:mosaic2 show the optical and the 1 velocity maps for the galaxy NGC 7020. From the optical image it is seen that this galaxy has an inner ring and an outer ring. A ring of 1 gas coincides with the location of the outer ring and the 1 velocity map shows fairly ordered rotation.

gmw_00003833.name: IC 4612
gmw_00003833.overview: IC 4612 is a galaxy classified as both a star-forming galaxy and a narrow-line active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2010.11196]. It hosted the supernova SN 2011cc and exhibits variability attributed to its central active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2010.11196].

sources.summary: The paper identifies IC 4612 as the host of SN 2011cc, classifies it as star-forming and narrow-line AGN, and attributes many false precursor detections to AGN variability.

quotes.quote: A large number of false detections is found at the position of SN 2011cc, which exploded at a distance of 2.4'' from the center of its host galaxy. The host, IC 4612, is classified as a star-forming galaxy in the SDSS catalog ahumada2019 and as a narrow-line AGN by liu2011 . We therefore hypothesize that the variability observed in ZTF data is caused by AGN activity. Requiring a reduced ^2 of <1.4 at the SN location removes most detections, because the background AGN is slightly offset from the SN position.

gmw_00003834.name: SDSS J141457.72-002058.6
gmw_00003834.overview: SDSS J141457.72-002058.6 is an elliptical galaxy associated with radio and X-ray emissions [arXiv:1811.07689]. Its broadband spectral properties suggest it is a candidate high synchrotron peaked blazar [arXiv:1811.07689].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J141457.72-002058.6 as an elliptical galaxy and possible HSP blazar candidate within the Source A neutrino/gamma-ray region.

quotes.quote: In particular, based on its radio/X-ray positional association and flux intensity, one of the most interesting neutrino source candidates within this sky region is the source labeled as 3 in Fig. fig4 (R.A., Decl. (J2000)=213.74038, -0.34967 deg). The radio and X-ray emissions are positionally consistent with the elliptical galaxy SDSS J141457.72-002058.6, whose broadband spectral properties resemble those ones typical of a 1 high synchrotron peaked (HSP) blazar (see Fig. fig4 , right panel ) 1 Indeed, this object have been recently included in the 3rd edition of the extreme and HSP blazars catalog as 3HSP J141457.7-002058 3HSP .

gmw_00003835.name: 3FGL J0627.9-1517
gmw_00003835.overview: 3FGL J0627.9-1517 is classified as a high synchrotron peaked blazar [arXiv:1811.07689]. It exhibits a low steady average gamma-ray flux above 100 MeV and has shown no sustained activity above 1 GeV [arXiv:1811.07689].

sources.summary: The paper lists 3FGL J0627.9-1517 as an HSP BL Lac candidate for Source B, notes its low steady gamma-ray flux, and finds no sustained GeV activity.

quotes.quote: Among them, we found two flat spectrum radio quasars (FSRQ), one 3FGL source, 3FGL J0627.9-1517, and three possible blazars of the HBL sub-class. Details of each of the 10 sources are reported in Table ic170321_candidates . Assuming the HBL sub-class of blazars as one of the most promising neutrino emitter candidates 2016MNRAS.457.3582P, 2017MNRAS.468..597R , one of the most interesting source within the SkyExplorer search radius appears to be the 3FGL J0627.9-1517 source ( 6 in Fig. fig5 , right panel ), which has been recently classified as the high synchrotron peaked (HSP) blazar 2WHSP J062753.2-151956 2017A&A...598A..17C . The steady average 3FGL flux above 100 MeV for this source is below 2 10^ 8 ph cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 . The gamma-ray light curve above 1 GeV produced with the FERMI -LAT online data analysis tool available at SSDC https://tools.asdc.asi.it/? searchtype=fermi , with a 7-day binning, did not show any relevant activity in the six months around the neutrino event T _0 , except for one little peak found some days after which it was not confirmed by a further analysis made with the official FERMI Science Tools (v10r0p5) https://fermi.gsfc.nasa.gov/ssc/data/analysis/software/ .

gmw_00003836.name: Mrk 290
gmw_00003836.overview: Mrk 290 is a spiral Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:1008.5079, arXiv:2107.11631] hosting a luminous active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2311.13135] with a compact, flat-spectrum radio core [arXiv:2307.13599]. The system exhibits complex ionized X-ray absorbers [arXiv:1008.5079, arXiv:1205.4222] and a fluctuating ionizing continuum [arXiv:1008.5079].

sources.summary: The paper treats Mrk 290 as a luminous AGN whose flat X-ray spectrum requires hybrid coronal electrons and a substantial non-thermal magnetic-energy fraction.

quotes.quote: However, there were four objects (Ton 730, Mrk 290, SBS 1136 + 594, and Mrk 1310) that were not well reproduced because their X-ray spectra are excessively flat. The X-ray spectra of these objects can be fitted by a power-law with photon indices _ 2-10 keV in the range of 1.53 - 1.64 . The accretion rates of these objects are around m 0.04-0.18 . Besides, PG 1138+222 is also included due to its accretion rate m =0.07 and its X-ray spectrum fitted by a power-law with photon index _ X =1.92^ +0.07 _ 0.07 2019MNRAS.487.3884C . In this section, we apply our model to explain the observational spectra of these AGNs in the optical/UV (UVOT) and X-ray ( 2 - 10 keV) wavelengths.

The modeling result for each object is plotted by the thick black line in Figure fig:fitting . The parameters are noted as m=M_ BH /M_ and m = M / M _ Edd . For Ton 730, Mrk 290, and PG 1138+222, their observed luminosity ratios L_ 2-10 keV /L_ UVOT are 0.26, 0.45, and 0.34, respectively. The number of f_ th is around 0.5 - 0.6 . In other words, more than 40 of the magnetic energy in the corona is allocated to the non-thermal electrons.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Mrk 290 as a bright Seyfert 1 galaxy with complex ionized X-ray absorbers, variable ionizing continuum, pressure-equilibrated warm gas, and likely torus-wind absorption.

quotes.quote: We present a detailed analysis of the High Energy Transmission Grating Spectrometer (HETGS) and high resolution spectra of the bright Seyfert 1 galaxy, Mrk 290. The HETGS spectra reveal complex absorption features that can be best described by a combination of three ionized absorbers. The outflow velocities of these warm absorbers are about 450 km s^ 1 , consistent with the three absorption components found in a previous far UV study. The ionizing continuum of Mrk 290 fluctuated by a factor of 1.4 during observations on a time scale of 17 days.

The three ionization components lie on the stable branch of the thermal equilibrium curve, indicating roughly the same gas pressure. Therefore the thermal wind from the torus is most likely the origin of warm absorbing gas in Mrk 290.

sources.summary: The paper includes Markarian 290 from Chandra and XMM-Newton grating analyses, using the Chandra HETG spectrum and two significant XSTAR outflow components.

quotes.quote: Finally, the last AGN in our sample, Markarian 290, is taken from . We note these authors use both Chandra and XMM-Newton grating spectra in their analysis. However, we only include the Chandra HETG spectra in our analysis. This particular spectrum had three coadded observations giving an exposure time of 166 ksec. The two XSTAR grid components that were statistically significant spanned a range of ionization states from = 1.62 0.15 to = 2.45 0.04 and a range of velocities from v_ out = 450 30 to v_ out =540 150 km s ^ 1 , respectively.

sources.summary: PG1534+580 has a flat-spectrum Gaia-aligned core and a steeper, lower-brightness extended component farther from Gaia, interpreted as possible outflow emission partly resolved out by VLBA.

quotes.quote: PG1534+580 has two components. The core component (C1) has a flat slope ( _ total = -0.4 ), a high T_ B ( > 10^ 6.7 K), and is consistent with the Gaia position ( 6.2 mas). The extended component (C2) has a steep slope ( _ total < -1.0 ), a lower T_ B ( 10^ 6.6 K), and is farther away from the Gaia position ( 10 mas), which is possibly an outflow emission. The extended emission is in a scale of being resolved out with the VLBA and unresolved with the VLA, as indicated by the VLBA core/total flux ratio > 0.6 and the VLBA/VLA flux ratio < 0.1 . This object shows a compact core with a flux density of 1.8-2.2 mJy at 4.8 GHz with the VLA A configuration Kellermann1989,Leipski2006 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J153552.40 + 575409.5 as an extended Seyfert source whose 1.4 GHz core is slightly resolved, while 5.0 GHz archive imaging shows it as unresolved.

quotes.quote: SDSS J153552.40 + 575409.5 -- This object is also classified as Mrk 290 (PG1534 + 580) at z = 0.0296 (NED) in the literature. The radio core is slightly resolved at 1.4 GHz. An image using archive VLA A array configuration data at 5.0 GHz (project-code AF0402, observe-date 2003-Jun-17) shows the source to be unresolved. The integrated spectral index using FIRST survey image and this archive data, _ 1.4 GHz ^ 5.0 GHz = -0.73 0.04 , typical of many other Seyfert galaxies Laletal2011 .

sources.summary: The paper describes J 1535 as a confirmed MaNGA AGN with compact [FeVII] emission to 1.3 kpc, nuclear-like CL distribution, and a slightly high measured CLR temperature.

quotes.quote: We measure [ FeVII ] emission in four MPL-8 galaxies: J 1454 ( [ FeVII ] 3760 ; z = 0.13; elliptical; merger), J 1349 ( [ FeVII ] 3760 ; z = 0.02; no GZ2 classification), J 0906 ( [ FeVII ] 3586 ; z = 0.11; spiral; merger), and J 1535 ( [ FeVII ] 6086 ; z = 0.03; spiral).

For J 1535 (a confirmed AGN in the MANGA AGN catalog), we detect [ FeVII ] 6086 emission out to 1.3 0.76 kpc. The CL distribution in J 1535 strongly resembles the nuclear-bound [ NeV ] 3347,3427 distributions (Section nevsection ).
However, 2/4 feature temperatures slightly above the 20,000 K threshold (for J 1535 , we determine a temperature of 21,088 K in the single spaxel we could measure).

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 290 as an AGN with a far-UV spectral break, complex X-ray warm absorption, and corresponding FUSE UV absorption near the predicted Ly region.

quotes.quote: Mrk 290 is a typical example of an AGN with a spectral break in the far UV that extrapolates directly to the soft X-ray Shang05 . It also has a complex warm absorber in the X-ray Zhang11 with corresponding UV absorption lines Zhang15 visible in the FUSE spectrum. The predicted location of Ly falls near the gap in the FUSE LiF1 detector, which is why the S/N drops abruptly at 1085 .

sources.summary: The paper reports PG 1534+580/Mrk 290 as unresolved in prior 5-GHz VLA imaging, with no published VLBI detection and no detection in the authors' VLBA image.

quotes.quote: PG 1534+580 (Mrk 290) PG 1534+580 (Mrk 290) was unresolved in the VLA-A image at 5 GHz 1994AJ....108.1163K . No VLBI detection of PG 1534+580 has been reported in literature. The source is not detected in our VLBA image.

gmw_00003837.name: SBS 1136 + 594
gmw_00003837.overview: SBS 1136 + 594 is a Seyfert 1.5 galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0612276] that hosts a luminous active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2311.13135]. This active nucleus exhibits a flat X-ray spectrum [arXiv:2311.13135] and displays a large amount of intrinsic reddening [arXiv:astro-ph/0612276].

sources.summary: SBS 1136+594 is treated as a ROSAT/2MASS Sy 1.5 with weak lines, AGN diagnostic ratios, strong reddening, and discrepant black-hole mass estimates.

quotes.quote: SBS 1136+594 This AGN is a ROSAT and 2MASS source. It is classified as a Sy 1.5 galaxy in the SBS catalogue and by goncalves . The \ lines are very weak ( / = 0.2), a characteristic already reported by martel and goncalves ( / = 0.25 and 0.1, respectively).

Comparing the broad and narrow components of the Balmer lines, the flux arising from the BLR is approximately 15 times the flux from the NLR, and the line ratios lie in the AGN region of the diagnostic diagrams (Fig. fig:diagnos ). The large / \ line ratio of 10.88 indicates the strength of the ultraviolet AGN continuum. However, the / \ ratio (7.83) is also the highest in the sample, denoting a large amount of intrinsic reddening (see Tables tab:ratios and tab:redden ). The \ FWHM has a value of only 200 . Hence the BH mass estimated from the \ FWHM ( 4 10^6 M _ ) is rather humble for a Seyfert galaxy, and it is also a factor approximately 44 smaller than the mass estimated from the \ width (Table tab:masses ).

sources.summary: The paper includes SBS 1136 + 594 among luminous AGNs with flat X-ray spectra and adopts a smaller thermal-energy fraction to reproduce its emission.

quotes.quote: However, there were four objects (Ton 730, Mrk 290, SBS 1136 + 594, and Mrk 1310) that were not well reproduced because their X-ray spectra are excessively flat. The X-ray spectra of these objects can be fitted by a power-law with photon indices _ 2-10 keV in the range of 1.53 - 1.64 . The accretion rates of these objects are around m 0.04-0.18 . Besides, PG 1138+222 is also included due to its accretion rate m =0.07 and its X-ray spectrum fitted by a power-law with photon index _ X =1.92^ +0.07 _ 0.07 2019MNRAS.487.3884C . In this section, we apply our model to explain the observational spectra of these AGNs in the optical/UV (UVOT) and X-ray ( 2 - 10 keV) wavelengths.

For SBS 1136+594 and Mrk 1310, their observed luminosity ratio L_ 2-10 keV /L_ UVOT are 0.81 and 1.15, respectively. A smaller f_ th number ( 0.2-0.3 ) is adopted to reproduce their flat X-ray spectra.

gmw_00003838.name: PG 1138+222
gmw_00003838.overview: PG 1138+222 is a luminous active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2311.13135]. It features a flat X-ray power-law spectrum and has an observed 2–10 keV to optical/UV luminosity ratio of 0.34 [arXiv:2311.13135].

sources.summary: The paper adds PG 1138+222 as a luminous AGN with a flat X-ray power-law spectrum and fits it with a hybrid thermal/non-thermal corona model.

quotes.quote: Besides, PG 1138+222 is also included due to its accretion rate m =0.07 and its X-ray spectrum fitted by a power-law with photon index _ X =1.92^ +0.07 _ 0.07 2019MNRAS.487.3884C . In this section, we apply our model to explain the observational spectra of these AGNs in the optical/UV (UVOT) and X-ray ( 2 - 10 keV) wavelengths. We take the numbers of the black hole mass, the accretion rate, and the outer boundary radius for each object as same as those in , and make sure that the optical/UV spectral data of the AGNs can be well reproduced. Due to the lack of higher-energy data, we only apply our model to produce the hard X-ray emission in the energy band of 2 - 10 keV , and set the upper limit of the electron Lorentz factor less than 50 .

The modeling result for each object is plotted by the thick black line in Figure fig:fitting . The parameters are noted as m=M_ BH /M_ and m = M / M _ Edd . For Ton 730, Mrk 290, and PG 1138+222, their observed luminosity ratios L_ 2-10 keV /L_ UVOT are 0.26, 0.45, and 0.34, respectively. The number of f_ th is around 0.5 - 0.6 . In other words, more than 40 of the magnetic energy in the corona is allocated to the non-thermal electrons.

gmw_00003839.name: Canes Venatici II
gmw_00003839.overview: Canes Venatici II is a low-mass ultra-faint dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1504.02480]. It experienced an extended star formation history, with the bulk of its stellar assembly occurring around redshift z ~ 2 and a star-forming burst commencing at z ~ 2.5 [arXiv:1504.02480].

sources.summary: The paper uses Canes Venatici II as a low-mass ultra-faint dwarf analogue, emphasizing its extended star formation, bulk stellar assembly near z 2, and later burst beginning around z 2.5.

quotes.quote: For example, Canes Venatici II formed stars until 8-11  Gyr ago, while Hercules and Leo IV formed 90  of their stellar mass by 11-12 Gyr.

This is consistent with the weisz14b star formation histories for systems such as Canes Venatici II, for which the best fit model formed the bulk of its stars at z 2 . The mass of Canes Venatici II is 1.4 10^6  M _ within the half-light radius wolf10 , suggesting that even very low-mass systems can form stars at the redshifts of the Cooke DLAs.

Leo T and Canes Venatici II have a more extended star formation history, although Leo T shows no star formation from z =1-5 . The best fit model to Canes Venatici II shows a burst of star formation commencing at z 2.5 , while the 1 uncertainty is consistent with 80 of the stars being formed in a single burst ending at z 2.5 .

gmw_00003840.name: DF X1
gmw_00003840.overview: DF X1 is an ultra-diffuse galaxy located in the Coma cluster [arXiv:1910.12867]. X-ray observations of the galaxy show no detections, which limits its dark matter halo mass and suggests it resembles a dwarf galaxy rather than a massive failed galaxy [arXiv:2009.07846].

sources.summary: The paper uses XMM-Newton limits for DF X1 to show its expected hot-gas and globular-cluster LMXB emission is absent, disfavoring a massive failed-L galaxy halo.

quotes.quote: In this work, I present Chandra and XMM-Newton observations of two archetypal UDGs, Dragonfly 44 and DF X1, and I constrain their dark matter halo mass based on the X-ray emission originating from hot gaseous emission and from the population of low-mass X-ray binaries (LMXBs) residing in GCs. Both Dragonfly 44 and DF X1 remain undetected in X-rays. The upper limits on the X-ray emission exclude the possibility that these galaxies reside in massive ( M_ vir 5 10^ 11 \ M_ ) dark matter halos, suggesting that they are not failed L_ galaxies. These results demonstrate that even these iconic UDGs resemble to dwarf galaxies with M_ vir 10^ 11 \ M_ , implying that UDGs represent a single galaxy population.
In addition, I present the XMM-Newton data available for DF X1, and constrain the X-ray luminosity associated with this UDG.

sources.summary: DFX1 is one of two Coma ultra-diffuse galaxies used to test MRF; after compact-source subtraction, the UDGs become the brightest objects and are readily identifiable.

quotes.quote: We focus on a 12 4 22 9 section that contains two UDGs, Dragonfly 44 and DFX1 (see van Dokkum et al. 2017). The galaxies are not easily distinguished from other sources in the field, which is why van Dokkum et al. (2015) used a complex multi-stage process to eliminate faint objects from an initial candidate list of 6624. The right panel shows the same region after applying the mrf code, with default parameters. The two UDGs are now the two brightest objects in this region, and they can readily be identified using standard image detection software.

gmw_00003841.name: host of GRB 210905A
gmw_00003841.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 210905A is a high-redshift galaxy complex located at a redshift of z=6.3118 [arXiv:2211.16524]. This complex spans 360 km/s, consists of two major systems [arXiv:2211.16524], and exhibits a star formation rate of approximately 16 to 20 solar masses per year [arXiv:2202.04544, arXiv:2211.16524].

sources.summary: The paper treats the likely GRB 210905A host as a rare z>6 GRB host, estimating its HST brightness, UV luminosity, possible star-formation rate, and uncertainty from foreground contamination.

quotes.quote: A jet break at 46.2 16.3 d ( 6.3 2.2 d rest-frame) has been observed in the X-ray light curve; however, it is hidden in the H band due to a constant contribution from the host galaxy and potentially from a foreground intervening galaxy. In particular, the host galaxy is only the fourth GRB host at z>6 known to date.

The likely discovery of the host of GRB 210905A is a rare discovery, given that up to July 2022 only three hosts (those of GRBs 050904, 130606A, and 140515A) had been confirmed at z>6 McGuire2016a , and four if we consider the possible detection of the GRB 060522 host Tanvir2012a . The observed brightness of the source detected with HST in the F140W band corresponds to a rest-frame m_ 1900 21 mag, which is consistent with the characteristic magnitude at 1600 of z=6-7 galaxies e.g. Bouwens2021a . Therefore, such a galaxy is not unusual, although it is more luminous in the UV than galaxies that contribute the most to the star formation at these redshifts. A host galaxy at z=6.3 with such a brightness and thus a rest-frame UV luminosity of L_ =1.47 10^ 29 erg s ^ 1 Hz ^ 1 , would have a SFR 16 M_ yr ^ 1 using equation 1 in . If its brightness is confirmed with further observations, the host of 210905A would also be the brightest.

sources.summary: The paper identifies a z=6.3118 GRB host galaxy complex, measuring its absorption-line kinematics, metallicity, dust depletion, HST counterpart, and UV-derived star formation rate.

quotes.quote: We detected neutral hydrogen, low--ionization, high-ionization and fine-structure metal lines, likely from the GRB host galaxy complex at redshift z=6.3118 . We detected also absorption lines from, at least, two intervening systems (at z=5.7390 and z=2.8296 ). The z=6.3 complex spans 360 km s ^ 1 and is composed of two major systems ( A at z=6.3118 and B at z=6.3186 ) separated by 300 km s ^ 1 , and formed by six components. We studied the abundance patterns and determined the galaxy metallicity, the overall strength of dust depletion and the DTM of the complex. We found evidence of element overabundance, a dust-corrected metallicity of [ M/ H ] _ tot =-1.72 0.13 , and overall strength of the dust depletion [ Zn/Fe ] _ fit =0.33 0.09 , indicating a moderate dust depletion.

The afterglow position coincides with an object that we consider to be the GRB host galaxy. Its F140W magnitude is 25.66 0.05 mag (AB), corresponding to SFR _ UV 20 M_ yr ^ 1 (see Rossi2022 ). The afterglow contamination is considered as negligible by Rossi2022 .

gmw_00003842.name: F150D JWIDF E01
gmw_00003842.overview: F150D JWIDF E01 is an obscured galaxy located at an intermediate redshift of z ~ 2-4 [arXiv:2310.20675]. It is a dusty, star-forming system that lies on the galaxy main sequence and exhibits high obscuration [arXiv:2310.20675].

sources.summary: The paper selects F150D JWIDF E01 as a red JWST dropout candidate, detects its NOEMA continuum, and finds that FIR-constrained SED modelling favors a dusty star-forming galaxy at z 2-4 rather than z>10.

quotes.quote: We study four objects detected in JWST F200W, F356W, F444W imaging of the PEARLS survey Windhorst2023 and selected as ultra-high-redshift candidates based on their colours Yan2023_darkfield . The four targets of this work were chosen to be the brightest, but also the reddest in the sample (F150D JWIDF E01,F150D JWIDF H17, F200D JWIDF M03, F200D JWIDF H08), guaranteeing a SNR 10 detection at 250 GHz if their redshift is z 3-5 , or SNR > 100 if they are at z>10 (see further Section sec:SED ).

We show the continuum imaging of the targets in Fig. fig:noema_continuum , where all the sources are clearly detected at SNR 10 -15. No additional continuum sources are detected in the NOEMA field of view ( 30 ). The sources are only marginally resolved, and the difference between the aperture-integrated fluxes and that of the central pixel is at most 10-20 depending on the aperture size used.

Using BAGPIPES and CIGALE to fit their optical-to-FIR SED, we find that the four sources are galaxies at intermediate redshift ( z 2-4 ), with star-formation rates SFR 20-150 M_ yr ^ 1 , stellar masses 9.7 < M_* / [ M_ ] < 11.4 , and high obscuration ( 2 A_V 7 ). The most important conclusion is that these objects are obscured galaxies at z 2-4 that lie on the galaxy main sequence (Fig. fig:ms ), similarly to the HST-faint/JWST-detected objects reported by Barrufet2023 .

sources.summary: The paper selects F200D JWIDF H08 as a red JWST dropout candidate, detects its NOEMA continuum, and finds that FIR-constrained SED modelling favors a dusty star-forming galaxy at z 2-4 rather than z>10.

The four targets of this work were chosen to be the brightest, but also the reddest in the sample (F150D JWIDF E01,F150D JWIDF H17, F200D JWIDF M03, F200D JWIDF H08), guaranteeing a SNR 10 detection at 250 GHz if their redshift is z 3-5 , or SNR > 100 if they are at z>10 (see further Section sec:SED ).

The most important conclusion is that these objects are obscured galaxies at z 2-4 that lie on the galaxy main sequence (Fig. fig:ms ), similarly to the HST-faint/JWST-detected objects reported by Barrufet2023 .

gmw_00003843.name: F150D JWIDF H17
gmw_00003843.overview: F150D JWIDF H17 is a dusty star-forming galaxy located at an intermediate redshift of z ~ 2-4 [arXiv:2310.20675]. It is characterized by high obscuration, a stellar mass between 9.7 and 11.4 solar masses, and a position on the galaxy main sequence [arXiv:2310.20675].

sources.summary: The paper selects F150D JWIDF H17 as a red JWST dropout candidate, detects its NOEMA continuum, and finds that FIR-constrained SED modelling favors a dusty star-forming galaxy at z 2-4 rather than z>10.

quotes.quote: We study four objects detected in JWST F200W, F356W, F444W imaging of the PEARLS survey Windhorst2023 and selected as ultra-high-redshift candidates based on their colours Yan2023_darkfield . The four targets of this work were chosen to be the brightest, but also the reddest in the sample (F150D JWIDF E01,F150D JWIDF H17, F200D JWIDF M03, F200D JWIDF H08), guaranteeing a SNR 10 detection at 250 GHz if their redshift is z 3-5 , or SNR > 100 if they are at z>10 (see further Section sec:SED ).

We show the continuum imaging of the targets in Fig. fig:noema_continuum , where all the sources are clearly detected at SNR 10 -15. No additional continuum sources are detected in the NOEMA field of view ( 30 ). The sources are only marginally resolved, and the difference between the aperture-integrated fluxes and that of the central pixel is at most 10-20 depending on the aperture size used.

Using BAGPIPES and CIGALE to fit their optical-to-FIR SED, we find that the four sources are galaxies at intermediate redshift ( z 2-4 ), with star-formation rates SFR 20-150 M_ yr ^ 1 , stellar masses 9.7 < M_* / [ M_ ] < 11.4 , and high obscuration ( 2 A_V 7 ). The most important conclusion is that these objects are obscured galaxies at z 2-4 that lie on the galaxy main sequence (Fig. fig:ms ), similarly to the HST-faint/JWST-detected objects reported by Barrufet2023 .

gmw_00003844.name: F200D JWIDF M03
gmw_00003844.overview: F200D JWIDF M03 is a disk galaxy [arXiv:2210.03106] located at an intermediate redshift of z ~ 2-4 [arXiv:2310.20675]. It is classified as a highly obscured, dusty star-forming galaxy that lies on the galaxy main sequence [arXiv:2310.20675].

sources.summary: The paper describes COS4 04943-M3 122667 as a dynamical-mass outlier whose LEGA-C velocity gradients are underestimated because the slit misses the full rotation curve.

quotes.quote: A second object in our dynamical mass sample has an ionised gas-based dynamical mass estimate from LEGA-C data by , COS4 04943-M3 122667. This is the only object in our sample with a significantly higher dynamical mass measurement from compared to the M_ dyn measurement from the LEGA-C integrated stellar velocity dispersion ( M_ dyn =0.43 dex). This time, the estimate from the LEGA-C ionised gas data is comparable to the estimate from integrated stellar velocity dispersion, with a difference of only M_ dyn =0.04 dex. Closer inspection of the LEGA-C data and the pseudo-slit extractions reveals that the kinematic centre is offset by about 0.4 ( 2 pixels) from the central pixel in the LEGA-C 2D data (see Figure f:COS3_05062 ).

Based on this alignment, the LEGA-C date do not sample to full extent of the rotation curve. This galaxy has a dynamical mass estimate from that is higher by 0.44 dex compared to the LEGA-C estimate (see discussion in Section s:compmdyn ). The comparison of the and LEGA-C extracted kinematics suggests that the integrated stellar velocity dispersion might be underestimated, which could explain this large difference.

sources.summary: The paper selects F200D JWIDF M03 as a red JWST dropout candidate, detects its NOEMA continuum, and finds that FIR-constrained SED modelling favors a dusty star-forming galaxy at z 2-4 rather than z>10.

quotes.quote: We study four objects detected in JWST F200W, F356W, F444W imaging of the PEARLS survey Windhorst2023 and selected as ultra-high-redshift candidates based on their colours Yan2023_darkfield . The four targets of this work were chosen to be the brightest, but also the reddest in the sample (F150D JWIDF E01,F150D JWIDF H17, F200D JWIDF M03, F200D JWIDF H08), guaranteeing a SNR 10 detection at 250 GHz if their redshift is z 3-5 , or SNR > 100 if they are at z>10 (see further Section sec:SED ).

We show the continuum imaging of the targets in Fig. fig:noema_continuum , where all the sources are clearly detected at SNR 10 -15. No additional continuum sources are detected in the NOEMA field of view ( 30 ). The sources are only marginally resolved, and the difference between the aperture-integrated fluxes and that of the central pixel is at most 10-20 depending on the aperture size used.

Using BAGPIPES and CIGALE to fit their optical-to-FIR SED, we find that the four sources are galaxies at intermediate redshift ( z 2-4 ), with star-formation rates SFR 20-150 M_ yr ^ 1 , stellar masses 9.7 < M_* / [ M_ ] < 11.4 , and high obscuration ( 2 A_V 7 ). The most important conclusion is that these objects are obscured galaxies at z 2-4 that lie on the galaxy main sequence (Fig. fig:ms ), similarly to the HST-faint/JWST-detected objects reported by Barrufet2023 .

gmw_00003845.name: G1
gmw_00003845.overview: G1 is a late-type companion galaxy with somewhat irregular spiral arms [arXiv:astro-ph/9810276]. It exhibits strong H-alpha and mid-infrared emission indicating a young stellar population [arXiv:astro-ph/9810276], alongside a population of ultraluminous X-ray sources and active star formation [arXiv:2303.15502].

sources.summary: The paper describes G1 as the western late-type companion to the Cartwheel, with H I, H-alpha, and LW3 mid-infrared detections indicating a young stellar population.

quotes.quote: The Companions The morphological types of the two nearby companions of the Cartwheel are distinctly different. The eastern companion (G2) is an early type S0 galaxy. No H i is directly associated with this galaxy (Higdon ) and no star formation activity has been detected (Amram et al. ). On the contrary, the western companion (G1), a late type galaxy with somewhat irregular spiral arms, has 2.7x10 ^ 9 M _ of H i and emits strongly in H , which indicates the presence of a young stellar population (see Fig.1 of Amram et al.

The LW2 fluxes of the two galaxies, presented in Table phot , are weak but very similar. This is not true though for the LW3 emission where only the western companion (G1), is detected. This contrast on the LW2 over LW3 emission from the two companion galaxies is in agreement with our current understanding of the typical mid-IR signature in galaxies (Vigroux ).

sources.summary: The companion galaxy G1 is analyzed through its ULX population, cumulative XLF, variable-source fraction, inferred star formation rate, and unresolved X-ray emission component.

quotes.quote: Here, we address the study of a population of ULXs in the Cartwheel Galaxy -- the archetypal collisional ring galaxy -- and in the companion galaxies G1, G2, G3 that together form a compact group of 4 members SGC 0035-3357; Iovino2002 . For completeness, we construct the cumulative XLF for the galaxy G1 (see figure fig:CumXLFG1 ; the luminosities used are reported in table tab:netcont and highlighted in italic). Its shape is more similar to the Grimm2003 function for HMXBs, with a SFR of 10 M_ yr ^ 1 , suggesting a larger SFR than the value derived by Crivellari2009 of 5 M_ yr ^ 1 .

Three of the variable sources are in G1, out of 7 detected ULXs, which gives an higher fraction of variable sources of 43 per cent. In G1 there are 7 ULXs and, assuming a SFR of 5 M_ yr ^ 1 Crivellari2009 , we obtain 1.4 ULXs/( M_ yr ^ 1 ), a similar result to that for the Cartwheel. If instead the SFR is 10 M_ yr ^ 1 , as suggested by the XLF (see section sec:lum_func ), the number of ULXs/( M_ yr ^ 1 ) would reduce to 0.7, possibly indicating a metallicity 8.6 in G1 (see table 3 in Lehmer2021 ).

gmw_00003846.name: G2
gmw_00003846.overview: G2 is an early-type S0 companion galaxy in the compact group SGC 0035-3357 [arXiv:2303.15502, arXiv:astro-ph/9810276]. It has no detected star formation activity or associated neutral hydrogen [arXiv:astro-ph/9810276], but it hosts a possible nuclear X-ray source [arXiv:2303.15502].

sources.summary: The paper treats G2 as a Cartwheel-group companion with a possible nuclear source and analyzes its unresolved X-ray emission together with G3.

quotes.quote: Here, we address the study of a population of ULXs in the Cartwheel Galaxy -- the archetypal collisional ring galaxy -- and in the companion galaxies G1, G2, G3 that together form a compact group of 4 members SGC 0035-3357; Iovino2002 . In summary, the total number of sources positionally associated to the group galaxies, for which we can investigate variability, are 37 in total: Cartwheel has 29 ULXs; G1 has 7 ULXs (plus 1 possibly nuclear source); G2 has only 1 possibly nuclear source; G3 has 1 ULX (plus 1 possibly nuclear source). We detect 44 sources, 29 of which positionally associated to the Cartwheel galaxy (plus two with non-significant flux detections), 8 to G1 (of which 1 possibly linked to the nucleus), 1 to G2 (possibly linked to the nucleus) and 2 to G3 (of which 1 possibly linked to the nucleus).

We repeat the same procedure for G2 and G3. For these galaxies, we have not enough statistics to use complex models and indeed to fit more than one parameter, so we fixed the n_ H at 1.2 10 ^ 21 cm ^ 2 , the value derived from the average spectrum of the point-like sources, while we leave the power-law index free. The best-fitting power-law index and the unabsorbed luminosity for G2 and G3 are indicated in Table tab:fit_g1_g2_g3 . From the same table we can see that the non-resolved emission constitutes 64 and 56 per cent of the total emission, respectively for G2 and G3.

sources.summary: The paper describes G2 as the eastern S0 companion to the Cartwheel, noting weak mid-infrared flux, no associated H I, and no detected star formation activity.

quotes.quote: The Companions The morphological types of the two nearby companions of the Cartwheel are distinctly different. The eastern companion (G2) is an early type S0 galaxy. No H i is directly associated with this galaxy (Higdon ) and no star formation activity has been detected (Amram et al.

In early type galaxies the mid-IR emission is dominated by the old stellar population. The LW2 flux is typically 6 times stronger than the LW3 emission which is consistent with temperatures of 5000 K (Madden ). Since the LW2 emission of G2 is just 4 times stronger than the rms noise of the LW3 emission any expected stellar contribution to the LW3 filter measurement would be below our detection limit.

gmw_00003847.name: G3
gmw_00003847.overview: G3 is a companion galaxy in the compact group SGC 0035-3357, which also includes the Cartwheel galaxy [arXiv:2303.15502]. The galaxy hosts one ultraluminous X-ray source, a possible nuclear source, and unresolved X-ray emission [arXiv:2303.15502].
